title: [ds-review-iter-008] OklchColorArea pointermove spams RAF + no cleanup on drag-mid-unmount
description: File: OklchColorArea.svelte:104-117 — handlePointerMove wraps updateFromPosition in requestAnimationFrame but does NOT coalesce. Every pointermove schedules a new RAF → 120+/sec queued on high-refresh displays. Also no cleanup on component destroy: pointer capture survives unmount if mid-drag. Fix: (a) coalesce — store latestPointer = {x,y}, use rafPending flag so only one RAF in flight at a time; (b) add $effect return cleanup releasing pointer capture if held: 'return () => { if (canvas?.hasPointerCapture(id)) canvas.releasePointerCapture(id); }'. Rule: 04-motion.md §4 frame-rate independence + 06-performance.md RAF hygiene. This class of bug should become a new skill §'Pointer event coalescing in RAF'.
close_reason: Addressed in P0 rewrite per new ref 04 §Pointer event coalescing in RAF: rafPending guard + latestPointer cache — at most 1 RAF in flight. $effect return-cleanup releases pointer capture on unmount if drag active.

comments.author: brucemckay
comments.text:
SCOPE: End-to-end integration tests for form validation flows

GOAL: Test realistic multi-field form scenarios with validation

DEPENDS ON: Codex-8sp (form component unit tests)

INTEGRATION TEST SCENARIOS:

1. SIGNUP FORM FLOW:
   - User fills email, password, confirm password
   - Validates email format
   - Validates password strength (min length, special chars)
   - Validates passwords match
   - Shows field-specific errors
   - Prevents submit with errors
   - Allows submit when valid
   - Shows loading state during submission
   - Shows success message on completion

2. LOGIN FORM FLOW:
   - User fills email and password
   - Validates required fields
   - Shows error for invalid credentials
   - Remembers form state on error
   - Allows retry after error
   - Redirects on success

3. PROFILE EDIT FORM:
   - Pre-populates existing values
   - Validates on blur (not on every keystroke)
   - Shows unsaved changes warning
   - Allows cancel (resets to original)
   - Shows validation summary
   - Partial save (only changed fields)

4. MULTI-STEP FORM (if implemented):
   - Navigation between steps
   - Validates before allowing next step
   - Preserves data across steps
   - Summary/review step
   - Final validation before submit

5. ASYNC VALIDATION:
   - Username availability check (debounced)
   - Email uniqueness check
   - Shows loading indicator during check
   - Caches validation results

TESTING APPROACH:
Use Playwright for integration tests (not Vitest):
- Real browser environment
- Network mocking (MSW or Playwright route mocking)
- Test complete user journey

TEST PATTERNS:
- page.fill() to enter values
- page.click() to submit
- page.waitForSelector() for error messages
- page.waitForNavigation() for redirects
- Expect assertions on visible errors

FILES TO CREATE:
- apps/web/e2e/forms/signup.spec.ts
- apps/web/e2e/forms/login.spec.ts
- apps/web/e2e/forms/profile-edit.spec.ts

MOCK API RESPONSES:
- Success responses
- Validation error responses (field-specific)
- Network errors
- Timeout scenarios

DELIVERABLE:
- 5+ integration test scenarios
- Covers critical user paths
- Validates form UX patterns
- Documents expected behavior

title: [ds-review-iter-024] P0 SECURITY: Logo upload bypasses SVG sanitization — stored XSS vector via malicious SVG
description: VERIFIED P0 stored XSS in logo upload path. Malicious SVG uploaded by an org admin lives on R2 with 1-year cache, accessible at public CDN URL. CHAIN (all verified by direct read): 1. multipart-procedure.ts:282-309 (validateFiles): - Checks MIME type STRING against allowedMimeTypes ['image/png','image/jpeg','image/webp','image/svg+xml'] - Checks file size against maxSize - Does NOT check magic numbers (a JPEG could be renamed to .svg with forged Content-Type) - Does NOT call sanitizeSvgContent() - Does NOT call validateLogoUpload() 2. workers/organization-api/src/routes/settings.ts:282-314: - multipartProcedure handler receives ctx.files.logo (the passthrough from above) - Passes raw {buffer, mimeType, size} directly to ctx.services.settings.uploadLogo() - No sanitization step 3. packages/platform-settings/src/services/branding-settings-service.ts:254-310 (uploadLogo): - JSDoc at lines 248-251 claims 'File validation (MIME type, size, magic numbers, SVG sanitization) handled by @codex/validation validateLogoUpload() before calling this method' - No code calls validateLogoUpload() anywhere in the chain — grep confirms zero callers - Line 279: r2.put(r2Path, buffer, undefined, { contentType: mimeType, cacheControl: 'public, max-age=31536000' }) - 1-year cache means once uploaded, the malicious content persists 4. sanitizeSvgContent() DOES EXIST at packages/validation/src/primitives.ts:356 using DOMPurify 5. ImageProcessingService at packages/image-processing/src/service.ts:365 DOES call sanitizeSvgContent — but uploadLogo does NOT go through ImageProcessingService EXPLOIT: - Attacker registers an org (or is a legitimate org owner with misuse intent) - Uploads SVG containing as logo - SVG stored at https://cdn.codex/logos/{orgId}/logo.svg with 1-year cache - When another user visits the SVG URL directly (e.g., phishing link, admin preview, embed via
notes: Closed in commit cbd7dbf8. Full writeup (root cause, fix, tests, verification) at docs/brand-editor-investigation/implementation-summary.md — see the bead's named section.
close_reason: Fixed in commit

comments.author: brucemckay
comments.text:
## WP-4: Design System Implementation Complete

### Token Architecture Implemented

Created a complete 3-tier CSS token system following Material Design / Bootstrap hybrid approach:

**Files Created:**
- `apps/web/src/app.css` - Main entry point
- `apps/web/src/lib/theme/reset.css` - Browser normalization
- `apps/web/src/lib/theme/tokens/primitives.css` - Tier 1
- `apps/web/src/lib/theme/tokens/semantic.css` - Tier 2
- `apps/web/src/lib/theme/tokens/components.css` - Tier 3
- `apps/web/src/lib/theme/tokens/dark.css` - Dark mode
- `apps/web/src/lib/theme/base.css` - Element defaults
- `apps/web/src/lib/theme/utilities.css` - Helper classes

### Color Palette
- **Primary**: Terracotta (#e07850) - Warmth-first aesthetic
- **Accent**: Amber (#f59e0b)
- **Neutrals**: Warm grays (neutral-*) and cool slates (slate-*)
- **Status**: Green (success), Red (error), Yellow (warning), Blue (info)

### Typography Scale
- Font: Inter (sans-serif), JetBrains Mono (monospace)
- Sizes: text-xs (12px) to text-6xl (60px)
- Weights: 100-900 (thin to black)

### Spacing Scale
- Base unit: 0.25rem (4px)
- Scale: space-0 to space-32 (0 to 128px)
- Semantic: spacing-xs, spacing-sm, spacing-md, spacing-lg, spacing-xl

### Motion Tokens
- Durations: 150ms (fast), 300ms (normal), 500ms (slow)
- Easings: ease-in, ease-out, ease-in-out, ease-bounce

### Dark Mode Support
- Applied via .dark class on HTML element
- Complete token overrides for surfaces, text, borders, shadows

See design/frontend/STYLING.md for full documentation.

title: Design + implement trusted internal caller auth policy
description: Design + implement trusted internal caller auth policy. CRITICAL: User identity must be embedded in the HMAC-signed body, NOT as unsigned headers. workerFetch only signs timestamp:body. Create createTrustedApi() factory in SvelteKit that wraps userId/role into body. Add auth: 'trusted' policy to procedure() that extracts user from signed body after HMAC validation. Requires security review. See docs/auth-performance/04-trusted-internal-caller.md.
close_reason: Not needed: measurement shows auth page loads in 35-98ms warm (target was <1s). Trusted internal caller was contingent on redundant auth still being >200ms after Phase 1 — it isn't.

title: [ds-review-iter-012] ImmersiveShaderPlayer slider has tabindex=0 but no keyboard handler (ARIA slider contract violation)
description: File: ImmersiveShaderPlayer.svelte:255-263 (root role='application' with pointer handlers), :278 (slider child). The .immersive__seek slider has role='slider', tabindex=0, aria-value* attributes — focusable via keyboard — but NO onkeydown handler for ArrowLeft/Right seek. Keyboard users can focus the slider but cannot use it. Also root role='application' suppresses AT defaults and the svelte-ignore a11y_no_noninteractive_element_interactions hides this. Ref 05 §Media Elements §6 + ref 05 §4 slider contract. Fix: add onkeydown to .immersive__seek handling ArrowLeft/Right (seek ±5s), Shift+Arrow (±1s fine), Home/End (start/end). Also reconsider role='application' — overlay is more naturally role='dialog' (with focus trap) since Escape closes it.
close_reason: iter-012 Phase A — fixed in commit 3b351821. (a) role='application' replaced with role='dialog' + aria-modal='true' (Escape closes; focus trap via tabindex + auto-focus). (b) Added handleSeekKey to .immersive__seek: ArrowLeft/Right ±5s (Shift = fine ±1s), Home/End. Uses stopPropagation to avoid double-seek via the overlay-level handler. (c) aria-valuetext announces the position in natural language.

title: simplify(web): content detail server loaders share 70 lines of dup boilerplate (org + creators)
description:
**Finding** (denoise iter-027, F3):

Two content detail server loaders share 3 jscpd-detected clone clusters totaling ~70 lines:

- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/content/[contentSlug]/+page.server.ts:89-171`
- `apps/web/src/routes/_creators/[username]/content/[contentSlug]/+page.server.ts:125-209`

Clusters (from `/tmp/denoise-iter-027-jscpd.json/jscpd-report.json`):
1. **22 lines** — identical `subscriptionContext` 5-field empty fallback object (×2 in each file)
2. **22 lines** — identical `loadAccessAndProgress(...).catch(() => fallback)` block with the same 5-field denied-access fallback
3. **26 lines** — structurally identical isPublic-authenticated and gated-authenticated return shapes

The creators-loader differs only by adding `creatorProfile` and `username` fields.

**Fix options** (smallest → most structural):
- (a) Extract `EMPTY_SUB_CONTEXT` and `DENIED_ACCESS_RESULT` constants into `$lib/server/content-detail.ts` (~20-line diff)
- (b) Extract a shared `loadContentDetail(...)` helper that both loaders call, with creators-specific projection bolted on by the caller (~50-line diff)

Either passes the proof test.

**Proof test**: `apps/web/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-027/F3-content-detail-loader-dup.test.ts`
- Catalogue row 6 (clone-count assertion via jscpd-budget) + grep on the literal fallback object pattern
- MCP evidence: jscpd report at `/tmp/denoise-iter-027-jscpd.json/jscpd-report.json` (3 clusters)

**Report**: `docs/denoise/iter-027.md` §F3
close_reason: iter-006 team — F3 content-detail loader constants extracted to $lib/server/content-detail.ts; F3 proof passes

title: Analytics API expansion — backend

title: authFixture.registerUser() falls through to rate-limited /sign-in/email (post-Codex-bies6 finding)
description:
Discovered during Codex-bies6 e2e implementation (commit a58cad87). The shared @codex/test-utils/e2e authFixture.registerUser() helper attempts BetterAuth signup-with-verification, but BetterAuth's /sign-up/email endpoint with requireEmailVerification:true returns no Set-Cookie. The fixture then falls through to /sign-in/email which is rate-limited 5/15min/IP via createAuthRateLimiter. Result: any e2e test that calls registerUser() in rapid succession (or in CI alongside other auth-using tests) hits the rate limit and fails non-deterministically.

WORKAROUND used by bies6 e2e: inline a sign-up + verify-email registration flow that uses BetterAuth's autoSignInAfterVerification (which IS unrate-limited). 419-line spec at apps/web/e2e/brand-editor-hero-effects.spec.ts demonstrates the pattern.

ACTION: refactor authFixture.registerUser() in @codex/test-utils to use the verify-email path instead of /sign-in/email fallback. All existing e2e specs that use registerUser() should keep working transparently. Verify with a focused e2e suite run.

Reference: Codex-bies6 commit a58cad87, agent finding from round 20.

title: [ds-review-iter-013] Waveform slider missing Home/End/PageUp/Down keyboard handlers — ARIA slider contract fail
description: File: Waveform.svelte:490-498 — inline onkeydown handles ArrowLeft/ArrowRight only. role='slider' per WAI-ARIA APG REQUIRES Home (start) + End (end) + optionally PageUp/PageDown (coarse-grain). Current impl fails APG contract. Rule: ref 05 §Media §10 (to be added — canvas-based slider widgets). Fix: extend /components/media-a11y/keyboard.svelte.ts with createSliderKeyboardHandler({ onSeek, getCurrentTime, duration, stepSecs?, pageSecs? }) composable for canvas-based sliders (no HTMLMediaElement). Apply to Waveform. Home → onSeek(0); End → onSeek(duration); Arrow → ±stepSecs (default 5s); Shift+Arrow → ±1s fine; PageUp/Down → ±pageSecs (default 10% duration).

title: Standardize inline-type imports → separate 'import type' statements (post-Codex-fwtmu fix)
description:
Codex-fwtmu identified that fallow's import-graph analyzer doesn't track inline-type imports (TS 4.5+ 'import { type Foo }' syntax). Affected pattern: any file that imports a type alongside values via inline 'type' keyword instead of a separate 'import type' statement.

Action options:
1. NARROW: refactor jfa-sdf.ts:18-26 to separate the FBO type import. Remove the redundant FBO ignoreExports from .fallowrc.json. One-file change. Verify with fallow re-run.

2. WIDE: grep for all inline-type imports in the codebase (), refactor each to separate-statement form. Eliminates the FP class entirely. Larger scope.

3. NONE: keep inline-type imports + ignoreExports workaround. Each new occurrence costs an ignoreExports entry. Acceptable if rare.

Recommendation: NARROW first (one-file proof of fix), then evaluate WIDE based on grep count.

Reference: Codex-fwtmu close notes. Fallow rule pattern: type-only consumers in mixed-import statements are missed.
close_reason: Implemented in commit 8a4938f8 on fix/Codex-bm1tf-0vxa3 (stacked on qf7u8). bm1tf: removed inline ObservabilityClient interface from kv-secondary-storage.ts, replaced with type Logger import from @codex/observability, removed redundant ignoreExports entry. R11 collision resolved. 0vxa3: split inline 'type FBO' import in jfa-sdf.ts to separate 'import type { FBO }' statement (cleaner code) — INVESTIGATION FINDING: this did NOT silence fallow's FBO FP, so the inline-type-keyword hypothesis was wrong. Actual cause is jfa-sdf.ts reachability via dynamic-import + Svelte template, which fallow can't trace. FBO ignoreExports entry retained. Net: 1 inline export removed, 2 ignoreExports entries → 1, +7/-17 lines across 3 files. PR: https://github.com/brucemckayone/codex/pull/new/fix/Codex-bm1tf-0vxa3

title: ORG-00: ORG Foundation - Error Boundaries, i18n, and CSS Patterns
notes:
## Objective Set up the foundational infrastructure for all ORG pages: error boundaries, i18n message keys, and CSS design token patterns. This task unblocks and standardizes all other ORG frontend tasks. --- ## Cross-Cutting Requirements (from PR Review Agents) ### 1. Error Boundaries (CRITICAL - Local-First Agent) Every org route needs +error.svelte for graceful error handling: **Files to Create**: - apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/+error.svelte - apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/+error.svelte - apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/explore/+error.svelte - apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/creators/+error.svelte **Pattern**: ```svelte

{$page.status}

{errorMessages[$page.status] || m.org_error_unknown()}

{m.org_error_go_home()}
``` --- ### 2. i18n Message Keys (CRITICAL - Component Agent) Add paraglide message keys for all org-related text: **File**: apps/web/src/lib/paraglide/messages/en.js **Required Keys**: - org_navigation_home, org_navigation_explore, org_navigation_creators, org_navigation_library - org_landing_title, org_landing_subtitle, org_landing_explore_cta - org_landing_featured_title, org_landing_featured_empty - org_explore_title, org_explore_subtitle, org_explore_search_placeholder - org_explore_filter_all, org_explore_filter_video, org_explore_filter_audio, org_explore_filter_written - org_explore_sort_newest, org_explore_sort_popular, org_explore_sort_az - org_explore_empty, org_explore_no_results - org_creators_title, org_creators_subtitle - org_creators_empty, org_creators_content_count - footer_about, footer_terms, footer_privacy, footer_copyright --- ### 3. CSS Design Token Patterns (CRITICAL - CSS Agent) Establish base CSS patterns for org pages with dark mode support: **Color Tokens** (already exist, must use): - var(--color-primary-*) for brand colors - var(--color-text), var(--color-surface), var(--color-border) for neutrals - [data-org-brand] selector for org-specific theming **Spacing Tokens** (must use): - var(--space-0) through var(--space-24) - NO hardcoded pixels **Typography Tokens** (must use): - var(--font-sans), var(--font-heading), var(--font-mono) - var(--text-xs) through var(--text-4xl) **Dark Mode** (must include): - [data-theme="dark"] selector overrides **Responsive Pattern**: - Mobile-first: default styles = mobile - @media (min-width: 640px) for tablet - @media (min-width: 1024px) for desktop --- ## File Reference Summary | File | Action | Details | |------|--------|---------| | apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/+error.svelte | CREATE | Layout error boundary with i18n | | apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/+error.svelte | CREATE | Space error boundary | | apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/explore/+error.svelte | CREATE | Explore error boundary | | apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/creators/+error.svelte | CREATE | Creators error boundary | | apps/web/src/lib/paraglide/messages/en.js | MODIFY | Add org-related i18n keys | --- ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All 4 org routes have +error.svelte files - [ ] All error boundaries use paraglide i18n for messages - [ ] All org i18n keys defined in messages/en.js - [ ] Error boundaries handle 404, 401, 403, 500 with user-friendly messages - [ ] Documentation created for CSS token usage patterns --- ## Dependencies **Blocks**: ORG-01, ORG-02, ORG-03, ORG-04 (all need these foundations) **Blocked By**: None --- ## Effort Estimate 2-3 hours (error boundaries are straightforward, i18n keys require translation work)

title: Phase 1: Database Schema & Migrations
description:
## Overview
Create the email_templates database schema with support for global, organization, and creator scopes.

## Files to Create/Modify

### 1. Create Schema File
File: `packages/database/src/schema/notifications.ts`

```typescript
import { relations, sql } from 'drizzle-orm';
import {
  index,
  pgEnum,
  pgTable,
  text,
  timestamp,
  uniqueIndex,
  uuid,
  varchar,
} from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';
import { users } from './users';
import { organizations } from './content';

// Enums
export const templateScopeEnum = pgEnum('template_scope', ['global', 'organization', 'creator']);
export const templateStatusEnum = pgEnum('template_status', ['draft', 'active', 'archived']);

// Main table
export const emailTemplates = pgTable(
  'email_templates',
  {
    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    
    // Template identity
    name: varchar('name', { length: 100 }).notNull(), // e.g., 'email-verification'
    scope: templateScopeEnum('scope').notNull(),
    
    // Ownership (nullable based on scope)
    organizationId: uuid('organization_id').references(() => organizations.id, { onDelete: 'cascade' }),
    creatorId: text('creator_id').references(() => users.id, { onDelete: 'cascade' }),
    createdBy: text('created_by').references(() => users.id, { onDelete: 'set null' }),
    
    // Content
    subject: varchar('subject', { length: 500 }).notNull(),
    htmlBody: text('html_body').notNull(),
    textBody: text('text_body').notNull(),
    description: text('description'),
    
    // State
    status: templateStatusEnum('status').default('draft').notNull(),
    
    // Timestamps
    createdAt: timestamp('created_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow().notNull(),
    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at', { withTimezone: true }).defaultNow().notNull().$onUpdate(() => new Date()),
    deletedAt: timestamp('deleted_at', { withTimezone: true }),
  },
  (table) => ({
    // Regular indexes
    orgIdIdx: index('idx_email_templates_org_id').on(table.organizationId),
    creatorIdIdx: index('idx_email_templates_creator_id').on(table.creatorId),
    scopeIdx: index('idx_email_templates_scope').on(table.scope),
    statusIdx: index('idx_email_templates_status').on(table.status),
    
    // Partial unique indexes (unique name per scope+owner)
    uniqueGlobalName: uniqueIndex('idx_unique_template_global')
      .on(table.name)
      .where(sql`${table.scope} = 'global' AND ${table.deletedAt} IS NULL`),
    
    uniqueOrgName: uniqueIndex('idx_unique_template_org')
      .on(table.name, table.organizationId)
      .where(sql`${table.scope} = 'organization' AND ${table.deletedAt} IS NULL`),
    
    uniqueCreatorName: uniqueIndex('idx_unique_template_creator')
      .on(table.name, table.creatorId)
      .where(sql`${table.scope} = 'creator' AND ${table.deletedAt} IS NULL`),
  })
);

// Relations
export const emailTemplatesRelations = relations(emailTemplates, ({ one }) => ({
  organization: one(organizations, {
    fields: [emailTemplates.organizationId],
    references: [organizations.id],
  }),
  creator: one(users, {
    fields: [emailTemplates.creatorId],
    references: [users.id],
  }),
  createdByUser: one(users, {
    fields: [emailTemplates.createdBy],
    references: [users.id],
  }),
}));
```

### 2. Export from Schema Index
File: `packages/database/src/schema/index.ts`

Add:
```typescript
export * from './notifications';
```

### 3. Generate Migration
```bash
cd packages/database
pnpm db:gen:drizzle
```

This creates migration file in `packages/database/src/migrations/`

### 4. Run Migration
```bash
pnpm db:migrate
```

## Reference Patterns

### Partial Unique Index Pattern
See: `packages/database/src/schema/content.ts` lines 85-95
```typescript
// Content uses same pattern for slug uniqueness
uniqueSlugOrg: uniqueIndex('idx_unique_content_slug_org')
  .on(table.slug, table.organizationId)
  .where(sql`${table.organizationId} IS NOT NULL AND ${table.deletedAt} IS NULL`),
```

### Enum Pattern
See: `packages/database/src/schema/content.ts`
```typescript
export const contentStatusEnum = pgEnum('content_status', ['draft', 'published', 'archived']);
```

### Relations Pattern
See: `packages/database/src/schema/settings.ts`

## Constraints Logic
- Global templates: scope='global', organizationId=NULL, creatorId=NULL
- Org templates: scope='organization', organizationId NOT NULL, creatorId=NULL
- Creator templates: scope='creator', creatorId NOT NULL, organizationId optional (for visibility)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema file created with all fields and indexes
- [ ] Enums created for scope and status
- [ ] Partial unique indexes prevent duplicate names per scope
- [ ] Relations defined for organization, creator, createdBy
- [ ] Export added to schema/index.ts
- [ ] Migration generated and applied
- [ ] `pnpm db:studio` shows table correctly
close_reason: Closed

title: CONTENT-03: PreviewPlayer & Access Gate
description:
Create a PreviewPlayer component and access gate for unauthenticated/unpurchased users on the content detail page.

## Current State
- **No PreviewPlayer component exists** — no `components/player/` directory at all
- **No access-state module exists**
- **VideoPlayer exists** at `components/VideoPlayer/` — full HLS player with media-chrome (could potentially be reused/wrapped for preview)
- **Access check pattern** is confirmed: `getStreamingUrl(contentId)` returns signed URL on success, throws 403 on no access
- **Checkout remote functions exist**: `createCheckout` (form, progressive enhancement) and `createCheckoutSession` (command, SPA-style) in `checkout.remote.ts`
- **Content detail page** does NOT exist yet (CONTENT-01 dependency)

## Files to Create
1. `apps/web/src/lib/components/player/PreviewPlayer.svelte` — 30-second limited preview player
2. `apps/web/src/lib/components/player/access-state.ts` — access state type and factory
3. `apps/web/src/lib/components/player/index.ts` — barrel exports
4. `apps/web/src/lib/components/player/PreviewPlayer.stories.svelte` — stories

## PreviewPlayer Behavior
- Plays HLS preview clip (from `mediaItem.hlsPreviewKey` via R2 public URL)
- 30-second time limit — pauses at limit and shows CTA overlay
- NOT muted by default
- Minimal controls: play, mute, fullscreen only (no seek, no rate, no PiP)
- Custom progress bar showing preview limit
- "Preview" badge overlay
- On preview end: fade-in CTA overlay with purchase/sign-in button

## Access State Machine
```typescript
type AccessState =
  | { status: 'loading' }
  | { status: 'preview' }      // Can watch preview (unauthenticated or unpurchased)
  | { status: 'unlocked' }     // Full access (purchased/free+authenticated)
  | { status: 'locked'; reason: 'purchase_required' | 'auth_required' }
  | { status: 'error'; message: string };
```

## CTA Overlay
- For `auth_required`: "Sign in to watch" button -> `/login?redirect={currentUrl}`
- For `purchase_required`: Use `createCheckout` form from `checkout.remote.ts` for progressive enhancement
- All text via paraglide i18n keys: `player_preview_ended`, `player_preview_cta`, `purchase_sign_in`, etc.

## Technical Notes
- HLS setup: same pattern as VideoPlayer's `hls.ts` — reuse `createHlsPlayer`
- NO progress tracking needed for preview (no save)
- CSS: use design tokens, `var(--color-overlay)` for backdrop, dark mode via `[data-theme='dark']`
- Accessibility: `aria-label` on controls, `role="alert"` on errors, `:focus-visible` styles
- Wrap in `<svelte:boundary>` error boundary
- HLS cleanup in `onDestroy`

## Dependencies
- Depends on CONTENT-01 (Codex-b0rx) for the content detail page route
- PurchaseCTA depends on ECOM-01 (Codex-9dk) — use simple checkout form as placeholder

## Effort: 4-6 hours
notes:
## Verified Codebase State (2026-03-18)

### No Preview Components Exist
- No `apps/web/src/lib/components/player/` directory
- No PreviewPlayer, no access-state module
- VideoPlayer at `components/VideoPlayer/` could be referenced for HLS patterns but is a different component

### Reusable HLS Factory
`apps/web/src/lib/components/VideoPlayer/hls.ts` exports `createHlsPlayer({ video, src, onError })` — can be imported directly by PreviewPlayer for HLS setup. No need to duplicate.

### Checkout Remote Functions (exact)
- `createCheckout` — form() with schema `{ contentId: uuid, successUrl?, cancelUrl? }` — progressive enhancement, redirects to Stripe
- `createCheckoutSession` — command() returns `{ sessionUrl }` for SPA-style redirect

### Access Check Pattern (confirmed)
Server-side in +page.server.ts:
```typescript
try {
  const result = await getStreamingUrl(content.id);
  // success = has access
} catch {
  // 403 = no access, show preview/gate
}
```
There is NO `/api/access/content/:id/check` endpoint. The streaming URL request IS the access check.

### Preview URL Source
Content's mediaItem relation may have `hlsPreviewKey`. Preview URL = `{R2_PUBLIC_URL}/${mediaItem.hlsPreviewKey}`. If no hlsPreviewKey exists, show poster image with purchase CTA instead of preview player.

### i18n Keys Needed
- `player_preview_label` — "Preview"
- `player_preview_ended` — "Preview ended"
- `player_preview_cta` — "Get full access to continue watching"
- `purchase_sign_in` — "Sign in"
- `purchase_sign_in_to_buy` — "Sign in to purchase"
close_reason: Closed

title: Replace existence-only 'component module exports correctly' tests in 6 Melt UI compound components
description: Scope-check finding while closing Codex-ziwx (Dialog + ErrorBoundary). Six other compound-component test files have the same anti-pattern: 'expect(Component).toBeDefined(); expect(typeof Component).toBe(function);' — Tabs, Accordion, Tooltip, Toaster, Popover, DropdownMenu. Pattern for fixing: create a Harness .test.svelte file that wires the parent + all sub-components in a realistic composition, then assert Melt UI's real wiring (role, aria-controls, aria-labelledby, aria-haspopup, data-state) as done for Dialog in the ziwx ref commit. Do NOT assert on data-melt-* attributes (see Codex-is8u).
close_reason: Committed d5157f51. Replaced existence-only test anti-pattern across all 6 compound components (Tabs, Accordion, Tooltip, Toast/Toaster, Popover, DropdownMenu) with harness-backed real-ARIA behaviour assertions. 95 existence-only tests -> 89 behaviour tests; total suite 442 -> 448 passing. Harness files: TabsHarness, AccordionHarness (+AccordionMultipleHarness for multi-select due to wrapper type narrowing), TooltipHarness, PopoverHarness, DropdownMenuHarness. Toast uses store-based harness rather than component harness since its architecture is singleton store + Toaster. Tests assert role/aria-*/data-state public contract only (no data-melt-* per Codex-is8u). Added Element.animate + getAnimations shim to tests/setup.ts for Svelte 5 transitions in JSDOM. Typecheck delta: pre-existing 85 errors -> 83 (my rewrites removed 2 pre-existing errors in the old tests).

title: [PR2] Wire revocation key clears on access-restoring events
description:
On access-restoring events, clear the KV revocation key via AccessRevocation.clear(userId, orgId).

Events that CLEAR revocation:
- customer.subscription.updated transitioning back to ACTIVE
- invoice.payment_succeeded after a prior PAST_DUE period
- customer.subscription.resumed
- Explicit reactivate endpoint (POST /subscriptions/reactivate)

Files: workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/subscription-webhook.ts, workers/ecom-api/src/routes/subscriptions.ts

Spec: docs/subscription-cache-audit/phase-2-followup.md.

Verification:
- Integration tests: for each restoring event, KV revocation key is deleted
- Integration: cancelled user, subscription reactivated → wrangler kv:get returns 404
- Manual roundtrip: payment_failed → revocation written → payment_succeeded → revocation cleared
close_reason: AccessRevocation.clear wired on subscription.updated(active), invoice.payment_succeeded, /reactivate route. 3 call sites, mirror of revokeAccess helper. 17 new webhook clear tests + 7 new route tests (39/39 webhook + 20/20 route green). Roundtrip test: deleted writes → updated(active) clears. Conservative strategy: always clear on status=active (KV delete idempotent).

title: Remove duplicate metadata extraction in invalidateSubscriptionCache
description:
subscription-webhook.ts:54-56 pulls metadata.codex_user_id / codex_organization_id from stripeSubscription independently even though the service already returned result?.userId (see line 127).

Fix: extend WebhookHandlerResult interface to also return organizationId, have all handleSubscription* service methods populate it, and have the handler use result.userId / result.organizationId instead of re-reading metadata.

Bonus: consolidates metadata-reading into the service (where Zod validation lives per Codex-o0wq).

Sites:
- workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/subscription-webhook.ts:54-56, 127
- packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-service.ts WebhookHandlerResult type (~line 104-113)

Reference: skill backend-dev ref 06 §7 'Returning side-effect payloads from the service'.

Source: backend-dev-review iter 1.
close_reason: Extended WebhookHandlerResult with organizationId. All 5 handler methods (handleSubscriptionCreated, handleSubscriptionUpdated, handleSubscriptionDeleted, handleInvoicePaymentSucceeded, handleInvoicePaymentFailed) now populate userId+organizationId from either authoritative DB rows or Zod-validated metadata. subscription-webhook.ts invalidateSubscriptionCache now takes a WebhookHandlerResult (not the raw subscription) — handler no longer re-reads metadata.codex_user_id / codex_organization_id. 5 new tests verify each handler returns both fields; 1 webhook test verifies the handler uses service result even when Stripe metadata is empty.

title: Use safeTruncate for transcodingError — substring(0,N) breaks UTF-8
description:
packages/transcoding/src/services/transcoding-service.ts:430 and :921 use (errorMessage || 'Unknown').substring(0, 2000). If byte 2000 falls mid multi-byte UTF-8 sequence, invalid code unit tail lands in varchar — produces mojibake in logs/DB, can break JSON serialization on display.

Skill rule (new — ref 05 anti-pattern): substring(0, N) on user-provided strings before varchar. Use a code-unit-safe truncate helper.

Sites:
- packages/transcoding/src/services/transcoding-service.ts:430
- packages/transcoding/src/services/transcoding-service.ts:921

Fix: Add safeTruncate(str, maxBytes) to packages/validation/src/primitives.ts (or utils). Replace .substring() calls.

Reference: skill backend-dev ref 05 anti-patterns table.

Source: backend-dev-review iter 2.
close_reason: Added safeTruncate(str, maxBytes) in packages/validation/src/primitives.ts — encodes to UTF-8, cuts at maxBytes, backs up over continuation bytes (0x80-0xBF) so no code unit is split. Replaced .substring(0, 2000) in transcoding-service.ts handleWebhook (failure branch) + markTranscodingFailed. 5 new tests covering non-positive maxBytes, under-limit passthrough, ASCII cut, 3-byte JP char round-trip via TextDecoder(fatal:true), trailing multi-byte drop. Typechecks + all tests pass.

title: Refactor Test Infrastructure: Centralize Mocks & Utilities
description:
# Refactoring Report: Centralizing Test Mocks and Utilities

## Executive Summary
A comprehensive analysis of the Codex codebase reveals significant duplication in test setup code, particularly regarding database mocking (`@codex/database`), Hono context creation, and event payload generation. Currently, individual test suites (especially in `workers/*`) implement ad-hoc, brittle mocks that drift in consistency.

**Recommendation**: Centralize these patterns into `@codex/test-utils`. This will standardize how services interact with the database in tests, reduce boilerplate, and improve type safety across the platform.

---

## 1. Current State Analysis

### A. Fragmented Database Mocking
Tests currently mock the database layer manually in every file. This leads to inconsistent API surfaces and makes refactoring the actual database layer difficult.

*   **Pattern 1: Simple Object Mocking**
    *   **Location**: `workers/organization-api/src/__tests__/settings.test.ts`
    *   **Code**: `vi.mock('@codex/database', () => ({ dbHttp: {} }));`
    *   **Issue**: It provides no type safety and requires manual mocking of every used method (e.g., `db.query...`) inside the test body.

*   **Pattern 2: Factory Function Mocking**
    *   **Location**: `workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/__tests__/checkout.test.ts`
    *   **Code**: Mocks `createPerRequestDbClient` to return a plain object `{ db: mockDb, cleanup: vi.fn() }`.
    *   **Issue**: Re-implements the "transaction-capable" client contract in every test file.

### B. Duplicated Hono Context
Worker tests require a mock Hono `Context` to simulate headers, environment variables, and dependency injection.

*   **Observation**: `createMockContext` functions are defined locally in test files (e.g., `workers/ecom-api`).
*   **Duplication**: Logic for mocking `c.env`, `c.get('obs')` (observability), and logging spies is repeated. This boilerplate distracts from the actual test logic.

### C. Isolated Data Factories
Domain-specific test data generators are trapped inside specific worker tests instead of being shared.

*   **Observation**: `createMockCheckoutEvent` exists inside `workers/ecom-api`.
*   **Impact**: Other services that might need to test webhook handling or payment flows (e.g., a future reporting worker) cannot reuse this logic.

---

## 2. Proposed Architecture

We should expand `packages/test-utils` to include a new **Mocks Module** and enhance the existing **Factories Module**.

### New Module: `packages/test-utils/src/mocks.ts`

This module will provide standardized, type-safe mocks for core infrastructure.

**1. `createMockDatabase()`**
A helper that returns a fully mocked Drizzle-like client. It should allow chaining (e.g., `db.query.users.findFirst.mockResolvedValue(...)`) without manual setup.

**2. `createMockHonoContext<Env>(envOverrides?)`**
A factory that generates a Hono `Context` object with:
*   Pre-configured `env` (bindings).
*   Pre-configured Observability client (`c.get('obs')`).
*   Spy-attached logging methods for assertions.

### Enhanced Module: `packages/test-utils/src/factories.ts`

**1. `createMockStripeEvent(type, payload)`**
Generalize the `createMockCheckoutEvent` from `ecom-api` to support various Stripe event types, making it reusable for any payment-related tests.

---

## 3. Implementation Plan

### Step 1: Create Shared Mock Utilities
1.  Create `packages/test-utils/src/mocks.ts`.
2.  Implement `createMockDatabase`.
3.  Implement `createMockHonoContext`.
4.  Export these from `packages/test-utils/src/index.ts`.

### Step 2: Centralize Factories
1.  Move `createMockCheckoutEvent` logic from `workers/ecom-api` to `packages/test-utils/src/factories.ts`.
2.  Refactor it to be generic (e.g., accept event type and data overrides).

### Step 3: Refactor Consumer Tests
1.  **Refactor `workers/ecom-api`**:
    *   Replace local `createMockContext` with the imported utility.
    *   Replace local `createMockCheckoutEvent` with the imported factory.
    *   Update database mocks to use the shared pattern.
2.  **Refactor `workers/organization-api`**:
    *   Replace manual `vi.mock` for database with standard utilities where applicable.

---

## 4. Benefits
*   **Maintainability**: Changing the database client signature only requires updating the mock utility in one place, not 50+ test files.
*   **Developer Experience**: Developers stop writing boilerplate context setup code and focus on business logic.
*   **Consistency**: All tests run against the same "virtual" infrastructure behavior.
close_reason: Implemented: centralized test mocks (mocks.ts), Stripe/auth factories, refactored 3 test suites

Key: 2D wave equation, Fresnel reflection, caustic highlights, hover for soft ripples, click for splashes.

title: [ds-review-iter-02] JFA WebGL program cache has no disposal or handle validation
description: Iter-02 finding 6. jfa-sdf.ts:30-62 keeps module-scoped seedProg/stepProg/distProg across the life of the module. ensurePrograms only recompiles when the GL context itself changes. Two gaps: (a) if any other code calls gl.deleteProgram on these, the cache silently returns invalid handles — drawQuad errors with no fail-fast; (b) no dispose function — in SPA navigations where the context lives but the shader hero tears down, these leak. Fix options: (a) validate with gl.isProgram(seedProg) before returning true from ensurePrograms [minimal]; (b) export destroyJFACache(gl) for callers to run in their renderer destroy() [explicit, preferred]. Ship (b) and call from ShaderHero renderer disposal. Motivated skill patch: 06-performance.md §10 'Module-scoped GPU caches' bullet.
close_reason: Added gl.isProgram() validation in ensurePrograms (issue (a) — silent stale-handle returns). Exported destroyJFACache(gl) — idempotent dispose with context-mismatch early-return and per-handle isProgram guards. Wired into ShaderHero.svelte onDestroy so the cache stays warm across preset switches but releases on subsystem unmount, closing the SPA-nav leak path (issue (b)).

title: Add magic-byte validation for media container uploads (mp4, webm, etc.)
description:
MediaItemService.upload (media-service.ts:177-205) trusts the Content-Type header for binary uploads. Someone can PUT a 5GB text file with Content-Type: video/mp4 — worker stores it at creator's r2Key, RunPod transcodes and fails noisily, wasting budget + R2 quota.

Skill rule (new — ref 07 Media container magic bytes): defense-in-depth validation. MIME check alone is not enough.

Site: packages/content/src/services/media-service.ts upload path (from media.ts:184-191)

Fix:
1. Create validateMediaSignature(buffer, declaredMime) in packages/validation/src/media.ts (or similar), mirroring validateImageSignature. Tables for magic bytes per format:
   - MP4/MOV: 'ftyp' atom at offset 4
   - WebM: 1A 45 DF A3 (EBML)
   - AVI: 'RIFF...AVI '
   - MP3: 'ID3' or FF FB/FF FA
   - WAV: 'RIFF...WAVE'
   - OGG: 'OggS'
   - FLAC: 'fLaC'
   - AAC: FF F1 / FF F9
2. Call from MediaItemService.upload before r2.put — throw ValidationError on mismatch.

Reference: skill backend-dev ref 07 magic bytes section (new) + anti-pattern row 'Skipping magic-byte validation on media/binary uploads'.

Source: backend-dev-review iter 2.
close_reason: Added validateMediaSignature(buffer, mime) helper in packages/validation/src/primitives.ts covering MP4/MOV ftyp, WebM EBML, AVI RIFF/AVI, MP3 ID3/frame-sync, WAV RIFF/WAVE, OGG OggS, FLAC fLaC, AAC ADTS. Called from MediaItemService.upload before r2.put — ValidationError on mismatch. 9 new tests (positive MP4/WebM/WAV/OGG/MP3 + negative text-as-mp4 + short buffer). Typechecks clean.

title: Org footer shows 2025 instead of 2026, mismatches platform footer

title: Studio sidebar uses 64→260 hover-expand instead of always-expanded (WP-10 T-07 spec deviation)
description:
Discovered in iter-012 WP-10 verification (Codex-u498 slice 4). T-07 spec says 'studio sidebar always expanded (NOT collapsed rail)'. Actual: studio sidebar (apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/StudioSidebar/StudioSidebar.svelte) uses the same 64px → 260px hover-expand pattern as the public SidebarRail.

Two interpretations:
(a) Spec drift — design changed mid-implementation to unify rail behaviour across public + studio contexts. WP-10-verification.md was authored against an earlier design.
(b) Real bug — studio was supposed to be visually distinct (always-expanded sidebar to indicate 'authoring context, not browsing context').

Decision needed before fix lands. Suggested research: check WP-09 (studio view transition) spec + design tokens for any 'studio-rail-permanent-expanded' signals; check git blame on StudioSidebar.svelte for whether always-expanded was ever shipped and reverted.
close_reason: Confirmed as spec drift: studio rail uses unified hover-expand pattern; spec updated. New bead Codex-ekbwz tracks optional pin toggle.

title: ORG-01: Org Landing Page Enhancement
description:
Enhance the org landing page at `_org/[slug]/(space)/` with hero section, featured content grid, org branding, and SEO.

## Current State
- **+page.svelte** is a minimal placeholder: shows org name, description, "Explore Content" link, "Featured Content" heading with placeholder text. NO `<style>` block, no CSS at all.
- **+page.server.ts** only sets `CACHE_HEADERS.DYNAMIC_PUBLIC` — no data fetching beyond what the parent layout provides.
- **Parent layout** (`+layout.server.ts`) already fetches real org data: `{ id, slug, name, description, logoUrl, brandColors }` via `getOrganization(slug)`.
- **ContentCard** EXISTS at `$lib/components/ui/ContentCard/` — full implementation with thumbnail, duration, type badges, creator info, actions snippet, loading/skeleton state, dark mode, i18n. NOT basic.
- **Error boundary** exists at `(space)/+error.svelte`.
- **`getPublicBranding(slug)`** exists in `org.remote.ts` — fetches from KV cache, returns `{ logoUrl, primaryColorHex, platformName }`.

## What the Page Already Has from Parent Layout
Via `data.org` (from parent layout load):
- `id`, `slug`, `name`, `description`, `logoUrl`, `brandColors` (primary, secondary, accent)

No need to re-fetch org data in the page server load.

## Tasks

### 1. Enhance +page.server.ts
- Optionally fetch `getPublicBranding(slug)` for additional branding (primaryColorHex) — OR use `data.org.brandColors` from parent layout
- Fetch featured content: use `listContent({ organizationId: data.org.id, status: 'published', limit: 6, sortBy: 'newest' })` from `content.remote.ts`
  - NOTE: `listContent` requires auth (`api.content.list` uses authenticated API client). If public access is needed, this is blocked by Codex-qv2. Mock data as fallback.
- Keep `CACHE_HEADERS.DYNAMIC_PUBLIC`

### 2. Enhance +page.svelte
- Hero section with org logo, name, description, branded gradient background using `data.org.brandColors`
- Featured content grid using ContentCard component (import from `$lib/components/ui/ContentCard`)
- ContentCard props: `{ id, title, thumbnail?, description?, contentType?, duration?, creator?, href, actions? }`
- Empty state when no content
- Full `<style>` block with design tokens, responsive grid, dark mode
- All text via paraglide i18n (`m.org_explore_content()`, `m.org_featured()`, etc.)
- SEO: `<svelte:head>` with og:title, og:description, og:type

### 3. Required i18n Keys
- `org_explore_content` — "Explore Content" CTA
- `org_featured` — "Featured Content" section heading
- `org_no_content` — "No content yet" empty state
- `org_view_all` — "View all" link

## Files to Modify
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/+page.server.ts` — add content fetching
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/+page.svelte` — full redesign

## Files to Reference
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ContentCard/ContentCard.svelte` — content card component
- `apps/web/src/lib/remote/content.remote.ts` — `listContent` function
- `apps/web/src/lib/remote/org.remote.ts` — `getPublicBranding` if needed

## Backend Blocker
`listContent` uses authenticated API. Public content listing (Codex-qv2) is not yet available. Workaround: mock featured content array until backend is ready, OR require auth for content fetch and gracefully handle no-auth by showing empty state.

## Effort: 4-6 hours
notes:
## Verified Codebase State (2026-03-18)

### Current +page.svelte (exact)
Minimal placeholder — org name as h1, description paragraph, "Explore Content" anchor, "Featured Content" heading with "Content grid will be displayed here" text. No <style> block. No CSS.

### Current +page.server.ts (exact)
Only sets `CACHE_HEADERS.DYNAMIC_PUBLIC`. No data fetching.

### ContentCard — EXISTS and is FULL-FEATURED
Located at `$lib/components/ui/ContentCard/ContentCard.svelte` (NOT `components/content/ContentCard.svelte`).
Props: `{ id, title, thumbnail?, description?, contentType?, duration?, creator?, actions?: Snippet, href?, loading? }`
Has: i18n, dark mode, loading skeleton, type badges, duration display, creator avatar, hover effects.
The old description's claim that ContentCard is "basic" or "may not exist" is WRONG.

### Org Data Available from Parent Layout
```typescript
data.org = { id, slug, name, description, logoUrl, brandColors: { primary?, secondary?, accent? } }
data.user = LayoutUser | null
```

### Remote Functions
- `listContent(params?)` from content.remote.ts — requires auth (uses `createServerApi` with cookies)
- `getPublicBranding(slug)` from org.remote.ts — no auth, returns `{ logoUrl, primaryColorHex, platformName }`

### PR Review Findings — STILL VALID
- No CSS at all — needs full <style> block
- No i18n — needs paraglide messages
- Missing hydrateIfNeeded pattern — only needed if using live queries (may not be needed for simple server-rendered page)

### Error Boundary
`(space)/+error.svelte` exists — no need to create one.

### Dependency Note
Codex-64e8 (UI-03: Shared ContentCard) is listed as a dependency but ContentCard already exists and is complete. This dependency should be marked resolved.
close_reason: Closed

title: [ds-review-iter-008] HueSlider --_hue private token is never set — thumb always hue=0
description: File: HueSlider.svelte:70,85 — thumb uses background: oklch(0.7 0.15 var(--_hue, 0)) but NO code sets --_hue. The fallback 0 makes the thumb always red regardless of selection. Fallback masks dead wiring (01-tokens.md §8 anti-pattern). Fix: bind style='--_hue: {hue}' on the <input> element so the private token reflects current state. Removes dead code, makes thumb actually show selected hue. Rule: 01-tokens.md §8, §9.
close_reason: HueSlider input now carries style='--_hue: {hue}' → thumb background is oklch(0.7 0.15 var(--_hue, 0)) reflects selected hue. Also consolidated thumb DRY (Codex-mgnnq) in same edit.

title: [Follow-up] Augment account layout.server.ts to read per-org subscription versions
description:

1. Enumerate each org the user has a subscription to (reuse api.subscription.getMine() result if it's already been fetched at the page level, else fetch minimally)
2. Read COLLECTION_USER_SUBSCRIPTION(userId, orgId) version keys from KV for each
3. Return them in a versions manifest compatible with the existing client $effect + getStaleKeys() pattern
4. Extend the client-side account $effect to branch on the new 'user:{id}:subscription:{orgId}' prefix

Part of: Codex-v8bub (Subscription cache & access validation audit)
Spec: docs/subscription-cache-audit/phase-1-p0.md (Phase 2 Step 1)

Why this was deferred: the account layout runs for all /account/* sub-pages (profile, notifications, payment, subscriptions). Adding getMine() at the layout level taxes pages that don't need it. Cleaner to wait until the cross-device subscription collection is in place.

Verification:

- Integration: cross-device scenario (cancel on device A) reflects on device B within one visibility tick
close_reason: Implemented per-org subscription version manifest on /account/subscriptions page (page-scoped, not layout-scoped, to avoid taxing other /account/* pages). Added depends('cache:versions'), readSubscriptionVersions() helper, and $effect calling invalidateCollection('subscription') on stale keys. 7 unit tests pass (4 new versions paths + 3 existing). Branch fix/Codex-1tsyd-subscription-versions pushed for PR.

title: ds-review PR conflict resolution: rebase 5 stacked branches onto main
description:
Discovered 2026-05-06 during PR mergeability audit. Five ds-review/fallow-followup PRs have merge conflicts because their branches were stacked off older base points and accumulated unrelated commits.

PRs needing rebase + force-push:

1. **PR #130** (fix/Codex-rg4da, Codex-rg4da) — 4 commits including unrelated 7c81ed9a, 15def1c8, a0fc2700. Conflicts in .fallowrc.json + several apps/web/src/lib files.

2. **PR #131** (fix/Codex-0ct9w, Codex-0ct9w) — 5 stacked commits (f201f397, 9ef6f347, 89cfdc7d, 6a6a3495, d95af6f5). Conflicts in same .fallowrc.json + lib files.

3. **PR #132** (fix/Codex-7u5wn, Codex-7u5wn) — 4 commits stacked similarly to rg4da. Same conflict surface.

4. **PR #148** (fix/Codex-97pi, Codex-97pi) — 1 commit but conflicts in apps/web/e2e/nav-redesign/* and apps/web/src/app.d.ts (these files changed on main since branch was created).

5. **PR #152** (fix/ds-review-css-injection-bundle, Codex-ag8l8) — 1 commit but conflicts in same nav-redesign e2e + app.d.ts surface as #148.

Resolution path (per PR):
- git fetch origin main
- git checkout <branch>
- git rebase origin/main
- Resolve conflicts (likely just keep main's version for the e2e/app.d.ts files; for .fallowrc.json, merge the entries)
- git push --force-with-lease

Why deferred: requires destructive force-push on each branch + careful per-conflict review. The work itself is real and verified ✅ on each branch by the prior audit; only the merge surface is tangled.

Companion: Codex-<new bead> tracks the CI-blocker (migration 0043) preventing test signal validation post-rebase.

title: [iter-04] Streaming URL expiry handling — end-to-end chain
description:
Backend returns expiresAt; frontend discards it; VideoPlayer has no src-change effect; HLS.js infinite-retries on 403.

Fix: Add refresh flow — propagate expiresAt, schedule refresh before expiry, handle 403 recovery.

Source: iter-04.
close_reason: Shipped via subagent in iter-09 parallel burst. Backend already emitted expiresAt; chain now threads it through: content-detail.ts → page.svelte accessState → ContentDetailView → VideoPlayer/AudioPlayer. New refreshStreamingUrl remote query re-runs access check server-side. hls.ts rewritten with { hls, cleanup } handle; 403 network errors destroy HLS.js and fire onUrlExpired callback (no more infinite startLoad loop). Safari native HLS path attaches error listener with MEDIA_ERR_NETWORK detection. VideoPlayer retry() refreshes URL first. (src) swaps HLS source without remount, preserving currentTime. (expiresAt) schedules pre-emptive refresh 60s before expiry. AudioPlayer symmetric — waveform URL refetched in tandem.

title: Fix avatarUrl vs image column usage in users table
description:
## Problem
Two separate issues with avatar column usage in ImageProcessingService:

### Issue 1: processUserAvatar writes to correct column (avatarUrl) ✅
The upload path at service.ts:146 correctly uses \`avatarUrl\`. This was already fixed.

### Issue 2: deleteUserAvatar reads from WRONG column and uses fragile URL parsing
- service.ts:263 reads \`user.image\` instead of \`user.avatarUrl\`
- service.ts:271 parses URLs by splitting on \`.s3.amazonaws.com/\` — fragile
- service.ts:290 clears \`image\` column instead of \`avatarUrl\`

### Issue 3: Frontend fallback logic not implemented
Need: display \`avatarUrl\` if set, otherwise fall back to \`image\` (OAuth avatar)

## Solution
1. deleteUserAvatar: read \`avatarUrl\` column, use getUserAvatarKey() helpers for R2 keys
2. deleteUserAvatar: clear \`avatarUrl\` (not \`image\`) on delete
3. API response: add avatarUrl field with fallback logic

## Files to Modify
- \`packages/image-processing/src/service.ts\` (deleteUserAvatar)
- \`workers/identity-api/src/routes/users.ts\` (avatar endpoint response)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] deleteUserAvatar reads avatarUrl column
- [ ] deleteUserAvatar uses getUserAvatarKey() for R2 key construction
- [ ] deleteUserAvatar clears avatarUrl (not image) column
- [ ] API responses include effective avatar URL with fallback
- [ ] Tests updated
close_reason: Bug already fixed in commit ef86ff7. All three issues resolved: (1) processUserAvatar writes to avatarUrl ✅ (2) deleteUserAvatar reads from avatarUrl and uses key helpers ✅ (3) Fallback logic documented in schema - frontend/BetterAuth handles avatarUrl→image fallback. Verified implementation in service.ts:329-353.

title: Route-layer tests for /checkout, /verify, /current, /mine
description: workers/ecom-api/src/routes/subscriptions.ts registers 11 procedure endpoints; the existing routes-invalidation test covers only the 4 mutations. Add HTTP-boundary tests for read+create endpoints: body validation 400s, requireAuth 401, ForbiddenError -> 403 (esp. /verify session-belongs-to-different-user), shape of /current (null vs empty), pagination/soft-delete filtering on /mine.
design:
## Workflow

**Skills:** /backend-dev, /tdd

**MCPs:** none required (route tests are pure HTTP boundary, no external calls).

**Reference patterns:**
- workers/ecom-api/src/routes/__tests__/subscriptions-route-invalidation.test.ts — copy the `buildApp()` harness verbatim. It mocks `procedure` to a passthrough so we can drive real handlers with synthesized contexts.
- workers/ecom-api/src/routes/subscriptions.ts:105-184 — the four endpoints
- packages/worker-utils/src/procedure/procedure.ts — for understanding policy + mapErrorToResponse behaviour

**Approach:**
1. For each of /checkout, /verify, /current, /mine: positive path, missing auth → 401, invalid body → 400, service throws known error → mapped HTTP status, service throws unknown → 500 with redacted message.
2. /verify SPECIFICALLY: session-belongs-to-different-user → 403 (the service test at subscription-service.test.ts:461 asserts the throw; this task asserts the HTTP mapping).
3. /current: assert null-vs-empty response shape — the frontend differentiates.
4. /mine: pagination params honoured, soft-deleted subs excluded.
5. Pre-close: `pnpm --filter ecom-api test routes`.
close_reason: Merged via PR #159 + PR #160. Test-only diffs. CI failures (static-analysis + E2E) noted on PRs for follow-up — tests themselves landed on main.

title: Phase 1.3: rich confirmation dialog with proration preview on org pricing page
description:

Dialog body (use design tokens — var(--space-*), var(--text-*), var(--color-*), no hardcoded values per feedback_no_hardcoded_css):

  Prorated [old tier] credit  -£A.AA
  Prorated [new tier] charge   +£B.BB
Then £Y.YY/mo starting [next period date]
[Cancel] [Confirm and pay £X.XX]

For downgrade (Phase 1 fallback): 'Charged today £0.00. Prorated credit of £A.AA on next invoice. New price £Y.YY/mo.' Phase 2 replaces this with scheduled-change copy.

close_reason:
Phase 1.3 complete: rich confirmation dialog replaces inline two-step.

DIALOG IMPLEMENTATION:
- Dialog.Root from $lib/components/ui/Dialog (same primitive as cancel-subscription dialog).
- Preview-first flow: tier card 'Switch' button calls openTierChangeDialog(tier) → fetches preview via previewSubscriptionTierChange remote → shows skeleton → renders amount-due, proration line items, recurring price, period-end date.
- 'Confirm and pay £X.XX' button (or 'Confirm switch' for no-charge cases) calls handleSwitchTier with the preview's prorationDate threaded through.
- Stale-preview guard: if user closes dialog or opens different tier mid-fetch, the result is dropped silently.
- Error handling: PAYMENT_REQUIRED code maps to 'Payment was declined. Update your payment method and try again.' Other errors surface inline in dialog (preview phase) or as Alert (commit phase).
- Reuses existing primitives: Dialog.*, Alert, Button, formatPrice, formatDate. No new utils.

CLEANUP:
- Removed confirmSwitchTierId state, armSwitchTier(), cancelSwitchTier(), and the second inline-confirm block on the same-tier interval-switch path.
- Removed redundant in-card 'proration note' and 'tier-switch-error' display (dialog covers both now).
- Removed dead .card__switch-note CSS.

NEW STYLES (all design tokens):
- .tier-change-skeleton + .tier-change-skeleton__row with shimmer @keyframes.
- .tier-change-summary with __amount, __lines, __credit, __recurring, __period, __note.

TYPE-CLEAN: tsc --noEmit passes (3 pre-existing errors in unrelated denoise/studio-access tests).
SVELTE-CHECK: pre-existing type-narrowing issue with SubscriptionCommandFailure discriminated union surfaces in 5 sites of this file (handleSubscribe, handleReactivate, handleResume, handleSwitchTier, openTierChangeDialog) — runtime behaviour correct, established pattern in the file.

NEXT: Phase 1.4 (explicit negative-path Playwright integration test) — unit-level coverage already in Phase 1.2's '402 → SubscriptionPaymentRequiredError' test.

title: Spike + wire password-changed confirmation email
description: The 'password-changed' email helper sendPasswordChangedEmail() exists in workers/auth/src/email.ts:99 and its template is seeded, but it has zero imports anywhere — spec docs/email-notifications/progress.md:82 tag it 'Wire when BetterAuth hook or custom middleware is available'. BetterAuth v1.4.11 has NO native afterPasswordChange callback; resolution requires a brief spike before implementation.
acceptance_criteria:
- Spike documented (inline in this bead's notes or a sibling doc): which BetterAuth mechanism will invoke the send — databaseHooks.user.update.after with a password-hash-diff check, a custom middleware matcher on POST /api/auth/change-password, or an upgrade path.
- Helper is invoked after a user-initiated password change.
- Password RESET flow does NOT trigger the password-changed email (reset has its own confirmation).
- New unit test covers the happy path: change password → password-changed email appears in audit log with correct user name + support contact.
- Guard documented: email sends at most once per password change event.
notes:
SPIKE RESOLUTION (2026-05-05):

DECISION: BetterAuth top-level `hooks.after` middleware (better-auth/api `createAuthMiddleware`) with explicit `ctx.path === '/change-password'` matcher.

Wired in workers/auth/src/auth-config.ts; handler in workers/auth/src/password-changed-hook.ts.

Why this over the three options the bead proposed:

1. databaseHooks.user.update.after with password-hash diff — REJECTED. Both /change-password and /reset-password call internalAdapter.updateAccount(account.id, { password: hash }) (better-auth update-user.mjs:172 for change, password.mjs:152 for reset). DB-layer hooks fire for both and cannot distinguish them without inspecting the in-flight HTTP request out-of-band, which is brittle. A path filter at the request layer is the robust signal. Also: password is on the `account` table, not `user`, so the bead's user-update wording was a false lead.

2. Custom Hono middleware on POST /api/auth/change-password — REJECTED. Sits OUTSIDE BetterAuth, runs BEFORE BetterAuth decides the request was successful. Would require either re-parsing BetterAuth's response or accepting that we fire on validation errors. The BetterAuth hooks.after mechanism gives the success/error signal in ctx.context.returned for free (success: { token, user: { id, email, name, ... } }; failure: APIError).

3. BetterAuth upgrade — REJECTED. Disproportionate scope.

Once-per-event guarantee: each successful POST /change-password → one BetterAuth invocation → one hooks.after call → one email send. On error, ctx.context.returned is APIError (no .user.email) and isChangePasswordSuccess returns false — early-return without sending. Reset (/reset-password), set-password (/set-password), sign-in, and other paths early-return on the path matcher.

Failure isolation: sendPasswordChangedEmail swallows network errors internally; hook also wraps in try/catch.

Audit-log behaviour: notifications-api /internal/send writes to emailAuditLogs for every send attempt. One email per change → one audit entry.

VERIFIED: 13 unit tests pass in workers/auth/src/__test__/password-changed-hook.test.ts (happy path, 4 path-exclusion cases, 5 response-shape exclusion cases, 2 failure-isolation cases). 30/30 auth tests still pass (no regressions). pnpm --filter auth typecheck clean.

STATUS 2026-05-05: implementation + tests complete locally; commit deferred — auth-config.ts is also being modified by the in-flight Codex-v38zb (welcome email) wiring (afterEmailVerification + executionCtx threading). My change (createAuthMiddleware import + hooks.after block) is textually non-overlapping with the welcome-email change (afterEmailVerification block + executionCtx parameter), but both touch the same file in the working tree. Coordinated commit pending — either bundle as a single email-wiring commit covering both beads, or land Codex-v38zb first and follow with a clean Codex-2arpf-only commit.
close_reason: Implemented and merged in commit 83e30581 on fix/Codex-v38zb. Verified: 30/30 auth tests pass (typecheck clean, no regressions). v38zb wired afterEmailVerification + sendWelcomeEmail dedupe via emailAuditLogs. 2arpf wired hooks.after path-matched on /change-password with type-narrowed success-shape gate.

title: Verify purchases table scope filters — soft-delete column check
description:
PurchaseService.verifyPurchase (line 505-522) and getPurchaseHistory (line 558) filter by customerId + contentId + status but do NOT use scopedNotDeleted. If the purchases table has a deletedAt column, a soft-deleted row could leak as 'valid purchase'. If the table does not have deletedAt, document the exception in code comments.

Investigation:
- Check packages/database/src/schema for purchases table definition
- If deletedAt exists → add whereNotDeleted to verifyPurchase and getPurchaseHistory
- If not → add a code comment noting 'purchases are immutable; refunds flip status' and leave as-is

Sites:
- packages/purchase/src/services/purchase-service.ts:505-522 (verifyPurchase)
- packages/purchase/src/services/purchase-service.ts:558 (getPurchaseHistory)

Reference: skill backend-dev ref 03 scoping rules — 'Every DB query uses scopedNotDeleted'.

Source: backend-dev-review iter 0.
close_reason: Verified packages/database/src/schema/ecommerce.ts purchases table has NO deletedAt column — purchases are immutable once created (status transitions only: pending -> completed -> refunded). No soft-delete filter needed in verifyPurchase or getPurchaseHistory. Existing scoping by customerId+status=completed is sufficient. No code changes required.

title: CRITICAL: Investigate API data unwrap heuristic — full dataflow audit
description: api.ts:182-189 unwraps any response with a 'data' key. This could strip pagination from endpoints returning {data:[...], pagination:{...}}. REQUIRES: (1) Trace every endpoint through procedure() to document which wrap in {data:...} envelope. (2) Identify any endpoint returning natural 'data' field. (3) Determine if unwrap should check for {success:true, data:...} instead. (4) Fix duplicated unwrap in deprecated fetch() method. (5) Add warning comment about the heuristic. This is the highest-risk item — touches every API call.
close_reason: Fixed: Added __procedure marker to all 3 procedure envelope files. Updated api.ts request() and fetch() to check for marker instead of heuristic data-key detection. Eliminates risk of incorrect unwrapping for non-procedure endpoints.

title: Debug & Fix: Org Content Detail Page (/[slug]/content/[contentSlug])
description:
## Page Purpose
Content detail/playback page within an org. Shows content info, video player, handles streaming. THE PAGE MOST LIKELY TO HAVE CACHING ISSUES.

## Key Files
- Page: apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/content/[contentSlug]/+page.svelte
- Server: apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/content/[contentSlug]/+page.server.ts
- Remote: apps/web/src/lib/remote/content.remote.ts (getContentBySlug, getPublicContent)
- Remote: apps/web/src/lib/remote/library.remote.ts (getStreamingUrl, getPlaybackProgress)
- VideoPlayer: apps/web/src/lib/components/VideoPlayer/
- Progress: apps/web/src/lib/collections/progress.ts, progress-sync.ts

## Data Flow
1. Server load: resolves content by slug via getPublicContent or getContentBySlug
2. If auth: checks access (purchased/free), gets streaming URL
3. Returns content detail, streaming info, playback progress
4. Client: VideoPlayer handles HLS streaming
5. Progress tracked via progressCollection (localStorage)
6. Progress synced to server via progress-sync.ts (30s interval, visibility, beforeunload beacon)

## What to Verify with Playwright
- Content loads by slug
- Content metadata displays (title, description, creator, thumbnail)
- VideoPlayer renders (if user has access)
- Purchase CTA shows (if user doesnt have access)
- Playback progress saved locally on play
- Progress restored on return visit (localStorage)
- Streaming URL generation works (signed R2 URLs)
- CACHE_HEADERS: PRIVATE if auth, DYNAMIC_PUBLIC if public view
- Navigation from studio to this page: IS IT A FULL RELOAD? (KNOWN ISSUE)
- Console errors during playback
- HLS.js initialization
- Responsive video player

## CRITICAL KNOWN ISSUE
- Studio to content navigation causes full page reload
- Content uses QueryClient (session cache, not localStorage)
- Navigating between route groups may force full reload
- Check: does SvelteKit treat this as client-side nav or full reload?
- Check: is content data being cached anywhere for cross-navigation?
close_reason: Content detail page verified: client-side nav from org home works (no full reload), VideoPlayer with graceful error state, content metadata (title, type, duration, creator), About section. Video streaming errors expected in local dev (no R2 media). Navigation between org pages uses SvelteKit client router correctly.

title: STUDIO-08: Media Management Page
description:
## Objective
Build a media management page in the Studio for uploading, organizing, and managing media assets (images, videos, audio) with upload progress, thumbnails, and transcoding status.

## What Already Exists
- **API client**: `apps/web/src/lib/server/api.ts` does NOT have media methods — these need to be added. The media-api worker (port 42076) has endpoints: POST /api/media, GET /api/media, GET /api/media/:id, PATCH /api/media/:id, DELETE /api/media/:id, POST /api/media/:id/upload-complete
- **No media.remote.ts**: No remote function file for media exists. Must be created.
- **UI components**: Table (full suite), Badge, Button, Card, Skeleton, Dialog, Input, Pagination all exist
- **Studio layout**: Provides org.id and userRole from parent data
- **No media route under studio**: `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/media/` does not exist
- **Transcoding**: @codex/transcoding package exists for pipeline details
- **Upload flow**: (1) POST /api/media to create record + get presigned URL, (2) PUT to R2, (3) POST /api/media/:id/upload-complete to trigger transcoding

## What Needs to Be Created

### 1. API Client Methods
**File**: `apps/web/src/lib/server/api.ts` — Add media namespace:
- api.media.create(data) — POST /api/media
- api.media.list(params) — GET /api/media
- api.media.get(id) — GET /api/media/:id
- api.media.update(id, data) — PATCH /api/media/:id
- api.media.delete(id) — DELETE /api/media/:id
- api.media.uploadComplete(id) — POST /api/media/:id/upload-complete

### 2. Media Remote Functions
**File**: `apps/web/src/lib/remote/media.remote.ts` (NEW)
- listMedia query (paginated)
- createMedia command (create record + return presigned URL)
- updateMedia command (metadata edits)
- deleteMedia command
- completeUpload command

### 3. Media Route
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/media/+page.svelte` — Media management page
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/media/+page.server.ts` — Server load with pagination
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/media/+error.svelte` — Error boundary

### 4. Media Components
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/MediaGrid.svelte` — Grid/list view of media items
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/MediaCard.svelte` — Individual media item card with thumbnail + status badge
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/MediaUpload.svelte` — Drag-and-drop upload zone with progress tracking

### 5. Upload Flow Implementation
Client-side upload flow: (1) Call createMedia to get presigned URL, (2) PUT file directly to R2 with progress tracking via XMLHttpRequest, (3) Call completeUpload to trigger transcoding. Track upload progress in local state. Handle multi-file uploads with queue.

## Key Patterns
- Upload requires client-side JS for R2 direct upload — cannot use progressive enhancement for upload step
- Transcoding status: Badge variants (pending=neutral, processing=warning, complete=success, failed=danger)
- Media type detection from file MIME type
- Thumbnail display: use presigned R2 URLs or placeholder by type

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Media grid/list page at /studio/media
- [ ] Drag-and-drop upload zone
- [ ] Upload progress tracking (per file)
- [ ] Multi-file upload support
- [ ] Transcoding status badges
- [ ] Metadata editing (title, description)
- [ ] Delete with confirmation dialog
- [ ] Pagination for large libraries
- [ ] Empty state when no media
- [ ] paraglide i18n for all text

## Dependencies
- STUDIO-01 (Studio Layout Shell) — COMPLETE

## Effort Estimate
8-10 hours (largest studio task — includes API client, remote functions, upload flow, 3 components)
notes:
## Verified State (Mar 2026)

### CRITICAL: More Work Than Other Studio Tasks
This task requires creating the API client media namespace AND remote functions from scratch — unlike other studio tasks where admin.remote.ts and content.remote.ts already exist.

### What EXISTS
- Media-API worker (42076) with all endpoints operational
- @codex/transcoding package for pipeline context
- Table, Badge, Button, Card, Dialog, Skeleton, Pagination UI components
- Studio layout shell

### What DOES NOT EXIST
- No api.media.* methods in apps/web/src/lib/server/api.ts
- No media.remote.ts file
- No studio/media/ route directory
- No MediaGrid, MediaCard, or MediaUpload components
- No apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/ directory

### Upload Flow Reference
1. POST /api/media → creates DB record, returns { id, presignedUploadUrl }
2. Client PUTs file directly to R2 presigned URL (with progress tracking)
3. POST /api/media/:id/upload-complete → triggers transcoding pipeline

### No Backend Blockers
All media endpoints exist in media-api worker. Fully unblocked.

Choose (A) unless the DB schema requires top-level columns for indexing/querying. title: Q5/Q6: Platform-wide subscription-health banner (past_due + paused) on home + library + account
description:
Product decision (Q5 + Q6 answered): platform-wide health banner for users with past_due or paused subscriptions. Banner shows on home feed, library, and account — not just on the account/subscriptions page.

Engineering:
- New HealthBanner component (src/lib/components/ui/ or src/lib/components/subscription/)
- Mounted in (platform)/+layout.svelte and _org/[slug]/+layout.svelte (once each, guarded to avoid duplicate render)
- Derived from subscriptionCollection (client-side): any entry with status ∈ {past_due, paused} renders the banner

- Click-through:
  - past_due → /account/subscriptions with focus on the Update Payment button
  - paused → /account/subscriptions with focus on the Resume button
- Copy uses existing i18n (subscription_past_due_message, subscription_paused_message) + new i18n for the cross-subscription aggregated case ('1 subscription needs attention' / '{count} subscriptions need attention')

Accessibility: banner is a polite aria-live region, has dismissible button, doesn't trap focus.

Design token compliance: warning color for past_due, info color for paused. No hardcoded hex.

Tests: unit-test the derivation logic (given [{status: 'past_due'}, {status: 'active'}] → banner visible). Playwright for the click-through.

Scope exclusion: in-app push notifications and email dunning reminders — those live in the notifications service.

title: Lint: forbid uuidSchema on userId/creatorId fields — BetterAuth IDs are alphanumeric text
description:
## Problem

BetterAuth generates user IDs as alphanumeric strings (e.g. `4d675eb3e923d47695567467fd1017bb` — 32 hex chars, no dashes). The `users.id` column is `text`, not `uuid`. The codebase already has `userIdSchema` in `@codex/validation/primitives` for this, but it's easy to reach for `uuidSchema` (or `z.string().uuid()`) by muscle memory when wiring up a new filter.

When this mismatch happens, every real request returns 400 Bad Request — discovered via the `/explore?creator=<username>` bug where both the remote function and `publicContentQuerySchema`/`contentQuerySchema` had `creatorId: z.string().uuid()`. Because SvelteKit renders remote-function validation failures as bare "Bad Request" pages with no error body, these bugs are invisible until someone clicks through.

## Fix

Add an ESLint rule (or a codebase-search-based CI check) that flags:

1. Any Zod schema field named `userId`, `creatorId`, `ownerId`, `memberId`, `inviterId`, `invitedBy`, `uploadedBy`, `createdBy`, `assigneeId` etc. that is typed as `uuidSchema` or `z.string().uuid()`.
2. Suggest: use `userIdSchema` from `@codex/validation/primitives`.

Fields named `organizationId`, `contentId`, `mediaItemId`, `purchaseId`, `tierId`, `subscriptionId` etc. are genuinely UUIDs — those DB columns use `uuid()`. Only user-identifying fields are BetterAuth text.

## Implementation options

- ESLint custom rule: AST-match `z.string().uuid()` and `uuidSchema` used as values of object properties matching a "user-like" name pattern. Low-friction, IDE feedback.
- Grep-based CI check: simple `rg` pattern run in CI, fails the build if any match found. Fastest to ship.
- Document in `packages/validation/CLAUDE.md` as a strict rule (passive, but at least discoverable).

## Acceptance

- [ ] CI fails when someone writes `creatorId: z.string().uuid()` in a new schema
- [ ] `packages/validation/CLAUDE.md` documents the user-id-vs-uuid distinction with the table of known user-ID field names
- [ ] One-time audit of existing schemas — grep `(user|creator|owner|member|invit|upload|assign|createdBy).*uuidSchema` across `packages/validation/`
