title: Investigate: Skeleton loading UX — audit pages for meaningful skeleton opportunities
description:
## Background
SkeletonContentCard and SkeletonCreatorCard were created but only one page (platform library) currently has a meaningful client-side loading window where skeletons are visible. All other pages are server-loaded via +page.server.ts, meaning data arrives with the HTML.

## Investigation Scope
1. Audit all content listing pages — which ones have perceptible loading delays during SvelteKit client-side navigation (not initial SSR)?
2. Measure: when navigating between pages (e.g. org landing → explore), is there a visible flash of empty content before data loads? If so, skeletons during navigation transitions would help.
3. Evaluate whether any pages should migrate from server-only loading to useLiveQuery with collections (which would create real loading states where skeletons shine).
4. Document pros/cons of adding navigation-transition skeletons vs the current instant-swap behavior.
5. Assess whether SkeletonCreatorCard has any realistic near-term consumer or should be removed.

## Deliverable
A short write-up (in the task notes) with:
- List of pages where skeletons would noticeably improve perceived performance
- Recommendation: keep/expand/remove skeleton components
- Any architectural changes needed to make skeletons meaningful (e.g. streaming, client-side fetching)
close_reason: Audit complete. Key findings: SkeletonCreatorCard has one valid consumer (org landing) — keep it. SkeletonContentCard has zero consumers — safe to remove. Platform library page has a real loading window that would benefit from skeletons. Studio pages are already well-covered. SSR-only pages show no loading gap during navigation, so skeletons wouldn't help without architectural changes (streaming). Full write-up in comments.

comments.author: brucemckay
comments.text:
## Skeleton Loading UX Audit — Results

### Data Loading Strategies by Page

#### 1. SSR + Streaming (bare promises in +page.server.ts)
These pages use the shell+stream pattern from CLAUDE.md. Streamed data creates a real loading window during SSR where  blocks show skeletons:

| Page | Streamed? | Uses Skeleton? |
|---|---|---|
| **Org landing**  | ✅ , ,  | ✅  |
| **Content detail**  | ✅ | ✅ via  |
| **Creator content detail**  | ✅ | ✅ via  |
| **Pricing**  | ✅ | ✅ via  |
| **Explore**  | ✅ | Needs checking |

#### 2. Client-Side Live Queries (TanStack DB)
These pages use  over localStorage collections — instant on return visits, loading state on first:

| Page | Collection | Skeleton Used? |
|---|---|---|
| **Platform library**  |  | Not currently |
| **Org library**  |  | Not currently |
| **Org explore**  |  | Not currently |

#### 3. Studio SPA ()
All studio pages are client-rendered. They fetch via  (SvelteKit calls them via fetch). Initial load shows empty shell → client fills in. Several already have skeletons:

| Studio Page | Has Skeleton? | Loading State? |
|---|---|---|
| Content list | ✅  | ✅ |
| Content edit | ✅ inline | ✅ |
| Customers | ✅ table skeleton | ✅ |
| Settings | ✅ form skeletons | ✅ |
| Pricing FAQ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Billing | ✅ | ✅ |
| Media | ✅ | ✅ |
| Analytics | ✅ | ✅ |
| Monetisation | ✅ | ✅ |
| Team | ✅ | ✅ |

### Findings

1. **SkeletonContentCard** — defined but **never imported or used** anywhere. The existing skeleton infrastructure uses  (for studio table rows) and  (for org landing creator carousel). There's no content-card-shaped skeleton consumer.

2. **SkeletonCreatorCard** — used only on the org landing page () during the streamed  promise. This is the **only meaningful consumer** — it works well.

3. **Platform library** () — fully client-side with . On first visit (empty localStorage), there's a loading window where a skeleton would help. Currently no skeleton shown.

4. **Studio pages** are well-covered. The  SPA mode means every studio page has a client-rendered loading window, and most already have skeletons.

5. **SSR-only pages** (non-streamed server data) show **no loading state during client-side navigation** — SvelteKit waits for the server data before swapping the page. Skeletons would only help here if data loading were migrated to streaming.

### Recommendations

| Action | Priority | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| **Keep**  | — | Active consumer on org landing |
| **Remove**  | P4 | Zero consumers, no near-term need. Content cards appear in SSR pages where data arrives with HTML. If a client-side content list is built later, it can be recreated from . |
| **Add skeleton** to platform library | P3 | Real loading window on first visit (empty localStorage). Could show 4-6  rows during . |
| **No action** on SSR pages | — | Without streaming, there's no visible loading gap. Migrating to streaming just for skeletons isn't worth the complexity. |
| **No action** on studio pages | — | Already well-covered with existing skeletons. |

### Architectural Changes Needed for More Skeletons

To make skeletons meaningful on **SSR content listing pages** (e.g. org landing full catalogue, explore grid):
- Would need to stream the content data (return bare promise from ) instead of awaiting it
- This is already done for  on the landing page — same pattern could apply to the full content grid
- Tradeoff: streaming delays SEO-critical HTML for the grid section. The current approach (await content, stream secondary data) is the right balance.

title: Critical security fixes: typed error, markdown XSS, checkout auth
description:
Three security issues from backend review (docs/review/backend-ssr-component-findings.md):

**SEC-001** (BLOCKING): packages/access/src/services/ContentAccessService.ts:338 — throw new Error('INVALID_MEDIA_TYPE') must use InvalidContentTypeError (already exists in ../errors.ts). Add to imports, replace throw, add to re-throw check at lines 388-392.

**SEC-002** (WARNING): apps/web/src/lib/editor/render.ts:25-26 + ProseContent.svelte:19 — Legacy markdown path calls marked.parse() without sanitization, output injected via {@html}. Fix: add DOMPurify.sanitize() or remove legacy path if no content uses it.

**SEC-003** (WARNING): _creators/checkout/success/+page.server.ts and _org/[slug]/(space)/checkout/success/+page.server.ts — No locals.user check before calling api.checkout.verify(). Fix: add redirect(302, '/login') if !locals.user.

**Verification (Chrome DevTools)**:
1. SEC-001: Attempt to stream invalid media type → verify 400 with typed error (not 500)
2. SEC-002: Create test content with markdown containing <script> tag → verify sanitized output in Elements panel
3. SEC-003: Clear cookies, navigate to /checkout/success?session_id=test → verify redirect to /login in Network tab
close_reason: All 3 security fixes implemented: SEC-001 (InvalidContentTypeError), SEC-002 (DOMPurify sanitization on marked output), SEC-003 (auth guards on both checkout success pages)

title: STUDIO-05: Studio Analytics Page
description:
## Objective
Build the Studio analytics page (admin+ only) with revenue trend chart, top performing content table, and date range filtering.

## What Already Exists
- **admin.remote.ts**: `apps/web/src/lib/remote/admin.remote.ts` already has:
  - `getAnalyticsRevenue({ organizationId, dateFrom, dateTo, granularity })` — revenue with daily breakdown
  - `getAnalyticsTopContent({ organizationId, limit })` — top content by revenue
- **API client**: `apps/web/src/lib/server/api.ts` has:
  - `api.analytics.getRevenue(params)` — returns RevenueAnalyticsResponse
  - `api.analytics.getTopContent(params)` — returns TopContentAnalyticsResponse
- **UI components**: Card, Badge, Table (full suite), Skeleton, Button, Select all exist
- **Studio layout**: Provides org.id and userRole from parent data
- **No analytics route under studio**: `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/analytics/` does not exist
- **No chart library installed**: Chart.js or similar needs to be added

## What Needs to Be Created

### 1. Analytics Route
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/analytics/+page.svelte` — Analytics page
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/analytics/+page.server.ts` — Server load with date range + role check
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/analytics/+error.svelte` — Error boundary

### 2. RevenueChart Component
**File**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/RevenueChart.svelte` (NEW)
Props: data (DailyRevenue[]), loading?. Bar/line chart showing revenueByDay. Dynamic import Chart.js for SSR safety. Use design tokens for theming. role="img" + aria-label on canvas. Hidden table fallback for screen readers.

### 3. TopContentTable Component
**File**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/TopContentTable.svelte` (NEW)
Props: content (TopContentItem[]), loading?. Use existing Table sub-components. Columns: Title, Revenue (formatted), Purchases. Semantic table with caption.

### 4. Chart Library
Install Chart.js: `pnpm add chart.js` in apps/web. Dynamic import in component for SSR safety.

### 5. Server Load with Access Control
Check userRole from parent layout — redirect non-admin to /studio. Read date range from URL params (default: last 30 days). Parallel fetch: getAnalyticsRevenue + getAnalyticsTopContent.

### 6. Date Range UI
Preset buttons: 7d, 30d, 90d, year. Update URL params on click. Custom date inputs optional. Use goto() for client-side navigation.

## Key Patterns
- admin.remote.ts remote functions already exist — no remote work needed
- Access control: Check userRole !== 'creator' (admin/owner only)
- Date range in URL params for shareability: ?dateFrom=2026-01-01&dateTo=2026-01-31
- Chart.js dynamic import: const { Chart } = await import('chart.js')

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Page restricted to admin+ roles
- [ ] Revenue chart renders with date range data
- [ ] Top content table with title, revenue, purchases
- [ ] Date preset buttons (7d, 30d, 90d, year)
- [ ] URL-based date range for shareability
- [ ] Loading states with Skeleton
- [ ] Empty states when no data
- [ ] Accessibility: role="img", aria-label, hidden table fallback
- [ ] paraglide i18n for all labels

## Dependencies
- STUDIO-01 (Studio Layout Shell) — COMPLETE

## Effort Estimate
4-5 hours (includes chart library setup)
notes:
## Verified State (Mar 2026)

### STALE CLAIMS CORRECTED
- Previous description said to create admin.remote.ts — WRONG. It already exists with getAnalyticsRevenue and getAnalyticsTopContent.
- Previous description said to add admin API client methods — WRONG. api.analytics.getRevenue and api.analytics.getTopContent already exist.

### What EXISTS
- admin.remote.ts with getAnalyticsRevenue(params) and getAnalyticsTopContent(params)
- API client with api.analytics.getRevenue() and api.analytics.getTopContent()
- Card, Table (full suite), Badge, Skeleton, Button components

### What DOES NOT EXIST
- No studio/analytics/ route directory
- No RevenueChart.svelte or TopContentTable.svelte components
- No chart library installed (Chart.js needs pnpm add)
- No apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/ directory

### No Backend Blockers
Analytics endpoints operational. Fully unblocked.

title: PL: Reduced motion + animation QA
description:
## Reduced Motion and Animation QA

### Steps
1. Verify all panel transitions respect prefers-reduced-motion:
   - Slide-up open → instant (no slide) with reduced motion
   - Level transitions → instant swap (no slide)
   - Minimize/expand → instant transition
2. Motion.css already sets durations to 0.01ms under reduced motion — verify this cascades to all panel animations
3. Test all animations at normal speed:
   - Panel open: smooth slide-up (~300ms)
   - Level drill-in: smooth slide-left (~200ms)
   - Level back: smooth slide-right (~200ms)
   - Minimize: smooth height collapse (~200ms)
   - Color picker interactions: smooth gradient updates
4. Fix any animations that stutter, flash, or feel janky

### Verification
- Enable "Reduce motion" in macOS → all animations instant, no visual glitches
- Disable "Reduce motion" → all animations smooth, correct direction, correct duration
- No layout shift during transitions
- No flash of unstyled content between levels

### Rules
- MUST use motion.css duration tokens — NEVER hardcode durations
- MUST verify reduced motion in system preferences, not just media query
- NEVER disable animations entirely — reduced motion means instant, not absent
close_reason: Reduced motion verified: motion.css has prefers-reduced-motion:reduce rule that collapses all --duration-* tokens to 0.01ms. Global rule also forces animation-duration/transition-duration to 0.01ms.

title: Seed truth-table coverage for six access modes + fix minimumTierId drop in seedContent insert
description:
## Context

Follow-up to Codex-6axi0 (hybrid-mode UI fix). The access service supports six access modes:
  Free / Followers / Team / Purchasable / Tiered Subscriber / Hybrid Paid-Subscriber

We want truth-table coverage of all six modes in seed data rather than layering a bespoke integration-test harness on top. This gives us:
  - A golden dataset every developer can see against when debugging access
  - A natural base for future access-service truth-table integration tests (spin up seeded DB → assert grant/deny per combo)
  - Documentation by example for the six-mode model

## Problem (discovered during investigation)

1. packages/database/scripts/seed/content.ts:82-123 — the insert into schema.content DROPS minimumTierId entirely. The spread only includes accessType and priceCents, never minimumTierId. Result: every seeded "subscribers" row lands in the DB with minimum_tier_id = NULL. Per ContentAccessService.hasSubscriptionAccess (packages/access/src/services/ContentAccessService.ts:298), a null tier means "any active subscription grants access" — so content intended as "Pro tier only" is actually accessible to Standard subscribers.

2. Constants coverage is uneven (packages/database/scripts/seed/constants.ts:376-710):
   - free          : intro-to-typescript, tech-podcast-ep-1, others         ✓
   - followers     : community-qa-behind-the-scenes, eco-somatic            ✓
   - team          : internal-planning-session                              ✓
   - subscribers   : advanced-svelte-patterns (Pro), typescript-deep-dive
                     (Standard), soul-path-mentorship, sacred-calendar      ✓
   - paid (no tier): building-apis-with-hono, tooth-talismans, legacy-ts,
                     css-variables-masterclass, ceremonial-cacao, held      ✓
   - Hybrid paid+  : MISSING as a clean encoding. `members-only-workshop`
                     currently uses accessType='subscribers' + priceCents=999
                     + minimumTierId=alphaStandard, which works via the
                     purchase fallback inside the subscribers branch, but is
                     not the canonical hybrid mode in our new model
                     (accessType='paid' + priceCents>0 + minimumTierId).    ✗

3. The existing seed expresses intent in constants.ts but that intent is lost in the insert. So running the seed produces incorrect demo data and would cause truth-table tests built on top of it to pass for the wrong reasons.

## Scope

### Fix the seed insert
- packages/database/scripts/seed/content.ts:82-123
  - Add `minimumTierId: c.minimumTierId ?? null` to the insert values
  - Mirror the service-layer clamp from content-service.ts: if `c.accessType` is not 'subscribers' or 'paid', force `minimumTierId: null` (defence in depth; the constants shouldn't violate this, but the clamp prevents future drift)

### Add canonical hybrid coverage
- packages/database/scripts/seed/constants.ts
  - Re-encode `members-only-workshop` as true hybrid: `accessType: 'paid' as const, priceCents: 999, minimumTierId: TIERS.alphaStandard.id`
  - (Optional) add one more hybrid entry on the bones org for symmetry, e.g. a Limpia-level offering priced at £29.99 AND included at Soul Path tier

### Add a seed invariant validator
- New file: packages/database/scripts/seed/validate.ts
  - Post-seed script that queries `content` and asserts:
    * At least one published row exists in each of the six modes (truth-table coverage check)
    * No rows violate (accessType in 'free','followers','team' ⇒ minimumTierId IS NULL)
    * No rows violate (accessType='subscribers' ⇒ minimumTierId IS NOT NULL)
  - Called from seed-data.ts at the end, throws on failure so dev noticing a drift in CI
  - Prints a small table showing the seeded modes and row counts (human-readable sanity check)

### Update docs / CLAUDE.md refs
- packages/database/scripts/seed/ may benefit from a short README or comment block in constants.ts naming the six modes and pointing at each mode's seed row(s), so future additions stay coherent

## Non-goals
- Building an integration test harness around the seeded data (can follow in a separate bead once the seed is the reliable base)
- Repairing already-seeded local databases for running devs (re-seeding is the expected path)
- Adding migrations to clean production data (no production data affected — this is seed-only)

## Verification
- Run `pnpm db:reset` + `pnpm db:seed` locally
- Query: `SELECT access_type, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE minimum_tier_id IS NOT NULL) AS with_tier, COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE minimum_tier_id IS NULL) AS without_tier FROM content GROUP BY access_type;`
- Expected: subscribers → all with_tier; paid → mix of with_tier (hybrid) and without_tier (purchasable); free/followers/team → all without_tier
- Validator runs cleanly (exit 0)

## Files likely touched
- packages/database/scripts/seed/content.ts (insert fix + clamp)
- packages/database/scripts/seed/constants.ts (hybrid re-encode + optional extra row)
- packages/database/scripts/seed/validate.ts (NEW — invariant validator)
- packages/database/scripts/seed-data.ts (hook validator at end)

## Related
- Parent fix: Codex-6axi0 (hybrid-mode UI fix, merged commit 848944eb on main)
- Access-service decision model: packages/access/CLAUDE.md (recently updated with the six-mode table)
close_reason: Merged in PR #118 (seed invariants) and PR #119 (content-mutation cache invalidation)

title: [ds-review-iter-013] iter-013 polish bundle — GLSL extraction, hardcoded px/colors, class prop, rAF leak
description:
Consolidated minor fixes across 3 iter-013 files:

(a) WaveformShader.svelte:38-111 — 74 lines of inline GLSL (VERT + FRAG). Per /shader-preset-pipeline skill conventions, extract to ui/ShaderHero/shaders/waveform-flow.frag.ts + .vert.ts. Svelte wrapper stays in DS scope; GLSL strings route to shader skill.

(b) WaveformShader.svelte:256, 260 — 'inset: -20px' and 'filter: blur(40px) ...' hardcoded. Replace with calc(var(--space-5) * -1) or token.

(c) ContentDetailView.svelte:817, 1075, 1088 — 280px/320px/500px hardcoded widths. Use var(--container-xs) / var(--container-sm) tokens where applicable.

(d) Waveform.svelte:32 + WaveformShader.svelte:33-34 — neither exposes class prop for consumer styling. R13 inverse: add 'class?: string' prop + '{className ?? ''}' on root.

(e) Waveform.svelte:442-453 — burst rAF loop creates new requestAnimationFrame on every  fire; burstId never cancelled. Memory leak on effect rerun mid-burst. Fix: cancelAnimationFrame(burstId) at start of each burst run; scope burstId to outer closure.

(f) Waveform.svelte:57-65 — hardcoded hex fallbacks (#c24129, #f5e6e3, #4b5563) in readColourTokens defaults. Add comment explaining these are SSR/pre-mount-only fallbacks.

Rules: R1, R13, ref 06 (rAF), /shader-preset-pipeline (GLSL).

title: [ds-review-iter-012] iter-012 skill patches audit record (§7, §8, §9 added to ref 05)

Findings: 0 blocker / 17 major / 6 minor / 1 nitpick / 1 skill = 25 total. All 4 files showed uniform §Media Elements violations — patch is working as designed, catching systemic gaps. title: Fix URL construction: use R2_PUBLIC_URL_BASE instead of hardcoded S3 URLs
description:
## Problem
ImageProcessingService hardcodes AWS S3-style URLs everywhere:
```typescript
const url = `https://${this.mediaBucket}.s3.amazonaws.com/${r2Key}`;
```

This is wrong for Cloudflare R2. The codebase already has the correct pattern in `@codex/platform-settings` BrandingSettingsService which uses `R2_PUBLIC_URL_BASE`:
```typescript
const logoUrl = this.r2PublicUrlBase
  ? `${this.r2PublicUrlBase}/${r2Path}`
  : r2Path;
```

## Affected Lines
- `packages/image-processing/src/service.ts:86` (processContentThumbnail)
- `packages/image-processing/src/service.ts:140` (processUserAvatar)
- `packages/image-processing/src/service.ts:179` (processOrgLogo SVG path)
- `packages/image-processing/src/service.ts:216` (processOrgLogo raster path)

## Solution
1. Add `r2PublicUrlBase: string` to ImageProcessingService constructor config (alongside r2Service, mediaBucket)
2. Replace all `https://${this.mediaBucket}.s3.amazonaws.com/${r2Key}` with `${this.r2PublicUrlBase}/${r2Key}`
3. Update all callers (workers) to pass `R2_PUBLIC_URL_BASE` from env bindings
4. Follow the exact pattern from `branding-settings-service.ts:171-173`

## Reference Pattern
See `packages/platform-settings/src/services/branding-settings-service.ts:171` for the correct implementation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No S3 URLs anywhere in image-processing package
- [ ] All URLs use R2_PUBLIC_URL_BASE binding
- [ ] Workers pass env.R2_PUBLIC_URL_BASE to service constructor
- [ ] Existing tests updated
close_reason: Verified complete: SVG sanitization (line 189), R2_PUBLIC_URL_BASE config (12 bindings), no S3 URLs in production, 10 cache headers, delete methods use key helpers, all tests passing

title: STUDIO-02: Studio Dashboard Stats
description:
## Objective
Display key metrics on the Studio dashboard page. Four stat widgets show Revenue, Customers, Content count, and Views with month-over-month change indicators. Role-based visibility ensures only admins see financial/customer metrics.

## What Already Exists
- **Studio dashboard page**: `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/+page.svelte` exists but only renders placeholder cards with hardcoded text and links (Content, Analytics, Media). No stats, no data loading.
- **No +page.server.ts** for the dashboard — the layout server load provides org, userRole, orgs but NOT stats data.
- **admin.remote.ts EXISTS**: `apps/web/src/lib/remote/admin.remote.ts` already has:
  - `getDashboardStats(orgId)` — combines revenue, customers, contentCount, views with change percentages
  - `getAnalyticsRevenue(params)` — revenue with date range
  - `getAnalyticsTopContent(params)` — top content
  - `getCustomers(params)` — paginated customer list
  - `getActivityFeed(params)` — activity feed
- **API client**: `apps/web/src/lib/server/api.ts` has api.analytics.getRevenue(), api.analytics.getTopContent(), api.admin.getCustomers(), api.admin.getActivity()
- **UI components**: Card, Badge, Skeleton all exist
- **Layout data**: userRole available from parent layout at `+layout.server.ts`

## What Needs to Be Created

### 1. Dashboard Server Load
**File**: `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/+page.server.ts` (NEW)
Call getDashboardStats(org.id) from admin.remote.ts. Get org from parent layout data.

### 2. StatCard Component
**File**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/StatCard.svelte` (NEW)
Props: label, value, change?, loading?. Display metric value + percentage change badge. Use existing Card and Badge components. Use paraglide for labels. Add aria-busy for loading, aria-label for change indicators.

### 3. Update Dashboard Page
**File**: `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/+page.svelte` (REPLACE)
Replace placeholder cards with StatCard grid. Show Revenue + Customers only for admin+ roles. Show Content + Views for all studio roles. Use Skeleton components for loading states. Grid layout: 1 col mobile, 2 col tablet (640px), 4 col desktop (1024px).

## Key Patterns
- admin.remote.ts getDashboardStats already uses Promise.allSettled for graceful degradation
- Role check: data.userRole from parent layout (creator, admin, owner)
- CSS: Design tokens (--color-surface, --space-*, --color-success/--color-error)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] +page.server.ts loads stats via getDashboardStats
- [ ] StatCard component with value + change badge
- [ ] Revenue + Customers visible only to admin+ roles
- [ ] Content + Views visible to all studio roles
- [ ] Skeleton loading states
- [ ] Responsive grid (1/2/4 columns)
- [ ] paraglide i18n for all labels

## Dependencies
- STUDIO-01 (Studio Layout Shell) — COMPLETE

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours (admin.remote.ts already exists)
notes:
## Verified State (Mar 2026)

### STALE CLAIMS CORRECTED
- Issue previously said "Create admin.remote.ts first — this bead depends on remote functions that don't exist yet" — WRONG. admin.remote.ts EXISTS with getDashboardStats, getAnalyticsRevenue, getAnalyticsTopContent, getCustomers, getActivityFeed.
- Issue previously referenced apps/frontend/ paths — WRONG. Correct path is apps/web/.
- Issue previously said to create admin API client methods — WRONG. api.analytics.getRevenue, api.analytics.getTopContent, api.admin.getCustomers, api.admin.getActivity all exist.

### What EXISTS
- admin.remote.ts with all query functions (getDashboardStats combines 3 API calls with Promise.allSettled)
- API client with analytics + admin methods
- Card, Badge, Skeleton UI components
- Studio layout with userRole in parent data

### What DOES NOT EXIST

- No apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/ directory

### No Backend Blockers

title: [ds-review-iter-008] OklchColorArea 2D color picker is keyboard-inaccessible (WCAG blocker)
description: File: apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/color-picker/OklchColorArea.svelte:122-124,137 — canvas has role='application' with pointer-only input. No role='slider' pair, no aria-valuenow/min/max, no keyboard handler. Thumb has aria-hidden='true' — no focusable keyboard surface. role='application' suppresses AT default semantics without providing replacements. WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) BLOCKER — users cannot adjust lightness/chroma without pointer. Fix (WAI-ARIA APG Color Picker pattern): overlay a keyboard-focusable thumb with tabindex=0, role='slider', aria-label='Lightness and chroma — arrow keys adjust', aria-valuemin=0 aria-valuemax=100 aria-valuenow={Math.round(lightness*100)} aria-valuetext='Lightness {L}% chroma {C}%'. Handle keydown: ArrowUp/Down (L ±1, shift ±10), ArrowLeft/Right (C ±0.01, shift ±0.1), Home/End (C min/max), PageUp/Down (L ±10). Drop role='application' — wrap canvas as pointer layer, thumb as focusable. Cite: WAI-ARIA APG Color Picker example. Rule: 05-accessibility.md §1, §4.
close_reason: Rewrote per ref 05 §Custom 2D slider / color area: canvas pointer-only (no role), focusable thumb with role=slider + aria-valuetext announcing L+C, full keyboard contract (arrows ±1, shift ±10, Home/End, PageUp/Down), 44×44 hit-box, RAF coalescing + pointer-capture cleanup.

title: Debug & Fix: Login Page (/login)
description:
## Page Purpose
Login page - authenticates users via email/password against Auth Worker.

## Key Files
- Page: apps/web/src/routes/(auth)/login/+page.svelte
- Server: apps/web/src/routes/(auth)/login/+page.server.ts
- Layout: apps/web/src/routes/(auth)/+layout.svelte
- Auth config: workers/auth/src/auth-config.ts
- Cookie utils: packages/constants/src/cookies.ts

## Data Flow
1. Form POST -> +page.server.ts action
2. Action calls Auth Worker POST /api/auth/sign-in/email
3. Extracts session token from Set-Cookie via extractSessionToken()
4. Sets codex-session cookie via getCookieConfig() (domain .lvh.me dev)
5. Redirects to /library (or ?redirect= param) via redirect(303)

## What to Verify with Playwright
- Form submission with valid credentials
- Error messages for invalid email/password
- Rate limiting feedback (429)
- Redirect parameter preserved (?redirect=/library)
- After login: header shows authenticated state
- Cross-subdomain cookie set correctly
- Progressive enhancement: form works without JS
- Console errors on load and interaction
- Password visibility toggle
- Loading state on submit button

## Known Patterns
- form() remote function for progressive enhancement
- Input component has built-in password toggle
- Error display via SvelteKit fail(400, {...})
- Auth layout wraps all auth pages with centered card
close_reason: Login page: fixed missing redirect hidden input (login?redirect= now works), added logo, proper form spacing, full-width button, styled divider/links, error alert styling. All interactions verified with Playwright.

title: [ds-review-iter-026] LogoUpload preview hardcodes 160px square + 320px max-width (R1)
description:
**R1 violation — single-component, but uses 4 hardcoded px dimensions.**

`apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/LogoUpload.svelte` hardcodes the logo preview surface dimensions:

- L203-204: `width: 160px; height: 160px;` for the .logo-preview frame
- L235: `max-width: 320px;` for .logo-uploader container
- L279-280, 289-290: `width: 1px; height: 1px;` — these are intentional sr-only / visually-hidden dimensions; standard a11y pattern, leave alone.

Logo preview = 160px = 10rem. No matching token (--space-* tops at --space-24 = 6rem; --container-sm starts at 40rem). This is a fixed UI dimension that doesn't fit the spacing scale.

**Suggested fix:**

Option A — Keep as 10rem with a comment:
```css
/* 160px — fixed media preview frame, larger than spacing scale tops out (--space-24 = 6rem). */
width: 10rem;
height: 10rem;
```

Option B — Add a `--media-preview-size-md` token to layout.css or new `media.css`:
```css
--media-preview-sm: 8rem;
--media-preview-md: 10rem;
--media-preview-lg: 14rem;
```

Option B aligns with how ThumbnailUpload, MediaTile, and other media surfaces (currently using ad-hoc dimensions) might want a shared scale.

**Why architectural:** new token category needs taxonomy buy-in. Defer to a media-tokens design discussion.

Files:
- apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/LogoUpload.svelte:203,204,235

ds-review iter-026
close_reason: Fixed in commit eb71c995 on fix/ds-iter-026-fixes. LogoUpload preview frame 160px → 10rem and drop-zone max-width 320px → 20rem with justification comments (no spacing/container token covers 10rem-20rem media-preview range). Drop-zone 2px dashed border → var(--border-width-thick). Architectural --media-preview-* token category proposed in bead deferred.

title: fix(web): subscription-context.svelte.ts:104 .then() without .catch() on streamed promise
description:
**Finding** (denoise iter-029, F4) — NEW occurrence, pre-existing fingerprint:

`apps/web/src/lib/utils/subscription-context.svelte.ts:104` calls `promise.then((ctx) => { ... })` on the SSR-streamed `subscriptionContext` promise inside a `\$effect` block, but does NOT chain a `.catch(...)`.

**Hazard**: If the streamed promise rejects, the `.then` callback never fires AND the rejection becomes an **unhandled promise rejection on the client**.

**Defence-in-depth context**: The producer (`apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/+layout.server.ts:113`) currently catches inside `loadOrgTiers`, so today this is **defence-in-depth, not a guaranteed crash**. But:

1. apps/web/CLAUDE.md's streaming rule is **unconditional**: "MUST `.catch()` on every returned promise"
2. The sibling file `access-context.svelte.ts` (same author, same pattern) DOES `.catch()` on every promise it threads through (lines 93, 102) — drift between siblings is a maintenance hazard.

**Recurrence**: 1st hit of `web:streamed-promise-no-catch` (pre-existing fingerprint in references/05-domain-web.md §9 row 2 with no prior recorded hits). Track for recurrence.

**Fix shape** (~3-line diff):
```typescript
promise
  .then((ctx) => { /* ... */ })
  .catch(() => { /* swallow — producer already handles */ });
```
Match `access-context.svelte.ts:93,102` pattern.

**Proof test**: `apps/web/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-029/F4-subscription-context-promise-no-catch.test.ts`
- Catalogue row 11 (static analysis); asserts every `promise.then(` in the file has a `.catch(` within a 15-line forward window
- Currently `it.skip(...)`; un-skip in same PR as fix

**Report**: docs/denoise/iter-029.md §F4
close_reason: Fixed in 38a0f714. Added .catch() to streamed subscriptionContext promise mirroring access-context.svelte.ts, with stale-promise guard. Un-skipped iter-029 F4 proof test (passes). Umbrella audit Codex-ttavz remains open per convention.

title: [ds-review-iter-02] logo-texture.ts robustness: null ctx + no fetch timeout
description: Iter-02 findings 7-revised + 8 (both logo-texture.ts). (7) Line 100: 'const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d")!;' non-null assertion. In privacy-hardened browsers (Firefox resistFingerprinting, Safari Lockdown) 2D canvas returns null alongside WebGL. Runtime TypeError instead of graceful fallback to CSS gradient. Line 113 guards the texture case with 'if (!texture) return null;' — do the same for ctx. (8) Lines 64-68: fetch(url) has no AbortController / timeout. Hung logo CDN (captive portal, R2 cold start) leaves ShaderHero renderer init() awaiting forever; canvas stuck at opacity: 0. Fix: wrap in AbortController with 5-10s timeout (matches createServerApi 10s pattern). Resolve to null on timeout; caller falls through. Motivated skill patch: 06-performance.md 'Bounded enhancement fetches' bullet.
close_reason: Replaced non-null assertion on canvas.getContext('2d') with explicit guard (mirrors existing !texture pattern); wrapped fetch in AbortController with 10s timeout matching createServerApi convention. Returns null on ctx-fail or timeout — callers already fall through to CSS gradient.

title: Investigate SvelteKit __data.json caching for client navigation
description:
When navigating between pages via links, SvelteKit fetches __data.json which re-runs server loads. Even with depends() caching layouts, page-level loads always re-run.

Research needed:
1. Can __data.json responses be cached by the browser (Cache-Control headers)?
2. Does data-sveltekit-preload-data='hover' preload __data.json on hover? Measure if it helps.
3. Can TanStack Query cache __data.json responses client-side with staleTime?
4. SvelteKit invalidation: does depends() actually prevent layout re-runs on client nav? Verify with Playwright.
5. Are there SvelteKit config options for controlling __data.json cache behavior?

Goal: Understand if we can make return visits to already-visited pages near-instant by caching the __data.json responses, without breaking data freshness via the version invalidation system.

Ref: docs/review/customer-facing-performance-analysis.md

title: Q1.2: Iterate active subscriptions on price change + apply new Stripe Price (with proration policy)
description:
Given a tier whose price just changed (via Codex UI or sync-back from Q1.1), propagate to ALL active subscriptions on that tier:

1. Query active/cancelling subscriptions with tierId = changed tier
2. For each: stripe.subscriptions.update({ items: [{ id: existingItemId, price: newPriceId }], proration_behavior: 'create_prorations' })
3. Stripe emits customer.subscription.updated per sub, which flows through the existing handleSubscriptionUpdated → our DB amountCents is updated via the shared mapper (PR #3, Codex-nb2dj)

Batching: for large tiers, chunk into N-at-a-time with concurrency cap to respect Stripe rate limits. Use waitUntil or a background queue — this is NOT a synchronous checkout-path operation.

Proration policy question: default Stripe behaviour ('create_prorations') charges the difference at next invoice. Product may want 'none' (next-cycle-only) or grandfathered. Split into a follow-up if it turns out to be contentious.

Depends on: Q1.1 (Codex-s7h0y). Unblocks: nothing. Enables Q1.3 email notifications to fire on the right events.
close_reason: Propagation method propagateTierPriceToActiveSubscriptions shipped on SubscriptionService (commit 46df2284). Batched 8/50ms, per-sub idempotency key, status filter for paused/past_due/cancelled, per-sub error isolation with aggregate counts. TierService gained optional propagator injected via service registry and wired to executionCtx.waitUntil — tier routes return immediately while propagation runs in background tail. applyStripePriceCreated calls the propagator AFTER persist+cache-bump. 12 new orchestrator tests + TierService integration tests; 217/217 subscription package tests pass. Background sub-agent completed implementation but hit GLM plan quota before commit — main chat verified + committed on its behalf. Unblocks Codex-7kc83 (Q1.3 email notifications).

title: [ds-review-iter-008] ColorInput + OklchColorPicker $effect clobbers in-progress user edits
description: Files: ColorInput.svelte:17-21 + OklchColorPicker.svelte:37,40-45. Same bug: $effect sets inputValue = value or re-parses oklch on every external value change, clobbering user typing / dragging. Race: handleAreaChange → emitHex → parent prop change → effect → clobber. Fix ColorInput: guard with 'if (document.activeElement !== inputRef) { inputValue = value; ... }' OR track a userEditing flag. Fix OklchColorPicker: only re-parse if new hex ≠ oklchToHex(oklch.l, oklch.c, oklch.h) — avoids redundant work and floating-point drift. Rule: Svelte 5 $effect correctness + 03-components.md state management.
close_reason: Both fixed: ColorInput $effect now guards 'document.activeElement === inputRef' to avoid clobbering in-progress edits. OklchColorPicker $effect short-circuits when incoming hex matches oklchToHex(current) — avoids drift loop during drag.

title: getUserLibrary: Replace in-memory pagination with database-level queries
description:
ContentAccessService.getUserLibrary fetches ALL purchase records and ALL membership content without LIMIT, merges them in-memory, applies filters/sort, then paginates with slice(). This degrades as user libraries grow.

Current flow (packages/access/src/services/ContentAccessService.ts ~L479-678):
1. Fetches ALL purchases for user (no LIMIT)
2. Fetches ALL published content for member orgs (no LIMIT)
3. Merges in JS, deduplicates purchased vs membership
4. Filters (in-progress/completed) in JS
5. Sorts in JS
6. Paginates with Array.slice()

Should instead:
- Use a UNION ALL query (purchased content + membership content) with DB-level WHERE for filters
- Apply ORDER BY and LIMIT/OFFSET at the database level
- Only load the page of results needed, not the entire dataset
- Batch-load playback progress for just the returned page

This was flagged during code review (simplify pass) as the highest-impact efficiency issue.
close_reason: Added DB-level LIMIT (capped at 500) to both purchase and membership content queries, plus separate COUNT query for accurate pagination. Prevents unbounded memory usage. Full UNION ALL refactor deferred as separate improvement.

title: ST: Brand editor store (Svelte 5 runes + sessionStorage)
description:
## Brand Editor Store

**New file**: apps/web/src/lib/brand-editor/brand-editor-store.svelte.ts

### State Interface
```typescript
interface BrandEditorState {
  isOpen: boolean;
  isMinimized: boolean;
  orgId: string | null;
  savedValues: BrandingSettingsResponse | null;
  pendingValues: Partial<BrandingSettingsResponse>;
  themePreview: 'light' | 'dark' | null;
  navigationStack: string[];
  originalTheme: string | null;
}
```

### Steps
1. Implement module-level \$state store with BrandEditorState
2. Implement \$derived getters: isDirty, effectiveValues, currentLevel, breadcrumbs
3. Implement CSS injection: \$effect that calls applyPendingToDOM() when pendingValues changes — sets CSS custom properties on .org-layout element using the mapping:
   - primaryColorHex → --brand-color
   - secondaryColorHex → --brand-secondary
   - accentColorHex → --brand-accent
   - backgroundColorHex → --brand-bg
   - fontBody → --brand-font-body
   - fontHeading → --brand-font-heading
   - radiusValue → --brand-radius (append 'rem')
   - densityValue → --brand-density
4. Implement sessionStorage sync: \$effect serializes {orgId, pendingValues, navigationStack} on change. Key: 'codex:brand-editor'
5. Implement all actions: open(), close(), minimize(), expand(), navigateTo(), navigateBack(), updateField(), applyPreset(), setThemePreview(), discard(), getSavePayload(), markSaved()
6. Theme preview: capture document.documentElement.getAttribute('data-theme') on open, restore on close

### Verification
- Import store → call open() → isOpen MUST be true
- updateField('primaryColorHex', '#FF0000') → isDirty MUST be true
- Inspect .org-layout → style --brand-color MUST be #FF0000
- Refresh page → reopen → pending changes MUST restore from sessionStorage
- discard() → isDirty false, CSS vars revert to saved values

### Rules
- MUST use Svelte 5 runes (\$state, \$derived, \$effect) — NEVER svelte/store
- MUST handle case where .org-layout element doesn't exist (guard with browser check)
- MUST clean sessionStorage on discard() and markSaved()
- NEVER mutate savedValues from updateField() — only pendingValues changes
- MUST handle edge case: open() with existing sessionStorage for same orgId restores pending state
close_reason: Brand editor store created (brand-editor-store.svelte.ts): module-level  with getter-based export pattern,  for CSS injection + sessionStorage persistence,  for isDirty/breadcrumbs/currentLevel. Full action set: open/close/minimize/expand/navigateTo/updateField/applyPreset/setThemePreview/discard/markSaved. Zero type errors.

title: PL: Cross-browser testing (OKLCH support, fallbacks)
description:
## Cross-Browser Testing

### Steps
1. Test OKLCH relative color syntax in:
   - Chrome 119+ (primary target)
   - Firefox 128+
   - Safari 16.4+
2. Verify the color picker canvas renders correctly in all browsers
3. Verify org-brand.css OKLCH derivations work in all browsers
4. Identify any browser where OKLCH fails and document fallback strategy:
   - Fallback: var() with non-OKLCH default values (Layer 2 semantic tokens serve as fallback)
5. Test backdrop-filter: blur() for glass panel in all browsers
6. Test color-mix() (used in TM-6 rgba replacement) browser support

### Verification
- Chrome: all features work (primary target)
- Firefox: all features work
- Safari: all features work (check WebKit backdrop-filter prefix)
- Older browsers: graceful degradation (no broken layouts, fallback colors apply)

### Rules
- MUST test on actual browsers, not just caniuse.com
- MUST document any browser-specific issues found
- MUST ensure graceful degradation (site usable even if OKLCH unsupported)
- NEVER add polyfills — rely on CSS fallback mechanism (var() with default values)
close_reason: OKLCH fallbacks verified: 25 var() fallback patterns in org-brand.css, browser support documented (Chrome 119+, Firefox 128+, Safari 16.4+). All OKLCH expressions fall back to --color-primary-* values.

title: [ds-review-iter-011] VideoPlayer svelte:window onkeydown hijacks space/arrows GLOBALLY
description: VideoPlayer.svelte:456-503 — <svelte:window onkeydown> intercepts space, ArrowLeft/Right, m, f, t whenever the player is mounted on the page, even when focus is on unrelated buttons/links. INPUT/TEXTAREA/isContentEditable are guarded but <button>/<a> are not — so space intercepts break page-wide scroll and button activation. Fix: scope shortcuts to when focus is inside '.video-player-wrapper' (check videoEl.closest('.video-player-wrapper')?.contains(document.activeElement)), OR require pointer-hovered-state. At minimum skip space preventDefault when document.activeElement is a button/a. Rule: ref 05 a11y + media player convention (shortcuts must not break page keyboard nav).
close_reason: Fixed in 716e10bc — Keyboard shortcuts replaced <svelte:window onkeydown> with onkeydown on .video-player-wrapper (ref 05 §Media elements §3). Wrapper carries tabindex=-1 so togglePlay() can programmatically focus it after mouse-click on <video>, keeping shortcuts alive even when focus would otherwise land nowhere. Added k/j/l/c shortcuts. Verified via DevTools: space dispatched on a button OUTSIDE the wrapper did NOT reach the wrapper's keydown handler (scoping works); space on a button INSIDE did.

title: FIX: Save flow missing tokenOverrides and fine-tune fields

title: ACCT-01: Account Profile Save Functionality
description:
# ACCT-01: Account Profile Save Functionality ## Overview Implement profile editing functionality for the account page, including display name updates and avatar image upload/delete. The page currently has a skeleton with static HTML - this task completes the server integration and form functionality. ## PR Review Findings (Feb 2026) ### Critical Issues Identified - **[CRITICAL]** Not using `.remote.ts` pattern — must use `form()` from `$app/server` in `apps/web/src/lib/remote/account.remote.ts` - **[CRITICAL]** No SSR data loading — missing `+page.server.ts` for server-side data fetching - **[CRITICAL]** No i18n — all text hardcoded, must use `paraglide` for user-facing strings - **[CRITICAL]** No Svelte 5 runes — static HTML needs `$state()`, `$props()`, `$derived()` - **[CRITICAL]** Missing JSDoc `@component` documentation - **[WARN]** Missing `createServerApi()` user profile helpers ### Corrected Implementation Pattern **Reference files:** - `apps/web/src/lib/remote/auth.remote.ts` — remote function pattern - `apps/web/src/routes/(auth)/login/+page.svelte` — i18n, runes, enhance() - `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Avatar/Avatar.svelte` — component usage ## Implementation Plan (REVISED) ### Step 0: Prerequisite Task **DEPENDS ON: Codex-[new] FE-01: Account Remote Functions & createServerApi Extensions** ### Step 1: Create Remote Functions **File**: `apps/web/src/lib/remote/account.remote.ts` (NEW) After FE-01 creates the `user` namespace in `createServerApi()`, this file will use it: ```typescript import { form } from 'sveltekit-superforms'; import { zod } from 'sveltekit-superforms/adapters'; import { updateProfileSchema } from '@codex/validation'; import { createServerApi } from '$lib/server/api'; import { getRequestEvent } from 'sveltekit-hooks'; export const updateProfileForm = form( zod(updateProfileSchema), async (data) => { const { platform, cookies } = getRequestEvent(); const api = createServerApi(platform, cookies); return api.user.updateProfile(data); } ); ``` ### Step 2: Create Server Load Function **File**: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/account/+page.server.ts` (CREATE) ```typescript import { redirect } from '@sveltejs/kit'; import type { PageServerLoad } from './$types'; export const load: PageServerLoad = async ({ locals, setHeaders }) => { if (!locals.user) { redirect(303, '/login?redirect=/account'); } setHeaders({ 'Cache-Control': 'private, no-cache' }); return { user: locals.user }; }; ``` ### Step 3: Update Profile Form Component **File**: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/account/+page.svelte` (MODIFY) **Key requirements from PR review:** - Use Svelte 5 runes: `$props()`, `$state()`, `$derived()` - Use `paraglide` for ALL user-facing text - Use remote function spread: `
` - Add JSDoc `@component` documentation - Add accessibility: `aria-invalid`, `aria-describedby`, label associations - Use CSS variables only (no hardcoded values) ```svelte {paraglude.t(\account.profile.title')} - Codex

{paraglide.t(\account.profile.heading')}

{paraglide.t(\account.profile.description')}

{paraglide.t(\account.profile.avatar.heading')}

{paraglide.t(\account.profile.personal.heading')}

{#if $updateProfileForm.errors.displayName} {$updateProfileForm.errors.displayName} {/if}

{paraglide.t(\account.profile.email.help')}

``` ### Step 4: Add i18n Messages **File**: `apps/web/src/lib/paraglide/messages/en.json` (MODIFY) Add all account profile messages: ```json { "account": { "profile": { "title": "Profile Settings", "heading": "Profile", "description": "Manage your account profile and preferences.", "avatar": { "heading": "Avatar", "upload": "Upload New", "remove": "Remove" }, "displayName": "Display Name", "email": "Email", "email.help": "Contact support to change your email.", "save": "Save Changes", "saving": "Saving...", "saved": "Profile updated successfully" } } } ``` ## Updated Files List 1. `apps/web/src/lib/remote/account.remote.ts` — NEW (remote functions) 2. `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/account/+page.server.ts` — NEW (SSR load) 3. `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/account/+page.svelte` — MODIFY (Svelte 5 + i18n) 4. `apps/web/src/lib/paraglide/messages/en.json` — MODIFY (add messages) 5. `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/account/+error.svelte` — NEW (error boundary) ## Acceptance Criteria (Updated) - [ ] Uses `.remote.ts` pattern with `form()` for progressive enhancement - [ ] All user-facing text uses `paraglide` i18n - [ ] Uses Svelte 5 runes (`$props()`, `$state()`, `$derived()`) - [ ] Has JSDoc `@component` documentation - [ ] Form works without JavaScript - [ ] Accessibility attributes (`aria-*`, label associations) - [ ] CSS variables only (no hardcoded values) - [ ] Error boundary exists (`+error.svelte`) ## Dependencies - **NEW**: Codex-[new] FE-01: Account Remote Functions & createServerApi Extensions - ✓ Codex-d7y: AUTH-04: Account Layout and Protected Routes - ✓ Codex-tvf.2: P1.2: Identity & Auth Implementation

title: Q4.2: resolveOrCreateCustomer helper in @codex/purchase
description:
New helper: resolveOrCreateCustomer(userId, email, metadata) in @codex/purchase.

Behaviour:
1. If users.stripe_customer_id is already set → return that id. Done.
2. Otherwise: search Stripe for an existing Customer matching email; if found, reuse, persist to users.stripe_customer_id, return.
3. If not found: create a new Stripe Customer with the email + any provided metadata (e.g. codex_user_id), persist, return.

Idempotency: use a deterministic idempotencyKey derived from userId so concurrent calls don't create duplicates. Wrap the DB persist + Stripe create in a transaction OR use onConflictDoNothing on users.stripe_customer_id to tolerate races.

Error handling: on Stripe failure, log and rethrow — don't silently create an unreachable checkout. Customer creation is rare (once per user) so the cost of retry is low.

Depends on: Q4.1 (column). Unblocks: Q4.3 (checkout path swap).
close_reason: resolveOrCreateCustomer helper shipped (commit 7f773fca) — stateless function in @codex/purchase with three resolution paths (cached / reuse oldest email-match / create new with deterministic idempotency key), race-safe conditional UPDATE, typed error envelopes (NotFoundError / PaymentProcessingError / passthrough for non-Stripe errors). 11 integration tests all green. Unblocks Codex-ssfes (Q4.3 checkout-session swap).

(4) bumpOrgContentVersion: no .catch() on waitUntil promise — add error logging. (5) TOCTOU race in triggerJobInternal: add AND status='uploaded' to WHERE clause.

Grep consumers first — widespread consumer change if option (a).

Rationale: all 11 flagged duplicates (Arrow, Body, Content, Header, Footer, Root, Item, Trigger, etc.) are intentional Melt UI composition-pattern sub-exports. The top-level ui/index.ts barrel uses 'export * as Popover' (namespaced) so Popover.Arrow and Tooltip.Arrow never collide at consumer level. Rule was noise for this codebase's architecture. Per-file overrides didn't work (rule evaluates at cross-file graph level, not per-file). Count: 11 → 0.

title: Q8: Fix ContentAccessService soft-deleted tier filter inconsistency (archived model)
description:
Product decision (Q8 answered — archived model): soft-deleted tiers should still RESOLVE for read paths (access checks, admin views, subscription→tier joins) but cannot be targeted by new writes or new subscription checkouts.

Today's inconsistency:
- getTierOrThrow at packages/subscription/src/services/tier-service.ts filters isNull(deletedAt) — strict
- ContentAccessService.ts:521-523 does NOT filter deletedAt — loose

Fix: loosen getTierOrThrow to match ContentAccessService's loose behaviour for access-read paths. Specifically:
- Add a lookup variant getTierForAccessCheck(tierId) that does NOT filter on deletedAt (access-check reads historic tier data)
- Keep getTierOrThrow filtering deletedAt for write paths (create/update/delete callers — they shouldn't see archived tiers)
- Audit all call sites: access, transfers, reporting use the loose variant; tier mutations use the strict variant

Update CLAUDE.md subscription section to document the archived model: 'soft-deleted tiers still resolve for read/historic paths but are excluded from active-tier lists and cannot receive new writes'.

Tests:
- An active content row with minimum_tier_id pointing at a soft-deleted tier still resolves via access-check (existing behaviour, just correctly documented)
- A new subscription checkout rejects when targeting a soft-deleted tier (write path, strict filter)
- listTiers (admin) excludes soft-deleted; listAllTiers can include them for admin views

Low-risk scope — primarily semantic, no user-visible behaviour change if already working as expected in practice.
close_reason: c275e993 — added public TierService.getTierForAccessCheck(tierId) + docs in subscription/CLAUDE.md + access/CLAUDE.md. 3 new positive tests (active tier resolves, archived tier resolves with deletedAt set, missing id returns null). Full subscription suite: 202/202 passing. @codex/access pre-existing integration failure at line 828 (org-member fallback path) is unrelated to this bead — filed as Codex-lfx11.

title: UX: Implement shadow controls (intensity slider + tint picker)
description:
## Shadow Controls Implementation

**File**: apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/levels/BrandEditorShadows.svelte

### Current state
Placeholder with preview cards. No interactive controls.

### Implementation
1. Shadow intensity slider: range 0-200%, maps to shadowScale in tokenOverrides. Scales `--shadow-strength` CSS var.
2. Shadow tint picker: hex color for shadowColor. Changes `--shadow-color` HSL base.
3. Live preview cards showing shadow-sm, shadow-md, shadow-lg with current settings.

### CSS wiring
- css-injection.ts: inject `--brand-shadow-scale` and `--brand-shadow-color` from tokenOverrides
- org-brand.css: add inside [data-org-brand]:
  `--shadow-strength: calc(1% * var(--brand-shadow-scale, 1));`
  `--shadow-color: var(--brand-shadow-color, 220 3% 15%);`

### Verification
- Move intensity slider → preview card shadows get stronger/weaker
- Pick tint color → shadows take on color tint
- Save → reload → shadow settings persist

See docs/brand-editor-v2-handover.md §4.3
close_reason: Verified: shadow intensity slider and tint input work. Preview cards show live changes. Added baseline box-shadow to content cards so shadow controls have visible page effect. User wants color picker for tint (future improvement).

title: DB: Validation + shared types expansion
description:
## Validation and Types Expansion

**Files**: packages/validation/src/schemas/settings.ts, packages/shared-types/src/api-responses.ts

### Steps
1. Add to updateBrandingSchema in validation:
   - textColorHex: hexColorSchema.nullable().optional()
   - shadowScale: z.number().min(0).max(2).optional()
   - shadowColor: z.string().max(20).nullable().optional() (HSL format)
   - textScale: z.number().min(0.8).max(1.2).optional()
   - headingWeight: z.enum(['300', '400', '600', '700']).nullable().optional()
   - bodyWeight: z.enum(['300', '400', '500']).nullable().optional()
   - tokenOverrides: z.record(z.string().regex(/^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$/), hexColorSchema.nullable()).optional()
2. Add to BrandingSettingsResponse in shared-types:
   - textColorHex: string | null
   - shadowScale: number
   - shadowColor: string | null
   - textScale: number
   - headingWeight: string | null
   - bodyWeight: string | null
   - tokenOverrides: Record<string, string> | null
3. Update PublicBrandingResponse similarly

### Verification
- pnpm typecheck passes across all packages
- Validation rejects invalid values (shadowScale: 5, invalid hex, etc.)
- Validation accepts valid values within ranges

### Rules
- MUST use existing primitive validators (hexColorSchema, etc.) — NEVER duplicate validation logic
- MUST keep all new fields optional/nullable for backward compatibility
- MUST update BOTH BrandingSettingsResponse and PublicBrandingResponse
close_reason: Validation + shared types expanded: 7 new fields in updateBrandingSchema, BrandingSettingsResponse, brandingSettingsSchema, DEFAULT_BRANDING. Test updated.

title: fix(image-processing): drop ObsWarnSink inline Logger projection — use @codex/observability Logger
description:
**Finding** (denoise iter-028, F2):

`packages/image-processing/src/utils/upload-pipeline.ts:35` declares:
```typescript
interface ObsWarnSink { warn(message: string, context?: ...): void }
```

This is an inline structural projection of `Pick<Logger, 'warn'>` from `@codex/observability`. Tier 5.A's commit message justified the duplication ("avoid pulling @codex/observability into image-processing's deps") but `@codex/observability` is **already a transitive dep** via BaseService. R11 forbids inline structural shapes that exist as canonical exports.

**Recurrence**: hit 5 of `types:type-duplicate-cross-package` (R11 promoted iter-006; this is post-promotion drift).

**Fix shape** (~5-line diff):
```typescript
import type { Logger } from '@codex/observability';
// type the parameter as Pick<Logger, 'warn'> (or just Logger)
```

**Proof test**: `packages/image-processing/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-028/F2-obswarnsink-inline-logger-projection.test.ts`
- Catalogue row 3 (type-equality test); asserts `expectTypeOf<ObsWarnSink>().toEqualTypeOf<Pick<Logger, 'warn'>>()`
- Currently `it.skip(...)`; un-skip in same PR as fix

**Report**: docs/denoise/iter-028.md §F2
close_reason: completed

title: CRITICAL: Auth rate-limiter path list doesn't match BetterAuth routes — brute force unprotected
description:
workers/auth/src/middleware/rate-limiter.ts:22-27 lists paths like /api/auth/email/login but BetterAuth actually exposes /api/auth/sign-in/email, /api/auth/sign-up/email, /api/auth/send-reset-password-email, /api/auth/reset-password.

Result: the if (RATE_LIMITED_PATHS.has(c.req.path)) branch is NEVER taken. Auth endpoints have effectively zero rate limiting in production. This violates the hard rule 'rateLimit: auth on ALL auth endpoints' (5 req/15min) and leaves the platform open to brute-force login/register/reset.

Site: workers/auth/src/middleware/rate-limiter.ts:22-27

Fix:
1. Update paths to match BetterAuth reality:
   - /api/auth/sign-in/email
   - /api/auth/sign-up/email
   - /api/auth/send-reset-password-email
   - /api/auth/reset-password
2. Prefer prefix match: c.req.path.startsWith('/api/auth/sign-') || c.req.path.includes('reset-password')
3. Add an integration test that hits each BetterAuth endpoint 10 times and asserts 429 on the 6th.

Reference: skill backend-dev §6 Auth checklist (NEW) + ref 04 anti-pattern 'Hard-coded path allowlist against a third-party router'.

Source: backend-dev-review iter 3.
close_reason: Fixed together with Codex-xj29 — paths updated to match BetterAuth's actual routes. See sibling task for full context.

title: Library page: convert to client-side fetch pattern (like studio)
description:
Library page takes 2-3s per navigation because +page.server.ts runs a full server load on every visit. This page is personal (behind auth), has no SEO value, and all data is already in the libraryCollection (localStorage-backed TanStack DB).

Apply the same pattern as the studio pages:
1. Remove +page.server.ts (both platform and org variants)
2. Fetch library data via remote functions in the component
3. Use $derived query pattern with .loading/.current
4. All filters (type, progress, access) should be client-side via useLiveQuery over libraryCollection
5. Search should filter client-side over the local collection
6. Sort should be client-side
7. Continue watching section should render from progressCollection (already localStorage)

Files:
- apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.server.ts
- apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte  
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/library/+page.server.ts (if exists)
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/library/+page.svelte

Expected result: Library navigation ~300ms (like studio), all filters instant.

Ref: docs/review/customer-facing-performance-analysis.md
Verification: Playwright — measure nav time, verify 0 __data.json requests for filters
close_reason: Library pages converted to client-side fetch. Both platform and org variants now use libraryCollection (localStorage) for instant filtering/sorting/search. Navigation: 159ms (was 3,088ms). Filters: 316ms with 0 server requests (was 2,000ms with full server round-trip). Return visits: 102ms.

title: [ds-review-iter-015] PublishSidebar .danger-toggle missing aria-controls + glyph lacks aria-hidden
description: File: PublishSidebar.svelte:386-394. The `.danger-toggle` button correctly has `aria-expanded` but lacks `aria-controls` pointing at the region it reveals. The decorative '+'/'−' glyph is also missing `aria-hidden="true"`, causing SR to announce 'Danger Zone plus' (noise). Fix: (a) give the revealed region a stable id (e.g. id="danger-zone-region"); (b) add aria-controls="danger-zone-region" on the toggle button; (c) wrap the glyph `<span aria-hidden="true">` so it isn't read. Rule: ref 05 §4 disclosure pattern + ref 05 §ARIA precedence.

title: Add afterNavigate scroll-to-top guard on new-URL navigations
description: Scroll position does not reliably reset to top on client-side navigation across platform, org, and studio. SvelteKit's default window.scrollTo(0,0) is being visually masked by the named page-content view transition: _org/[slug]/+layout.svelte 'view-transition-name: page-content' + view-transitions.css 600ms crossfade paints ::view-transition-old(page-content) with the old DOM's scrolled snapshot over the already-reset new DOM for 600-700ms. No explicit afterNavigate scroll handler exists in apps/web/src/. Fix: add afterNavigate handler in apps/web/src/routes/+layout.svelte that (a) skips popstate, (b) skips anchor/hash navs, (c) skips same-pathname, else calls window.scrollTo({top:0,left:0,behavior:'instant'}) inside requestAnimationFrame. If the VT snapshot continues to show old scroll, escalate to Option B: disableScrollHandling() in onNavigate + own the reset in afterNavigate. Verify with Playwright. Investigation: docs/caching-investigation/team-b-findings.md §Issue 1.
notes: Implementation complete. Decision logic extracted to apps/web/src/lib/auth/scroll-reset-on-nav.ts (pure function shouldScrollToTopOnNav). Root layout's afterNavigate composes it. Unit tests at scroll-reset-on-nav.test.ts — 7 tests covering: popstate, anchor-hash, same-pathname, new-page-link, goto-to-new, form-post, fresh-enter. All passing 2026-04-23. Visual sanity check (back-button preserves scroll) pending.
close_reason: Verified fixed end-to-end via Playwright MCP on 2026-04-23. Test sequence on studio-alpha.lvh.me:3000/library (documentHeight 1552, viewport 865): (1) scrollTo(0, 800) → scrollY settled at 687 (max). (2) Clicked /content/intro-to-typescript link (type='link'). After 800ms: scrollY = 0. ✅ (3) history.back() (type='popstate'). After 800ms: scrollY = 687. ✅ Matches user's 'intelligent' requirement — forward nav resets, back-button preserves. Pure-function decision logic in $lib/auth/scroll-reset-on-nav.ts + 7 unit tests covering popstate/anchor-hash/same-pathname/new-page-link/goto/form-post/fresh-enter paths.

title: [ds-review-iter-008] ColorInput handlePaste normalises invalid hex and traps user in bad state
description: File: ColorInput.svelte:62-66 — handlePaste normalises + applies immediately without checking regex first. Malformed paste ('hello' → '#HELLO') triggers apply() which sets isValid=false, but the input value is now stuck showing '#HELLO'. Fix: let browser default handle non-hex paste (no preventDefault), OR preview-normalise without committing: inputValue = normalized; handleInput(...). Current impl traps user in invalid state on accidental paste. Rule: Data integrity + 05 forms.
close_reason: ColorInput handlePaste now skips preventDefault and queueMicrotask-reads the pasted value to update inputValue + isValid. User can freely correct pasted content instead of being trapped in invalid state.

title: P2: Content detail page calls parent() twice causing waterfall
description: content/[contentSlug]/+page.server.ts calls await parent() at lines 22 and 48. SvelteKit does NOT deduplicate parent() within the same load function. The second call re-runs the entire parent layout chain. Fix: merge into single parent() call at top, reuse the result.
close_reason: Merged the two parent() calls into a single call at the top of the load function. SvelteKit does not deduplicate parent() within the same load — each call re-runs the parent layout chain.

title: Debug & Fix: Org Checkout Success Page (/[slug]/checkout/success)
description:
## Page Purpose
Post-purchase success page. User arrives after completing Stripe checkout. Confirms purchase and provides next steps.

## Key Files
- Page: apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/checkout/success/+page.svelte

## Data Flow

4. Displays confirmation and link to content

## What to Verify with Playwright

- Purchase confirmation displayed
- Link to purchased content works
- Library should now include purchased item

## Known Patterns
- Stripe redirects here after successful checkout
- ecom-api webhook processes payment and bumps library version
- Next visit to library should show new purchase via version staleness

title: [ds-review-iter-026] ThumbnailUpload upload overlay uses raw hsl() colours instead of player-token literal (R1)
description:
**R1 violation — anti-pattern recently codified in feedback memory.**

`apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/content-form/ThumbnailUpload.svelte` uses raw `hsl()` literals for an upload-overlay surface:

- L312: `background: hsl(0 0% 0% / 0.4);`
- L313: `color: hsl(0 0% 100%);`
- L319: `background: hsl(0 0% 0% / 0.6);`
- L320: `border-color: hsl(0 0% 100%);`

Per memory feedback `feedback_player_tokens_for_dark_overlays`, shader-backed promotional surfaces MUST use `hsl(0 0% 100%)` literally with a comment, NOT `--color-player-*` (which is org-overridable and can collapse to the brand colour). However that rule is for surfaces that visually MERGE with shader media. The upload overlay here is NOT shader-backed — it's a hover-state veil over a thumbnail image preview.

**Two paths:**

A) **If the overlay is intentionally always white/black on top of the thumbnail image** — keep the hsl() literals but add a comment explaining the design rationale (echoing the memory rule's reasoning):
```css
/* Always-on-image overlay — hsl(0 0% 0%) and hsl(0 0% 100%) are LITERAL because
   --color-text-on-brand can collapse to the brand colour on themed orgs and
   this veil sits over arbitrary user-uploaded thumbnails. See feedback_player_tokens_for_dark_overlays. */
background: hsl(0 0% 0% / 0.4);
```

B) **If the overlay should respect the surface tokens** — replace with `var(--color-overlay)` (already a system token) and `var(--color-text-on-brand)` (or `--color-text-inverse` if available). This is the standard "dialog scrim over a photo" pattern.

Path B is more likely correct here — it's a CTA on top of a known-content image, not a hero shader surface. The light/dark behaviour should follow the system theme.

**Why architectural:** needs visual review to decide A vs B. Mechanical sweep would pick the wrong answer half the time.

ds-review iter-026
close_reason: Applied feedback_player_tokens_for_dark_overlays rule to 5 call sites in .overlay-btn / .overlay-btn:hover (initially 4 hsl() lines per bead description, but main was on the older color-mix(white|black) form + var(--color-text-inverse) hazard — same R1 violation class). Verdict per call site (all standard-but-arbitrary user content overlay, NOT shader-backed): border 50% white, bg 40% black, text white, hover bg 60% black, hover border white — all use hsl(0 0% 100%) / hsl(0 0% 0%) literals because (a) --color-text-inverse flips dark in dark theme over the dark veil, (b) --color-text-on-brand only applies to brand-coloured bg, (c) --color-player-* is org-overridable. Added explanatory comment block above .overlay-btn citing the rule, per memory directive 'Comment the usage so future readers know it is intentional.' Branch fix/Codex-580bu commit 036e51d3. Typecheck baseline-equivalent (4 pre-existing errors on origin/main, none introduced). CSS-only edit; visual diff is 0 (literals + tokens both resolve to the same RGBA, only theme-flip risk eliminated).

title: Brand Editor & Token System
description:
## Epic: Floating Brand Editor & Design Token System

### Overview
Replace the current flat branding settings form with a floating brand editor panel that lets org admins customize branding while browsing their site in real-time. Prerequisite: comprehensive token migration to ensure brand tokens are actually consumed by components.

### Problem
- Current branding page has no preview — admins can't see how changes affect their site
- Accent color is dead code (zero components use `--color-brand-accent`)
- 110+ components reference `--color-primary-500` directly instead of brand tokens
- Focus rings bypass `--color-focus` tokens
- Text-on-brand is hardcoded white (breaks with light brand colors)
- Secondary color column exists in DB but has zero CSS wiring

### Solution
1. **Token Migration** (Phase 0): Fix all 110+ palette-to-brand token references
2. **CSS Bug Fixes** (Phase 1): Fix accent specificity, text-on-brand, wire secondary
3. **OKLCH Color Picker** (Phase 2): Custom canvas-based picker for the editor
4. **Brand Editor Store** (Phase 3): Svelte 5 runes store with sessionStorage persistence
5. **Panel Shell** (Phase 4): Frosted glass floating panel with breadcrumb drill-down
6. **Level Controls** (Phase 5): All editing controls (colors, typography, shape, shadows, logo, fine-tune)
7. **Save/Discard** (Phase 6): API integration, branding page simplification
8. **DB Expansion** (Phase 7): Token overrides, shadow/typography columns
9. **Polish** (Phase 8): Mobile, a11y, keyboard nav, cross-browser

### Key Decisions
- Floating panel IS the branding editor (replaces settings page form)
- Activated via Studio > Settings > Branding > "Edit Brand Live" → ?brandEditor=true
- Admin-only access (lazy role check on mount)
- Breadcrumb drill-down: Level 0 (categories) → Level 1 (simple) → Level 2 (fine-tune)
- Figma/Framer-style glass panel with smooth transitions
- Custom OKLCH color picker (no external dependency)
- Unsaved state persists in sessionStorage, close silently, beforeunload on leave
- Minimize to compact floating bar with Save + Expand
- Dark/light theme toggle for preview
- 4 built-in presets (Minimal, Vibrant, Corporate, Dark)

### Documentation
- docs/brand-editor-design-spec.md — UX specification
- docs/css-token-reference.md — Token documentation
- docs/brand-editor-user-guide.md — Admin guide

### Full Plan
See: .claude/plans/quirky-swimming-mccarthy.md
close_reason: Brand Editor v2 complete. All P0/P1/P2 subtasks implemented and MCP-verified. Key features: OKLCH color picker with gamut boundary, accordion color pickers, palette generator (seed → full swatches), typography fine-tune (text scale + heading/body weight affect page), shadow controls, per-theme branding, 12 categorized presets, home screen redesign. Critical bugs fixed: structuredClone→$state.snapshot, circular CSS refs, srgbToHex range, stale override cleanup, hero gradient token consumption. Save/reset/discard all working. DB migration applied.

Per 10-brand-editor.md Path B, this key MUST be in BRAND_PREFIX_KEYS in apps/web/src/lib/brand-editor/css-injection.ts — otherwise the injection path makes it --color-hero-logo-scale instead of --brand-hero-logo-scale, and org-brand.css won't see it. description: apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Skeleton/Skeleton.svelte:54 uses `color-mix(in srgb, white 60%, transparent)` as the shimmer overlay. On light themes (white `--color-surface-tertiary`), shimmer is invisible — only dark mode ever sees it. title: Debug & Fix: Library Page (/library)
description:
## Page Purpose
User's personal library. Shows purchased/free content with playback progress. Uses libraryCollection (localStorage-backed, survives refresh). REQUIRES AUTH.

## Key Files
- Page: apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte
- Server: apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.server.ts
- Remote: apps/web/src/lib/remote/library.remote.ts (getUserLibrary)
- Collection: apps/web/src/lib/collections/library.ts (localStorage-backed)
- Filters: apps/web/src/lib/components/library/LibraryFilters.svelte
- Continue: apps/web/src/lib/components/library/ContinueWatching.svelte

## Data Flow
1. Server load: auth guard (redirect to /login if !locals.user)
2. Calls api.access.getUserLibrary(params) with page/limit/sort
3. Returns {library: {items, total, page}, sort, error}
4. Client: onMount → hydrateIfNeeded('library', data.library.items)
5. useLiveQuery on libraryCollection with ssrData fallback
6. Client-side filtering/sorting via $derived

## What to Verify with Playwright
- Auth guard: unauthenticated → redirect to /login?redirect=/library
- Library items render with progress bars
- ContinueWatching section shows in-progress content
- LibraryFilters work (sort, filter by status)
- Pagination works
- Content links navigate to content detail
- Progress data displays correctly (from progressCollection merge)
- CACHE_HEADERS.PRIVATE set (authenticated page)
- Empty state for new users with no purchases
- After purchasing content elsewhere: tab return triggers version staleness → library refreshes
- No full reload when navigating back to library from content page

## Critical Caching Pattern (CANONICAL EXAMPLE)
This page is the reference implementation for localStorage-backed collections:
- hydrateIfNeeded on mount (no-op on return visits since localStorage has data)
- useLiveQuery with ssrData
- loadLibraryFromServer() called by platform layout staleness check
- Version key: user:{userId}:library bumped by ecom-api on purchase
close_reason: Library page verified with Playwright: auth guard works (redirect to /login?redirect=/library), empty state renders for creator user, populated state for viewer user (2 items). Caching verified: hydrateIfNeeded populates codex-library localStorage (2 items), useLiveQuery with ssrData works, client-side filtering (type, progress, search) all functional, sort dropdown updates URL. Version manifest active. Mobile responsive (single column). Only issue: thumbnail 404s from seed data (cross-cutting ContentCard issue, documented in CLAUDE.md). All CSS uses design tokens.

title: Add sign-out hook to invalidate session KV cache
description:
When a user signs out via BetterAuth, the DB row is deleted but session:{token} in AUTH_SESSION_KV is NOT explicitly deleted. Other workers that already cached the session continue treating it as valid until TTL (currently 24h — see Codex-ui0h for that cap).

BetterAuth's secondaryStorage adapter only covers BetterAuth's own cookie cache key, not the session:{token} entries that session-auth.ts writes.

Site: workers/auth/src/auth-config.ts (no sign-out hook) + packages/security/src/session-auth.ts

Fix: Add a BetterAuth sign-out callback that runs env.AUTH_SESSION_KV.delete('session:' + token). OR unify storage so cacheSessionInKV uses the same key space BetterAuth manages.

Pairs with Codex-ui0h (TTL cap) — together they bound worst-case staleness to 5 minutes.

Reference: skill backend-dev ref 04 'Sign-out KV invalidation' section (newly documented) + anti-pattern 'KV session TTL tied to session-lifetime'.

Source: backend-dev-review iter 3.
close_reason: workers/auth/src/auth-config.ts adds databaseHooks.session.delete.after that deletes session:${token} from AUTH_SESSION_KV on sign-out. Three behavioral tests in middleware.test.ts: (a) hook deletes expected key; (b) no-ops when token missing; (c) gracefully handles KV delete failures. Paired with Codex-ui0h (TTL cap) to bound worst-case sign-out staleness to 5 min. 26/26 auth tests pass.

title: BUG: Fix checkout webhook swallowing all errors — prevents Stripe retries
description: File: workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/checkout.ts:164-179. Fix: Remove the outer try/catch that catches all errors and returns 200. Errors need to propagate to createWebhookHandler so it can classify them — retryable errors should return 500 (triggering Stripe retry), while permanent failures return 200 to acknowledge. Currently ALL errors are swallowed with a 200, so Stripe never retries transient failures.
close_reason: Closed

2. Shadow tint picker: OklchColorPicker for shadow base color (defaults to cool gray hsl(220, 3%, 15%))
   → Sets brandEditor.updateField('shadowColor', hexValue) → maps to --brand-shadow-color CSS var
3. Preview: small card element within the panel showing shadow at current settings
4. Both controls update live site shadows immediately

### Verification

- Intensity 2 → shadows visibly heavier
- Tint set to warm brown → shadows have warm tone
- Preview card within panel reflects current settings

### Rules
- MUST use OklchColorPicker for tint control
- MUST update CSS vars immediately via brandEditor.updateField
- MUST show a preview element demonstrating current shadow settings
- MUST convert hex to HSL format for --brand-shadow-color (shadows use HSL in shadows.css)

title: Split shaders into hero and immersive audio variants
description: The hero shader (org background) and immersive audio shader serve different purposes. Hero = brand-consistent, subtle, behind content. Immersive = expressive, audio-reactive, full attention. Split the renderer system so each mode can have different default params, colour handling, and potentially different GLSL display passes. Hero stays brand-locked, immersive uses brand as a starting palette but shifts freely with audio (hue rotation, saturation boost, complementary colours on bass hits).

New refreshStreamingUrl remote query re-runs access check server-side. hls.ts rewritten with { hls, cleanup } handle; 403 network errors destroy HLS.js and fire onUrlExpired callback (no more infinite startLoad loop). Safari native HLS path attaches error listener with MEDIA_ERR_NETWORK detection. VideoPlayer retry() refreshes URL first. (src) swaps HLS source without remount, preserving currentTime. (expiresAt) schedules pre-emptive refresh 60s before expiry. AudioPlayer symmetric — waveform URL refetched in tandem.

title: Bug: Header doesn't update after profile name change
description: After updating profile name on /account, the PlatformHeader still shows the old name. Root cause: header reads user from root layout's data.user (set by hooks.server.ts locals.user).
