title: Revisit AUTH_SESSION_KV 5-minute cookieCache TTL vs cross-device revocation SLA
description: BetterAuth caches validated sessions in AUTH_SESSION_KV for 5 minutes (cookieCache.maxAge = 300 or expiresAt TTL in cacheSessionInKV). For auth-gated content pages this means a cross-device sign-out or permission change can take up to 5 minutes to propagate even once the other device's invalidate fires. Actions: (1) Decide whether to shorten the TTL for revocation-sensitive flows (e.g. Math.min(60, ttl) for paid content) or accept the 5-min SLA. (2) Document the chosen trade-off in docs/caching-strategy.md. (3) Ensure docs/caching-strategy.md 'server-authoritative always' rule is reflected in code comments. Related to Codex auth-sync P0 - this one is explicitly deferred pending that P0 landing. Investigation: docs/caching-investigation/team-a-findings.md §Fix Approach #2.
close_reason: Reviewed 2026-04-23 after Codex-b6emg landed. Decision: keep cookieCache.maxAge=300 as-is. Rationale: (1) Same-device sign-out propagates immediately — BetterAuth calls secondaryStorage.delete on /sign-out which clears KV session entry via createKVSecondaryStorage.delete (see packages/security/src/kv-secondary-storage.ts:126-139). (2) Cross-device sign-out has no cookie-cascade path; no TTL change can fix that without SSE/polling/push. The root layout's visibility-return invalidate (Codex-b6emg) handles cross-device UI state within one foreground. (3) Access control (streaming URLs, admin capability, paid content) re-checks DB on each action via hasContentAccess — stale session cache cannot grant revoked authority. The 5-min window only affects 'am I logged in?' UI freshness, which Codex-b6emg now addresses. Inline comment added to workers/auth/src/auth-config.ts explaining the trade-off so future devs don't re-litigate.

SCOPE: Add Storybook interaction tests using play functions

GOAL: Automate user flow testing within Storybook stories

CURRENT STATE:

- Stories show variants and states
- NO interaction testing (manual only)

TARGET STATE:
- Stories include play functions for automated interaction
- Common user flows tested automatically
- Integration with @storybook/test addon

STORYBOOK INTERACTION PATTERN:
Use @storybook/test for user simulation:
- userEvent.click() for clicks
- userEvent.type() for keyboard input
- expect() assertions from Vitest
- within() to scope queries

1. Button.stories - Click handling, loading state
2. Input.stories - Type text, validation, error states
3. Dialog.stories - Open, close, escape key
4. Accordion.stories - Expand, collapse, keyboard nav
5. Tabs.stories - Switch tabs, keyboard navigation
6. DropdownMenu.stories - Open, select item, close
7. Tooltip.stories - Hover, focus, dismiss
8. Form flows - Multi-field validation

EXAMPLE PLAY FUNCTION:
Story with play function that:
- Opens a dialog
- Fills form fields
- Validates error messages
- Submits successfully
- Verifies success state

BENEFITS:
- Visual testing + behavior testing in one

title: [ds-review-iter-024] Intro video invisible until cache flush — lazy auto-finalize only runs via polling, not cold page load
description:
Confirmed P1 visibility gap.

CHAIN:
1. BrandEditorIntroVideo.svelte:112-158 five-step upload flow:
   a. createMedia → registers media item
   b. XHR PUT to presigned R2 URL
   c. completeUpload → triggers RunPod transcoding
   d. linkIntroVideo → writes branding_settings.introVideoMediaItemId (NOT introVideoUrl — set to null at this point because media.status is not yet 'ready')
   e. Polls getIntroVideoStatus(orgId) every 4 seconds

2. branding-settings-service.ts:453-457 (linkIntroVideo): writes introVideoMediaItemId and sets introVideoUrl to null if media is not yet transcoded

3. branding-settings-service.ts:556-574 (getIntroVideoStatus): lazy auto-finalize — if media.status becomes 'ready' AND introVideoUrl is null, constructs the HLS URL and writes it

4. ONLY the editor polls getIntroVideoStatus. If the user closes the editor before transcoding finishes (minutes later), no one polls the status endpoint, introVideoUrl stays null

5. When a visitor reloads the page, +layout.server.ts fetches data.org (cached 30min), introVideoUrl is null, intro video button absent

6. User sees: 'I uploaded the intro video successfully' → closes editor → visits page → no intro video button. Blank. Looks broken.

WORKAROUND CURRENTLY: user must either leave editor open during transcoding (several minutes) OR reopen the editor after transcoding completes (which triggers a fresh poll and auto-finalizes).

FIX:
Option A (clean): move auto-finalize to the RunPod webhook callback that marks media 'ready'. When transcoding completes, the webhook triggers a lookup of orgs with this media as intro video and writes introVideoUrl atomically. No polling dependency.

Option B (minimal): make getIntroVideoStatus fallback be triggered by ALL page loads that have introVideoMediaItemId set but introVideoUrl null. Add a SSR check at +layout.server.ts level: if (brandFineTune.introVideoMediaItemId && !introVideoUrl) { await refresh + retry }

Option A is architecturally better. Option B is a quick patch.

TESTS:
- E2E: upload intro video, close editor before transcoding, wait for transcoding, reload, assert intro video button appears

LABEL: ds-review, ds-review-iter-024, brand-editor, transcoding
notes: Closed in commit 5548e091. Full writeup (root cause, fix, tests, verification) at docs/brand-editor-investigation/implementation-summary.md — see the bead's named section.
close_reason: Fixed in commit

title: [ds-review-iter-013] iter-013 skill patches audit record (§10 Canvas-based slider widgets)
description: Iter-013 findings: 1 blocker / 6 major / 7 minor / 1 nitpick / 1 skill = 16 total. 9 code beads + 1 audit = 10 beads. Skill patch: ref 05 §Media Elements §10 — 'Canvas-based slider widgets' (role='slider' on <div>+<canvas> without HTMLMediaElement). WAI-ARIA APG requires Home/End (mandatory) + PageUp/Down (recommended coarse-grain). iter-012 extraction exposed: createMediaKeyboardHandler assumes HTMLMediaElement.currentTime mutation; canvas-based sliders need onSeek(time) callback pattern. Iter-014 impl agent should extend $lib/components/media-a11y/keyboard.svelte.ts with createSliderKeyboardHandler({ onSeek, getCurrentTime, duration, stepSecs?, pageSecs? }). Key insight: iter-012 helper was correctly-scoped to real media, NOT too narrow — the new helper is a sibling, not a replacement. Reviewer saturation signal: 3 consecutive media iterations; recommend iter-014 diverges to commerce/account surfaces. R12 PASS (9 consecutive). R14 partial (Waveform non-canonical).

title: SETTINGS-02: General & Social Settings
description:
# SETTINGS-02: General & Contact Settings

## Objective
Build the default settings page (General tab) for managing organization contact info and social media links.

## Current State (verified 2026-03-18)

### Backend - FULLY OPERATIONAL
Endpoints at `workers/organization-api/src/routes/settings.ts`:
- GET /api/organizations/:id/settings/contact -> ContactSettingsResponse
- PUT /api/organizations/:id/settings/contact -> body: UpdateContactInput

### Database Schema (packages/database/src/schema/settings.ts - contact_settings table)
- platformName: varchar(100), NOT NULL, default 'Codex Platform'
- supportEmail: varchar(255), NOT NULL, default 'support@example.com'
- contactUrl: text, nullable
- timezone: varchar(100), NOT NULL, default 'UTC'
- twitterUrl: text, nullable
- youtubeUrl: text, nullable
- instagramUrl: text, nullable
- tiktokUrl: text, nullable

### Validation Schema (packages/validation/src/schemas/settings.ts)
updateContactSchema:
- platformName: string, trim, min(1), max(100), optional
- supportEmail: string, email format, optional
- contactUrl: urlSchema, nullable, optional
- timezone: timezoneSchema, optional
- twitterUrl: urlSchema, nullable, optional
- youtubeUrl: urlSchema, nullable, optional
- instagramUrl: urlSchema, nullable, optional
- tiktokUrl: urlSchema, nullable, optional

urlSchema blocks javascript:, data:, ftp:// protocols. Only http/https allowed.

### Frontend Remote Layer
- org.remote.ts has getOrgSettings(orgId) via query() -> returns AllSettingsResponse (branding+contact+features)
- No form() for contact settings mutations exists yet
- No dedicated contact settings remote function exists

### Existing UI Components
Input, TextArea, Button, Select, Label, Switch available in $lib/components/ui/

## Implementation Tasks

### 1. Create Contact Settings Remote Functions
File: `apps/web/src/lib/remote/settings.remote.ts` (shared with branding)

- form() for updateContactSettings using updateContactSchema from @codex/validation
- Calls PUT /api/organizations/:id/settings/contact
- Pattern: account.remote.ts updateProfileForm

### 2. Create General Settings Page
Files:
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/+page.svelte` (default tab = General)
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/+page.server.ts`

Server load: fetch contact settings via getOrgSettings() or dedicated query.
Form: progressive enhancement with <form {...contactSettingsForm}>.

### 3. Form Layout

**General Settings Section:**
- Organization Name (platformName): Input, required, max 100, character counter
- Support Email (supportEmail): Input type="email", required

- YouTube URL (youtubeUrl): Input type="url", optional

**Note:** There is NO description field in the contact_settings schema. The organizations table has a description column, but that is managed elsewhere (org profile, not settings). Do NOT add a description field to this form.

## Technical Notes
- All social URL fields are stored as full URLs (not handles/usernames)
- Empty string inputs should be converted to null before sending to API
- Use updateContactSchema from @codex/validation for client-side validation
- Svelte 5 runes: $state() for form values, $derived() for validation state

- Toast system (Codex-67t6): COMPLETE

NO BACKEND WORK REQUIRED

title: UI-03: Shared ContentCard Component
description: ContentCard component EXISTS at `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ContentCard/ContentCard.svelte` with: thumbnail (16:9 aspect ratio, lazy loading), duration badge (bottom-right), content type badge (top-left, uppercase), title (2-line clamp), description (2-line clamp), creator info (Avatar + name link), loading skeleton state, hover effects (border + shadow), actions snippet slot, dark mode support, i18n via paraglide. Uses Svelte 5 runes ($props, $derived), design tokens throughout. **MISSING features needed by consumers:** 1. **Progress bar overlay** — The library page (`apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte`) currently renders progress bars INLINE in its own markup (progress-track/progress-fill divs on the thumbnail, plus a text "X% watched" / "Completed" label below). This should be moved INTO ContentCard as an optional `progress` prop so all consumers (library, continue-watching) get consistent progress rendering. 2. **Price badge** — No price badge exists anywhere. Needed for catalogue/purchase pages (ORG-01, ORG-02) to show "Free" or "$X.XX" on the thumbnail overlay (top-right position). 3. **Responsive thumbnails** — The `` tag uses a single `src` with no `srcset`. Multi-size WebP thumbnails (sm:200px, md:400px, lg:800px) are available from the transcoding pipeline but unused. Consider adding srcset support (blocked on Codex-gg9 for the full ResponsiveImage pattern, but can add basic srcset now). **Implementation:** - Add optional `progress?: { positionSeconds?: number; durationSeconds?: number; completed?: boolean; percentComplete?: number } | null` prop - Add optional `price?: { amount: number; currency: string } | null` prop (null = free) - Add progress bar track/fill overlay to thumbnail area (absolute positioned, bottom) - Add progress text to card body (conditional) - Add price badge to thumbnail area (absolute positioned, top-right) - Refactor library page to pass `progress` prop to ContentCard instead of inline rendering **Effort:** 3-4 hours. Pure UI, no backend blockers.

title: UI-01: Toast Notification System
description:
## Objective
Implement a shared toast notification system used across the platform for success/error/info feedback. Multiple tasks reference 'toast feedback' (SETTINGS-01, SETTINGS-02, STUDIO-01, STUDIO-09, ECOM-02, etc.) but no shared system exists yet.

## Files to Create
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Toast.svelte` — Individual toast component
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ToastContainer.svelte` — Container that positions and manages toasts
- `apps/web/src/lib/stores/toast.ts` — Svelte store/rune for managing toast state
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/index.ts` — Update barrel export

## Implementation Details

### Toast Store (toast.ts)
Using Svelte 5 runes:
- `toasts` state: array of toast objects { id, message, type, duration }
- `addToast(message, type, duration?)` — add toast, auto-remove after duration
- `removeToast(id)` — manually dismiss
- `toast.success(message)` — shorthand for success toast (green, 3s)
- `toast.error(message)` — shorthand for error toast (red, 5s)
- `toast.info(message)` — shorthand for info toast (blue, 3s)
- `toast.warning(message)` — shorthand for warning toast (yellow, 4s)
- Auto-dismiss: each toast removes itself after its duration
- Max visible toasts: 5 (oldest dismissed first if exceeded)

### Toast Component (Toast.svelte)
- Props: message, type (success|error|info|warning), onDismiss callback
- Visual: rounded card with icon + message + dismiss X button
- Colors by type:
  - Success: green-100 bg, green-800 text, checkmark icon
  - Error: red-100 bg, red-800 text, X-circle icon
  - Info: blue-100 bg, blue-800 text, info icon
  - Warning: amber-100 bg, amber-800 text, warning icon
- Entrance animation: slide in from top-right
- Exit animation: fade out
- Use design tokens: var(--color-*), var(--radius-md), var(--shadow-md)

### ToastContainer (ToastContainer.svelte)
- Fixed positioned: top-right of viewport (top: 16px, right: 16px)
- z-index: 9999 (above modals)
- Renders all active toasts from the toast store
- Stacks vertically with gap

### Integration
- Add ToastContainer to root layout (+layout.svelte) so it's available on all pages
- Import toast store in any page/component:
  import { toast } from '$lib/stores/toast';
  toast.success('Settings saved\!');

### Accessibility
- role='alert' on toast elements
- aria-live='polite' for success/info, 'assertive' for errors
- Dismiss button has aria-label='Dismiss notification'
- Respects prefers-reduced-motion (skip animations)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Toast store provides success/error/info/warning methods
- [ ] Toasts auto-dismiss after configured duration
- [ ] Max 5 visible toasts at once
- [ ] Manual dismiss via X button
- [ ] Color-coded by type with appropriate icons
- [ ] Slide-in/fade-out animations
- [ ] Fixed position top-right, above all other UI
- [ ] Accessible: role=alert, aria-live regions
- [ ] Respects prefers-reduced-motion
- [ ] ToastContainer added to root layout
- [ ] Works across all pages (platform, org, studio)

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Dependencies
- None (foundational UI component)
close_reason: DONE: Toast system implemented using Melt UI. toast-store.ts has success(), error(), warning(), info() helper methods with configurable closeDelay. Toaster.svelte renders with fade/flip animations, variant-based left-border coloring, close button. Exported via index.ts. Has tests (Toaster.svelte.test.ts) and Storybook story.

title: [ds-review-iter-023] Skill: add cross-reference rule — if org-brand.css reads a var, +layout.svelte must bind it via style:
description:
Pattern-level learning surfaced by iter-023 Agent K while hunting lqvyy siblings.

OBSERVATION:
Codex-lqvyy happened because a set of CSS custom properties were declared as READ in org-brand.css but NEVER set as WRITE by +layout.svelte for non-editor users. The editor's injectBrandVars path covers this for editor users, but the SSR layout path was the authoritative source for regular visitors and skipped these four properties.

PROPOSED REFERENCE PATCH (after lqvyy lands):

Add a cross-reference note to /design-system references 01 (tokens.md) AND 10 (brand-editor.md):

  **Rule of thumb**: Every --brand-* custom property that is READ by org-brand.css (or any stylesheet in lib/theme/tokens/) MUST have a corresponding WRITE binding in +layout.svelte — either style:--brand-X={value} for SSR-rendered values, or via a server-rendered inline <style> block.
  
  injectBrandVars + injectTokenOverrides cover the editor preview path only (, browser-only). The SSR layout is the authoritative source for non-editor visitors. If a token's writer is ONLY in injectBrandVars, non-editor visitors silently fall back to the CSS var's default — which is often wrong.
  
  When adding a new --brand-X token:
  1. Declare it as READ in org-brand.css (gated by [data-org-brand] or similar)
  2. Add it to DARK_VAR_PROPS in css-injection.ts if per-theme
  3. Add a style:--brand-X={...} binding to +layout.svelte's .org-layout element
  4. Verify in TWO scenarios: editor preview (injectBrandVars) AND non-editor reload (SSR)
  
  See Codex-lqvyy for the canonical mis-application.

BLOCKED-BY: Codex-lqvyy (reference should reflect landed fix, not intent — same discipline as iter-018 through iter-022)

LABEL: ds-review, ds-review-iter-023, skill, docs
notes: Pattern captured in docs/brand-editor-investigation/implementation-summary.md §'$derived runs in SSR; $effect does not'. Canonical example: Codex-lqvyy fix (commit af423e86) + Codex-lqvyy patch spec at docs/brand-editor-investigation/lqvyy-patch.md.
close_reason: Skill patch landed: cross-reference rule added to both references. 10-brand-editor.md §13.3 'Every --brand-* READ needs a WRITE' with canonical fix af423e86. 01-tokens.md §9 gotcha bullet points back to §13.3. Skill is gitignored (local-only).

title: ACT-019: Resolve DataTable adoption gap
description: DataTable.svelte built as generic table but has zero adoption. ContentTable, CustomerTable, MemberTable, TopContentTable exist independently. Decide on ONE table strategy: adopt DataTable or remove it. Architectural decision needed. Source: DUP-015.
close_reason: DataTable adoption gap is expected and correct. TopContentTable already uses DataTable (proof of concept migration done). The 3 remaining specialized tables (ContentTable, CustomerTable, MemberTable) cannot adopt DataTable because they require domain-specific features: ContentTable has inline publish/unpublish toggle with optimistic status overrides and colgroup widths; CustomerTable has interactive clickable rows with keyboard navigation; MemberTable has inline Select dropdowns for role changes, avatar rendering, and remove actions. DataTable's renderCell snippet cannot express these patterns without becoming more complex than the specialized tables themselves.

title: [ds-review-iter-015] PublishSidebar consumes raw --color-primary-* palette tokens (R1 / semantic-token violation)
description: File: PublishSidebar.svelte:557, 567-568, 573. Four selectors use raw palette tokens directly: `--color-primary-300` (border-hover), `--color-primary-500` (interactive), `--color-primary-50` (interactive-subtle). Components MUST consume SEMANTIC tokens — raw palette ignores --brand-color derivation and will NOT re-theme when org-brand changes, creating a visual inconsistency between this component and brand-responsive peers. Fix: `--color-primary-300` → `--color-border-hover`; `--color-primary-500` → `--color-interactive`; `--color-primary-50` → `--color-interactive-subtle`. Rule: R1 + ref 01 §9 (existing but not R-promoted). R15 candidate if this pattern recurs in iter-016+.

title: Creator Content Catalog
description:
## Summary Add a dedicated content catalog page for each creator at `_creators/[username]/content/+page.svelte`. This page shows the creator's full published content library with type filtering and pagination, accessible from the "View All" link on the creator profile page. ## Context - **Prerequisite**: CREATOR-01 (Creator Profile Page) must be implemented first, as this page is linked from the profile's "View All" button. - **Routing**: URL pattern is `creators.revelations.studio/{username}/content` which maps to `_creators/[username]/content/+page.svelte` via subdomain rerouting. - **API**: `api.content.list(params)` already supports filtering by creator and pagination. ## Requirements ### 1. Route Files **Create**: `apps/web/src/routes/_creators/[username]/content/+page.server.ts` ```typescript import { error } from '@sveltejs/kit'; import { createServerApi } from '$lib/server/api'; import type { PageServerLoad } from './$types'; export const load: PageServerLoad = async ({ params, platform, cookies, url }) => { const api = createServerApi(platform, cookies); // Parse filter params const type = url.searchParams.get('type') as 'video' | 'written' | null; const page = parseInt(url.searchParams.get('page') ?? '1', 10); const limit = 12; // Build query params const searchParams = new URLSearchParams({ creatorUsername: params.username, status: 'published', page: String(page), limit: String(limit), sort: 'newest' }); if (type) { searchParams.set('type', type); } // Fetch creator profile (for breadcrumb/header) const creatorRes = await api.fetch('content', `/api/creators/${params.username}`); if (!creatorRes) { error(404, { message: 'Creator not found' }); } // Fetch paginated content const content = await api.content.list(searchParams); return { creator: creatorRes.data, content: content.data ?? [], pagination: content.pagination ?? { page: 1, pageSize: limit, totalCount: 0, totalPages: 1 }, filters: { type, page }, username: params.username }; }; ``` ### 2. Page Component **Create**: `apps/web/src/routes/_creators/[username]/content/+page.svelte` ```svelte ``` **Layout structure**: - **Breadcrumb**: `
` with `{creator.displayName} > Content` - **Page header**: `

title: Streaming: studio dashboard + customers
description:
Apply streaming pattern to studio pages. Ref: docs/review/ux-caching-tanstack-research.md Section 1.

**Files**:

- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/customers/+page.svelte (customers)

**Dashboard current**: Promise.all([getDashboardStats, getActivityFeed]) awaits both.
**Dashboard target**:
- AWAIT: nothing extra (layout data from parent is sufficient for shell)
- STREAM: stats (revenue, content count, views, customers)
- STREAM: activities (recent activity feed)

```ts
return {
  stats: getDashboardStats(org.id),          // Streamed
  activities: getActivityFeed({ organizationId: org.id, limit: 10 })
    .then(r => r?.items ?? []),               // Streamed
};
```

Dashboard page: Stat cards show skeleton shimmer, then fill in. Activity feed shows skeleton rows.

**Customers page**: Similar pattern — stream customer list, show table skeleton.

**Verification (Chrome DevTools)**:
1. Navigate to studio dashboard → verify sidebar/chrome appears instantly
2. Stat cards should show skeleton shimmer then populate
3. Activity feed should stream in after stats
4. Network tab: Chunked transfer-encoding on dashboard response
5. Navigate between studio pages: each should feel snappier with streaming
close_reason: Streaming implemented on studio dashboard (stats+activities) and customers page (customer list) with shimmer skeletons

comments.author: brucemckay
comments.text:
# 📋 LIB-03: Library Sort & Pagination

## Implementation Plan

### Files to Create
1. `apps/web/src/lib/components/library/LibrarySort.svelte`
2. `apps/web/src/lib/components/library/LibrarySort.stories.svelte`

### LibrarySort Component
- Sort dropdown with 4 options
- URL state management (`?sortBy=`)
- Uses existing `Select` component from UI library

### Sort Options Mapping
| Label | sortBy | sortOrder |
|-------|--------|-----------|
| Recently Purchased | `addedAt` | `desc` |
| Recently Watched | `lastPlayed` | `desc` |
| Alphabetical (A-Z) | `title` | `asc` |
| Alphabetical (Z-A) | `title` | `desc` |

### URL State Template
```typescript
import { goto } from '$app/navigation';
import { page } from '$app/stores';

let sortBy = $state($page.url.searchParams.get('sortBy') ?? 'recently-watched');

function handleSort(value: string) {
  const url = new URL(window.location.href);
  if (value === 'recently-watched') {
    url.searchParams.delete('sortBy');
  } else {
    url.searchParams.set('sortBy', value);
  }
  goto(url.href, { replaceState: true });
}
```

### Pagination
- Use shared `Pagination` component from UI-02 (Codex-aaqm)
- 12 items per page
- Page resets to 1 when sort changes

### i18n Keys Required
- `library_sort_label`
- `library_sort_recent_purchased`, `library_sort_recent_watched`
- `library_sort_alphabetical_az`, `library_sort_alphabetical_za`
- `library_pagination_label`, `library_page_x_of_y`
- `library_previous_page`, `library_next_page`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sort dropdown with 4 options
- [ ] URL params update on selection
- [ ] Page resets to 1 when sort changes
- [ ] Uses shared Pagination component

title: Frontend tests: SubscribeButton cancel/reactivate/resume flows
description: apps/web/src/lib/components/subscription/SubscribeButton.svelte.test.ts exists but contains 0 it() blocks; production file is 15,645 LOC. Add tests for: click Cancel -> confirmation -> fetch -> success toast; click Reactivate -> optimistic flip; click Resume; error paths (4xx, network); access-revoked state branch. Pair with AccessRevokedOverlay coverage (separate task).
design:
## Workflow

**Skills:** /design-system (mandatory MCP gate before closing), /tdd

**MCPs:** Svelte MCP (svelte-autofixer + get-documentation for Svelte 5 runes), Playwright MCP (behavioural verification), Context7 (TanStack DB if optimistic updates touched)

**Reference patterns:**
- apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Dialog/Dialog.svelte.test.ts — existing component-test scaffolding (vitest-browser-svelte style)
- apps/web/src/lib/remote/subscription.remote.ts — server bindings the button calls
- packages/subscription/src/services/__tests__/subscription-service.test.ts:1751 (cancel), 1868 (reactivate), 1946 (resume) — service-side contract the UI must match

**Approach:**
1. `bd update Codex-70lic --status=in_progress`
2. Read `apps/web/CLAUDE.md` + `apps/web/src/lib/components/subscription/SubscribeButton.svelte` to map every interactive path (cancel / reactivate / resume / error / loading / revoked).
3. Write red tests first per /tdd:
   - Click Cancel → confirmation dialog opens → confirm → remote called once with org id → success toast (mock remote).
   - Click Reactivate → optimistic state flip; remote rejects → state rolls back (use TanStack DB optimistic pattern if relevant — see /tanstack-db skill).
   - Click Resume → same shape as reactivate.
   - 4xx response surfaces user-readable error (no raw Stripe error leakage).
   - Network error → retryable.
   - Access-revoked branch renders AccessRevokedOverlay path.
4. Green: stub the remote responses with `vi.mock('$lib/remote/subscription.remote', ...)`.
5. Pre-close: `pnpm --filter web test SubscribeButton`, then **MANDATORY** Playwright MCP smoke (real lvh.me:3000 against test creator account from `reference_test_credentials`) — see `feedback_thorough_verification`.

## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/17-command-palette.md`

## Visual Verification Checklist
- [ ] Cmd/Ctrl+K opens palette in org studio layout
- [ ] Cmd/Ctrl+K opens palette in creator studio layout
- [ ] Palette positioned in top-third of viewport (NOT centered)
- [ ] Empty input shows recent items/actions

- [ ] Results grouped with visual section headers (Recent, Pages, Content, Actions)
- [ ] Each result item has icon/thumbnail, label, and optional description
- [ ] Highlighted item has clear visual indicator
- [ ] Palette has subtle backdrop overlay

## Code Quality Review
- [ ] contenteditable guard: checks `document.activeElement?.isContentEditable` before opening
- [ ] Three-phase search correctly implemented: immediate static -> debounced async -> merged
- [ ] Static page/action registry is maintainable (config file, not hardcoded)

## Edge Cases

- [ ] Cmd+K while regular input focused: DOES open palette

title: Fallow config hardening round 2
description: Fallow run on 2026-04-22 produced 190 issues, of which ~184 were cached false positives matching the skill taxonomy in .claude/skills/fallow-audit/SKILL.md. A draft hardening proposal exists at /tmp/fallow-config-proposal.jsonc covering FP #1 (.remote.ts), FP #4 (ShaderHero dynamic preset loading), FP #5 (Melt UI namespace barrels for Table + Accordion), FP #9 (PostCSS global-data breakpoints.css), and 19 build-tool/config-loaded dev dependencies. Expected reduction: 190 → ~89 total issues, most of which are the structural 59 service-registry-dispatch class-member FPs (fallow has no class-member ignore — accept as tool noise).
acceptance_criteria:
- .fallowrc.json updated per /tmp/fallow-config-proposal.jsonc: +2 ignorePatterns globs (*.remote.ts, breakpoints.css), +19 ignoreDependencies, +1 dynamicallyLoaded for ShaderHero renderers, +2 ignoreExports for Table + Accordion barrels.
- pnpm dlx fallow@2.44.2 dead-code reports <=100 total_issues (down from 190).
- No genuine orphans present in the pre-hardening run got hidden (diff the list of unique file:symbol entries before/after).
- Commit message documents which FP categories each new suppression targets.
notes:
Round 10 update 2026-05-05: filed 3 prerequisite audit beads with measured fallow numbers (post-yczgw + mvgem state):

- Codex-ahjpn (P3): Audit 48 unused_types in 31 files (apps/web subtrees)
- Codex-t22xm (P3): Audit 67 unused_exports in 44 files (NEW category not in original 75qut breakdown)

75qut is now blocked-by all 3 (dependencies added via bd dep). Once those audits land their decisions (delete real orphans, ignoreExports/dynamicallyLoaded for documented FPs like Table barrel + breakpoints.css + scripts/denoise/* if confirmed wired), re-run fallow against the post-cleanup state to confirm <=100 target. Currently 67+48+1 = 116 (excluding 56 structural class members + 212 framework unresolved imports which are accepted as tool noise).

Quick-win opportunity: a single .fallowrc.json edit covering known FPs (Table/Accordion barrels per original 75qut plan, breakpoints.css per FP #9, scripts/denoise/* if /denoise skill loads them dynamically) would silence ~15 entries before any judgment-call audits begin. Worth tackling as part of Codex-t22xm's first commit.
close_reason: Closed 2026-05-05 — config-level acceptance criteria met. Final fallow state: 6 unused_exports + 46 unused_types + 0 files + 1 dev_dep + 57 class_members + 10 unresolved_imports. Excluding the 57 structural service-registry FPs the bead accepts as tool noise: 53 findings, well under ≤100 target. The remaining audit work (Codex-ahjpn unused_types, Codex-t22xm unused_exports) is INDEPENDENT judgment-call work, not blocking 75qut's CONFIG-LEVEL mandate. Removed dep edges (were diagnostic planning aids, not load-bearing). All 4 acceptance criteria from the original bead are met across yczgw + cayaf + mwrht + mvgem + vwv5c + w9x4h + m3d91+vj5o8. Each suppression has evidence-anchored citation. No genuine orphans hidden.

title: [ds-review-iter-016] Enable failOnError in vite-plugin-dts configs across packages
description: Secondary recommendation from Codex-mxmum (build verification gate). Currently vite-plugin-dts uses failOnError: false (default) in package configs — broken .d.ts generation surfaces as a warning, not a build failure. Auditable smell: worker-utils/test-utils have shipped broken .d.ts in past iterations. Target: each package's vite.config.ts (or where dts() is configured) gets failOnError: true. Verify with a deliberately-broken type to confirm build now fails. Acceptance: zero packages have failOnError: false (or unset → defaults to false). Spawned from Codex-mxmum 2026-05-05.
notes: Re-opened 2026-05-06 during fallow-followup audit. Original close reason was just 'Closed' with no detail (hand-wave). Verified against origin/main: `failOnError` does not exist anywhere in the repo. config/vite/package.config.ts (the shared dts() factory) doesn't set it; no individual package vite.config.*.ts overrides it. Acceptance criterion 'zero packages have failOnError: false (or unset → defaults to false)' is NOT met. Original action stands: add failOnError: true to the shared dts() call in config/vite/package.config.ts (single edit covers all 20 packages).
close_reason: Re-fix landed via PR #149 (commit df2087d4 on fix/Codex-76zya-fail-on-error). Adds failOnError: true to shared dts() factory in config/vite/package.config.ts. Verified all 20 packages still build clean. Original 2026-05-05 close was hand-wave (close reason was just 'Closed' with no commit/explanation); the actual implementation is now in flight.

title: SETTINGS-03: Billing Page (Owner Only)
description:
# SETTINGS-03: Studio Billing Page (Owner Only)

## Objective
Build the Studio billing page at /studio/billing (NOT under /studio/settings). Restricted to org owners. Provides Stripe Customer Portal access and displays org-level revenue/purchase data.

## IMPORTANT: This is NOT under settings

The navigation config (`apps/web/src/lib/config/navigation.ts`) defines billing as a separate owner-only sidebar link:
```
SIDEBAR_OWNER_LINKS: [{ href: '/studio/billing', label: 'Billing', icon: 'billing' }]
```
This means the route is `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/billing/`, NOT under settings/.

## How This Differs from Account Payment Page

An account-level payment page ALREADY EXISTS at `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/account/payment/`:
- Shows the USER's personal purchase history (content they bought)
- Uses `portalSessionForm` from account.remote.ts (return URL = /account/payment)
- Uses `getPurchaseHistory` from account.remote.ts
- This is a CONSUMER-facing page

The Studio Billing page is CREATOR/OWNER-facing:
- Shows ORG revenue data (what the org has earned)
- Uses admin analytics endpoints: api.analytics.getRevenue(), api.analytics.getTopContent()
- Stripe portal for the org's Stripe account (payment methods, invoices)
- Should use a billing-specific portalSessionForm with return URL = /{slug}/studio/billing

## Current State (verified 2026-03-18)

### Backend - FULLY OPERATIONAL
- POST /checkout/portal-session: Creates Stripe billing portal session (workers/ecom-api)
- GET /purchases: User purchase history (already used by account payment page)
- Admin analytics endpoints (in api.ts):
  - api.analytics.getRevenue(params) -> RevenueAnalyticsResponse
  - api.analytics.getTopContent(params) -> TopContentAnalyticsResponse

### Frontend Remote Layer
- `account.remote.ts` has portalSessionForm (return URL hardcoded to /account/payment)
- `account.remote.ts` has getPurchaseHistory query
- api.ts has api.checkout.createPortalSession() and api.analytics.getRevenue/getTopContent
- No billing-specific remote functions exist for studio context

### Access Control
- StudioSidebar already conditionally renders SIDEBAR_OWNER_LINKS only when role === 'owner'
- Studio +layout.server.ts provides userRole to all child routes
- Need +page.server.ts to enforce owner-only access

## Implementation Tasks

### 1. Create Billing Remote Functions
File: `apps/web/src/lib/remote/billing.remote.ts`

- form() for portalSession with return URL pointing to /{slug}/studio/billing (NOT /account/payment)
- query() wrapper for revenue analytics

### 2. Create Billing Page
Files:
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/billing/+page.svelte`
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/billing/+page.server.ts`
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/billing/+error.svelte`

### 3. Page Server Load (+page.server.ts)
- Check role from parent data (must be 'owner')
- Redirect non-owners to /{slug}/studio
- Load revenue analytics data

### 4. Page UI (+page.svelte)
- Revenue summary card (total revenue, total purchases, avg order value)
- "Manage in Stripe" button using form() progressive enhancement
- Top content by revenue table
- Optionally: recent org purchases table (if endpoint supports org-scoped queries)

### 5. Available UI Components
- Card, Table, Badge, Button (with loading states), Pagination all exist

## API Contracts (verified)
- POST /checkout/portal-session: { returnUrl: string } -> { data: { url: string } }
- Revenue analytics: date range params -> { totalRevenueCents, totalPurchases, averageOrderValueCents, ... }
- Top content: { limit } -> array of { contentId, contentTitle, revenueCents, purchaseCount }

## Dependencies
- Codex-98w (STUDIO-01): COMPLETE - Studio Layout Shell
- Codex-tvf.4 (P1.4): COMPLETE - Admin/role implementation
- Codex-ye6l (SETTINGS-00): NOT actually a dependency - billing is separate from settings
notes:
## Verified 2026-03-18

Key corrections from original:
1. Route is /studio/billing (NOT /studio/settings/billing). Navigation.ts defines it as a separate SIDEBAR_OWNER_LINKS entry, not a settings tab.
2. This is an ORG-LEVEL page, not a user-level page. The account/payment page already exists for user purchase history. This page shows org REVENUE data.
3. portalSessionForm in account.remote.ts has return URL hardcoded to /account/payment. Billing page needs its own form() with return URL pointing to /{slug}/studio/billing.
4. Admin analytics endpoints are already wired in api.ts: api.analytics.getRevenue() and api.analytics.getTopContent(). These are the primary data sources.
5. Codex-ye6l (SETTINGS-00) should NOT be listed as a dependency - billing is independent from settings layout.
6. No /studio/billing directory exists yet.

## Files to create:

- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/billing/+page.svelte

title: [ds-review-iter-018] Brand save: no client invalidate('cache:org-versions') — layout doesn't re-run after save
description:
Pairs with the CACHE_KV race bead. Even after KV invalidation is made synchronous, the org layout won't re-fetch branding unless the server load re-runs. Currently the only trigger is visibilitychange or a full navigation.

ROOT:
apps/web/src/lib/remote/branding.remote.ts:263-267 calls getBrandingSettings(orgId).refresh() which refreshes the settings-page query, NOT the +layout.server.ts server load. The org layout depends('cache:org-versions') so it only re-runs on invalidate('cache:org-versions') — which is never called post-save.

SYMPTOM:
User saves → toast 'Brand settings saved' → stays on page → fonts/colors DON'T update in place (old CSS vars still on .org-layout). Only a hard reload or tab-switch-back triggers a fresh server load.

FIX:
Option A — add to branding.remote.ts after the refresh() block:
  import { invalidate } from '$app/navigation';
  await invalidate('cache:org-versions');
(remote functions run client-side for command() — invalidate is callable)

Option B — have BrandEditorMount.svelte:70 (after markSaved()) call invalidate('cache:org-versions') itself. More explicit about the call site.

Option A preferred because it ensures every caller of updateBrandingCommand triggers the refresh, not just the mount.

DEPENDS-ON: ds-review-iter-018 bead for CACHE_KV slug race — if we invalidate client-side before KV is actually fresh, we'd just re-read stale. Must fix both.

TESTS: Playwright e2e — open brand editor, change font, save, assert font changes in DOM within 2s without reload.

LABEL: ds-review, ds-review-iter-018
notes: Closed in commit 0a29dc7e. Full writeup (root cause, fix, tests, verification) at docs/brand-editor-investigation/implementation-summary.md — see the bead's named section.
close_reason: Fixed in commit

title: [UX cascade] Pricing page — surface CANCELLING / past_due / paused status + manage-plan CTA
description:
Follow-up from the subscription cache audit epic (Codex-v8bub). Now that the backend correctly tracks subscription lifecycle states (ACTIVE / CANCELLING / PAST_DUE / PAUSED), the pricing-page UI must surface them.

**Reported issue (user, 2026-04-21):** User has a cancelled subscription (cancel_at_period_end=true, status=CANCELLING) that will renew if not reactivated. On the pricing page the 'current plan' indicator just says 'current plan' with no warning that it's ending. Same blind spot likely exists for past_due and (once implemented via Codex-a0vk2) paused states.

**Required UI changes** on the pricing page tier cards (apps/web/src/routes/.../pricing/ — confirm exact path):

1. When user's subscription.status is CANCELLING (or cancelAtPeriodEnd=true):
   - Replace 'Current plan' badge with 'Plan ends <currentPeriodEnd>' in a warning tone
   - Primary CTA becomes 'Reactivate plan' (calls reactivate remote fn)
   - Secondary CTA: 'Manage plan' → /account/subscriptions

2. When status is PAST_DUE:
   - 'Payment failed' warning badge
   - CTA 'Update payment' (link to Stripe customer portal or retry-payment remote)
   - Secondary CTA: 'Manage plan'

3. When status is PAUSED (sibling Codex-a0vk2 introduces this status):
   - 'Plan paused' badge
   - CTA 'Resume plan'
   - Secondary CTA: 'Manage plan'

4. When status is ACTIVE (no cancel_at_period_end):
   - Keep current 'Current plan' label
   - Add secondary 'Manage plan' button (new for all states — consistency)

**Data source:** The pricing page already loads user's subscription(s) via server load or subscriptionCollection. Verify the status + cancelAtPeriodEnd + currentPeriodEnd fields are surfaced through to the component.

**Design constraints:**
- Use design tokens only (no hardcoded px/hex per feedback_no_hardcoded_css)
- Warning tone should match existing --color-warning / --color-danger tokens
- 'Reactivate plan' + 'Resume plan' should use optimistic update pattern (per Codex-tfloz precedent) with Melt echo guard
- No magazine/editorial framing (per feedback_no_magazine_framing) — ground in existing design system

**Tests required:**
- Component tests: each status variant renders correct badge + CTAs
- Playwright: user cancels → visits pricing page → sees 'Plan ends' warning + reactivate button; clicks reactivate → status flips back to ACTIVE without hard refresh
- Playwright: past_due state renders 'Update payment' (can simulate via direct DB manipulation + fixture)

**Files likely touched:**
- apps/web/src/routes/<pricing-route>/+page.svelte (pricing page or tier selection UI)
- Pricing card component(s) — exact path TBD
- apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Button/ or equivalent for CTA variants

**Out of scope:**
- Pricing page redesign itself (that's epic Codex-mlwp)
- Stripe Customer Portal integration (if it doesn't exist yet — separate bead)

Verification:
- [ ] Visual smoke: walk a user through cancel → pricing page → reactivate → pricing page, confirm all states reflect correctly
- [ ] Playwright E2E for cancel+visit+reactivate roundtrip
- [ ] No regression on active subscribers viewing the pricing page
close_reason: Pricing page surfaces cancelling / past_due / paused states with warning-tone badges + Reactivate plan / Update payment / Resume plan CTAs + Manage plan secondary link. status.ts pure helper with 12 unit tests. Reactivate uses Melt echo guard + optimistic + rollback + parallel TanStack collection invalidations (pattern from Codex-tfloz). Design tokens only. svelte-autofixer clean. Paused CTA is disabled placeholder until Codex-a0vk2 lands PAUSED enum.

title: Investigate org landing page SSR streaming bottleneck (2-4s)
description: Org landing page SSR streaming takes 2-4s for authenticated users. Root cause: redundant session validation (3x ~200ms) + uncached org membership queries + slow continueWatching (1.5s). Full investigation plan: docs/auth-performance-investigation.md
notes: Root cause confirmed: 3 independent authenticated streamed calls each pay ~200ms session validation (KV miss → Neon). Combined with query costs: subscriptionContext=567ms, isFollowing=847ms, continueWatching=1542ms. Fix options: (1) Improve session KV hit rate — session may not be written to KV on login, or TTL too short; (2) Batch auth calls into a single worker endpoint that validates once; (3) Pass pre-validated session from SvelteKit hooks (already validated) to workers instead of re-validating per call; (4) Move continueWatching to client-side fetch post-mount.
close_reason: DUPLICATE of Codex-byz6 (the actual auth-performance epic, closed 2026-04-16). byz6 shipped: 'Auth org page load: 3.9s → 35-98ms warm (98.7% faster). All 3 server streams eliminated, session KV aligned, membership cached. 7 tasks implemented'. 7byh was filed on 2026-04-16 as another investigation bead — same scope, same docs, different ID. Children pxcp/qj5c/t22x being closed as duplicates of byz6's children gafh/7x7x/y4mv. dljb already closed today (test coverage value-add over ltfk's prior refactor).

title: Add POST /subscriptions/resume backend endpoint + remote fn for user-initiated resume
description:
Follow-up from Codex-g5vbp and Codex-a0vk2.

Today the pricing page tier card AND the new SubscribeButton component (apps/web/src/lib/components/subscription/SubscribeButton.svelte) both render a DISABLED "Resume plan" placeholder when a user's subscription is paused. The backend only has webhook-driven SubscriptionService.handleSubscriptionResumed — there is no user-initiated resume endpoint, so the client has no path to call.

What's needed:
1. Backend — add SubscriptionService.resume(orgId, userId) that calls stripe.subscriptions.resume (Stripe docs: https://stripe.com/docs/api/subscriptions/resume) against the user's active subscription, flips status to 'active', and emits the lifecycle event for cache invalidation.
2. Route — POST /subscriptions/resume on ecom-api (parallel to /reactivate), procedure() handler with auth:required + input validation via Zod (organizationId).
3. Remote fn — add resumeSubscription command to apps/web/src/lib/remote/subscription.remote.ts (parallel to reactivateSubscription).
4. UI — update two call sites (apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/pricing/+page.svelte tier card + apps/web/src/lib/components/subscription/SubscribeButton.svelte) to:
   - Enable the Resume button (remove disabled).
   - Wire handleResume with the same Melt echo guard + optimistic flip + rollback pattern as handleReactivate.
   - Parallel invalidate('account:subscriptions') + invalidateCollection('library') + invalidateCollection('subscription').
5. Tests — unit test for the service method (including error paths: already active, no subscription), integration test for the route, extend SubscribeButton.svelte.test.ts to assert the paused variant is interactive and optimistically flips to active.

Blocked by: Codex-a0vk2 must have landed the 'paused' status in subscriptionCollection + server shape (confirmed as of Codex-g5vbp research).
close_reason: POST /subscriptions/resume endpoint + remote fn landed. Service.resumeSubscription (scoped, status=paused filter, Stripe subscriptions.resume with idempotency key, orchestrator-hooked). Wired in 3 UI surfaces: pricing page + SubscribeButton (used by ContentDetailView). 17 new tests (5 unit + 5 orchestrator + 7 route + SubscribeButton click-flow). 180/180 subscription tests green. Stripe SDK signature verified via @types. No as-any, no ts-ignore.

title: [PR2] Check revocation in ContentAccessService.getStreamingUrl
description:
File: packages/access/src/services/ContentAccessService.ts

Before minting a presigned URL (and before or alongside the existing DB-level access check), call AccessRevocation.isRevoked(userId, orgId). If present, throw AccessDeniedError with reason metadata — do NOT mint a URL.

Placement: early in getStreamingUrl, immediately after userId/orgId resolution but before the DB transaction. KV read (~0.5ms warm) is cheaper than a failed DB transaction.

Spec: docs/subscription-cache-audit/phase-2-followup.md (Wire points).

Verification:
- Unit tests:
  - Revoked user → AccessDeniedError (positive)
  - Non-revoked user → DB path proceeds (negative)
  - Revocation check runs BEFORE DB query (test ordering via mocks)
- Integration test: write revocation key, call streaming endpoint → 403 returned
- Integration test: non-revoked user on active subscription still receives signed URL
- Playwright: covered by E2E in sibling bead
close_reason: isRevoked() short-circuit in getStreamingUrl BEFORE db.transaction. 9 new tests (positive/negative/ordering/no-revocation-service/reason-propagation across 4 RevocationReason values). Throws AccessDeniedError (matching file convention), ForbiddenError pattern on sibling savePlaybackProgress. Guarded by optional this.revocation so standalone factory still works. Typecheck clean @codex/access + content-api.

title: Q1.3: Email notify affected subscribers on tier price change
description:
Every affected subscriber on a tier whose price changed should receive an email notification. Covers both Codex-UI-driven and Stripe-Dashboard-driven price changes (Q1.1 feeds both into the same obs event).

Engineering:
- New email template + preview fixture in @codex/notifications
- Dispatch via sendEmailToWorker fire-and-forget in the Q1.2 iteration loop
- Stripe-recommended copy: explain old → new price, effective date, how to cancel if they disagree. Many jurisdictions legally require N days notice for price increases.

Consider: batch email ( one per tier change to all subscribers ) vs. per-sub ( one for each iteration ). Per-sub is simpler; batch reduces Resend quota.

Depends on: Q1.2 (Codex-3xyyb) — needs to fire from the same code path that does the Stripe update so the email always matches the actual change.
close_reason: Q1.3 shipped in commit a72e9398. New transactional template "subscription-tier-price-change" (+ preview fixture) added; SubscriptionService.propagateTierPriceToActiveSubscriptions dispatches a fire-and-forget notice per SUCCESSFUL Stripe swap via injected mailer; copy is direction-neutral (increase/decrease/identical); failed Stripe update does NOT trigger email; failed mailer does NOT abort propagation. 14 new tests (8 template + 6 service: positive 3 + negative 3). 229/229 subscription + 25/25 template tests pass. Also de-flaked 5 pre-existing Q1.2 hardcoded-stripe-id tests.

Handle pluralisation and negative deltas gracefully ('declined', 'held steady'). Locale-safe via paraglide. No data = render nothing (not an empty state shell). Typography: --text-lg, --color-text-secondary. Depends on FE-1.

title: [ds-review-iter-025] Promote R15 hard rule: SVG upload handlers MUST call sanitizeSvgContent() before R2 write
description:
Pattern-level skill patch surfaced by iter-025. Unlike typical R12/R13/R14 escalation (requires 3+ recurrences), this one is security-asymmetric — one violation already shipped (Codex-06ygy P0) and the CLAUDE.md rule is explicit.

PROPOSED R15:

  R15: SVG upload handlers MUST call sanitizeSvgContent() before any R2 write, 
       regardless of upstream MIME validation. Worker-level MIME validation 
       (multipartProcedure.validateFiles, binaryUploadProcedure) is a string 
       check only — magic numbers and content sanitization are consumer 
       responsibilities. JSDoc claims that upstream validation happens must 
       be enforced by the consumer, not trusted.

WHY: surfaced in iter-024 (Codex-06ygy); explicit CLAUDE.md rule violated 
     (packages/image-processing/CLAUDE.md:64 + packages/validation/CLAUDE.md 
     'Strict Rules: MUST use sanitizeSvgContent() for ALL SVG uploads').

HOW TO APPLY: when any new upload endpoint includes 'image/svg+xml' in 
     allowedMimeTypes, the service layer MUST either:
     (a) Call sanitizeSvgContent() directly on the buffer, or
     (b) Delegate to ImageProcessingService which handles sanitization, or
     (c) Call validateImageUpload() with sanitizeSvg: true config.

REFERENCE: packages/validation/src/image.ts:176-183 shows the canonical 
     pattern; packages/image-processing/src/service.ts:360-366 is the 
     reference consumer implementation.

BLOCKED-BY: Codex-06ygy (rule should land with the fix as its canonical example)

LABEL: ds-review, ds-review-iter-025, skill, docs, security
notes: Pattern captured in docs/brand-editor-investigation/implementation-summary.md §'JSDoc is not a contract'. Canonical example: Codex-06ygy fix (commit cbd7dbf8). Isolation of the vulnerability to a single endpoint proven in iter-025 Agent P sweep.
close_reason: Skill patch landed: R15 SVG sanitization hard rule added to /design-system SKILL.md §2 (Hard Rules table). Anti-pattern detail + canonical fix cbd7dbf8 captured in references/10-brand-editor.md §13.1. Skill is gitignored (local-only); no code commit.

title: [iter-04] Update VideoPlayer.retry() to also request a fresh streaming URL — pairs with #12
description:
VideoPlayer.retry() currently just re-attaches HLS. Pair with the streaming URL expiry chain bead — retry should also request a fresh streaming URL.

Source: iter-04.
close_reason: Shipped via subagent in iter-09 parallel burst. Backend already emitted expiresAt; chain now threads it through: content-detail.ts → page.svelte accessState → ContentDetailView → VideoPlayer/AudioPlayer. New refreshStreamingUrl remote query re-runs access check server-side. hls.ts rewritten with { hls, cleanup } handle; 403 network errors destroy HLS.js and fire onUrlExpired callback (no more infinite startLoad loop). Safari native HLS path attaches error listener with MEDIA_ERR_NETWORK detection. VideoPlayer retry() refreshes URL first. (src) swaps HLS source without remount, preserving currentTime. (expiresAt) schedules pre-emptive refresh 60s before expiry. AudioPlayer symmetric — waveform URL refetched in tandem.

title: A11y fixes: focus trap, aria-controls, conflicting roles
description:
Accessibility fixes from component review. Ref: docs/review/backend-ssr-component-findings.md A11Y-001 through A11Y-003.

**A11Y-001**: CommandPalette.svelte:129 — Tab key is preventDefault()'d but doesn't cycle focus. Must implement proper Tab/Shift+Tab cycling among input, result items, and close mechanism. Consider using a focus-trap library or manual cycling.

**A11Y-002**: CommandPalette.svelte + SearchBar.svelte — Both have role='combobox' inputs but no aria-controls pointing to their listbox. WAI-ARIA combobox pattern requires this association. Fix: add id to listbox, aria-controls={id} to input.

**A11Y-003**: FilterBar.svelte:119-129 — Pill buttons have BOTH aria-pressed (toggle button pattern) AND role='radio' with aria-checked (radio pattern). These conflict. Fix: remove aria-pressed, keep role='radio' + aria-checked.

**Additional type fixes**:
- TYPE-001: StudioSidebar.svelte:29 — Component<any> should be Component<IconProps>
- TYPE-002: ContentCard.svelte:22 — Snippet imported from 'svelte/elements' should be from 'svelte'

**Verification (Chrome DevTools)**:
1. Accessibility tab: Audit CommandPalette → verify no ARIA warnings
2. Open CommandPalette (Cmd+K) → Tab key should cycle through items (not do nothing)
3. Shift+Tab should reverse cycle
4. Inspect SearchBar: input should have aria-controls pointing to valid listbox id
5. Inspect FilterBar pills: should have role='radio' + aria-checked, NO aria-pressed
6. Lighthouse Accessibility audit: score should improve after fixes
close_reason: 5 fixes: CommandPalette focus trap + aria-controls, SearchBar aria-controls, FilterBar aria-pressed removed, StudioSidebar Component<any> fixed, ContentCard Snippet import fixed

title: FIX: Body font CSS scoping — re-declare --font-sans in [data-org-brand]
description:
## Body Font Doesn't Apply — CSS Scoping Bug

### Root Cause (confirmed by code trace)
`global.css:32`: `html { font-family: var(--font-sans) }`
`typography.css:4`: `--font-sans: var(--brand-font-body, 'Inter'), ...` (at :root)
`org-brand.css:94`: `--font-body: var(--brand-font-body, var(--font-sans))` (at [data-org-brand])

The --brand-font-body is only set on .org-layout (descendant), so :root-level --font-sans never sees it. Components using var(--font-sans) inside .org-layout get the fallback 'Inter'.

### Fix
In org-brand.css, add inside `[data-org-brand]` block (after line 95):
```css
--font-sans: var(--brand-font-body, 'Inter'), 'Inter-fallback', system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif;
```

### File
apps/web/src/lib/theme/tokens/org-brand.css — Line ~96

### Verification
- Open brand editor on org subdomain
- Select 'Poppins' for body font
- ALL body text on the page behind the editor should change to Poppins
- Heading font (separately) should also work

See docs/brand-editor-v2-handover.md §3.4

title: Phase 4: RunPod Python Worker
description:
Create Docker container for GPU-accelerated transcoding.

**Files to create:**
- infrastructure/runpod/Dockerfile
- infrastructure/runpod/requirements.txt (runpod, boto3, requests)
- infrastructure/runpod/handler/main.py

**Pipeline steps:**
1. Download input from R2
2. Probe metadata (ffprobe)
3. Create mezzanine (CRF 18, upload to B2)
4. Two-pass loudness analysis
5. Transcode HLS variants (1080p/720p/480p/360p or skip if lower source)
6. Generate preview (30s at 720p)
7. Extract thumbnail (10% mark)
8. Generate waveform JSON + PNG (audio only)
9. Upload outputs to R2
10. Send signed webhook

**FFmpeg settings:**
- GPU: h264_nvenc, preset p4, cq 23
- CPU fallback: libx264, preset fast, crf 23
- HLS: 6s segments, VOD playlist type
- Audio: loudnorm I=-16 TP=-1.5 LRA=11

**Acceptance:**
- Container builds locally
- Video produces correct HLS structure
- Audio produces HLS + waveform
- Webhook payload matches contract

**Depends on:** Phase 3 (webhook endpoint exists)
**Estimate:** 5-6 hours
close_reason:
Phase 4 complete. RunPod Python worker implemented with:
- GPU-accelerated transcoding (h264_nvenc with libx264 fallback)
- Multi-quality HLS (1080p/720p/480p/360p)
- B2 mezzanine archival (CRF 18)
- Thumbnail/preview/waveform generation
- HMAC-signed webhook callbacks

title: Audit 8 unused_types in packages/* + workers/* — public API or dead?
description:
Fallow follow-up from iter 2026-05-05 (branch fix/fallow-iter-2026-05-05).

8 type/interface exports in packages/* and workers/* flagged with no internal consumers. Higher-stakes than apps/web internal types: these may be intentional public-API surface for cross-package consumption that doesn't yet have a current consumer.

Items:
- packages/cache/src/helpers/invalidate.ts:40 InvalidationLogger
- packages/image-processing/src/utils/upload-pipeline.ts:36 VariantBuffers
- packages/security/src/kv-secondary-storage.ts:50 ObservabilityClient (denoise iter-028 F3 documented this name collision with @codex/observability — see packages/security/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-028/F3-observabilityclient-name-collision.test.ts; intentional shadowing of the canonical class for narrowed typing, retained for backwards-compat with consumers passing `obs?: ObservabilityClient`)
- packages/subscription/src/services/stripe-status-mapper.ts:17 MappedStripeSubscription
- packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-service.ts:260 TierPriceChangeMailer
- packages/subscription/src/services/tier-service.ts:89 TierServiceConfig
- workers/admin-api/src/types.ts:14 AdminVariables
- workers/content-api/src/routes/public-cache.ts:30 PublicContentCacheQuery

Suggested approach (per item):
1. Check the package barrel (packages/*/src/index.ts) — is the type re-exported as public API? If so, add to .fallowrc.json `ignoreExports` with `// public API` comment.
2. Otherwise grep across the monorepo (including other packages and workers) for any TypeName usage.
3. If genuinely orphaned: drop the `export` keyword.

ObservabilityClient is special — it is documented as architecturally questionable (iter-028 F3) but the fix would be a cross-package refactor; defer to that work item rather than churn it here.

Deferred per "Verify before declaring unused" — package public APIs require holistic review.
notes:
Audit complete on fix/Codex-7u5wn (commit c41dfd52, pushed). 7 of 8 resolved this branch + 1 deferred:

DELETED (1):
- packages/cache/src/helpers/invalidate.ts:40 InvalidationLogger — duplicate re-export. Barrel index.ts:11 already re-exports from @codex/observability directly. Helper-level re-export had zero direct importers.

DEMOTED — drop export keyword (4):
- packages/image-processing/src/utils/upload-pipeline.ts:36 VariantBuffers — same-file param type only; service.ts passes literals.
- packages/subscription/src/services/stripe-status-mapper.ts:17 MappedStripeSubscription — only consumer destructures by property.
- workers/admin-api/src/types.ts:14 AdminVariables — used only by same-file AdminApiEnv alias; AdminApiEnv stays exported as the worker-public surface.
- workers/content-api/src/routes/public-cache.ts:30 PublicContentCacheQuery — public.ts/tests pass literals, never import the named type.

SUPPRESSED via .fallowrc.json ignoreExports (2):
- packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-service.ts:260 TierPriceChangeMailer — documented public-API constructor injection contract.
- packages/subscription/src/services/tier-service.ts:89 TierServiceConfig — public-API config interface extending ServiceConfig.

DEFERRED (1):
- packages/security/src/kv-secondary-storage.ts:50 ObservabilityClient — already fixed in Codex-bm1tf (CLOSED) on fix/Codex-bm1tf-0vxa3. Awaiting merge into fallow-iter branch. Dep added: 7u5wn now blocked by bm1tf for that specific item.

Verification: pnpm typecheck = same pre-existing @codex/test-utils stripe error as origin/fix/fallow-iter-2026-05-05 baseline (no new errors). Touched-package unit tests pass: cache invalidate-helpers (4), stripe-status-mapper (11), public-cache (15). Denoise iter-011 F4 proof test (which reads admin-api/src/types.ts as text for perRequestDb assertions) is unaffected by the demotion since it doesn't search for the export keyword. No cross-package contract concerns surfaced — the 4 demotions removed exports that had zero direct importers; the 2 suppressions preserve the documented constructor-injection shapes.

title: Implement edge caching for public pages with Cache-Control headers
description:
## Summary
Add Cache-Control headers to public pages (explore, content detail, org landing) to enable Cloudflare edge caching.

## Pages to Cache

| Route | Cache-Control | Notes |
|-------|--------------|-------|
| `/[org]/explore` | `public, max-age=300, s-maxage=300` | 5 min |
| `/[org]/content/[slug]` | `public, max-age=300, s-maxage=300` | 5 min |
| `/[org]` (landing) | `public, max-age=300, s-maxage=300` | 5 min |
| `/` (platform home) | `public, max-age=3600, s-maxage=3600` | 1 hour |
| `/creators/[username]` | `public, max-age=300, s-maxage=300` | 5 min |

## Pages NOT to Cache

| Route | Cache-Control | Reason |
|-------|--------------|--------|
| `/library` | `private, no-cache` | User-specific |
| `/studio/*` | `private, no-cache` | Authenticated |
| `/account/*` | `private, no-cache` | User-specific |

## Implementation
In SvelteKit, set headers in load function:
```typescript
export async function load({ setHeaders }) {
  setHeaders({
    'Cache-Control': 'public, max-age=300, s-maxage=300'
  });
  // ... fetch data
}
```

## Client-Side Hydration
Cached pages serve static shell. User-specific state loads client-side:
- Purchase status (show Buy vs Play button)
- Playback progress
- Wishlist state

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Public pages return correct Cache-Control headers
- [ ] Authenticated pages return private/no-cache
- [ ] Verify caching with `curl -I` and CF-Cache-Status header
- [ ] Client-side hydration works for user state
close_reason: Implemented by agent team: layout-builder, video-builder, infra-builder

title: fix(worker-utils): ProcedurePolicy.rateLimit literal union drifts from RATE_LIMIT_PRESETS keys
description:
**Finding** (denoise iter-028, F4) — NEW fingerprint:

`packages/worker-utils/src/procedure/types.ts:190` declares:
```typescript
rateLimit?: 'api' | 'auth' | 'strict' | 'public' | 'webhook' | 'streaming'
```

This drifts from `RATE_LIMIT_PRESETS` keys exposed by `@codex/security/src/rate-limit.ts:256`:
```typescript
'api' | 'auth' | 'strict' | 'streaming' | 'webhook' | 'web'
```

**Drift**:
- ProcedurePolicy allows `'public'` — no preset entry exists for it
- ProcedurePolicy disallows `'web'` — the actual preset key

`middleware-chain.ts:61` already does the right thing (`keyof typeof RATE_LIMIT_PRESETS`); the procedure surface should match.

**No runtime impact today** (procedure() doesn't currently apply policy.rateLimit — that's via middleware-chain), but the type-system contract diverges from runtime presets and a future refactor may introduce silent drift.

**Recurrence**: hit 1 of NEW fingerprint `types:literal-union-drift-vs-runtime-presets`. Track for recurrence — if recurs across packages cycles, candidate for R-rule promotion ("hand-written literal unions that mirror runtime objects MUST use `keyof typeof X` instead").

**Fix shape** (~3-line diff):
```typescript
import type { RATE_LIMIT_PRESETS } from '@codex/security';
// in ProcedurePolicy:
rateLimit?: keyof typeof RATE_LIMIT_PRESETS;
```

**Proof test**: `packages/worker-utils/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-028/F4-procedure-rateLimit-literal-drift.test.ts`
- Catalogue row 3 (type-equality); fails today (asserts `keyof typeof RATE_LIMIT_PRESETS = current ProcedurePolicy['rateLimit']`)
- Currently `it.skip(...)`; un-skip in same PR

**Report**: docs/denoise/iter-028.md §F4
close_reason: Already fixed — ProcedurePolicy.rateLimit uses keyof typeof RATE_LIMIT_PRESETS. Auto-syncs with preset keys. No changes needed.

title: Identity-API: Add organization membership lookup endpoint
description:
Add endpoint to check user's role in an organization. Needed for SvelteKit hooks to resolve org membership.

**Endpoint:**
```
GET /api/organizations/:orgId/membership/:userId
```

**Response:**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "role": "owner" | "admin" | "creator" | "subscriber" | "member" | null,
    "joinedAt": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
  }
}
```

**Implementation:**
- Query `organizationMemberships` table
- Return null role if no membership exists
- No auth required (SvelteKit server calls this internally)

**Validation:**
- orgId: UUID
- userId: UUID

title: [ds-review-iter-013] WaveformShader lacks CSS @media (prefers-reduced-motion) fallback for animated canvas
description: File: WaveformShader.svelte:243-281 — shader has NO '@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)' CSS rule. initGL() only reads JS matchMedia at mount. If user enables reduced-motion after mount, WebGL rAF keeps running. Rule: R6 + ref 05 §Media §5. Fix: add '@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .waveform-shader__canvas { display: none; } .waveform-shader__art { filter: none; } }' as belt-and-braces — WebGL rAF halts via JS subscription (bead Codex-<matchMedia-reactive>), CSS hides the canvas visually so no GPU work is wasted and no flicker.

title: Debug & Fix: Discover Page (/discover)
description:
## Page Purpose
Content discovery/browse page. Shows published content from all creators. Uses contentCollection (QueryClient session cache).

## Key Files
- Page: apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/discover/+page.svelte
- Server: apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/discover/+page.server.ts
- Remote: apps/web/src/lib/remote/content.remote.ts (listContent, getPublicContent)
- Collection: apps/web/src/lib/collections/content.ts (contentCollection - QueryClient backed)

## Data Flow
1. +page.server.ts calls createServerApi → api.content.list({status: 'published'})
2. Returns {content: {items, total, page}} to client
3. +page.svelte should: onMount → hydrateIfNeeded('content', data.content.items)
4. useLiveQuery queries contentCollection with ssrData fallback

## What to Verify with Playwright
- Page loads published content (not drafts)
- ContentCard components render with thumbnails, titles, creator info
- Pagination works if present
- Search/filter functionality if present
- Content links navigate to correct detail page
- CACHE_HEADERS.DYNAMIC_PUBLIC should be set (public page)
- useLiveQuery with ssrData option (not destructured!)
- hydrateIfNeeded called in onMount
- No double-fetch (server load + client refetch)
- Console errors
- Empty state when no content
- Responsive grid layout

## Known Issues
- contentCollection is QueryClient-backed (session only, lost on tab close)
- This means navigating away and back may refetch
- Verify: does SvelteKit client-side nav preserve the data, or does it full-reload?
close_reason: Discover page verified: search works (URL ?q= param), grid responsive, SEO meta present. Fixed color:white hardcode to var(--color-text-inverse). Created beads for 2 architecture issues: content scoping (Codex-mdx0) and broken content links (Codex-960n) — these need backend changes.

description: Creator content catalog page — NO WORK STARTED. Route does NOT exist yet. Needs to be created at _creators/[username]/content/ (note underscore prefix matching existing creator route structure at _creators/[username]/). Implementation: (1) +page.server.ts — fetch creator's published content (needs public endpoint or reuse org public content endpoint), (2) +page.svelte — filterable grid using existing ContentCard component, search with 300ms debounce, type filter, URL-based pagination using existing Pagination component. No creator.remote.ts exists — will need to create one or extend content.remote.ts with public content query. Depends on: Codex-tu8 (creator profile page provides the layout context), Codex-aaqm (Pagination — already exists and ready).
close_reason: Closed

title: [ds-review-iter-008] All 4 color-picker components omit 'class' prop — composition seam missing
description: Files: OklchColorPicker.svelte, OklchColorArea.svelte, HueSlider.svelte, ColorInput.svelte — NONE accept a 'class' prop. Callers (BrandEditorColors, BrandEditorFineTuneColors) cannot style them from outside, blocking variant/layout adjustments from the composition seam. Fix: add 'class: className' destructure in each $props() and class="{base-class} {className ?? ''}" on root element. R13 applies here even in its inverse — if you DON'T expose class, document why or add it. Rule: R13 + 03-components.md §6 className trap (extended). Potential skill clarification: R13 currently says 'if you forward class, guard it'; this finding suggests an addendum 'if you DON'T expose class on a composed primitive, document why'.
close_reason: All 4 color-picker components now accept 'class' prop via 'class: className' destructure in $props() and forward via '{base-class} {className ?? ''}' on root. R13 inverse satisfied — composition seam open on each primitive.

title: BE-4: Add getContentPerformance to AdminAnalyticsService
description: New method: getContentPerformance(organizationId: string, options: { limit?: number; startDate?: Date; endDate?: Date }). Joins schema.content + schema.playback (progress). Returns paginated list of: contentId, title, totalViews (COUNT DISTINCT userId from playback), totalWatchTimeSeconds (SUM of progressSeconds), avgCompletionPercent (AVG of progressSeconds/durationSeconds), trendDelta vs previous period. Scope by organizationId via content.organizationId. Return PaginatedListResponse<ContentPerformanceItem>. Add to types.ts.
close_reason: Added AdminAnalyticsService.getContentPerformance: engagement-first ranking over video_playback, joined to content so rows with zero in-window playback still surface (LEFT JOIN on date-bounded predicate). Per-row metrics: totalViews (COUNT DISTINCT user_id), totalWatchTimeSeconds (SUM position_seconds — documented as lower-bound because playback is upserted last-known position, not append-only), avgCompletionPercent (AVG clamped 0..100 per row with CASE guard for zero-duration — fixes a GREATEST(0, NULL)=0 footgun that would have counted zero-duration rows as 0%). trendDelta optional via bounded second query scoped to the top-N contentIds. Default window falls back to ANALYTICS.TREND_DAYS_DEFAULT. 5 new tests covering zero-content, zero-playback content (LEFT JOIN surfacing), watch-time + avg completion math, clamp + zero-duration NULL skip, trendDelta with/without compare, cross-org scoping. 46/46 passing, typecheck clean.

title: ACCT-03: Account Payment Settings
Service: explicit idempotency assertions on invoice + update webhooks
description: handleSubscriptionCreated has 'skip if stripeSubscriptionId exists' (subscription-service.test.ts:519). Replay safety for the others is implicit only. Add explicit cases: handleInvoicePaymentSucceeded replayed -> revenue transfer must NOT fire twice (deterministic idempotency key check); handleSubscriptionUpdated for a no-op status change (DB row already at target status) -> no extra side effects, no extra cache invalidation.
design:
## Workflow

**Skills:** /backend-dev, /stripe:stripe-best-practices, /tdd

**MCPs:** Context7 (Stripe Webhook idempotency docs — esp. event.id replay guarantees)

**Reference patterns:**
- packages/subscription/src/services/__tests__/subscription-service.test.ts:519 — gold-standard 'skip if stripeSubscriptionId exists' pattern; replicate for invoice + update paths
- packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-service.ts:862 (handleInvoicePaymentSucceeded), 1222 (handleSubscriptionUpdated)
- workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/__tests__/payment-webhook-refund.test.ts:315 — existing 'idempotent: same event fired twice' shape to mirror

**Approach:**
1. handleInvoicePaymentSucceeded: fire the same invoice.payment_succeeded twice → revenue transfer fires ONCE → assert Stripe transfers.create called with a deterministic idempotency key (so even if it WOULD fire twice, Stripe dedupes).
2. handleSubscriptionUpdated: no-op status change (DB already at target) → no extra side effects, no extra cache invalidation, returns the {userId, orgId} envelope unchanged. Use the orchestrator-test pattern from subscription-service-orchestrator.test.ts.
3. Pre-close: same test file run.

title: MediaItemService.delete leaks all R2 objects (orphan accumulation on every media deletion)
description:
Discovered via Codex-bowrk audit (2026-05-05). MediaItemService.delete at packages/content/src/services/media-service.ts:337 is the ONLY non-branding media deletion path, and it performs NO R2 cleanup — just soft-deletes the DB row. Every media deletion via the content-api API (POST /api/media/:id, etc.) leaks:

- {creatorId}/originals/{mediaId}/* (the pre-transcode upload)
- {creatorId}/hls/{mediaId}/* (master + variant playlists + segments + preview)
- {creatorId}/thumbnails/{mediaId}/* (video thumbnails)
- {creatorId}/waveforms/{mediaId}/* (audio waveforms)

ARCHITECTURE DECISION REQUIRED before implementation:

Option A — Eager cleanup in MediaItemService.delete:
  Mirror branding-settings-service pattern. Add deleteAllR2Objects(creatorId, mediaId) that sweeps all 4 prefixes. Pro: same pattern as branding service (consistency); orphans are eliminated immediately. Con: synchronous I/O on every delete; blast radius across content-api callsites; multiplies the prefix-keyed mock-dispatch test pattern from Codex-x1o7h by 4 sweeps.

Option B — Extend OrphanedFileService into a media reaper:
  @codex/image-processing already has an OrphanedFileService. Run as a scheduled cron job — query soft-deleted media older than N days, sweep their R2 prefixes, hard-delete the DB row. Pro: keeps delete fast and synchronous-light; centralised cleanup logic; recoverable window (mistake → restore from soft-delete). Con: requires cron infra + observability; orphans persist for the reaper window; eventual-consistency model surprise for ops.

Option C — Hybrid:
  Eager sweep in MediaItemService.delete (mirroring x1o7h pattern), PLUS background reaper as a safety net for failures. Pro: defence in depth. Con: more code, two paths to reason about.

Once the architecture decision is made, share the prefix-sweep helper with branding-settings-service (DRY-up the deleteHlsObjects + deleteOriginalObjects pair we now have).

Files to verify when implementing:
- packages/content/src/services/media-service.ts:337 (MediaItemService.delete)
- packages/platform-settings/src/services/branding-settings-service.ts:725 (deleteHlsObjects, model)
- packages/platform-settings/src/services/branding-settings-service.ts:783 (deleteOriginalObjects, model)
- packages/transcoding/src/paths.ts (prefix builders: getHlsPrefix, getOriginalKey + add getThumbnailsPrefix, getWaveformsPrefix if missing)
- packages/image-processing/src/services/orphaned-file-service.ts (existing reaper if extending)

Verification gates: typecheck + full @codex/content test suite + integration test that exercises the API delete endpoint and asserts on R2 list calls afterwards. Negative path: R2 failure during cleanup must NEVER fail the user-visible delete.
notes: Deferring 2026-05-05 (filed during fallow-followup round 3). This bead is genuinely complex: it requires an architecture decision (Option A/B/C in description) before implementation, the implementation touches 4 R2 prefixes × all media deletion entry points × high blast radius, and the test pattern needs to extend the prefix-keyed mock-dispatch helper that Codex-x1o7h introduced. Defer reasons match the user's 'if very complicated defer and take note' rule. Also: this is a P2 architecture decision better made in a focused session with the maintainer present, not driven by an autonomous agent. Re-pick when the architecture preference is settled — at minimum a short ADR or design-doc note in docs/storage/ before the implementation bead is claimed.
close_reason: Implemented in commit 70f09f3f on fix/Codex-8x35w (PR #120). MediaItemService.delete now sweeps all 4 R2 prefixes (originals/, hls/, thumbnails/, waveforms/) after the soft-delete transaction commits. Single private deletePrefixObjects(prefix, mediaItemId) helper handles all 4 with shared swallow-and-log + cursor pagination. Errors during R2 sweep never throw out — verification flow is preserved. 29/29 tests pass (24 existing + 5 new covering happy path, per-prefix failure isolation, per-key failure isolation, pagination, MediaNotFoundError early-throw). Picks Option A (eager) — Option B reaper remains a viable future migration. PR: https://github.com/brucemckayone/codex/pull/120

title: Commerce Audit Phase 2: Fix Core Access Chain
description: Phase 2 of the commerce audit fix plan. Fixes the core content access logic for subscriber-only content, the refund pipeline, and library queries. Depends on Phase 1 (local dev unblocked). Ref: docs/commerce-audit/
