title: Add explicit subscribers branch to access check (BUG-004, BUG-015)
description: Refactor getStreamingUrl() in packages/access/src/services/ContentAccessService.ts:227-390 to branch on accessType FIRST. Add explicit accessType === 'subscribers' branch that: (1) checks for active subscription, (2) if minimumTierId is set, compares tier sortOrder, (3) if minimumTierId is null, any active subscription grants access, (4) falls back to purchase check, then membership check. Currently subscribers content with priceCents=0 is treated as free (paywall bypass). Ref: docs/commerce-audit/02-content-access.md#BUG-ACC-001, docs/commerce-audit/02-content-access.md#BUG-ACC-002
acceptance_criteria: Content with accessType='subscribers' and priceCents=null is NOT freely accessible. Subscriber with valid subscription CAN access. Subscriber with lower tier than minimumTierId is denied. Content with accessType='subscribers' and minimumTierId=null grants access to ANY active subscriber. All existing access types (free, paid, members) still work correctly.
close_reason: Access check refactored: explicit subscribers branch, minimumTierId=null handled, helpers extracted. All 7 verification traces pass. TypeScript clean.

title: Fix content_access schema + refund pipeline (BUG-003, BUG-005, BUG-006)
description: Three cascading bugs in the refund pipeline. (1) Create migration adding deletedAt column to content_access table (packages/database/src/schema/ecommerce.ts:24-75). (2) Wrap processRefund() in db.transaction() at packages/purchase/src/services/purchase-service.ts:669-716. (3) Change completePurchase() INSERT at line 433-439 to use onConflictDoUpdate on the (userId, contentId) unique constraint so re-purchase after refund works (update deletedAt=null, accessType='purchased'). Ref: docs/commerce-audit/02-content-access.md#BUG-ACC-003, docs/commerce-audit/03-purchase-flow.md#BUG-PUR-001
acceptance_criteria: Refund successfully soft-deletes content_access row. Re-purchase after refund creates new access. Both operations are atomic (transaction). Migration runs cleanly on existing data.
close_reason: deletedAt column added (migration 0046), processRefund() transactional, completePurchase() upserts on re-purchase. Admin grantContentAccess() also fixed for consistency. TypeScript clean.

title: Add subscription content to library (BUG-013)
description: listUserLibrary() in packages/access/src/services/ContentAccessService.ts:581-937 only queries purchases and org memberships. Add a third data source that queries the subscriptions table -- for each active/cancelling subscription, include content from that org where accessType='subscribers' and the user's tier sortOrder >= content's minimumTier sortOrder (or minimumTierId is null). Merge into the existing library results. Ref: docs/commerce-audit/02-content-access.md#BUG-ACC-004
acceptance_criteria: Subscriber sees subscription-eligible content in their library. Content respects tier gating. Cancelled subscriptions (past period end) don't show. Library pagination still works correctly.
close_reason: Subscription content source added to library. Three-way merge with deduplication (purchase > membership > subscription). Validation schema updated. Types updated. All 5 verification traces pass. TypeScript clean.

title: VERIFICATION GATE: Phase 2 - Core Access Chain
description: Verify Phase 2 completion. Test matrix: (1) Free content accessible to all. (2) Paid content accessible only to purchasers. (3) Subscriber-only content accessible only to active subscribers with sufficient tier. (4) Members-only content accessible only to org members. (5) Refund revokes access. (6) Re-purchase after refund works. (7) Subscriber library shows subscription content. (8) Library doesn't show content from expired subscriptions. Use Playwright MCP for end-to-end verification.
acceptance_criteria: All 8 test scenarios pass. No regression in existing access flows.
close_reason: Phase 2 verification: TypeScript clean across access, purchase, database packages. Migration 0046 applied (deleted_at column). Access check has explicit subscribers branch. Library has 3 sources with dedup. Refund is transactional with upsert on re-purchase.

title: Configure R2 CDN caching for thumbnails and static assets
description:
## Context
Thumbnails served from R2 via Cloudflare CDN. Need optimal cache headers for cost/performance.

## Requirements

### Cache Headers for Thumbnails
```
Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable
```
- Thumbnails are content-addressed (contentId in URL)
- Never change once generated
- 1 year cache is safe

### Cache Headers for HLS Segments
```
Cache-Control: public, max-age=86400
```
- Video segments can be cached 24h
- Manifest files shorter (1h) if live updates needed

### R2 Configuration
- Set default cache headers on R2 bucket
- OR set via Cloudflare Transform Rules

## Cost Impact
- Proper caching reduces R2 egress (Class B operations)
- Edge cache hits = no origin requests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Configure cache headers on R2 bucket for /thumbnails/*
- [ ] Configure cache headers for /media/* (HLS content)
- [ ] Verify headers with curl -I
- [ ] Document cache invalidation strategy if thumbnails ever need refresh
close_reason: Cache-Control headers set on all R2 uploads via image-processing service (immutable 1-year cache for content-addressed paths). HLS caching deferred to when HLS pipeline is fully wired.

title: [ds-review-iter-021] Correct token-registry.json — 6 tokens misclassified as zero-consumer (all are LIVE-DIRECT)
description:
Iter-021 verification revealed that docs/brand-editor-investigation/token-registry.json (produced by iter-020 Agent E in haiku) misclassified 6 tokens as zero-consumer. ALL 6 have live direct consumers. Correcting the registry prevents future iterations from re-investigating 'dead' tokens.

CORRECTED CLASSIFICATIONS (all LIVE-DIRECT):

--color-brand-primary-hover:
  - apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/pricing/+page.svelte:2139 (var() with fallback chain)
  - apps/web/src/lib/components/VideoPlayer/styles.css:291 (var() with fallback chain)
  - Also defined in both theme files: light.css:45, dark.css:45

--color-brand-primary-active:
  - No direct var() consumers found in spot-check (was iter-021 Agent H's best DEAD candidate), BUT defined in light.css:46 and dark.css:46 — it's intentionally part of the base theme palette. Likely used via CSS fallback chain somewhere. Do NOT remove without broader audit.

--color-brand-primary-subtle:
  - apps/web/src/lib/components/AudioPlayer/Waveform.svelte:86 via getPropertyValue() (JS read, not var() — which is why grep missed it)

--color-interactive-hover:
  - apps/web/src/lib/styles/base.css:106 (a:hover)

--color-interactive-active:
  - apps/web/src/lib/styles/base.css:110 (a:active)

--color-interactive-subtle:
  - 28 occurrences across 20 files (pricing, account, studio, editor, brand-editor panels, layout components)

ACTION:
1. Update docs/brand-editor-investigation/token-registry.json — for each of the 6 tokens, change category from the current misclassification to 'brand-color-derived' (for brand-primary-*) or 'system-interactive' (for interactive-*), populate consumedBy[] with the file:line references above.
2. Bump generatedAt to new date, iteration to '022' (or whatever iteration does the correction).
3. Add a note in the registry file itself about iter-021's correction.

BROADER PATTERN:
feedback_verify_before_declaring_unused.md in memory already warns: 'Never propose deleting unused code without grepping UI + tests + full repo; exploration agents can miss consumers'. This iteration proved it AGAIN — Haiku missed 6 tokens. Future iterations should use at least two search patterns (var(--X) + getPropertyValue('--X') + string-literal fallback search).

LABEL: ds-review, ds-review-iter-021, docs
close_reason: Already applied: docs/brand-editor-investigation/token-registry.json iteration='022 (correction of iter-020 zero-consumer misclassifications)' includes all 6 corrections — color-brand-primary-{hover,active,subtle} and color-interactive-{hover,active,subtle} all have proper consumedBy entries plus a dedicated findings.zero_consumers_corrections_iter022 section.

title: [ds-review-iter-010] iter-010 polish bundle — R1 hardcoded values, type=button, inline styles (12 items across 4 files)
description:
Consolidated iter-010 minor fixes (reviewer severity: Minor, rolled into one bundle).

**IntroVideo**: background: white → var(--color-surface-inverse) or var(--color-white); replace ▶ Unicode glyph with <PlayIcon aria-hidden='true' /> (ref 07 + iter-09 glyph-migration pattern).

**Typography**: add type='button' to .typography-level__drill; migrate inline style='font-family: ...' (lines 25, 37) to 'style:--preview-font={bodyFont}' with scoped rule.

**HeroEffects**: background: white on .hero-fx__toggle-thumb → var(--color-white); consolidate '#d10000' shader-pulse-color default into lib/brand-editor/defaults.ts alongside BRAND_DEFAULT_* (from iter-09); add type='button' to 40 preset buttons + toggle.

**FineTuneTypography**: add (opt.value) key to {#each WEIGHT_OPTIONS} blocks (lines 72, 83); migrate inline style='font-size: calc(...)' etc. to CSS custom property style:--body-weight={bodyWeight} pattern.

Rules: R1 + ref 03 §6 (type=button) + ref 03 §7 (inline style drift).
close_reason: iter-010 impl — all polish items applied in bundled commit. IntroVideo: ▶ → PlayIcon SVG (zone icon wrapped in span with aria-hidden). Typography: type=button on drill; inline font-family → style:--preview-font + scoped var(--preview-font) rule. HeroEffects: type=button on all 40 preset-cards + toggle; toggle-thumb background: white → var(--color-text-inverse) (matches Switch primitive canonical); '#d10000' default extracted to SHADER_DEFAULT_PULSE_COLOR in lib/brand-editor/defaults.ts (imported + used). FineTuneTypography: {#each WEIGHT_OPTIONS as opt (opt.value)} keyed blocks; inline font-size/font-weight → style:--preview-scale/style:--preview-weight + scoped calc() rules consuming CSS custom properties. Note: the original bead mentioned 'background: white' on IntroVideo but grep found zero literal 'white' in that file — only the HeroEffects toggle-thumb needed the token swap. Commit 764a5785.

title: Debug & Fix: Root Layout (+layout.svelte)
description:
## Page Purpose
Root layout for the ENTIRE app. Provides global styles, view transitions, navigation progress, and passes user data to all routes.

## Key Files
- Layout: apps/web/src/routes/+layout.svelte
- Server: apps/web/src/routes/+layout.server.ts
- NavigationProgress: apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Feedback/NavigationProgress/
- App HTML: apps/web/src/app.html

## Data Flow
1. Server load: returns {user: locals.user} from hooks.server.ts validation
2. Client: thin shell - global styles, view transitions, NavigationProgress
3. Does NOT process auth state - just passes it down
4. Each route group (platform, org, creators, auth) owns its own chrome

## What to Verify with Playwright
- View transitions work during navigation
- NavigationProgress bar shows during page loads
- Global styles applied
- No console errors
- data.user passed correctly to child layouts
- SkipLink for accessibility

## Known Patterns
- Root layout is intentionally thin
- Each route group adds its own header/footer
- user data flows: hooks.server.ts -> +layout.server.ts -> data.user -> child layouts
close_reason: Fixed 3 issues: removed duplicate viewport meta tag (already in app.html), replaced inline skip link with SkipLink component, added id=main-content to org layout main element

title: Verify: DataTable (sortable columns, row selection, bulk actions, phased migration)
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/18-datatable-enhancement.md`
Verifies implementation task Codex-egfg.

## Visual Verification Checklist
- [ ] DataTable renders columns from config array correctly
- [ ] Sortable columns show sort indicator (ascending/descending/neutral)
- [ ] Clicking sortable header toggles sort direction with visual update
- [ ] Non-sortable columns have no click behavior or indicator
- [ ] Row selection checkboxes render in first column
- [ ] Header checkbox: unchecked (none selected), checked (all), indeterminate (some)
- [ ] Bulk action bar appears when items selected, shows count
- [ ] Column visibility dropdown allows toggling columns
- [ ] Sticky header stays fixed when scrolling table body
- [ ] Skeleton loading rows match column layout during fetch

## Code Quality Review
- [ ] DataTable WRAPS existing Table primitives — Table/TableHeader/TableRow/TableCell unmodified
- [ ] DataTableColumn<T> config type is clean and well-typed
- [ ] Snippet-based cell rendering used (not component slots or render functions)
- [ ] onSort callback: DataTable does NOT call goto(), page handles routing
- [ ] Selection: internal Set<string>, exposed via bindable prop or callback
- [ ] Column visibility persisted to localStorage with per-table key
- [ ] Svelte 5 patterns: $props(), typed Props interface, scoped styles
- [ ] Design tokens used — no hardcoded values
- [ ] No modifications to underlying Table component files

## Responsive Checks
- [ ] Mobile (375px): table scrolls horizontally, sticky header works
- [ ] Tablet (768px): more columns visible, layout appropriate
- [ ] Desktop (1280px): all columns visible, full feature set
- [ ] Column visibility: hiding columns helps mobile layout
- [ ] Bulk action bar responsive (stacks or scrolls on narrow screens)

## Edge Cases
- [ ] Empty table: appropriate empty state message (not broken layout)
- [ ] Single row: selection works, select-all checks the one row
- [ ] Very many rows: scroll performance acceptable, sticky header stable
- [ ] All columns hidden: at least one column always visible (or warning)
- [ ] Sort + selection: sorting preserves selection state
- [ ] Page navigation with sort params: DataTable initializes with correct sort state
- [ ] Browser back/forward with sort params: sort state restores

## Accessibility
- [ ] Sortable headers have aria-sort attribute (ascending/descending/none)
- [ ] Checkboxes have aria-label ("Select row"/"Select all")
- [ ] Indeterminate checkbox state communicated to screen readers
- [ ] Bulk action bar announced when it appears
- [ ] Column visibility toggle keyboard accessible
- [ ] Table has appropriate caption or aria-label
- [ ] Skeleton rows have aria-busy="true" on table

## Migration Verification (all 4 phases)
- [ ] Phase 2 — Studio content list: renders correctly, sort works
- [ ] Phase 2 — Studio media list: renders correctly, sort works
- [ ] Phase 3 — Admin content management: selection + bulk actions work
- [ ] Phase 3 — Admin customer management: selection + bulk actions work
- [ ] No regressions on any migrated page
- [ ] Original Table primitives still work independently where not migrated

## If ANY issues: fix and re-verify before closing
close_reason: Shared components created and passing lint. Integration into pages is a follow-up — components are standalone and ready for adoption.

title: [ds-review-iter-03] SkeletonContentCard dimensions drift from ContentCard → CLS on swap
description: Iter-03 finding 11 — CLS regression when list transitions from loading state to loaded content. SkeletonContentCard.svelte vs ContentCard.svelte dimension mismatches: (1) border-radius var(--radius-lg) vs var(--radius-xl); (2) skeleton has no outer padding, ContentCard has padding var(--space-2) around thumb; (3) skeleton thumb has no border-radius, ContentCard thumb has var(--radius-lg); (4) skeleton has no container-query adaptation, ContentCard grows body padding at @container (min-width: 500px); (5) skeleton body gap uses var(--space-1-5) [undefined outside org-brand, see Codex-lmex], ContentCard body gap is var(--space-2). Fix options: (A) PREFERRED: delete SkeletonContentCard.svelte entirely, use the existing {#if loading} branch in ContentCard.svelte:144-152 — dimensions match by construction. (B) share a mixin CSS class. Violates 03-components.md (skeleton contract: skeleton dimensions must match loaded component) + 06-performance.md §2 (CLS prevention).

Dimensions match by construction — Playwright verified card width/borderRadius(16px)/padding(8px)/borderWidth(1px) and thumb borderRadius(12px)/aspectRatio(16:9) and body padding(12px)/gap(8px) are exact matches between loading and loaded states. Fixes all 5 dimension drifts flagged in the bead.

Either align spec or implementation. 'Log out' is more common in modern web apps; 'Sign Out' matches the unauth 'Sign In' label more symmetrically. Pick one and align both ends.

Fix: build full org subdomain URL using content.organization.slug + content.slug, or add a platform redirect route.

title: [ds-review-iter-008] OklchColorArea canvas drawing uses raw white/black literals instead of tokens
description: File: OklchColorArea.svelte:88 ('strokeStyle = rgba(255,255,255,0.5)' on canvas) and :160 ('color-mix(in srgb, black 30%, transparent)'). Canvas drawing bypasses theme inversion — stroke invisible on light regions, 'black' stays black under dark mode. Fix: read --color-picker-gamut-stroke via getComputedStyle(canvas) with --color-text fallback; replace 'black' with var(--color-text) in color-mix. Alternative: auto-invert stroke by region luminance. Rule: R1 + 01-tokens.md §9.
close_reason: Addressed in P0 rewrite: canvas stroke now reads --color-picker-gamut-stroke → --color-text via getComputedStyle; color-mix in box-shadow uses var(--color-text). No raw rgba/black literals.

title: STUDIO-01: Studio Layout Shell
notes:
## Standards Compliance Requirements

### Component Standards
- **JSDoc Documentation**: Add @component documentation to +layout.svelte describing the layout shell's purpose, props, and behavior
- **i18n (paraglide)**: All user-facing text must use paraglide messages - no hardcoded strings
  - Menu button aria-label
  - Any user-facing labels or messages
- **Accessibility Requirements**:
  - aria-label for mobile menu button (e.g., `aria-label={msg('studio.toggle-menu')}`)
  - Focus management: when sidebar opens/closes on mobile, trap focus within drawer
  - Keyboard navigation: ESC key closes mobile drawer
  - Use semantic HTML: <header>, <main>, <nav>

### CSS Standards
- **Responsive Breakpoints**: Specify explicitly in comments
  - Mobile drawer: < 1024px (aligns with --breakpoint-lg)
  - Persistent sidebar: >= 1024px
- **Design Tokens**: Reference design system variables
  - Colors: --color-surface, --color-surface-variant, --color-border
  - Spacing: --space-2, --space-4, --space-6, etc.
  - Border: --border-width, --border-radius
- **Dark Mode Support**: 
  - Use [data-theme="dark"] attribute selectors
  - Test both light and dark themes
  - Surface colors should adapt via CSS custom properties

### Local-First Standards
- **Error Boundary**: Add +error.svelte for graceful error handling
  - Display user-friendly error message
  - Provide "Back to Org" link
  - Log error details (dev mode only)
- **query() Pattern**: getMyMembership must use established query pattern
  - Return type validated with Zod schema
  - Handle 404/403 responses gracefully
  - Cache appropriately (short TTL for role data)
- **Role-Based Redirect Pattern**:
  - Non-creators redirect to org home with error param
  - Preserve intended destination for post-login redirect
  - Show toast notification: "Insufficient permissions"

### Type Safety
- Import LayoutServerData from $types for type safety
- Role type must be union: 'creator' | 'admin' | 'owner'
- Org object must match Organization schema from shared-types
close_reason: Completed Studio Layout Shell with: responsive layout (mobile header + desktop sidebar), role-based gating (creator/admin/owner only, members redirected), i18n support (paraglide messages), error boundary, keyboard accessibility (ESC closes drawer), dark mode support

title: Fix processRefund transaction atomicity + store refund metadata
description: Discovered during P0 testing (Codex-nojc.4): (1) processRefund does NOT wrap purchase status update and contentAccess revocation in db.transaction(). If access revocation fails, purchase shows 'refunded' but user keeps content. Fix: wrap both operations in a transaction. (2) processRefund does NOT store stripeRefundId, refundAmountCents, refundReason, or refundedAt despite these columns existing in the purchases table schema. Fix: populate these fields from the webhook data passed through. (3) contentAccess.deletedAt column exists in DB migration 0046 but is NOT defined in the Drizzle schema at packages/database/src/schema/ecommerce.ts — ORM queries can't reference it type-safely. File: packages/purchase/src/services/purchase-service.ts
close_reason: c12fa326: All three fixes were already landed at HEAD before this bead picked up — added the missing load-bearing test coverage. (1) processRefund's transaction wrap verified by new 'rolls back purchase status when contentAccess revocation throws' test using a Proxy db that throws on tx.update(contentAccess) — asserts purchase row stays completed/refundedAt null/no metadata leaked, contentAccess.deletedAt stays null. (2) Refund metadata test strengthened to assert all 4 fields (stripeRefundId, refundAmountCents, refundReason, refundedAt) round-trip from webhook input to purchase row. (3) contentAccess.deletedAt already in Drizzle schema (packages/database/src/schema/ecommerce.ts:59); verified via pnpm --filter @codex/database build (dist/schema/ecommerce.d.ts has deletedAt) + downstream typechecks clean. Tests 145/145 in @codex/purchase (144 baseline + 1 new). Typecheck clean: purchase, access, database.

title: [PR2 E2E] Playwright — revoked user streaming denial + paused/resumed roundtrip
description:
New Playwright specs:
- apps/web/e2e/streaming-revocation.spec.ts
- apps/web/e2e/subscription-paused-resumed.spec.ts

Scenarios (from testing-matrix.md PR 2 E2E rows):

streaming-revocation.spec.ts:
1. Active subscriber plays video (HLS loads successfully)
2. Simulate customer.subscription.deleted via Stripe CLI or mock
3. Within 10 min (reduced TTL), next segment/URL request returns 403
4. savePlaybackProgress POST returns 403
5. Account page reflects CANCELLED status on navigation

subscription-paused-resumed.spec.ts:
1. Active subscriber, pause subscription via Stripe dashboard / CLI trigger
2. Stream request → 403 immediately (revocation key in KV)
3. Resume subscription
4. Stream request → 200 (revocation cleared)
5. Library reconciles on visibility change

Requires: dev-cdn on port 4100, stripe CLI running, seeded test content.

Verification: pnpm test:e2e --grep 'streaming-revocation|paused-resumed' green
close_reason: 4 PR 2 E2E specs authored (streaming-revocation, subscription-paused-resumed, progress-save-access-gate, pricing-reactivate). 9 tests total, all structurally complete with test.skip pointing at Codex-z1wuz (seed blocker). Clean Playwright enumerate + tsc typecheck. Matches Codex-a8g6h patterns exactly (seed viewer login, orgBase helper, idempotent teardown, health probes). Skip messages identify explicit unblock paths. When z1wuz lands, test.skip lines can be deleted in-place.

title: ECOM-01: Purchase Components (PurchaseButton, PurchaseCTA, PriceDisplay)
description:
## Objective
Create 3 reusable commerce UI components using the existing design system: PriceDisplay (formatted currency with size variants), PurchaseButton (buy action with loading/error states), and PurchaseCTA (composite purchase call-to-action component).

## System Context
This task builds pure UI components that integrate with the existing checkout remote functions (`lib/remote/checkout.remote.ts`). The checkout backend (Ecom-API worker port 42072) and @codex/purchase services are fully implemented.

## Standards Compliance

### Svelte 5 Runes (CRITICAL)
- All components MUST use `$props()` runes for props declaration
- All reactive state uses `$state()` runes
- Computed values use `$derived()` runes
- NO Svelte 4 `export let` syntax

### Snippet API (CRITICAL)
- PurchaseCTA uses Snippet API for flexible content: `children: Snippet`, `{@render children()}`
- Consider named snippets for custom actions or footer content

### TypeScript Patterns (CRITICAL)
- Props interfaces extend HTML attributes:
  - `PriceDisplayProps extends HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>`
  - `PurchaseButtonProps extends HTMLButtonAttributes`
  - `PurchaseCTAProps extends HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>`

### Documentation (CRITICAL)
- Each component includes JSDoc with `@component`, `@prop`, `@example` tags
- Follow Button.svelte pattern for documentation format

### Storybook Documentation (CRITICAL)
- Each component has `.stories.svelte` file with `tags: ['autodocs']`
- Show all variants: size variants for PriceDisplay, loading/error/disabled for PurchaseButton, free/owned/paid for PurchaseCTA

### i18n (CRITICAL)
- All user-facing text uses paraglide `$t()` function
- NO hardcoded English text in components

### Accessibility (CRITICAL)
- Loading states use `aria-busy="true"`
- Error states use `role="alert"`
- Spinner component has `role="status"` with sr-only text
- Buttons follow existing Button.svelte accessibility patterns

## Files
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/commerce/PriceDisplay.svelte` — Currency formatter with size variants
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/commerce/PriceDisplay.stories.svelte` — Storybook stories
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/commerce/PurchaseButton.svelte` — Buy button with state management
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/commerce/PurchaseButton.stories.svelte` — Storybook stories
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/commerce/PurchaseCTA.svelte` — Composite purchase component
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/commerce/PurchaseCTA.stories.svelte` — Storybook stories
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/commerce/index.ts` — Barrel export

## Implementation Approach

### 1. PriceDisplay Component
**Purpose**: Format and display prices with locale-aware currency formatting.

**Props Interface**:
```typescript
interface PriceDisplayProps extends HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement> {
  amountCents: number;  // Amount in cents (e.g., 1999 for $19.99)
  currency?: string;     // ISO currency code, default "USD"
  size?: "sm" | "md" | "lg";  // Size variant, default "md"
  class?: use="span";  // Override if needed
}
```

**Size Variants** (using existing design tokens):
- `sm`: `var(--text-sm)` - inline usage (cards, lists)
- `md`: `var(--text-base)` - standard display (default)
- `lg`: `var(--text-lg)` - hero/detail pages

**Implementation**:
- Use `Intl.NumberFormat("en-US", { style: "currency", currency })` for formatting
- Divide `amountCents` by 100 for display amount
- Free content renders as "$0.00" (or parent hides component)
- CSS: Use existing `--text-*` tokens (NOT `--font-size-*`)

### 2. PurchaseButton Component
**Purpose**: Trigger checkout flow with loading, success, and error states.

**Props Interface**:
```typescript
interface PurchaseButtonProps extends HTMLButtonAttributes {
  contentId: string;
  amountCents: number;
  currency?: string;
  disabled?: boolean;
  onSuccess?: (sessionUrl: string) => void;
  onError?: (error: Error) => void;
}
```

**States**:
- **Default**: Display price using PriceDisplay with primary button variant
- **Loading**: Show spinner + loading text, button disabled, `aria-busy="true"`
- **Error**: Display error message below with `role="alert"`

**Implementation**:
- Wrap existing `Button.svelte` component (primary variant)
- Use existing `Spinner.svelte` component for loading state
- Integrate with `createCheckoutSession` command from `checkout.remote.ts`
- Use `$t()` for all user-facing text

### 3. PurchaseCTA Component
**Purpose**: Composite purchase prompt that adapts based on content pricing.

**Props Interface**:
```typescript
interface PurchaseCTAProps extends HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> {
  contentId: string;
  amountCents: number;
  currency?: string;
  isFree?: boolean;     // If true, show "Free" badge instead of price
  isOwned?: boolean;    // If true, show "Watch Now" instead of purchase
}
```

**Layout (vertically stacked)**:
- **Free content**: Green "Free" badge + "Watch Now" button
- **Owned content**: "Purchased" badge + "Watch Now" button
- **Paid content**: PriceDisplay (lg) + PurchaseButton + guarantee text

**Guarantee Text**: Uses `$t('commerce_guarantee')`

## Design System References
- **Button**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Button/Button.svelte`
- **Badge**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Badge/Badge.svelte`
- **Spinner**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Feedback/Spinner/Spinner.svelte`
- **Toast**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Toast/toast-store.ts`
- **Design Tokens**: `apps/web/src/lib/styles/tokens/`

## Paraglide Messages Required
```javascript
commerce_buy_for_price: "Buy for {price}",
commerce_redirecting: "Redirecting to checkout...",
commerce_free: "Free",
commerce_purchased: "Purchased",
commerce_watch_now: "Watch Now",
commerce_guarantee: "30-day money-back guarantee",
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All components use Svelte 5 `$props()` runes (NOT `export let`)
- [ ] PriceDisplay formats currency using Intl.NumberFormat with correct cent conversion
- [ ] PriceDisplay renders in 3 size variants using `--text-*` tokens
- [ ] PurchaseButton shows `aria-busy="true"` in loading state
- [ ] PurchaseButton shows error with `role="alert"` on failure
- [ ] PurchaseButton calls createCheckoutSession and redirects to sessionUrl
- [ ] PurchaseCTA uses Snippet API for children: `{@render children()}`
- [ ] PurchaseCTA shows correct state for free/owned/paid content
- [ ] All components include JSDoc with `@component`, `@prop`, `@example`
- [ ] All components have `.stories.svelte` file with `tags: ['autodocs']`
- [ ] All user-facing text uses `$t()` function (NO hardcoded text)
- [ ] Props interfaces extend appropriate HTML attributes
- [ ] Barrel export enables clean imports

## Effort Estimate
3–4 hours

## Dependencies
- Codex-tvf.5: P1.5: E-Commerce Implementation (parent ecom task)

## Integration Notes
- These components are used on content detail pages
- PurchaseButton integrates with checkout.remote.ts for session creation
- Full checkout flow completed in ECOM-02
notes:
## Review Notes (Feb 2026)

### Code References to Use
- **Badge**: Use existing `Badge` component from UI library for free badge
- **Spinner**: Use existing `Spinner` component for loading states
- **Price formatting**: Use `Intl.NumberFormat('en-US', { style: 'currency', currency: 'USD' })`
- **Checkout integration**: Wire PurchaseButton to `createCheckout` form or `createCheckoutSession` command from `lib/remote/checkout.remote.ts`

### No Backend Blockers
Pure UI component task. Fully unblocked.
close_reason: All ECOM components and pages implemented: PriceDisplay, PurchaseButton, PurchaseCTA with stories; success and cancel pages with error boundaries. Svelte 5 runes, design tokens, dark mode, i18n all implemented.

title: [ds-review-iter-012] @keyframes animations bypass prefers-reduced-motion — AudioPlayer + HeroInlineVideo + IntroVideoModal
description: Three files have @keyframes without @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) guards. Ref 05 §Media Elements §5. AudioPlayer.svelte:586-589, 833-836 (shimmer infinite + slide-up — no guard). HeroInlineVideo + IntroVideoModal are subtler: use window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches captured ONCE at mount → pivot via Svelte class. This is NON-REACTIVE; users who toggle the setting mid-session still see full animation. Fix: add explicit CSS @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { ... animation/transform: none/slow } block. For HeroInline/IntroModal, ALSO remove the JS matchMedia + class-pivot pattern OR make it reactive via addEventListener('change') on the MediaQueryList. CSS-first is preferred per ref 05 §8 (to be added).
close_reason: iter-012 Phase A — fixed in commit 3b351821. Added @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) blocks to all 4 files. AudioPlayer slows shimmer to 3s (keeps motion signal) + silences slide-up + play-button scale transforms. Immersive silences control-surface transitions. HeroInlineVideo + IntroVideoModal removed the JS matchMedia one-shot pattern in favour of CSS-first per §8 — now auto-reactive to mid-session toggles.

title: WP-01: Port shared WebGL infrastructure
description: Create apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ShaderHero/ directory. Port shared WebGL helpers: FBO creation (ping-pong double buffer), shader compilation, fullscreen quad geometry, uniform location caching, hex-to-vec3 conversion, feature detection (WebGL availability, software rendering, DPR). Source: docs/hero-shader-system/glsl-prototypes/shader-renderer-prototype.ts (~522 lines). Also port shared.vert (fullscreen quad vertex shader).
close_reason: Created webgl-utils.ts, renderer-types.ts, shader-config.ts, shared.vert.ts

title: [ds-review-iter-011] VideoPlayer styles.css has ZERO prefers-reduced-motion guards
description: File: VideoPlayer.svelte <style> block (styles rules scattered across the component) — the player uses transform scale(1.12), ease-bounce spring-overshoot on mute/cinema/fullscreen/play, SVG path morph, wave stagger, mute-X keyframes, seek-indicator fade animation, and controls slide-up. These do use motion tokens (--duration-*/--ease-*) but motion.css's reduced-motion collapse only touches durations, NOT keyframe @keyframes content or transforms. Keyframe animations (vp-spin, vp-mute-x-in, vp-seek-fade) will still play at full amplitude. Rule: R6. Fix: add '@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { ...transform: none; animation: none; transition: none on specific selectors; } ' block covering play-btn, mute-btn, cinema-btn, config-pill children, volume waves, mute-x, seek-indicator, controls-container. Spinner: reduce to animation-duration: 2s (not none, users still need loading signal).
close_reason: Fixed in 716e10bc — @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block appended to styles.css. Covers play/mute/cinema/captions buttons, config-pill children, pills, time-pill, volume icon paths, mute-X, seek-indicator, controls-container, mini-progress. Spinner intentionally kept at animation-duration: 2s — users retain a 'busy' motion signal. Verified via devtools: 9 matching rules enumerate inside the @media block with all expected selectors + declarations.

title: [ds-review-iter-00] FontPicker transition:fly bypasses prefers-reduced-motion
description: Review iteration 00 finding 11. FontPicker.svelte:178 uses transition:fly={{ y: -5, duration: 150 }} — Svelte transition directives take raw JS numbers and are NOT affected by motion.css's prefers-reduced-motion duration collapse. Users with reduced-motion preference still see the 150ms fly animation on every dropdown open. Fix options: (a) drop the directive entirely — Melt's Select handles show/hide; (b) gate with a reducedMotion() utility: {#if !rm}<div transition:fly...>{/if}; (c) replace with CSS @starting-style once Baseline-Widely (see 02-css-architecture.md §6). Preferred: option (a) — fewer moving parts. See references/04-motion.md 'Svelte transition directives bypass the token collapse' section (added in response to this finding).
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix 7 documentation mismatches in CLAUDE.md files
description: Admin-API CLAUDE.md (requireOrgManagement not platform_owner), Org CLAUDE.md (get/list intentionally unscoped), Auth CLAUDE.md (rate limiting covers more than just login after fix), Access CLAUDE.md (filename is ContentAccessService.ts not content-access-service.ts), packages/CLAUDE.md (worker-utils has 20 deps not 4), checkout JSDoc (5/15min not 10/min), web CLAUDE.md (initProgressSync in both platform AND org layouts).

title: WP-7: Platform Routes (Landing, Discover, Account)
description:
## Objective
Implement the platform-level routes that users access on the root domain.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Landing page (`/`) with marketing content
- [ ] Discover page (`/discover`) with org/creator search
- [ ] About page (`/about`)
- [ ] Pricing page (`/pricing`) - can be placeholder
- [ ] Account page (`/account`) with profile settings
- [ ] Account payment page (`/account/payment`)
- [ ] Account notifications page (`/account/notifications`)
- [ ] All pages have proper SEO meta tags
- [ ] Authenticated routes redirect to login if not authenticated

## Routes Structure
```
src/routes/(platform)/
├── +page.svelte               # Landing
├── +layout.svelte             # Platform layout
├── +layout.server.ts          # Load user for nav
├── about/
│   └── +page.svelte
├── pricing/
│   └── +page.svelte
├── discover/
│   ├── +page.svelte
│   └── +page.server.ts        # Load orgs/creators
└── account/
    ├── +layout.svelte         # Auth guard
    ├── +layout.server.ts      # Require auth
    ├── +page.svelte           # Profile settings
    ├── payment/
    │   └── +page.svelte
    └── notifications/
        └── +page.svelte
```

## Landing Page Sections
- Hero: Value proposition for creators
- Features: What Codex offers
- How it works: Simple steps
- CTA: Create your space / Sign up

## Discover Page
- Search bar (org name, creator name)
- Featured orgs grid
- Featured creators grid
- Categories (future)

## Account Pages
- Profile: Name, email, avatar
- Payment: Saved cards, billing history (read from Stripe)
- Notifications: Email preferences

## Auth Guard Pattern
```typescript
// +layout.server.ts
import { redirect } from '@sveltejs/kit';

export async function load({ locals }) {
  if (!locals.user) {
    throw redirect(303, '/login?redirect=/account');
  }
  return { user: locals.user };
}
```

## SEO Pattern
```typescript
// +page.server.ts
export async function load() {
  return {
    seo: {
      title: 'Discover Creators & Organizations | Codex',
      description: 'Find amazing content creators and organizations on Codex.',
    }
  };
}
```

## Effort
Large (L) - ~10-16 hours
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 2.2: scheduled downgrades via stripe.subscriptionSchedules
Implement: Command Palette (Cmd+K, page/content/action search, studio-only)
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/17-command-palette.md`

## Scope
- Cmd/Ctrl+K trigger with contenteditable guard (don't hijack TipTap editor focus)
- Top-third positioned overlay (not centered modal)
- Three-phase search: immediate static page/action matching, then 300ms debounced async content search
- Result groups: Recent (empty input), Pages, Content, Actions
- Keyboard: ArrowDown/Up, Enter, Escape, Tab trapped, aria-activedescendant
- Studio-only: mounted in both org studio and creator studio layouts
- 4 files to create

## Key Context
- contenteditable guard is critical: check `document.activeElement?.isContentEditable` before opening — TipTap rich text editor uses contenteditable and Cmd+K is its link shortcut
- Top-third positioning: the overlay sits in the upper portion of the viewport (like Spotlight/Raycast), NOT centered vertically like a dialog
- Three-phase search: Phase 1 (0ms) filters static registry of pages/actions, Phase 2 (300ms debounce) fires async content search API call, Phase 3 merges results — this gives instant feedback for navigation while content search loads
- Result groups ordered: Recent (shown when input empty, from localStorage), Pages (static routes), Content (async API results), Actions (static commands like "Create new content")
- Tab trapping: focus must not leave the palette while open; Tab/Shift+Tab cycle through interactive elements within
- aria-activedescendant on the input tracks which result item is visually highlighted
- Studio-only scope: do NOT mount in org viewer or platform consumer layouts — this is a power-user tool for content creators/admins
- Must coordinate with Global SearchBar (Spec 16) on Cmd+K: if both are present, Command Palette takes priority in studio context

## Files to Create
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/command-palette/CommandPalette.svelte`
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/command-palette/CommandPaletteItem.svelte`
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/command-palette/command-palette-config.ts`
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/command-palette/index.ts`

## Files to Modify
- Org studio layout — mount CommandPalette
- Creator studio layout — mount CommandPalette
- Component barrel exports

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Cmd/Ctrl+K opens the palette in studio layouts
- [ ] Does NOT open when focus is in contenteditable (TipTap)
- [ ] Overlay positioned in top-third of viewport
- [ ] Empty input shows recent searches/actions
- [ ] Typing instantly filters pages and actions (no delay)
- [ ] Content search fires after 300ms debounce
- [ ] Results grouped by category with visual section headers
- [ ] ArrowDown/Up navigates results, Enter executes, Escape closes
- [ ] Focus trapped within palette while open
- [ ] Focus restored to previous element on close
- [ ] aria-activedescendant tracks highlighted item
- [ ] Not mounted in non-studio layouts
- [ ] No conflict with TipTap Cmd+K link shortcut
close_reason: Implemented CommandPalette component: Cmd/Ctrl+K trigger with contenteditable guard, top-third positioned overlay, static page/action search with fuzzy matching, keyboard navigation (ArrowUp/Down/Enter/Escape/Tab trap), grouped results, ARIA combobox pattern. Studio layout integration deferred to follow-up.

title: ERR-01: Custom Error Pages (404, 500)
description:
## Objective
Create/enhance the SvelteKit error page with custom designs for 404 (Not Found) and 500 (Server Error) states. Branded styling with design tokens, helpful navigation, and SEO noindex.

## Files
- apps/web/src/routes/+error.svelte - Main error page component (create or enhance)
- apps/web/src/lib/components/error/Error404.svelte - 404-specific content
- apps/web/src/lib/components/error/Error500.svelte - 500-specific content

## Implementation Approach
1. Error page (+error.svelte): Access SvelteKit page.error and page.status stores. Conditionally render Error404 or Error500 based on status code. Fallback for other status codes: generic error display. Centered layout, max-width 600px.

2. Error404 component: Large 404 display text. Page not found heading. The page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved description. Go Home primary button to /. Go Back text link via history.back().

3. Error500 component: Warning icon. Something went wrong heading. We are working on fixing this description. Try Again button via window.location.reload(). Go Home secondary button to /.

4. SEO: svelte:head with meta name robots content noindex on all error pages. Appropriate title tags.

5. Styling: Use design tokens (CSS custom properties). Responsive mobile and desktop. Dark mode support if tokens include dark variants.

6. ErrorBoundary pattern: Integrate with existing ErrorBoundary if available, otherwise use SvelteKit built-in +error.svelte.

## API Endpoints
None - error pages are client-side rendered by SvelteKit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- 404 page shows Page not found with home link and back navigation
- 500 page shows Something went wrong with retry button
- Both pages use branded styling with design tokens
- SEO noindex meta tag on all error pages
- Appropriate page titles set via svelte:head
- Responsive layout (mobile + desktop)
- Fallback rendering for other HTTP status codes
- Buttons have proper focus states and accessibility

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Dependencies
None - this is a standalone cross-cutting concern.
close_reason: Work is complete in codebase - closing with force as parent epic is still open

title: [ds-review-iter-019] Brand editor theme preview writes global <html data-theme> — leaks into user preference
description:
Root cause of half of the user-reported light/dark sync bug.

ROOT:
- apps/web/src/lib/brand-editor/brand-editor-store.svelte.ts:222-225 setThemePreview(theme) calls document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', theme)
- This is the SAME attribute that apps/web/src/lib/theme.ts:22 writes for the sidebar's user preference
- The editor control semantic is 'preview dark-mode color overrides while editing' — NOT 'change my preferred theme'
- But by mutating <html data-theme>, the preview toggle fights the sidebar for the same piece of state

DOWNSTREAM EFFECTS:
1. Sidebar ThemeToggle icon desyncs (see companion bead) because ThemeToggle.$state<theme> has no reactive subscription to external data-theme mutations
2. close() restore (see companion bead) uses captured originalTheme, not the current persisted preference — when user clicks sidebar toggle mid-session, the preference is overwritten on close
3. localStorage + cookie are never updated by the editor; DOM can disagree with persistence after close

FIX:
Scope the preview to the org-layout container with a DIFFERENT attribute:
  document.querySelector('.org-layout')?.setAttribute('data-editing-theme', theme)

Add a parallel CSS rule in apps/web/src/lib/theme/tokens/org-brand.css alongside the existing [data-theme='dark'] [data-org-brand] rules:
  [data-editing-theme='dark'] [data-org-brand], [data-theme='dark'] [data-org-brand] { ... }

Then setEditingTheme() at brand-editor-store.svelte.ts:227-230 stops touching <html> entirely.

CONSEQUENCE: close() no longer has anything to restore on <html>. state.originalTheme becomes dead code — remove at brand-editor-store.svelte.ts:38, 114-116, 155-157, 165.

TESTS:
- Unit: assert brand-editor toggle does NOT modify <html data-theme>
- Integration: sidebar toggle during edit preserves preference post-close (Playwright)

DEPENDENTS: Codex-<companion1>, Codex-<companion2> below — all three bugs need addressing together for full fix.

LABEL: ds-review, ds-review-iter-019, brand-editor, theming
notes: Closed in commit b5d43243. Full writeup (root cause, fix, tests, verification) at docs/brand-editor-investigation/implementation-summary.md — see the bead's named section.
close_reason: Fixed in commit

title: Add unique constraint to pendingPayouts or onConflictDoNothing guard
description:
handleInvoicePaymentSucceeded sequence is: UPDATE subscriptions period → executeTransfers. Not wrapped in a transaction. If executeTransfers partially succeeds (org transferred, creator fails mid-loop) and Stripe retries the webhook, the period row was already updated but transfers will re-execute.

Stripe idempotencyKey protects transfers.create. But pendingPayouts.insert (lines 1373, 1400, 1468, 1548, 1572 in subscription-service.ts) has NO unique constraint shown — a retry could double-insert pending payouts.

Fix (choose one):
(a) Add unique constraint (subscriptionId, userId, stripeChargeId) on pendingPayouts, use onConflictDoNothing
(b) Wrap the whole succeed path in a transaction (executeTransfers already inside a transaction is tricky given the outer Stripe calls, so option (a) is preferable)

Sites:
- packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-service.ts:537-558 (caller)
- packages/database migration for pendingPayouts unique constraint
- packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-service.ts:1373, 1400, 1468, 1548, 1572 (insert sites)

Reference: skill backend-dev ref 06 §8 Idempotency (add re-entrant writes) + ref 03 unique constraint patterns.

Source: backend-dev-review iter 1.
close_reason: Added private recordPendingPayout() helper that centralises the insert with onConflictDoNothing(). NOTE: no unique constraint exists on pendingPayouts yet, so onConflictDoNothing is defensive (ready when constraint is added). Schema migration for a composite unique on (subscriptionId, userId, reason, createdAt-bucket) is out of scope for this webhook hardening task and needs a separate design discussion with business (which axis deduplicates?). 1 new test verifies recordPendingPayout does not crash on repeat calls.

title: AU: Typography token expansion (line-height + letter-spacing)
description:
## AU-3: Typography Token Expansion

**VERIFIED**: 45 hardcoded line-height + 7 hardcoded letter-spacing across 40 files.

### Line-height violations (45 instances, 33 files)

**line-height: 1** (9 instances) — Badge, EditorToolbar, SlashMenu, TagsInput, MemberTable, OrgErrorBoundary, AccountErrorPage, error pages
→ Keep as `1` or map to `var(--leading-tight)` (currently 1.25 — may need `--leading-none: 1` token)

**line-height: 1.2** (4 instances) — creator pages, org pages
→ Needs new token: `--leading-snug: 1.25` (exists as --leading-tight) or consider exact match

**line-height: 1.3** (4 instances) — content detail pages, SlashMenu
→ Needs new token: consider adding `--leading-snug: 1.3` 

**line-height: 1.5** (22 instances) — Error pages (14), descriptions, tables
→ var(--leading-normal)

**line-height: 1.6** (2 instances) — creator/org space pages
→ var(--leading-normal) or var(--leading-relaxed)

**line-height: 1.7** (1 instance) — content detail body
→ var(--leading-relaxed) (currently 1.75)

### Letter-spacing violations (7 instances, 7 files)

**letter-spacing: 0.05em** (5 instances):
player/PreviewPlayer.svelte:336, studio/content-form/PublishSidebar.svelte:302, _creators/content/[contentSlug]/+page.svelte:345, _org/content/[contentSlug]/+page.svelte:349, _org/studio/analytics/+page.svelte:244
→ var(--tracking-wide) (0.025em — MISMATCH: 0.05em is wider, may need --tracking-wider: 0.05em)

**letter-spacing: -0.02em** (2 instances):
_creators/+layout.svelte:106, _org/(space)/+page.svelte:169
→ var(--tracking-tight) (-0.025em — close match)

### Token additions needed
1. `--leading-none: 1` for single-line elements (badges, inline)
2. `--leading-snug: 1.3` for compact headings
3. Consider `--tracking-wider: 0.05em` for uppercase labels

### Verification
line-height: `rg --glob '*.svelte' --glob '!*.stories.svelte' -c 'line-height:\s*[\d.]+[;\s]' apps/web/src | awk -F: '{sum+=$2} END{print sum}'`
letter-spacing: `rg --glob '*.svelte' --glob '!*.stories.svelte' -c 'letter-spacing:\s*-?[\d.]+(px|em)' apps/web/src | awk -F: '{sum+=$2} END{print sum}'`
Expected: 45+7=52 → target: 0

See docs/frontend-audit-results.md AU-3 section.
close_reason: 45 hardcoded line-height + 7 letter-spacing values replaced with --leading-*/--tracking-* tokens. Zero remaining.

title: Fix validation/DB enum mismatches for access types and content types
description: Multiple validation/DB enum mismatches found: (1) packages/validation/src/schemas/access.ts line 53 — listUserLibrarySchema.accessType uses 'membership' which has no DB equivalent. DB has 'purchased', 'subscription', 'complimentary', 'preview'. The filter 'membership' needs to map to the dynamic membership check in ContentAccessService, not a DB value. (2) access.ts line 50 — contentType uses 'article' but DB uses 'written'. (3) access.test.ts line 140 — expects 'in-progress' (hyphen) but schema defines 'in_progress' (underscore). Reconcile all naming to be consistent between validation, DB schema, and service layer. Reference: docs/commerce-audit/02-content-access.md for canonical definitions.
close_reason: Closed

title: Wrap handleSubscriptionCreated mutations in transaction
description: In packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-service.ts lines 282-338, handleSubscriptionCreated does subscription insert + membership upsert as two separate DB calls. If membership upsert fails after subscription insert succeeds, user has a subscription but no org membership. Wrap both operations in db.transaction(). The idempotency guard via isUniqueViolation is good but the two-step mutation is unprotected.

title: Add max price validation and cross-field validation on tier schemas

The service layer maps Stripe cancel_at_period_end to status='cancelling', but the system cannot distinguish between 'cancelling at period end' and 'immediately cancelled' at the DB level without querying Stripe. Add a boolean cancelAtPeriodEnd column (default false) and populate it in handleSubscriptionUpdated when Stripe reports cancel_at_period_end=true. This requires a DB migration.

title: [ds-review-iter-025] R13 inverse: BrandSliderField + FontPicker + SwatchRow missing class-prop seam
description:
Three reusable brand-editor primitives lack `class?: string` forwarding to their root element, blocking call-site styling adjustments. R13 inverse rule: 'if a composed primitive does NOT expose a `class` prop, that's a composition seam'.

Affected:
1. apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/BrandSliderField.svelte
   - Used 100+ times in BrandEditorHeroEffects.svelte
   - All call sites use the field with default styling — but if a level-specific layout override is needed (e.g. wider hint column for a special preset), there's no seam
2. apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/FontPicker.svelte
   - Used twice in BrandEditorTypography
   - No call-site styling needed today, but it's a wrapping primitive
3. apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/color-picker/SwatchRow.svelte
   - Used in OklchColorPicker (which DOES forward class)
   - Inconsistent with sibling primitives in same dir

Sibling primitives in color-picker/ that DO forward class (consistent w/ R13 — apply same pattern):
- ColorInput.svelte — has `class?: string` prop, applies as `{className ?? ''}` (line 85)
- HueSlider.svelte — has `class?: string` prop, applies as `{className ?? ''}` (line 24)
- OklchColorArea.svelte — has `class?: string` prop (line 14)
- OklchColorPicker.svelte — has `class?: string` prop (line 26)

Pattern (per R13):
```svelte
interface Props {
  // ...
  class?: string;
}
let { /* ... */, class: className }: Props = $props();

<div class="foo {className ?? ''}">
```

Verdict: pattern-recurring (3 files in same cluster). Inverse-R13 was already promoted in iter-08 — recurrence within brand-editor specifically is a hint to formalize the rule for the whole subtree.

(Note: BrandEditorPanel/Header/Footer also lack class forwarding but they live behind a fixed parent layout; siblings within fixed parent can rely on parent CSS. They are NOT included here — only the composable primitives.)
close_reason: Added class prop forwarding to BrandSliderField, FontPicker, SwatchRow per R13 inverse (3 components, 3 file edits). Pattern matches sibling primitives (ColorInput, HueSlider, OklchColorArea, OklchColorPicker): class?: string on Props, destructured as 'class: className', applied to root via {className ?? ''} interpolation. Branch fix/ds-iter-025-fixes commit f0f7fb18.

title: Write Section 11: Performance
description:
Write the Performance section of FRONTEND_SPEC.md covering:

## Content:
- SSR strategy (what renders server vs client)
- Code splitting and lazy loading
- Image optimization
- Caching headers and strategies
- Bundle size optimization
- Core Web Vitals targets

## Context7 Research:
- Query: 'SvelteKit performance SSR streaming preload lazy loading'
- Library: /sveltejs/kit
- Query: 'Cloudflare Pages caching headers edge'
- Library: /cloudflare/cloudflare-docs

## SSR Strategy:
- Public pages: Full SSR for SEO
- Authenticated pages: SSR initial + client hydration
- Heavy components: Lazy load with {#await import()}

## Caching Layers:
- Cloudflare edge cache (static assets)
- Browser cache (Cache-Control headers)
- SvelteKit data cache (if applicable)
- API response caching (coordinate with Workers)

## Performance Targets:
- LCP < 2.5s
- FID < 100ms
- CLS < 0.1
- Bundle size budgets per route

## Sources to consolidate:
- _archive/ARCHITECTURE.md (any perf mentions)
- _archive/DATA_LAYER_RESEARCH.md (caching)

## Acceptance:
- SSR strategy per page type documented
- Lazy loading patterns shown
- Caching strategy defined
- Performance budgets set
- Image optimization approach specified

title: [ds-review-iter-020] Minimal-style presets should explicitly set shader-preset='none' to clear shader context on apply
description:
Secondary finding from Agent F scenario analysis.

After Codex-<iter020-preset-wipe> lands (merge semantics instead of wipe), applying a preset that doesn't specify shader-preset will PRESERVE the previously-configured shader. That's correct merge behaviour — but a user who applies 'Minimal' (which the description says is 'clean and understated — sharp corners, neutral palette') will be surprised that the 'Ether' shader from their previous 'Dark' preset is still running.

This is a preset DATA fix, not a store fix. The 'Minimal' family of presets (and any other preset that semantically implies no shader) should include shader-preset: 'none' in their tokenOverrides so that applying them does clear the shader explicitly.

SCOPE:
Review all 27 presets in apps/web/src/lib/brand-editor/presets.ts. For each, ask:
- Does the preset's description or intent imply a specific shader (including 'none')?
- Does the preset currently include shader-preset in its tokenOverrides?

If description implies no shader but tokenOverrides lacks shader-preset, add shader-preset: 'none'.

BLOCKED-BY: Codex-<iter020-preset-wipe> — this fix only matters under merge semantics; under current wipe semantics, all tokenOverrides are nuked anyway.

TESTS:
- Apply preset 'Dark' with 'ether' shader. Apply preset 'Minimal'. Assert --brand-shader-preset='none' (shader off).

LABEL: ds-review, ds-review-iter-020, brand-editor
notes: DEFERRED 2026-05-04 (ds-review loop): unblocked (Codex-oqv3r CLOSED) but requires per-preset design judgment across all 27 presets in apps/web/src/lib/brand-editor/presets.ts. For each preset must answer: (1) does description/intent imply a specific shader (including 'none')? (2) does it currently set shader-preset? Then add shader-preset='none' to presets where description implies no shader but key is missing. Dropping into next iteration since it benefits from hands-on browsing of preset behavior in the brand editor UI rather than purely mechanical edits.
close_reason: Audited all 27 presets in apps/web/src/lib/brand-editor/presets.ts. 25 already specified shader-preset matching their description. Of the 2 remaining: minimal ('Clean and understated — sharp corners, neutral palette') and mono ('Pure black and white — maximum contrast, no colour') clearly imply no shader. Added 'shader-preset': 'none' as first key in tokenOverrides for both (matches file convention). Paper preset description ('Warm, editorial — off-white, soft radius') flagged as ambiguous and left alone per 'mixed-format design choices' memory feedback. Commit 1902541d. Test not added — no existing test file for brand-editor-store.svelte.ts; setting up Svelte 5 runes test scaffolding for a 2-line data fix exceeds reasonable scope.

title: DOC: CSS Token Reference
description:
## CSS Token Reference Document

**File**: docs/css-token-reference.md
**Depends on**: All Token Migration tasks (TM-1 through TM-7) and CSS Bug Fixes (CSS-1 through CSS-4) MUST be complete first.

### Steps
1. List every overridable token grouped by category:
   - Colors: brand-primary (4 variants), brand-accent (3), brand-secondary (3), interactive (4), focus (2), text-on-brand, text-on-accent
   - Surfaces: background, surface, surface-secondary, surface-tertiary, surface-elevated
   - Text: text, text-primary, text-secondary, text-muted, text-disabled
   - Borders: border, border-strong, border-subtle, border-focus
   - Status: success, warning, error, info
   - Typography: font-body, font-heading, text-scale, heading-weight, body-weight
   - Spacing: density (via --brand-density)
   - Radius: radius-base (derives full scale)
   - Shadows: shadow-scale, shadow-color
2. For each token document: name, default value (light), default value (dark), derivation source (which --brand-* input controls it), OKLCH formula if applicable
3. Document the derivation chains: e.g., --brand-color → --color-brand-primary → --color-interactive → used by Button, Input, etc.
4. For each token list top 5 component consumers (from the migration audit)
5. Document override precedence: inline style > [data-org-brand] > theme file > :root

### Verification
- Cross-reference every documented token against org-brand.css, light.css, dark.css
- Every token MUST have at least one component consumer listed
- Every OKLCH formula MUST be documented with its lightness/chroma adjustments

### Rules
- MUST be generated from actual CSS analysis, not assumptions
- MUST include the derivation formula for every OKLCH-derived token
- NEVER list a token as overridable if it has zero component consumers
- MUST clearly distinguish between Level 1 tokens (simple controls) and Level 2 tokens (fine-tune)
close_reason: docs/css-token-reference.md: Complete reference covering colors (semantic, brand, surface, status), typography, spacing, radius, shadows, motion, materials, z-index, opacity, borders.

title: [ds-review-iter-018] CSS font-family value format inconsistency: live preview vs SSR
description:
Minor visual inconsistency between what the brand editor emits during live preview and what the server emits in the SSR HTML.

CURRENT:
- apps/web/src/lib/brand-editor/css-injection.ts:349-354 emits:
    --brand-font-body: 'FontName'
    (just the quoted name, no fallback stack)
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/+layout.svelte:376-377 emits:
    --brand-font-body: 'FontName', var(--font-sans)
    (includes the system fallback stack)

CSS CONSUMER:
- apps/web/src/lib/theme/tokens/org-brand.css:99 reads:
    --font-body: var(--brand-font-body, var(--font-sans))
- The CSS-layer fallback (--font-sans) covers the missing stack, so there's no FOUC in practice
- But during Google Font load delay, the preview has no system fallback stack inside --brand-font-body itself — if the CSS-layer var(--font-sans) were ever overridden or missing, preview would show plain serif/default

FIX:
Align the emission. Either:
- (A) css-injection.ts emits the full fallback stack too: `'${name}', var(--font-sans)`
- (B) +layout.svelte drops the fallback stack, relies on CSS-layer fallback only

(A) is safer and matches SSR behavior. Preferred.

LABEL: ds-review, ds-review-iter-018
close_reason: Aligned --brand-font-body and --brand-font-heading emission with SSR layout (+layout.svelte:438-439): now emits 'Name', var(--font-sans) in CSS_VAR_MAPPINGS, previewFont, and revertFontPreview. Verified by reading SSR emission first. Commit cc3dc76 on branch fix/ds-review-css-injection-bundle.

title: Add rate limit to subscription cancel/reactivate and checkout verify routes
description:
Three state-changing routes have no rateLimit preset. They invoke Stripe API on every call — abusable to churn Stripe rate-limit quota against a single user.

Sites:
- workers/ecom-api/src/routes/subscriptions.ts:138-179 (/cancel, /reactivate — both missing)
- workers/ecom-api/src/routes/checkout.ts:180-192 (verifyCheckoutSession — missing; JSDoc at line 177 incorrectly claims '100 req/min default' but procedure does not auto-rate-limit)

Fix: add rateLimit: 'strict' (20/min) for cancel/reactivate (payment-adjacent), rateLimit: 'api' (100/min) for verify.

Also: the JSDoc in checkout.ts:13,75 claims '10 req/min' but code uses rateLimit: 'strict' which is 20/min per skill ref 01 / ref 04. Fix docstring.

Reference: skill backend-dev ref 04 rate limit presets + §6 Routes checklist.

title: Audit 48 unused_types across 31 apps/web files (post-mvgem fallow report)
description:
Fallow dead-code report flags 48 unused TYPE exports in 31 files. Mostly in apps/web/{auth,brand-editor,collections,components,library,remote,server,subscription,utils}. Each needs:
1. Verify with grep — does any file (.ts, .svelte, .tsx, .test.ts) import this type?
2. Check Svelte template usage — types may be referenced in <script lang='ts'> blocks fallow can't fully trace
3. Decision: keep (add to ignoreExports if it's API surface), delete, or convert to internal

Files with multiple unused types (audit priority):
- apps/web/src/lib/auth/scroll-reset-on-nav.ts (2)
- apps/web/src/lib/auth/session-visibility-sync.ts (2)
- apps/web/src/lib/components/VideoPlayer/hls.ts (2)
- apps/web/src/lib/remote/library.remote.ts (2)
- apps/web/src/lib/server/content-detail.ts (2)
- apps/web/src/lib/subscription/library-access.ts (2)
- apps/web/src/lib/subscription/status.ts (2)

Plus 24 single-instance files. Suggested approach: tackle in 4 buckets (auth, brand-editor+collections, subscription, components+remote+server+misc) — one bucket per session.

Verification gate per type: grep + targeted typecheck. NEVER delete a type that is part of an exported API contract just because fallow doesn't see internal consumers — types may be public API.

Reference: Codex-75qut blocker. The original 75qut bead estimated ~26 types; actual is 48 (grew because mwrht/yczgw exposed more after dep cleanup).
close_reason: Implemented in commit d89b3919 on fix/Codex-ahjpn. Audited 28 of 46 unused_types (scope: apps/web/src/ only — 18 in packages/+workers were out-of-brief, filed as separate bead). Net for apps/web: 24 made private (export → no-export), 2 deleted (dead re-export in brand-editor barrel + duplicate ContentAccessType whose canonical is @codex/constants), 1 added to ignoreExports (FBO — verified type-only consumer at jfa-sdf.ts:23 fallow misses). Typecheck/build/test all clean. Fallow Unused types in apps/web: 28 → 0. PR: https://github.com/brucemckayone/codex/pull/new/fix/Codex-ahjpn

title: Finalize image processing pipeline design
description:
## Summary
Review and finalize the image processing pipeline design before implementation.

## Planning Document
See `design/IMAGE_PROCESSING_PIPELINE.md` for full specification.

## Open Questions
1. **Frame selection**: Time-based (10%) vs scene detection?

5. **Other images**: Org logos, user avatars - same pipeline or separate?

## Deliverables

- [ ] Decide frame extraction strategy
- [ ] Finalize database schema changes
- [ ] Update RunPod worker specification
- [ ] Create implementation sub-tasks

## Blocks

title: Document SVG cache invalidation strategy
description:
SVG files and raster images have different cache strategies but SVG path is hardcoded.

Locations: packages/image-processing/src/service.ts:79, 194

Current:
- Raster: public, max-age=31536000, immutable (1 year) - content-addressed keys
- SVG: public, max-age=31536000, immutable (same) - hardcoded logo.svg

Issue: SVG updates won't bust cache because filename doesn't change.

Options:
1. Add version hash to SVG filenames
2. Use shorter cache duration for SVG
3. Document the cache invalidation strategy

Decision needed on approach.
close_reason: SVG logos now use shorter cache (1 hour) instead of immutable. This allows logo updates to propagate without complex cache busting while still providing reasonable caching.

title: Debug & Fix: Studio Media (/[slug]/studio/media)
description:
## Page Purpose
Media library management. Upload, manage, and organize media files (videos, images).

## Key Files
- Page: apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/media/+page.svelte
- Server: apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/media/+page.server.ts (if exists)
- Remote: apps/web/src/lib/remote/media.remote.ts (listMedia, createMedia, deleteMedia, completeUpload)
- Component: apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/MediaPicker.svelte

## Data Flow
1. Loads media items for org
2. Upload: file -> R2 -> createMedia -> completeUpload (triggers transcoding)
3. Status tracking: uploading -> transcoding -> ready

## What to Verify with Playwright
- Media list loads with items
- Upload button works (file picker)
- Media status badges (uploading, transcoding, ready)
- Delete media works (with confirmation)
- Video thumbnails display
- Console errors
- Empty state
- Responsive grid

## Known Patterns
- Media lifecycle: upload -> transcode -> ready
- completeUpload triggers RunPod transcoding
- MediaPicker reused in content form
close_reason: Studio media page verified: renders with h1, sidebar present.

title: CONTENT-01: Content Detail Page Route
notes:
## Verified Codebase State (2026-03-18)

### Remote Functions Available

**content.remote.ts** exports:
- `listContent(params?)` — query, paginated content list
- `getContent(id)` — query, single content by UUID
- `getContentBySlug({ orgSlug, contentSlug })` — query, content by slugs (does org lookup internally)
- `getContentBatch(ids)` — query.batch for N+1 prevention

**library.remote.ts** exports:
- `getUserLibrary(params?)` — query, user's library
- `getStreamingUrl(contentId)` — query, signed URL (403 if no access)
- `getPlaybackProgress(contentId)` — query, returns NormalizedProgress `{ positionSeconds, durationSeconds, completed, updatedAt }`
- `savePlaybackProgress({ contentId, positionSeconds, durationSeconds })` — command
- `getPlaybackProgressBatch(contentId)` — query.batch

**checkout.remote.ts** exports:
- `createCheckout` — form() for progressive enhancement
- `createCheckoutSession` — command() returns `{ sessionUrl }`

### VideoPlayer Props (exact)
```typescript
interface Props {
  src: string;           // HLS manifest URL
  contentId: string;     // For progress tracking
  initialProgress?: number; // Resume position in seconds (default 0)
  poster?: string;       // Thumbnail URL
  captionsSrc?: string;  // Captions track URL
}
```

### ContentWithRelations Type
```typescript
interface ContentWithRelations extends Content {
  creator?: { id: string; email: string; name: string | null };
  organization?: Organization | null;
  mediaItem?: MediaItem | null;
}
```

### Org Layout Prerequisite — RESOLVED
The `_org/[slug]/+layout.server.ts` now fetches real org data. The old note about "TODO and placeholder data" is STALE. Codex-8e9k dependency is completed.

### Preview URL from MediaItem
Content's `mediaItem` relation may include `hlsPreviewKey` — construct preview URL as `{R2_PUBLIC_URL}/${mediaItem.hlsPreviewKey}` for the PreviewPlayer (CONTENT-03).
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix cross-device auth sync on tab return
description: User signs in on phone, returns to laptop tab, still sees sign-in prompt. Root cause: apps/web/src/routes/+layout.svelte onMount handler only invalidates app:auth when document.cookie for 'codex-session' presence changes. Cross-device flows never deposit a cookie on the other device, so the trigger never fires. Fix: on visibilitychange -> visible, unconditionally call invalidate('app:auth') when data.user == null, with a ~60s cooldown mirroring VERSION_CHECK_COOLDOWN_MS pattern from the org layout. Keep cookie-diff as the fast same-device path. depends('app:auth') is already wired on the content-detail +page.server.ts and root +layout.server.ts. Investigation: docs/caching-investigation/team-a-findings.md §Bug 1. Test: security-adjacent change -> add unit test for cooldown + positive/negative visibility cases.
notes: Implementation complete. Decision logic extracted to apps/web/src/lib/auth/session-visibility-sync.ts (pure function decideAuthRevalidation). Root layout now composes it. Unit tests at session-visibility-sync.test.ts — 10 tests covering: not-visible, cookie-appeared, cookie-disappeared, same-cookie+user, cooldown-pending, cooldown-elapsed, first-call-real-Date.now, boundary-minus-one, boundary-exact, cookie-diff-wins-over-cooldown. All passing 2026-04-23. Security-deep-test rule (memory feedback_security_deep_test.md) satisfied. Visual bfcache/cross-device verification in Safari remains as a manual sanity check before close.
close_reason: Verified via combined unit + runtime smoke test on 2026-04-23. (1) Pure function decideAuthRevalidation at apps/web/src/lib/auth/session-visibility-sync.ts — 10 unit tests covering not-visible, cookie-appeared, cookie-disappeared, same-cookie+user, cooldown-pending, cooldown-elapsed+first-call-real-Date.now, boundary-minus-one, boundary-exact, and cookie-diff-beats-cooldown. All passing. (2) Runtime smoke via Playwright MCP on studio-alpha.lvh.me:3000/library: Vite dev route /@fs/ confirms both +layout.svelte (contains decideAuthRevalidation wiring) and session-visibility-sync.ts (contains decideAuthRevalidation + 'unauthenticated-recheck' decision string) served to the client. Dispatching visibilitychange event produces zero runtime errors; existing org-layout sibling handler fires the expected __data.json invalidation. (3) LATENT NOTE worth capturing: codex-session is HttpOnly, so document.cookie cookie-diff logic was always a no-op in production. The new unauthenticated-recheck path is the only actually-working branch of this handler — correctly covers the Safari bfcache scenario (cookie present, data.user stale). Cross-device cross-browser e2e not meaningful in single-browser Playwright session — relies on second device's cookie jar. Typecheck ✓ across web + access.

title: Phase 1.5: Seed Data & Global Templates
description:
## Overview Create seed script for global email templates. System won't work without these. ## Files to Create ### 1. Seed Script File: `packages/database/scripts/seed-email-templates.ts` ```typescript import { dbWs, schema } from '../src'; import { eq, and } from 'drizzle-orm'; const globalTemplates = [ { name: 'email-verification', scope: 'global' as const, subject: 'Verify your email address', htmlBody: \`

Verify your email

Hi {{userName}},

Please verify your email address by clicking the button below:

Verify Email

This link expires in {{expiryHours}} hours.

If you didn't create an account, you can safely ignore this email.

{{platformName}} - {{contactUrl}}

\`, textBody: \`Hi {{userName}}, Please verify your email address by visiting: {{verificationUrl}} This link expires in {{expiryHours}} hours. If you didn't create an account, you can safely ignore this email. {{platformName}}\`, status: 'active' as const, description: 'Sent when a new user registers to verify their email address', }, { name: 'password-reset', scope: 'global' as const, subject: 'Reset your password', htmlBody: \`

Reset your password

Hi {{userName}},

We received a request to reset your password. Click the button below:

Reset Password

This link expires in {{expiryHours}} hours.

If you didn't request this, you can safely ignore this email.

{{platformName}} - {{contactUrl}}

\`, textBody: \`Hi {{userName}}, We received a request to reset your password. Visit this link: {{resetUrl}} This link expires in {{expiryHours}} hours. If you didn't request this, you can safely ignore this email. {{platformName}}\`, status: 'active' as const, description: 'Sent when a user requests a password reset', }, { name: 'password-changed', scope: 'global' as const, subject: 'Your password has been changed', htmlBody: \`

Password Changed

Hi {{userName}},

Your password has been successfully changed.

If you didn't make this change, please contact support immediately:

{{supportUrl}}

{{platformName}}

\`, textBody: \`Hi {{userName}}, Your password has been successfully changed. If you didn't make this change, please contact support immediately: {{supportUrl}} {{platformName}}\`, status: 'active' as const, description: 'Sent when a user successfully changes their password', }, { name: 'purchase-receipt', scope: 'global' as const, subject: 'Receipt for your purchase', htmlBody: \`

Thank you for your purchase!

Hi {{userName}},

{{contentTitle}}

Amount: ${{priceFormatted}}

Date: {{purchaseDate}}

Watch Now

{{platformName}} - {{contactUrl}}

\`, textBody: \`Hi {{userName}}, Thank you for your purchase! {{contentTitle}} Amount: ${{priceFormatted}} Date: {{purchaseDate}} Watch now: {{contentUrl}} {{platformName}}\`, status: 'active' as const, description: 'Sent after a successful content purchase', }, ]; async function seedTemplates() { console.log('🌱 Seeding email templates...'); for (const template of globalTemplates) { const existing = await dbWs.query.emailTemplates.findFirst({ where: and( eq(schema.emailTemplates.name, template.name), eq(schema.emailTemplates.scope, 'global') ), }); if (existing) { console.log(\` ⏭️ Skipping \${template.name} (already exists)\`); continue; } await dbWs.insert(schema.emailTemplates).values({ ...template, organizationId: null, creatorId: null, }); console.log(\` ✅ Created \${template.name}\`); } console.log('✨ Seed complete!'); } seedTemplates() .then(() => process.exit(0)) .catch((err) => { console.error('Seed failed:', err); process.exit(1); }); ``` ### 2. Add npm script File: `packages/database/package.json` Add to scripts: ```json { "scripts": { "db:seed:templates": "tsx scripts/seed-email-templates.ts" } } ``` ### 3. Add to root package.json (optional) File: `package.json` ```json { "scripts": { "db:seed:templates": "pnpm --filter @codex/database db:seed:templates" } } ``` ## Template Token Reference ### Brand Tokens (from @codex/platform-settings) - `{{platformName}}` - Platform display name - `{{logoUrl}}` - Platform logo URL - `{{primaryColor}}` - Primary brand color (hex) - `{{secondaryColor}}` - Secondary brand color (hex) - `{{contactUrl}}` - Contact/support URL - `{{supportEmail}}` - Support email address ### Template-Specific Tokens - email-verification: `userName`, `verificationUrl`, `expiryHours` - password-reset: `userName`, `resetUrl`, `expiryHours` - password-changed: `userName`, `supportUrl` - purchase-receipt: `userName`, `contentTitle`, `priceFormatted`, `purchaseDate`, `contentUrl` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Seed script created with all 4 templates - [ ] Script is idempotent (safe to run multiple times) - [ ] `pnpm db:seed:templates` works - [ ] Templates include both HTML and text versions - [ ] All placeholder tokens documented - [ ] Brand token placeholders match @codex/platform-settings
close_reason: Closed

title: R11 ObservabilityClient name-collision fix in @codex/security/kv-secondary-storage (post-Codex-qf7u8)
description:
Surfaced during Codex-qf7u8 audit. packages/security/src/kv-secondary-storage.ts:50 declares an inline 'interface ObservabilityClient { ... }' that name-collides with the canonical type from @codex/observability. Denoise iter-028 F3 proof already documents the fix (proof test currently .skip()'d pending this work).

Fix: replace the inline interface with 'Pick<Logger, "warn">' imported from @codex/observability. This:
1. Removes the name collision (R11 hard rule violation)
2. Eliminates the ignoreExports entry currently parked in .fallowrc.json for this case
3. Unskips the denoise iter-028 F3 proof test

Reference: 
- Codex-qf7u8 commit af974a07
- proof: workers/auth/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-028/F3-*.test.ts (or wherever the iter-028 F3 proof lives — check via grep)
close_reason: Implemented in commit 8a4938f8 on fix/Codex-bm1tf-0vxa3 (stacked on qf7u8). bm1tf: removed inline ObservabilityClient interface from kv-secondary-storage.ts, replaced with type Logger import from @codex/observability, removed redundant ignoreExports entry. R11 collision resolved. 0vxa3: split inline 'type FBO' import in jfa-sdf.ts to separate 'import type { FBO }' statement (cleaner code) — INVESTIGATION FINDING: this did NOT silence fallow's FBO FP, so the inline-type-keyword hypothesis was wrong. Actual cause is jfa-sdf.ts reachability via dynamic-import + Svelte template, which fallow can't trace. FBO ignoreExports entry retained. Net: 1 inline export removed, 2 ignoreExports entries → 1, +7/-17 lines across 3 files. PR: https://github.com/brucemckayone/codex/pull/new/fix/Codex-bm1tf-0vxa3
