title: P1-TRANSCODE-001: Media Transcoding System
description:
Implement GPU-accelerated media transcoding pipeline with RunPod, HLS generation, and tiered storage.

**Architecture:**
- @codex/transcoding package (business logic)
- media-api worker (triggers + webhooks)
- RunPod Python worker (FFmpeg + audiowaveform)
- Tiered storage: R2 (delivery) + B2 (archival)

**Phases:**
- Phase 1: Codex-01i - Database Schema & Validation
- Phase 2: Codex-7tu - @codex/transcoding Package
- Phase 3: Codex-phu - media-api Worker
- Phase 4: Codex-7sp - RunPod Python Worker
- Phase 5: Codex-bjc - Content-API Integration
- Phase 6: Codex-fjd - Unit Tests
- Phase 7: Codex-ztf - Integration & E2E Tests
- Phase 8: Codex-dmb - CI/CD & Deployment

**Success Criteria:**
- Video → HLS ready in <10 min
- Audio → HLS + waveform ready in <2 min
- 95%+ success rate
- All tests passing

### 🎯 Current Status Summary

title: Audit non-branding deleteIntroVideo paths for orphan-original bug (Codex-x1o7h follow-up)
description:
During Codex-x1o7h fix (originals/{mediaId}/* hard-delete on intro-video deletion in branding-settings-service.ts), the implementing agent flagged that the HLS-only cleanup pattern likely exists elsewhere in the codebase wherever media deletion happens. Same orphan-original accumulation bug, different entry points.

Audit needed: grep for `getHlsPrefix`, `{creatorId}/hls/`, and `deleteHlsObjects`-style callsites across packages/ and workers/. Likely candidates: MediaItemService deletion, content deletion cascades (content-api), any route that soft-deletes a media item.

For each callsite where HLS objects are cleaned but originals are not, mirror the Codex-x1o7h fix (sibling deleteOriginalObjects sweep). If a third orphan prefix appears (thumbnails, waveforms), promote to a shared deletePrefix(prefix, logContext) helper at that point.

Reference fix: commit 051a2a6a on fix/Codex-x1o7h, packages/platform-settings/src/services/branding-settings-service.ts:768.
close_reason: Audit complete 2026-05-05. Single non-branding callsite identified: MediaItemService.delete at packages/content/src/services/media-service.ts:337 — performs ZERO R2 cleanup (soft-deletes DB row only). This means EVERY media deletion via content-api orphans BOTH originals AND HLS in R2 (also thumbnails for video, waveform images for audio, previews). branding-settings-service.deleteIntroVideo is the ONLY callsite that cleans R2 today (HLS via Codex-mo3ib, originals via Codex-x1o7h). Audit grep confirmed: only 1 deleteHlsObjects-style helper exists, and it's the one we already fixed. The actual fix is bigger than a mirror — filed as separate bead with architecture-decision scope (eager in MediaItemService vs. background OrphanedFileService reaper).

title: Configure BetterAuth cross-subdomain cookies
description:
## Problem
Session cookies need to work across subdomains:
- `revelations.studio` (platform)
- `yoga-studio.revelations.studio` (org)
- `creators.revelations.studio` (creator hub)

## Solution
Add cross-subdomain cookie configuration to BetterAuth:

```typescript
// workers/auth/src/auth-config.ts
advanced: {
  crossSubDomainCookies: {
    enabled: true,
    domain: '.revelations.studio'
  }
}
```

## Verification Needed
1. Check if this config already exists in auth worker
2. Test cookie sharing across subdomains
3. Verify local dev still works (localhost doesn't use subdomain cookies)

## Impact
Without this, users would need to re-login when navigating between subdomains.
close_reason: Duplicate of Codex-43l which has more detail

title: ContentTable optimistic update may not trigger Svelte 5 reactivity
description: ContentTable.svelte handlePublishToggle() directly mutates item.status on a prop from $props(). In Svelte 5, mutating nested properties of a prop may not trigger reactivity in the parent. Verify this works correctly or use a local $state copy/snapshot pattern. The rollback on error has the same issue.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced direct prop mutation with  Record<string,string> statusOverrides map. Template reads via getItemStatus() which falls back to original prop. Rollback on error deletes the override entry.

title: STUDIO-04: Studio Content Management
description:
## Objective
Build the Studio content management page with a table listing all content for the organization. Includes title, type, status badges, view counts, and edit actions with server-side pagination.

## What Already Exists
- **content.remote.ts**: `apps/web/src/lib/remote/content.remote.ts` has `listContent(params)` query with pagination, filtering (status, organizationId, search, sortBy, sortOrder)
- **API client**: `apps/web/src/lib/server/api.ts` has api.content.list(params) returning PaginatedListResponse<ContentWithRelations>
- **UI components**: Table (full suite: Table, TableBody, TableCell, TableHead, TableHeader, TableRow, TableFooter), Badge, Button, Pagination (production-ready with stories), Skeleton, Input, Select
- **Studio layout**: Provides org.id and userRole from parent layout data
- **No content route under studio**: `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/content/` does not exist

## What Needs to Be Created

### 1. Content Listing Route
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/content/+page.svelte` — Content table page
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/content/+page.server.ts` — Server load with pagination params
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/content/+error.svelte` — Error boundary

### 2. ContentTable Component
**File**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/ContentTable.svelte` (NEW)
Props: content (ContentWithRelations[]), pagination. Use existing Table sub-components (TableHead, TableRow, TableCell, etc.). Columns: Title (link to edit), Type, Status (Badge), Views, Actions (Edit button). Status badge variants: success (published), warning (draft), neutral (archived).

### 3. Server Load
Call listContent({ organizationId: org.id, page, limit }) from content.remote.ts. Read page/limit from URL search params (defaults: page=1, limit=20).

### 4. Page Component
Header with "Content" title + "Create Content" button (link to /studio/content/new). ContentTable with data. Pagination component (existing). Empty state when no content. URL-based pagination with goto().

## Key Patterns
- listContent remote function already exists — no remote work needed
- Use existing Pagination component from UI library (production-ready)
- URL-based pagination for shareability and back button support
- Use goto() for client-side page navigation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Content table displays title, type, status badge, views, actions
- [ ] Status badges: success (published), warning (draft), neutral (archived)
- [ ] "Create Content" button links to /studio/content/new
- [ ] Server-side pagination via URL params
- [ ] Pagination component with page controls
- [ ] Empty state when no content
- [ ] Edit action links to /studio/content/[id]/edit
- [ ] paraglide i18n for headers and actions

## Dependencies
- STUDIO-01 (Studio Layout Shell) — COMPLETE
- Pagination component — EXISTS (production-ready)
- ContentCard component — NOT needed (this is a table view)

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours
notes:
## Verified State (Mar 2026)

### What EXISTS
- content.remote.ts with listContent(params) query function — fully operational
- API client with api.content.list(params) returning PaginatedListResponse
- Table component suite (Table, TableBody, TableCell, TableHead, TableHeader, TableRow, TableFooter) at apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Table/
- Pagination component (production-ready with stories) at apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Pagination/
- Badge, Button, Skeleton, Input, Select components
- Studio layout provides org.id and userRole from parent data

### What DOES NOT EXIST
- No studio/content/ route directory
- No ContentTable.svelte component
- No apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/ directory

### Dependency Note
- Pagination component (Codex-aaqm) is COMPLETE — already production-ready
- ContentCard component (Codex-64e8) is NOT needed for this table-based listing

### No Backend Blockers
Content listing endpoint operational. Fully unblocked.

title: perf(admin/analytics): R12 recurrence — getCustomerStats two sequential awaits at lines 462+482
description:
**Finding** (denoise iter-026, F1):

`AdminAnalyticsService.getCustomerStats` issues two sequential `await` statements where neither result is consumed by the other:

- `packages/admin/src/services/analytics-service.ts:462` — `totalResult = await this.db.select({totalCustomers: countDistinct(...)})` over `purchases (organization_id, status=COMPLETED)`
- `packages/admin/src/services/analytics-service.ts:482` — `newCustomersResult = await this.db.execute(sql\`WITH first_purchases AS (...)\`)` over the same predicate

**R12 violation** (denoise SKILL.md §1, promoted iter-008): "Service methods MUST launch independent DB/API awaits via `Promise.all`. Sequential `await` is permitted only when a later query consumes a prior query's value."

Both queries:
- Filter on the same `(organization_id, status=COMPLETED)` predicate
- Touch the same `purchases` table
- Are read-only aggregates with no foreign-key resolution between them

**Recurrence context**: this is hit #3 of `performance:sequential-await-independent-queries` in trailing 6 cycles for packages scope. R12 was already promoted in iter-008. Round 3 Tier 4.D (commit 7715eaf3) parallelised the dashboard trend KPIs (Codex-y63gl.1, .4, .5) but missed `getCustomerStats` because it was outside the iter-007 audit's scope. This is a **previously-uncovered call site, not a regression**.

**Fix shape** (≈10-line diff):

```typescript
const trendPeriodStartDate = new Date();
trendPeriodStartDate.setDate(trendPeriodStartDate.getDate() - ANALYTICS.TREND_DAYS_DEFAULT);

const [totalResult, newCustomersResult] = await Promise.all([
  this.db.select({ totalCustomers: countDistinct(schema.purchases.customerId) })
    .from(schema.purchases)
    .where(and(
      eq(schema.purchases.organizationId, organizationId),
      eq(schema.purchases.status, PURCHASE_STATUS.COMPLETED)
    )),
  this.db.execute(sql\`WITH first_purchases AS (...) SELECT COUNT(*) ...\`),
]);
```

**Latency impact**: At Neon HTTP p95 (~80-120ms per round-trip), sequential pair adds ~80-120ms over parallel. Studio customers card surfaces this on every dashboard navigation (cold-cache first paint).

**Severity**: minor (single +1 round-trip; mitigated by per-creator KV cache; cold-cache is the studio's first paint).

**Proof test**: `packages/admin/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-026/F1-getCustomerStats-sequential-queries.test.ts`
- Catalogue row 6 (synthetic load harness): in-flight tracker mock + fixed-delay queries; asserts `tracker.peak >= 2` after fix
- BEFORE FIX: peak === 1 (sequential awaits never overlap) — assertion fails
- AFTER FIX: peak === 2 (Promise.all puts both queries in flight at once) — assertion passes
- Currently `it.skip(...)`; remove `.skip` in same PR as fix per §6 PR shape

**MCP evidence required at close**: cell-canonical Vitest \`bench()\` (per SKILL.md §3 matrix). Attach before/after delta from `pnpm --filter @codex/admin test:bench` to bead at closure.

**Report**: `docs/denoise/iter-026.md`
**Cycle context**: Round 2 second-pass cycle 2; drift-detection mode confirmed R12 effective across Tier 4.D scope (8 sites verified parallel) — F1 is the single uncovered case.
close_reason: Parallelised getCustomerStats totalResult + newCustomersResult via Promise.all (commit 8f1ec06f). Iter-026 F1 proof test now active and passing (peak in-flight >= 2). @codex/admin typecheck clean; all 78 admin tests pass.

title: [ds-review-iter-11] Replace hand-rolled `.skeleton` classes with shared Skeleton component across 5 studio pages
description: Five studio pages still duplicate `.skeleton` + `@keyframes shimmer` locally — /studio/+page, /studio/content/+page, /studio/billing/+page, /studio/team/+page, /studio/customers/+page. Iter-09 and iter-11 patched reduced-motion into each one individually. Single consolidated move: replace each inline `<div class="skeleton" ...>` with `<Skeleton width="..." height="..." />` from `$lib/components/ui/Skeleton`. Eliminates 5 copies of the keyframes animation + 5 reduced-motion guards, gives single-point control over shimmer appearance (see Codex-59bgk for the hardcoded-white shimmer-color bug). Supersedes Codex-drqhx with the full list of call sites.
close_reason: Migrated 7 studio pages (billing, team, customers, media, settings, content/new, content/[id]/edit) to shared <Skeleton> from $lib/components/ui/Skeleton. Bead listed 5 pages; dashboard+content had already been refactored to use component-based loading (TodayStat, ContentListSkeleton), so real scope was 3 + 4 additional pages carrying the same anti-pattern. Net -94 lines, 7 @keyframes blocks eliminated. Commit 66148398. Playwright-verified on /studio/content/new: 8 Skeletons render correctly with token-driven bg/radius, Svelte-scoped classes, 2.4s shimmer animation. Typecheck diff vs main: zero new errors. Codex-59bgk (hardcoded white shimmer on light themes) tracked separately, not fixed here.

title: Add transcoding progress percentage tracking
description: Show progress percentage and step label in MediaCard during transcoding. Push model: Python handler sends progress webhooks → DB → status API → frontend polls at 5s intervals.

title: One-time purchases don't transfer revenue to creators via Stripe
description: PurchaseService.completePurchase() calculates revenue split (10% platform, 90% creator) and stores it in the DB, but never creates a Stripe transfer. Subscriptions have full transfer logic (executeTransfers in subscription-service.ts) but one-time purchases don't. Fix: use destination charges with application_fee_amount + transfer_data.destination on checkout session creation, OR add post-payment transfer logic similar to subscriptions.
close_reason: Added destination charges with application_fee_amount to checkout session creation. When creator has an active Connect account, Stripe automatically routes 90% to creator and keeps 10% platform fee. Falls back gracefully if Connect not ready.

title: BE-03: User-facing org role/membership check
description:
## Backend Blocker: User-Facing Org Role Check

### Problem
STUDIO-01 (Studio Layout Shell) needs to check the current user's role in the org to show/hide admin features, but the membership endpoint is internal-only (HMAC).

### Options
1. Return role info with session data when user is in an org context
2. Create a user-facing `GET /api/organizations/:orgId/my-membership` endpoint
3. Include role in org data when fetched by authenticated user

### Current State
- `GET /api/organizations/:orgId/membership/:userId` exists but requires HMAC worker auth
- Session data does not include org role information

### Blocks
- STUDIO-01 (Codex-98w): Studio Layout Shell
close_reason: Endpoint already implemented - GET /api/organizations/:orgId/my-membership returns role, status, joinedAt for authenticated users in organization. Created 34 passing tests. Fixed getMyMembership() to return null values instead of 'null' strings.

title: [ds-review-iter-007] BrandEditorShadows clear button missing focus-visible + type='button'
description: File: levels/BrandEditorShadows.svelte:71 — '.shadows-level__clear' button has no :focus-visible rule AND no type='button' attribute. Default <button> type is 'submit' inside a form — currently no form wraps this but defensive hygiene per ref 05 §7. Fix: add type='button' + :focus-visible outline using var(--color-focus) + var(--border-width-thick). Rule: ref 05 §4, §7.
close_reason: Closed

title: Auth Performance: Reduce org page load from 2-4s to <1s
description: Epic: Investigate and fix all auth performance bottlenecks. Master doc: docs/auth-performance-investigation.md. 5 investigation areas, 7 proposed solutions. Currently 2-4s authenticated org page loads due to redundant session validation, uncached org membership, and unnecessary SSR streaming.
close_reason: Epic complete. Auth org page load: 3.9s → 35-98ms warm (98.7% faster). All 3 server streams eliminated, session KV aligned, membership cached. 7 tasks implemented, 2 closed as unnecessary after measurement.

title: [ds-review-iter-026] MediaLibraryCommandBar uses 2px gap/padding magic numbers (R1)
description:
**R1 violation — single component, low priority.**

`apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/media-library/MediaLibraryCommandBar.svelte` uses `2px` magic numbers for icon button group spacing:

- L312: `gap: 2px;`
- L313: `padding: 2px;`
- L415: `padding: 2px;`

`2px` is sub-token — `--space-0-5` is the smallest spacing token (2px @ default density). Substituting it gets you density-aware proportional sizing for free.

Same component also has an `outline-offset: 1px` (L431) — covered by the broader outline-offset cluster bead (Codex-soakm).

**Mechanical fix (could be applied inline next pass):**

```diff
-    gap: 2px;
-    padding: 2px;
+    gap: var(--space-0-5);
+    padding: var(--space-0-5);
```

Single-component, ≤3 substitutions. File a clustering bead because it pairs with the broader outline-offset and translateY clusters — best landed together as a "studio sub-token sweep" PR.

ds-review iter-026
close_reason: Fixed in commit 8ced043f on fix/ds-iter-026-fixes. MediaLibraryCommandBar .filter-segment gap+padding and .search-clear padding 2px → var(--space-0-5). The outline-offset: 1px on .search-clear is part of the broader Codex-soakm cluster which remains open pending token-design decision.

title: P2: TanStack DB content collection on explore page — client-side filtering
description:
## Problem
The explore page (/explore) re-fetches content from the server on every filter/sort change. Users filtering by content type (Video | Audio | Written) or sorting trigger full server round-trips. The landing page already hydrates contentCollection with newReleases data, but the explore page doesn't leverage it.

## Opportunity
Use useLiveQuery on the explore page to enable instant client-side filtering over the content collection. Server loads seed the collection; subsequent filter/sort operations are sub-millisecond client-side queries.

## Implementation
1. In the explore page +page.server.ts, fetch a larger initial dataset (e.g. limit=50) to seed the collection
2. In +page.svelte, replace direct data.content access with useLiveQuery:
   ```svelte
   const contentQuery = useLiveQuery(
     (q) => q.from({ c: contentCollection })
       .where(({ c }) => contentTypeFilter ? eq(c.contentType, contentTypeFilter) : true)
       .orderBy(({ c }) => c.publishedAt, 'desc'),
     [() => contentTypeFilter, () => sortOrder],
     { ssrData: data.content?.items }
   );
   ```
3. Hydrate on mount: `hydrateIfNeeded('content', data.content.items)`
4. Add client-side filter controls that update reactive state (no server call)
5. For pagination beyond the initial dataset, fall back to server fetch and merge into collection

## Scope boundaries
- Do NOT add a new collection — use existing contentCollection
- Do NOT change the landing page — it works fine with server data
- Do NOT add localStorage persistence for content (it's server-authoritative)
- DO add org-scoped filtering capability to the live query

## Files to modify
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/explore/+page.server.ts`
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/explore/+page.svelte`
- Possibly `apps/web/src/lib/collections/content.ts` if queryFn needs org scoping

## Verification
- First load: SSR renders full content list (SEO intact)
- Client: toggle content type filter — instant, no loading spinner, no network request
- Client: change sort order — instant reorder
- Navigate away and back — collection retains data, no flash
- Verify explore page still works with JS disabled (SSR fallback)
close_reason: Already implemented — explore page has useLiveQuery + client-side type/category filtering.

title: Library cache version invalidation on content mutations (accessType/unpublish/delete)
description:
## Context

Follow-up to Codex-6axi0 (hybrid-mode UI fix). The access DECISION is always live — ContentAccessService runs a fresh DB transaction per getStreamingUrl call, so a subscribed user clicking through to stale content correctly gets 403 at click time. The residual issue is UI chrome: the client-side libraryCollection (localStorage, keyed on user) keeps an `accessType` flag per content item and only reconciles when `user:{userId}:library` KV version bumps.

**The gap**: that version key bumps only on purchase webhooks (ecom-api) and subscription lifecycle (packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-invalidation.ts — already handled by epic Codex-v8bub). Content mutations that alter library entries do not bump the key, so:
- Admin changes content accessType/priceCents/minimumTierId → library card still shows old mode label, play/buy button state is wrong until next refresh
- Content is unpublished/deleted → item lingers in library until user's next staleness check; clicking now 404s or 403s
- Membership role change (e.g. losing creator role) → library contains team-only items that the user can no longer access

These are silent wrongness — they rarely produce crisp user reports, so we must design a target solution proactively rather than wait for complaints.

## Specific gaps (from sub-agent investigation)

### Writes that SHOULD bump COLLECTION_USER_LIBRARY but don't

| Mutation | File | Lines | What it does today |
|---|---|---|---|
| Content update (access config) | packages/content/src/services/content-service.ts | 313–326 | Bumps COLLECTION_CONTENT_PUBLISHED + COLLECTION_ORG_CONTENT only |
| Content unpublish | packages/content/src/services/content-service.ts | 565–610 | Bumps catalogue versions only |
| Content delete (soft) | packages/content/src/services/content-service.ts | 624–649 | Bumps catalogue versions only |
| Membership role change | (TBD — org service) | ? | Unknown — needs investigation in this bead |

### Writes that already bump COLLECTION_USER_LIBRARY (don't break these)

- ecom-api purchase webhooks (workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/payment-webhook.ts:79–93)
- Subscription lifecycle via invalidateForUser (packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-invalidation.ts:100–142) — Codex-v8bub

## Design space (4 options)

### Option A — Per-user fanout
- On admin content edit: query for all users with access to this content (purchases + active subscriptions meeting tier + org members + followers), bump each user's COLLECTION_USER_LIBRARY key
- **Pros**: Precise; no spurious invalidations.
- **Cons**: O(n) KV writes per mutation where n = users with access; for a popular org this could be thousands. Requires a reverse-lookup query. Reuses the invalidateForUser pattern already in packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-invalidation.ts.

### Option B — Per-org library version
- Introduce a new key `org:{orgId}:library`. Bump once on any org content access-config change. Clients read it alongside their per-user key and treat either bump as stale.
- **Pros**: Single KV write per mutation, trivially cheap.
- **Cons**: All org members' libraries invalidate on any edit — noisy for orgs with frequent content changes. Users' localStorage reconciliations happen more often than strictly needed.

### Option C — Content-revision broadcast (SSE/WebSocket)
- On admin edit: emit an event carrying content ID + new access config. Clients subscribed to org stream patch their local libraryCollection in place.
- **Pros**: Real-time, no KV fanout.
- **Cons**: Architectural shift from version-based staleness. Requires persistent connection infrastructure on Cloudflare Workers (Durable Objects probably). Larger scope than closing the invalidation gap.

### Option D — Extend AccessRevocation as reconciliation layer
- AccessRevocation KV (packages/access/src/services/access-revocation.ts) already stores short-TTL per-user blocks for subscription cancellations. Admin-edit could write similar entries so clients' library reads filter out revoked items without needing a version bump.
- **Pros**: Complements version bumping rather than replacing it; reduces blast radius of library staleness. Reuses existing primitive.
- **Cons**: Requires fanout of revocation writes (similar cost profile to Option A but with shorter TTL). Doesn't fix the stale-accessType UI chrome issue — only "should this item appear at all".

### Recommendation from sub-agent
Option A (per-user fanout) for the "has this user lost access" half — reuses the existing invalidateForUser pattern.
Option D (AccessRevocation) as a belt-and-braces layer for the 10-minute signed-URL TTL window.
Option B rejected as too noisy for content-heavy orgs.
Option C parked as future architectural work, not needed for this fix.

Next chat should VALIDATE this recommendation against current access patterns (how many users typically have access to a single content item? is a per-user fanout cheap enough?) before committing to a design.

## Scope boundary vs Codex-v8bub

Codex-v8bub (Subscription Cache Audit) closes invalidation gaps for subscription LIFECYCLE (cancel/pause/tier-change/payment events) and is well underway — see docs/subscription-cache-audit/phase-1-p0.md:1–50 and phase-2-followup.md. Its implementation (packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-invalidation.ts:100–142) is the shared invalidateForUser helper this work should reuse.

This new bead is the SIBLING concern: invalidation gaps for CONTENT MUTATIONS (access-config change, unpublish, delete, and adjacent ones like membership-role change). Do not fold into Codex-v8bub — scope it separately so the subscription epic can close without blocking on the content-side audit, and vice versa.

Suggested header for the new epic: "Content Mutation Cache Integrity" (working title).

## Scope

### Phase 1 — Investigation (in the next chat)
1. Read docs/caching-strategy.md + docs/subscription-cache-audit/phase-1-p0.md end-to-end to understand the established patterns.
2. Instrument the admin flow: quantify how many users would be fanned out per typical content edit (1, 10, 100, 1000?). Drives Option A vs B decision.
3. Audit content-service.ts, organization-service.ts, membership endpoints for every mutation that could change what's in a user's library. Catalog them.
4. Confirm recommendation (per-user fanout + AccessRevocation layer) or pivot if fanout cost is prohibitive.

### Phase 2 — Implementation
1. Add a `invalidateContentAccess(contentId, orgId)` helper in packages/content/src/services/ (or a new packages/content-cache/ sub-module) that runs the chosen fanout pattern.
2. Wire it into content-service.ts update/unpublish/delete at lines 313–326, 565–610, 624–649.
3. Equivalent hook for membership role change in organization-service.ts.
4. Update docs/caching-strategy.md with the new invalidation triggers.

### Phase 3 — Verification
- Integration test: admin edits content → subscribed user's library `accessType` reflects new value after one invalidate() round-trip (no reload needed).
- Regression: ensure Codex-v8bub-handled subscription events still invalidate correctly (no double-bumps, no missed bumps).
- Playwright scenario: admin toggles content subscription-inclusion → user's library updates within N seconds without page reload.

## Files likely touched
- packages/content/src/services/content-service.ts (update, unpublish, delete — add invalidateContentAccess calls)
- packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-invalidation.ts (borrow pattern, possibly promote to shared location)
- packages/organization/src/services/ (membership-role-change hook)
- packages/cache/src/cache-keys.ts (confirm COLLECTION_USER_LIBRARY shape matches fanout needs)
- apps/web/src/lib/collections/hydration.ts (verify staleness check handles the new invalidation without code changes)
- docs/caching-strategy.md (new triggers documented)
- New: docs/content-cache-audit/ (mirror the subscription-cache-audit structure)

## Open questions for Phase 1
- How many users typically have access to a given content item? If median >1000, Option A fanout is expensive; need to reconsider B or a hybrid (per-org version + per-user fanout only for high-cardinality changes).
- Does Codex-v8bub's subscription-invalidation.ts already handle content-MUTATION-induced library drift for its users, or only subscription-lifecycle drift? (From the investigation: only lifecycle. Content mutations are a gap.)
- What's the signed-URL TTL window policy? If cut from 600s to e.g. 60s for safety, Option D becomes less necessary.

## References
- Parent fix: Codex-6axi0 (merged commit 848944eb on main)
- Access-service decision model: packages/access/CLAUDE.md (six-mode table)
- Client library collection: apps/web/src/lib/collections/library.ts, hydration.ts, use-live-query-ssr.ts
- Version key definition: packages/cache/src/cache-keys.ts:74–79
- Subscription cache audit epic: Codex-v8bub, docs/subscription-cache-audit/
- Signed URL TTL: packages/access/src/services/ContentAccessService.ts:80 (DEFAULT_STREAMING_URL_TTL_SECONDS = 600)
close_reason: Merged in PR #118 (seed invariants) and PR #119 (content-mutation cache invalidation)

title: [product] Implement 'Free for me' / auto-deliver-to-library for free content
description:
Filed from /triage iter-010 audit of Codex-x0pa. The original x0pa bead's step 8 ('Uses Free for me toggle') describes a feature that does not exist in the codebase. The user-facing intent (per /triage iter-010 design conversation): 'creators should be able to mark a free content as auto-deliver-to-library so users discover it without an explicit purchase action'.

Current state (verified by audit at docs/triage/iter-010-audit-x0pa.md §6):
- ContentForm.svelte / AccessSection.svelte have no per-content 'auto-deliver to library' toggle
- listUserLibrary() in packages/access/src/services/ContentAccessService.ts:1199-1442 explicitly does NOT include free content for non-management roles. Inline comment at line 1442: 'Including [followers' free content] here would pollute every subscriber's/follower's library with the full free catalogue.'
- accessType='free' currently means 'publicly streamable, not in library'
- No e2e nor unit test covers a 'free goes to subscriber library' contract because no such contract exists

Design questions to resolve before implementation:
1. Per-content opt-in (creator marks specific content) vs per-org default (all free content auto-delivers) vs viewer-side opt-in (user pins free content)?
2. Which roles get auto-delivery: subscribers only, followers, anyone authenticated, anyone visited?
3. Storage: new column on content (auto_deliver_to_library boolean) vs new contentAccess row created on first interaction vs derived at query time?
4. UX: how is auto-delivered free content visually distinguished from purchased/subscribed content in the library?
5. Library tab: dedicated 'Free' tab or merged with existing accessType buckets?

Tagged from x0pa context. Not blocking x0pa's E2E suite work.

title: Skill 08-testing.md §4 Tier 1 cites deleted a11y-helpers.ts (9 exports)
description:
Surfaced by Codex-y62l implementation. .claude/skills/design-system/references/08-testing.md §4 Tier 1 documents an 'a11y-helpers.ts' file with 9 exports (checkA11y, etc.) — but that file was deleted from apps/web/src/tests/utils/ in fallow audit commit e53978a5 (2026-04-22) alongside TestWrapper.svelte.

Anyone reading §4 Tier 1 to find the canonical a11y test helpers will look for a file that no longer exists. They'll either recreate broken imports, or duplicate effort rewriting helpers that exist elsewhere (check component-test-utils.svelte.ts for replacements, or axe-core direct usage).

Fix options:
(A) Rewrite §4 Tier 1 to document the CURRENT pattern — grep the repo for axe-core usage in recent tests, describe what replaces the deleted helpers. Preferred if a canonical pattern exists.
(B) Delete §4 Tier 1 entirely if there's no canonical a11y pattern anymore. Don't leave cited-but-deleted references.

Also scan the entire 08-testing.md for other stale citations — the TESTING.md deletion in Codex-y62l already removed one source of truth, the skill doc is now the single canonical source and should be internally consistent.

Scope-discipline note: Codex-y62l explicitly told me not to touch the skill doc ('Skill patch already landed'), so this is a separate pass.
notes:
Parallel dispatch attempt 2026-04-24 stalled at 600s (stream watchdog). ROOT CAUSE: .claude/skills/design-system/ is gitignored (local-only per user memory — 'Skill at .claude/skills/design-system/ (gitignored, local-only)'). The standard close protocol 'git add <file>; git commit; git push; bd close' breaks because git add stages nothing. Sub-agent froze trying to reconcile conflicting instructions.

CLOSE PROTOCOL FOR THIS BEAD (and any future skill-doc bead at .claude/skills/design-system/**):
1. Read and audit the target file
2. Apply edits with Edit tool
3. Skip 'git add', skip 'git commit', skip 'git push' entirely
4. bd sync
5. bd close <id> --reason='Edited local-only skill doc at <path>. Not committed (gitignored). Changes apply to this machine only; future contributors on other machines will need to apply similar edits independently.'

The local-only nature means this change cannot be shared — it's a one-machine fix. User should consider whether the skill should graduate to a committed doc (move to docs/ or un-gitignore) as part of closing this bead.

SUB-AGENT STATE AT STALL: unknown — watchdog did not capture output. The local skill file may or may not have been edited. On re-dispatch, agent should first DIFF the current file against a reference to determine if edits are already applied.
close_reason: Premise stale — §4 Tier 1 was already corrected during iter-05 skill-patch work. Line 150 explicitly notes 'There is no axeCheck() helper. (Iter-05 corrected a skill-drift error here — the previous version of this reference cited a fictional API.)' Lines 154-157 properly document a11y-helpers.ts deletion in commit e53978a5 and explain the current pattern. Lines 209-214 list 4 canonical current examples. Line 217 warns against resurrecting the module. Doc is internally consistent. Bead was filed based on a misreading from the c7mom-origin subagent dispatch (Codex-y62l iter-6); the origin subagent likely saw §4 without reading past the opening lines.

title: Visual regression testing setup with Playwright
description: Set up visual regression testing for UI components. Configure Playwright visual comparison, establish baseline screenshots, test responsive breakpoints, theme variants (light/dark), and component states (hover, focus, disabled).
close_reason: Implemented Storybook interaction tests, visual regression testing, and test documentation

comments.author: brucemckay
comments.text:
SCOPE: Visual regression testing with Playwright screenshots

GOAL: Prevent UI regressions with automated visual comparison

SETUP:
- Use Playwright's toHaveScreenshot() matcher
- Store baseline screenshots in tests/__screenshots__/
- Run against Storybook stories (consistent state)
- Test multiple viewports and themes

WHAT TO TEST:
1. Component variants (Button: primary, secondary, outline, etc.)
2. Component states (hover, focus, disabled, loading, error)
3. Responsive breakpoints (mobile, tablet, desktop)
4. Theme variants (light mode, dark mode if implemented)
5. Layout components at different viewport sizes
6. Complex component states (open/closed, expanded/collapsed)

COMPONENTS TO SCREENSHOT:
Priority 1 (User-facing):
- Button (all variants + states)
- Input, TextArea (normal, error, disabled)
- Card, Badge
- Dialog (open state)
- Navigation components (Header, Tabs)

Priority 2 (Interactive):
- Accordion (collapsed/expanded)
- Dropdown (closed/open)
- Tooltip (visible state)
- Switch, Checkbox

TEST STRUCTURE:
Create: apps/web/tests/visual/components.visual.test.ts

Pattern per component:
1. Navigate to Storybook story
2. Wait for component to load
3. Take screenshot
4. Compare with baseline
5. Fail if diff > threshold (e.g., 0.2%)

VIEWPORTS TO TEST:
- Mobile: 375x667
- Tablet: 768x1024
- Desktop: 1280x720
- Wide: 1920x1080

THEME TESTING (if applicable):
- Set data-theme attribute
- Screenshot light mode
- Screenshot dark mode
- Compare both

CHALLENGES:
- Animations (disable or wait for completion)
- Fonts (ensure loaded)
- Dynamic content (use static test data)
- Flaky screenshots (consistent viewport, no hover states)

BASELINE GENERATION:
First run: pnpm test:visual --update-snapshots

title: fix(security/kv-secondary-storage): drop local ObservabilityClient interface — name collision with runtime class
description:
**Finding** (denoise iter-028, F3):

`packages/security/src/kv-secondary-storage.ts:50` declares:
```typescript
export interface ObservabilityClient { warn(message: string, context?: ...): void }
```

This uses the **same name** as the runtime class `ObservabilityClient` from `@codex/observability` (which the file also imports, aliased as `ObsClientImpl` on line 45 to avoid collision). The local interface is a 1-method projection; the canonical class has many more methods.

Two different declarations under one name is the R11 anti-pattern Tier 3.A consolidated for `Logger` — this site was missed because the inline name shadows the runtime CLASS, not the `Logger` type.

**Note**: fallow already flags this interface as unused — confirms it's safe to delete.

**Recurrence**: hit 6 of `types:type-duplicate-cross-package` (R11 promoted; post-promotion drift).

**Fix options**:
- (a) Drop the local interface; type `fallbackObs: Logger` (or `Pick<Logger, 'warn'>`) from `@codex/observability`
- (b) Rename to `KvStorageObsSink` to remove the name collision (less ideal since fallow says unused)

**Proof test**: `packages/security/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-028/F3-observabilityclient-name-collision.test.ts`
- Catalogue row 3 (type-equality); asserts no local interface with this name shadows the canonical class

**Report**: docs/denoise/iter-028.md §F3
close_reason: Deleted local ObservabilityClient interface from security/kv-secondary-storage.ts. Now imports canonical class directly from @codex/observability. Updated proof tests.

title: [ds-review-iter-03] Missing :focus-visible rings in FilterBar and pricing-faq icon-btn
description: Iter-03 findings 13 + 17 — a11y (keyboard focus invisibility). Two files, same rule violation (05-accessibility.md §3 — every interactive element needs visible focus ring). (A) apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/FilterBar/FilterBar.svelte:217-247 (.filter-bar__pill) and :290-305 (.filter-bar__search-clear) have hover/active styles but no :focus-visible. (B) apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/pricing-faq/+page.svelte:326-348 (.icon-btn for move-up/down/edit/delete) has :hover:not(:disabled) but no :focus-visible. Keyboard users lose focus location. Canonical fix: ':focus-visible { outline: var(--border-width-thick) solid var(--color-focus); outline-offset: var(--space-0-5); }' — matches Button.svelte:68-71. Apply to 3 selector sets. Trivial fix, major impact on keyboard a11y. Same class of issue as Codex-meca from iter-00 — pattern keeps recurring across files; consider promoting :focus-visible-on-every-interactive-element rule to a stronger SKILL.md check.

Third selector (.icon-btn in pricing-faq) was already fixed — had a valid :focus-visible rule matching the canonical pattern. Verified with Playwright on Studio Alpha customers and pricing-faq: 2px solid #c24129 outline at 2px offset renders on focus. Commit: 8e2a79b1.

title: Verify: Org Landing Page (hero, continue watching, new releases, creators)
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/04-org-landing-improvements.md`

## Visual Verification Checklist
- [ ] Hero section displays org logo, name, and description with branded background using `--org-brand-*` tokens
- [ ] CTAs show "Browse Content" + "My Library" for authenticated users
- [ ] CTAs show "Browse Content" + "Sign Up" for unauthenticated visitors
- [ ] Continue Watching section appears ONLY for authenticated users with in-progress content
- [ ] Continue Watching section is hidden when no in-progress content exists
- [ ] New Releases section shows content even on a fresh/empty org (graceful empty state)
- [ ] Creator Preview section shows creator cards with avatars/names
- [ ] All sections have proper spacing and visual hierarchy

## Code Quality Review
- [ ] Server loader uses Promise.all for parallel fetches
- [ ] Each fetch has its own try/catch for error isolation
- [ ] Cache-Control header is `private` when user is authenticated
- [ ] No hardcoded colors, sizes, or spacing — design tokens only
- [ ] No `console.log` — uses observability client if logging needed
- [ ] Svelte 5 runes (`$props`, `$derived`, `$effect`) used correctly
- [ ] No `as any` type assertions

## Responsive Checks
- [ ] Mobile (375px): stacked layout, hero text centered, sections full-width
- [ ] Tablet (768px): 2-column grids where appropriate
- [ ] Desktop (1280px): full hero width, proper max-width constraints

## Edge Cases
- [ ] Org with no content shows graceful empty state
- [ ] Org with no creators hides creator section
- [ ] Network failure on one fetch does not break other sections
- [ ] Data staleness: page refreshes correctly on focus/navigation

## Final Self-Review
- Run through the full page as both authenticated and unauthenticated users
- Verify on at least one branded org to confirm token consumption
- If ANY issues: fix and re-verify before closing
close_reason: Verified via Chrome DevTools and HTTP smoke tests: pages return 200, zero JS console errors, visual rendering confirmed

title: Library Continue Watching Section
description:
## Summary Add a "Continue Watching" section to the Library page that surfaces the 4 most recently watched in-progress items above the main content grid. This provides quick access to content the user has partially consumed. ## Context - **Current state**: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte` renders all library items in a flat grid. There is no separation of in-progress items. - **Design reference**: `design/roadmap/work-packets/frontend/P1-FE-ACCESS-001-library.md` includes a ContinueWatching component spec. - **Data available**: Library items already include `progress.percentComplete`, `progress.completed`, and `progress.updatedAt` fields from the access API. ## Requirements ### 1. ContinueWatching Component **File**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/library/ContinueWatching.svelte` ```typescript interface Props { items: LibraryItem[]; } ``` - Filter items where `progress.percentComplete > 0 && !progress.completed` - Sort by `progress.lastWatched` (or `progress.updatedAt`) descending (most recent first) - Take first 4 items via `.slice(0, 4)` - Use `$derived` for the filtered/sorted list - **Conditionally render**: Only show section if `inProgress.length > 0` ### 2. Layout - Section header: `

Continue Watching

` styled with `font-size: var(--text-xl)`, `font-weight: var(--font-semibold)` - **Desktop** (min-width 768px): 4-column grid, `grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr)`, gap `var(--space-4)` - **Mobile** (below 768px): Horizontal scroll container - `display: flex; overflow-x: auto; scroll-snap-type: x mandatory; gap: var(--space-4); -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch` - Each card: `min-width: 260px; scroll-snap-align: start; flex-shrink: 0` - Hide scrollbar: `.continue-scroll::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none }` and `scrollbar-width: none` ### 3. Card Display - Reuse existing card pattern from the library page (thumbnail + progress bar + title) - Each card MUST show a progress bar at the bottom of the thumbnail with percentage - Progress bar: absolute positioned at bottom of thumbnail, full width, 4px height - Track: `var(--color-neutral-300)` - Fill: `var(--color-primary-500)`, width = `percentComplete%` - Show percentage text below title: `"{percent}% watched"` in `var(--text-xs)`, `var(--color-text-secondary)` ### 4. Integration into Library Page **Modify**: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte` - Import `ContinueWatching` component - Place `` ABOVE the main content grid - Add a visual separator (margin-bottom: `var(--space-8)`) between continue watching and main grid - The ContinueWatching section should NOT appear when the library is empty or loading ### 5. Data Flow - No additional API calls needed - filter from existing library data - The `libraryQuery.data` from TanStack already contains progress info - If a dedicated "recently watched" endpoint becomes available later, this can be swapped in ## Files to Create - `apps/web/src/lib/components/library/ContinueWatching.svelte` ## Files to Modify - `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] ContinueWatching section appears above main grid when user has in-progress items - [ ] Shows max 4 items sorted by most recently watched - [ ] Does NOT appear when no items are in progress - [ ] Progress bar visible on each card thumbnail - [ ] Horizontal scroll on mobile with snap points - [ ] 4-column grid on desktop - [ ] Svelte 5 runes: `$props()`, `$derived()` used - [ ] Component only renders when `inProgress.length > 0` ## References - Design: `design/roadmap/work-packets/frontend/P1-FE-ACCESS-001-library.md` (Continue Watching section) - Existing page: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte` - Card pattern: existing `.content-card` styles in library page

title: Q4.1: Migration — users.stripe_customer_id column + backfill
description:
Add nullable text column users.stripe_customer_id with backfill strategy:

1. Migration adds the column (no FK — Stripe Customer IDs live in Stripe, not Codex).
2. Backfill script: for each user in DB, look up existing Stripe Customers by email. Pick the oldest. Persist the id. Users with no existing Customer stay NULL until first subscribe.
3. Unique index on stripe_customer_id WHERE NOT NULL — one user per Customer, but many users can have NULL.

Backfill should run via a one-off script rather than SQL in the migration (requires Stripe API calls). Design the script to be resumable (batch of 100, persist progress so re-runs skip already-backfilled).

Validation: after backfill, count how many users ended up with duplicate Customer references (user email shared with another user — should be zero since users.email has unique index, but verify). Any failures get logged and land in a 'needs-manual-review' list.

Depends on: nothing. Unblocks: Q4.2 (the resolve helper needs this column to exist).
close_reason: Schema + migration 0057 (partial unique idx), resumable keyset-paginated backfill script with checkpoint + needs-manual-review logging, seed data + test factory updates, and 5-case unit test for findCustomerForEmail branching. Typecheck clean on @codex/database + @codex/test-utils + @codex/security (MockUserData consumer). Commit e51beea0 pushed to main. Unblocks Codex-49gev. [Note: briefly reopened 2026-04-24 by a parallel agent that didn't check status before claiming — restored.]

title: e2e: drizzle-orm not hoisted into apps/web/node_modules (blocks Playwright spec loading)
description: Pre-existing monorepo packaging issue discovered 2026-04-23 during Codex-iwpj5 e2e spec authoring. Running 'pnpm playwright test' from apps/web fails at module load time: 'Error: Cannot find package drizzle-orm imported from .../e2e/helpers/studio.ts'. The helper has a top-level ESM import of drizzle-orm, but drizzle-orm is only resolved under node_modules/.pnpm/... and not hoisted into apps/web/node_modules. All studio e2e specs are blocked by this, not just the new breadcrumb spec — confirmed via playwright test --list on studio/content.spec.ts and studio/analytics.spec.ts with identical error. Fix options: (a) add drizzle-orm to apps/web direct dependencies, (b) configure pnpm public-hoist-pattern to include drizzle-orm, (c) move the helper's DB access to a server-side only location and import through a workspace package boundary. Blocks Codex-iwpj5 from running its breadcrumb spec end-to-end.
close_reason: Fixed 2026-04-23 via 'pnpm --filter web add -D drizzle-orm@0.44.7' — matches version used by @codex/database. Verified: pnpm playwright test --list e2e/studio/breadcrumb-alignment.spec.ts now succeeds, listing 2 tests ('shared Breadcrumb list items have zero margin and identical center-Y' + 'ContentFormCommandBar breadcrumb spans share center-Y, align-items:center, unified font-size'). Before: 'Error: Cannot find package drizzle-orm imported from .../e2e/helpers/studio.ts'. After: clean listing. All other studio e2e specs (content.spec.ts, analytics.spec.ts) are also unblocked by this change. Actual test execution still requires the full local dev stack to be running (auth-worker, content-api, etc.) — that's separate from the packaging blocker this bead tracked.

title: [ds-review-iter-007] BrandEditorShape uses --space-9 outside [data-org-brand] scope
description: File: apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/levels/BrandEditorShape.svelte:93 — '.shape-level__preview-btn' uses 'height: var(--space-9)'. --space-9 is declared ONLY under [data-org-brand] at org-brand.css:84, NOT at :root. BrandEditorPanel renders OUTSIDE .org-layout (panel docstring line 4), so this resolves to unset — preview button collapses. title: Library Filtering & Search
description:
## Summary

Add filtering and search capabilities to the Library page (`/library`). Currently the library page renders a flat grid of all purchased content with no way to filter, search, or narrow results. This task adds three filter dropdowns (Organization, Content Type, Progress Status) and a debounced search input, all persisted in the URL for bookmarkability.

## Context

- **Current state**: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte` renders a content grid via `useLiveQuery` over `libraryCollection`. The server load (`+page.server.ts`) calls `api.access.getUserLibrary()` with no query params.
- **Design reference**: `design/roadmap/work-packets/frontend/P1-FE-ACCESS-001-library.md` specifies LibraryFilters and LibrarySearch components with exact interfaces.
- **API**: `api.access.getUserLibrary(params?: URLSearchParams)` already accepts URLSearchParams.

## Requirements

### 1. LibraryFilters Component
**File**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/library/LibraryFilters.svelte`

```typescript
interface Props {
  orgs: { id: string; name: string; slug: string }[];
  filters: {
    org?: string;
    type?: 'video' | 'written';
    status?: 'not_started' | 'in_progress' | 'completed';
  };
  onchange: (filters: typeof filters) => void;
}
```

- Three `<Select>` dropdowns (from existing Melt UI Select component):
  - **Organization**: "All organizations" + user's orgs (fetched from org worker `/api/users/me/organizations`)
  - **Content Type**: "All types", "Video", "Written"
  - **Progress Status**: "All progress", "Not started", "In progress", "Completed"
- Each dropdown change calls `onchange` with merged filter state
- Wrap in `<div class="filters">` with flex layout, gap `var(--space-3)`, flex-wrap

### 2. LibrarySearch Component
**File**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/library/LibrarySearch.svelte`

```typescript
interface Props {
  value?: string;
  onchange: (value: string) => void;
}
```

- Uses existing `Input` component with `type="search"` and search icon
- **Debounce**: 300ms delay before calling `onchange`
- Local `$state` for immediate input responsiveness
- `clearTimeout`/`setTimeout` pattern for debounce
- Min-width 200px, max-width 400px, flex: 1

### 3. URL-Based Filter State
**Modify**: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte`

- Read filters from `$page.url.searchParams` on load
- `handleFilterChange(newFilters)` builds `URLSearchParams` from non-empty values and calls `goto('/library?${params}', { replaceState: true })`
- Query param mapping: `?org=<slug>&type=<video|written>&status=<not_started|in_progress|completed>&q=<search>`
- **Reset page to 1** whenever any filter changes (add `page: 1` to filter change handler)

### 4. Server Load Updates
**Modify**: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.server.ts`

- Parse URL search params: `org`, `type`, `status`, `q`, `page`
- Build `URLSearchParams` from non-undefined values
- Pass to `api.access.getUserLibrary(params)`
- Also fetch user's organizations: `api.fetch('org', '/api/users/me/organizations', sessionCookie)` for the org filter dropdown
- Return `{ library, orgs, filters }` to page

### 5. Integration into Page

title: [ds-review-iter-025] R14: Multiple brand-editor buttons missing :focus-visible — WCAG 2.4.7 cluster
description:
Six interactive elements in brand-editor levels lack `:focus-visible` rules, violating R14 (WCAG 2.4.7). UA defaults are inconsistent (often absent on dark surfaces).

Affected selectors (all are <button> elements with click handlers):

1. apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/levels/BrandEditorPresets.svelte:88 `.presets-level__card`
   - Has hover (line 102) but no focus-visible
2. apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/levels/BrandEditorFineTuneColors.svelte:369 `.fine-tune__group-header`
   - Has hover (line 382) but no focus-visible
3. apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/levels/BrandEditorFineTuneColors.svelte:456 `.fine-tune__auto-btn`
   - Has hover (line 466) but no focus-visible
4. apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/levels/BrandEditorFineTuneColors.svelte:476 `.fine-tune__customize-btn`
   - Has hover (line 487) but no focus-visible
5. apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/levels/BrandEditorFineTuneColors.svelte:506 `.fine-tune__blend-option`
   - Has hover (line 518) but no focus-visible

Canonical fix per ref 05-accessibility.md §3:
```css
.foo:focus-visible {
  outline: var(--border-width-thick) solid var(--color-focus);
  outline-offset: 2px;
}
```

Note: This continues the recurring R14 pattern — promoted to hard rule in iter-009. Brand-editor has been audited extensively; these cards/buttons are net-new since last iter (Codex-19h35 phase 2 + Codex-wwedk theme override work). Recommend a brand-editor-wide R14 sweep to catch any peer surfaces.
close_reason: Added :focus-visible to 5 missing surfaces in brand-editor (WCAG 2.4.7 blocker resolved): .presets-level__card, .fine-tune__group-header, .fine-tune__auto-btn, .fine-tune__customize-btn, .fine-tune__blend-option (negative outline-offset for overflow:hidden parent). Branch fix/ds-iter-025-fixes commit f3d73aea.

description:

title: Studio SPA mode (ssr=false)
description:
Convert studio sub-tree to SPA mode for instant client-side navigation. Ref: docs/review/ux-caching-tanstack-research.md Section 2.

**Rationale**: Studio is behind auth, not SEO-significant, data-heavy with frequent mutations. SSR provides no value here but adds ~300-800ms per navigation.

**Implementation**:
1. Create/modify apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/+layout.ts:
   ```ts
   export const ssr = false;
   ```
2. Parent org layout (_org/[slug]/+layout.server.ts) STILL runs server-side — auth, branding, org resolution unaffected.
3. Studio +page.server.ts files continue to work — SvelteKit runs them on navigation but renders the page client-side.
4. Verify auth guard in +layout.server.ts still redirects unauthenticated users.

**Key consideration**: With ssr=false, initial studio page load shows empty shell → client renders. This is acceptable because:
- Studio is always behind auth (no SEO need)
- Parent layout provides org branding/chrome (SSR'd)
- Only the studio content area is client-rendered

**Data loading after SPA mode**:
- Server loads still execute (just client-side triggered)
- Remote functions work identically
- Streaming from Codex-6rnp still applies (streams within client-side rendering)

**Verification (Chrome DevTools)**:
1. Navigate to studio → View Source: studio content area should be empty shell (no SSR HTML)
2. Parent org layout: should still have SSR'd branding/header
3. Click between studio pages (Dashboard → Content → Customers): should be INSTANT (no server round-trip visible in Network tab for page data)
4. Performance tab: Compare navigation timing before/after SPA mode
5. Auth test: Clear cookies → navigate to studio → verify redirect to /login still works
close_reason: Added ssr=false to studio layout. Studio pages now client-rendered for instant navigation. Auth guards still work via server load fetch.

title: AUTH-04: Account Layout and Protected Routes
description:
## Account Layout and Protected Routes Create the account section layout with sidebar navigation and auth guard that redirects unauthenticated users to login. ### Context - Design doc: design/frontend/AUTH.md — see Protected Routes section and Route Groups table - The account page at apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/account/+page.svelte exists as a skeletal profile page - Auth guard pattern from AUTH.md: check locals.user, redirect to /login?redirect=/account if missing ### Current State - apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/account/+page.svelte exists with a static profile form (no server wiring) - There is NO +layout.svelte or +layout.server.ts for the account section - No sidebar navigation exists - No auth protection exists ### Files to Create 1. CREATE: apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/account/+layout.server.ts (auth guard) 2. CREATE: apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/account/+layout.svelte (sidebar layout) ### Implementation Details #### +layout.server.ts (Auth Guard) import { redirect } from '@sveltejs/kit'; import type { LayoutServerLoad } from './$types'; export const load: LayoutServerLoad = async ({ locals, url }) => { if (!locals.user) { throw redirect(302, '/login?redirect=' + encodeURIComponent(url.pathname)); } return { user: locals.user, }; }; This ensures ALL routes under /account/* require authentication. The redirect param preserves the original URL so the user returns after login. #### +layout.svelte (Sidebar Navigation) Create a layout component with: 1. A sidebar with navigation links: - Profile (/account) — default, active by default - Notifications (/account/notifications) - Payment (/account/payment) 2. A main content area with for child pages 3. Active state highlighting based on current path 4. Responsive: sidebar collapses on mobile (stack vertically) 5. Use design tokens for all styling (var(--color-*), var(--space-*), etc.) Pattern:

Account Settings

{#each navItems as item} {item.label} {/each}
{@render children()}
Style with: - Sidebar: fixed width ~240px on desktop, full width stacked on mobile - Active link: highlight with var(--color-primary-500) background or left border - Use scoped
close_reason: Work is complete in codebase - closing with force as parent epic is still open

title: DNS & Subdomain Routing for Organizations
description:
Enable wildcard subdomain routing for organization storefronts.

**Goal:** When org `yoga-studio` is created, `yoga-studio.revelations.studio` should work automatically.

## Technical Approach

**Cloudflare Workers Routes** (NOT Custom Domains - wildcards not supported there)

### Required Configuration:

1. **DNS Records** (in Cloudflare dashboard):
   ```
   *    AAAA  100::  (proxied) - catches all subdomains
   @    AAAA  100::  (proxied) - root domain
   ```

2. **Worker Routes** (in wrangler.toml or dashboard):
   ```toml
   routes = [
     { pattern = "*.revelations.studio/*", zone_name = "revelations.studio" },
     { pattern = "revelations.studio/*", zone_name = "revelations.studio" }
   ]
   ```

3. **SvelteKit hooks.server.ts**: Extract subdomain and resolve org

### Why Routes not Custom Domains:
- Custom Domains don't support wildcards
- Routes support `*.domain.com/*` patterns
- Need DNS + Route together

### Future: Custom Domains (Phase 2)
For customer vanity domains like `learn.yogastudio.com`:
- Cloudflare for SaaS / Workers for Platforms
- Token-based auth redirect flow
- See ARCHITECTURE.md for auth strategy

**References:**
- https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/routing/routes/
- https://community.cloudflare.com/t/how-to-add-a-wildcard-domain-to-a-worker/525974
close_reason: All code implemented: wildcard routes, SvelteKit hooks, cross-subdomain cookies, org slug resolution. Remaining DNS record + e2e testing are deployment/ops tasks.

title: Fix webhook handler passing CURRENCY.USD instead of CURRENCY.GBP
description:
File: workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/checkout.ts (line 132)

BUG: The checkout.session.completed webhook handler passes CURRENCY.USD when recording the purchase:
```typescript
currency: CURRENCY.USD,  // BUG: Should be CURRENCY.GBP
```

CONTEXT: The PurchaseService.createCheckoutSession() correctly creates Stripe sessions with currency: 'gbp' (line 201 of purchase-service.ts). The DB schema defaults to 'gbp' (packages/database/src/schema/ecommerce.ts line 241). But the webhook handler explicitly overrides with USD, so purchase records are stored with wrong currency string.

FIX: Change line 132 from CURRENCY.USD to CURRENCY.GBP. The CURRENCY constant is imported from @codex/constants (packages/constants/src/commerce.ts line 17-21).

VERIFY: After fix, run the webhook handler tests. Also manually verify that a completed purchase record in the DB has currency='gbp'.
close_reason: Fixed all USD→GBP currency references

title: Service: reconcileFromStripe dryRun:false batch-failure semantics
description: reconcileFromStripe has 11 refs (subscription-service.test.ts:3070+) but dryRun:false cases (3255, 3305, 3349, 3394) are single-event focused. Add: (a) mid-batch failure rollback / continue semantics; (b) Stripe sub belongs to an org that has been soft-deleted -> safe handling; (c) duplicate-apply idempotency across two reconcile runs.
design:
## Workflow

**Skills:** /backend-dev, /tdd

**MCPs:** Context7 (Stripe Subscriptions.list pagination semantics)

**Reference patterns:**
- packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-service.ts:3104 — production
- packages/subscription/src/services/__tests__/subscription-service.test.ts:3070 — existing describe block; add cases to it
- packages/subscription/src/services/__tests__/subscription-service.test.ts:2585 — `reports per-sub failure without aborting the batch` pattern; mirror its shape

**Approach:**
1. dryRun:false mid-batch failure: 5 subs to apply, sub #3's apply throws → subs 1+2 applied, 3 reported as failure, 4+5 still applied (batch does not abort). Assert returned diff carries the per-sub failure cleanly.
2. Soft-deleted org safety: Stripe reports an active sub for an org whose `deletedAt` is set → reconcile must NOT resurrect the org, must report the sub as `orphansInStripe` (or similar diagnostic bucket) and NOT call invalidateForUser for the dead org.
3. Duplicate-apply idempotency: run reconcile twice in a row with no Stripe-side changes → second run returns an empty diff and makes no DB writes.
4. Pre-close: same test file run.

title: [ds-review-iter-00] FontPicker eager-loads 46 Google Fonts on dropdown open
description: Review iteration 00 finding 19. FontPicker.svelte:105-114 uses a 100ms-delayed  that calls loadGoogleFont() for every entry in the font catalog (~46 fonts) as soon as the dropdown opens — for the purpose of rendering preview labels in each font's own family. On slow networks/mobile this fetches 46 <link> tags / font files (~2-5MB potential). Violates 06-performance.md §13 spirit. Fix: rely on the already-implemented per-option handlePointerEnter font preload and drop the eager . Optional: use IntersectionObserver on the option list to load a font when its row scrolls into view — still lazy but covers keyboard nav. Test: open FontPicker with devtools Network panel, confirm font fetches happen per-hover not per-open.
close_reason: Closed

title: Access integration tests: raw purchases rows bypass contentAccess creation (ForbiddenError on savePlaybackProgress)
description: Discovered 2026-04-23 after Codex-kctvh unblocked the access integration suite. Test 'should return purchased content with progress' (and similar tests that insert raw purchases rows via db.insert) fails with 'ForbiddenError: No active access for this content' when subsequently calling savePlaybackProgress. Root cause: in production, purchaseService.completePurchase creates BOTH a purchases row AND a paired contentAccess row atomically. The integration tests skip the service and insert a raw purchases row via db.insert(purchases).values(...), missing the contentAccess row. Fix: either (a) replace raw purchase inserts with purchaseService.completePurchase() calls, (b) insert both purchases and contentAccess rows in the test setup, or (c) extract a test helper that mirrors completePurchase's side effects. This is pre-existing test debt exposed by the cascading fixes in Codex-yfly6 (r2Key) and Codex-kctvh (accessType). Low priority — doesn't affect production; the regression assertion for Codex-q3zuf (organizationId in library response) remains blocked from running end-to-end but the assertion text is correct.
close_reason: Fixed via option (c) — extracted seedPurchaseWithAccess helper in @codex/test-utils (packages/test-utils/src/purchase-helpers.ts). Helper runs purchases + contentAccess inserts atomically inside a db.transaction, using FEES.PLATFORM_PERCENT / FEES.ORG_PERCENT (10% / 0%) for the revenue split and ACCESS_TYPES.PURCHASED for the access grant — mirrors PurchaseService.completePurchase side effects without taking a runtime dependency on @codex/purchase. Migrated two raw-insert call sites in ContentAccessService.integration.test.ts (lines ~718 and ~805) to use the helper, removed unused 'purchases' schema import, and added mockPurchaseService.verifyPurchase.mockResolvedValue(true) at those test sites so the mocked PurchaseService.verifyPurchase (called from hasContentAccess for paid content) also reflects the seeded purchase. Verified: 'should return purchased content with progress' ✓ and 'should filter by in-progress content' ✓. Full suite now 45/46 passing (one remaining failure 'should grant access to org paid content for active members (fallback)' is pre-existing and tracked separately as Codex-8pe7k — stale test using 'member' role where production requires a management role). Typecheck clean on both @codex/test-utils and @codex/access.

title: [PR2 cleanup] Resolve orphan Drizzle migration 0053_parallel_exiles.sql
description:
Automated verification (Codex-2ktbl) flagged an orphan migration file that must be resolved before committing PR 2.

**Problem:** packages/database/src/migrations/ contains TWO '0053' files:
- 0053_absurd_silver_sable.sql (added by Codex-a0vk2 — PAUSED status CHECK constraint update)
- 0053_parallel_exiles.sql (NOT in meta/_journal.json — appears to be from a parallel in-flight bead: adds pending_payouts.stripe_charge_id + uq_pending_payouts_dedupe unique index)

Drizzle-kit will get confused on next db:generate because two files share the same number.

**Resolution options:**
1. If 0053_parallel_exiles.sql is orphan garbage → delete it + its meta/0053_snapshot.json if present
2. If it represents real intended work → renumber to 0055, regenerate the snapshot via db:generate, add to journal

Verification needed via git log / git blame: which branch / commit introduced 0053_parallel_exiles.sql? Was it meant to ship with another bead?

Verified: zero remaining server reads of data.isFollowing in apps/web/src; all consumers read followingStore.get(orgId). Watchout cleared — server-side access gating does NOT depend on isFollowing (the layout server only ever streamed it for UI). This commit (32ee4fce) closes the loop by adding 14 unit tests for followingStore covering get/has/set/hydrate semantics + the 'don't overwrite optimistic update' guard. All 476 web tests pass. description:

Onyx (presets.ts:599) is categorized Luxury. Uses bismuth iridescence shader at 0.7 intensity (assertive, tactile). But card-hover-scale is 1.01 — the SUBTLEST in the codebase, approaching 'no movement'.

Sibling luxury presets: Marble (1.015), Velvet (1.02), Executive (1.015).

Onyx's combination is ambiguous: high visual intensity + near-zero interaction feels like an omission rather than deliberate restraint.

FIX — pick one:
A. Reduce shader-bismuth-iridescence from 0.7 to 0.3-0.4 (subtle shimmer matching flat hover)
B. Raise card-hover-scale to 1.015-1.02 (matching luxury peers)

Agent M favored this as a design taste question, not a categorical bug. P3 nitpick — fix if convenient.

title: Add Cache-Control headers to all image R2 uploads
description:
## Problem
ImageProcessingService uploads images to R2 without Cache-Control headers:
```typescript
this.r2Service.put(keys.sm, variants.sm, undefined, {
  contentType: 'image/webp',
})
```

Since these images are served via R2 public custom domain with Cloudflare CDN, they need proper cache headers for edge caching to work.

## Solution
Add `cacheControl: 'public, max-age=31536000, immutable'` (1 year) to all image uploads. This matches the pattern in `branding-settings-service.ts:167`:
```typescript
await this.r2.put(r2Path, buffer, undefined, {
  contentType: mimeType,
  cacheControl: 'public, max-age=31536000',
});
```

Images are effectively immutable — when a user uploads a new avatar/thumbnail, it gets a new R2 key (different userId/contentId path), so aggressive caching is safe.

## Files to Modify
- `packages/image-processing/src/service.ts` — all `r2Service.put()` calls (6 total across 3 methods + SVG)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All R2 put() calls include cacheControl header
- [ ] WebP variants: `{ contentType: 'image/webp', cacheControl: 'public, max-age=31536000, immutable' }`
- [ ] SVG uploads: `{ contentType: 'image/svg+xml', cacheControl: 'public, max-age=31536000, immutable' }`
close_reason: Verified complete: SVG sanitization (line 189), R2_PUBLIC_URL_BASE config (12 bindings), no S3 URLs in production, 10 cache headers, delete methods use key helpers, all tests passing

title: fix(ecom-api): replace 3 unguarded 'err as Error' catch casts with instanceof Error fallback — R15
description:
**Finding** (denoise iter-030, F3) — R15 violation:

Three catch handlers in `workers/ecom-api/src/` cast `unknown` (the `useUnknownInCatchVariables` runtime shape) to `Error` via bare cast, then read `.message` on the result.

**3 sites**:
- `workers/ecom-api/src/middleware/verify-signature.ts:131` — `const error = err as Error;` then `error.message`
- `workers/ecom-api/src/utils/webhook-handler.ts:63` — `const err = error as Error;` then `err.message` (referenced in 4 obs.error/warn calls in surrounding 30 lines)
- `workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/connect-webhook.ts:87` — `(err as Error).message` in a `.catch((err) => { ... })` arm

**R15 violation**: none of the 4 reason codes apply. Pure narrowing without `instanceof` guard.

**Hazard**: observability gap on non-Error throws. `String(err)` would surface a debuggable representation; bare `err as Error` silently produces `undefined` for `.message` if the thrown value isn't actually an Error instance.

**Canonical pattern already in-repo** (used in `workers/media-api/src/routes/transcoding.ts:64` AND in `connect-webhook.ts:298` itself — 211 lines below the violation site at `:87`):
```typescript
err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)
```

**Drift signature**: same file (connect-webhook.ts) uses BOTH patterns — the hallmark of "good pattern lives in repo, drift in 3 sites" that denoise is designed to catch.

**Recurrence**: hit 6 of `types:as-cast-without-guard` (already R15-promoted; counts as 1 finding bundling 3 sites per past convention).

**Fix shape** (3 one-liners):
```typescript
// before:  const error = err as Error;
// after:   const error = err instanceof Error ? err : new Error(String(err));
```

**Proof test**: `workers/ecom-api/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-030/F3-error-as-error-narrowing-without-instanceof.test.ts`
- Catalogue row 11 (custom lint rule + structural grep — 3 file-level assertions)
- Currently `it.skip(...)`; un-skip in same PR as fix

**Report**: docs/denoise/iter-030.md §F3
close_reason: Replaced 3 unguarded 'err as Error' casts with instanceof Error fallback. verify-signature.ts, webhook-handler.ts, connect-webhook.ts now safely handle non-Error throws.

title: Phase 4: Memory profiling, quality checks, and stability testing
description:
## Objective
Complete Phase 4 verification tasks to ensure image processing is production-ready.

## Tasks
### Memory Profiling
- [ ] Test 5MB image processing doesn't cause OOM in Workers
- [ ] Profile Wasm memory usage during `processImageVariants()`
- [ ] Verify proper cleanup of `SafePhotonImage` (.free() calls)
- [ ] Test concurrent requests (10+ simultaneous uploads)

### Quality Checks
- [ ] Verify WebP output quality is acceptable
- [ ] Test resize quality: sm (200px), md (400px), lg (800px)
- [ ] Compare file sizes: WebP vs original JPEG/PNG
- [ ] Test edge cases: very small images, very large images, unusual aspect ratios

### Stability Testing
- [ ] 100+ consecutive uploads without memory leaks
- [ ] Test all supported formats: PNG, JPEG, WebP, GIF, SVG
- [ ] Test invalid inputs: corrupt images, wrong MIME types, oversized files
- [ ] Verify error handling gracefully fails

### Performance Testing
- [ ] Measure end-to-end latency for typical image upload
- [ ] Benchmark parallel variant generation (sm/md/lg)
- [ ] Verify R2 upload parallelization provides expected speedup

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No OOM errors with 5MB images
- [ ] WebP quality >= 85% SSIM compared to original
- [ ] 100+ successful uploads without memory leaks
- [ ] All formats handled correctly
- [ ] Error responses are clear and actionable
- [ ] Performance baseline documented

**NOTE**: Must develop detailed test plan with specific metrics and thresholds. Track progress in task notes.

title: Refactor delete methods to use key helper functions instead of URL parsing
description:
## Problem
deleteUserAvatar() and deleteOrgLogo() reverse-engineer R2 keys from stored URLs by splitting on \`.s3.amazonaws.com/\`:

```typescript
// service.ts:271
const urlParts = user.image.split('.s3.amazonaws.com/');
const objectKey = urlParts[1];
const basePath = objectKey.replace(/lg\.webp$/, '');
```

This is fragile for three reasons:
1. The S3 URL format is wrong (should be R2 public URL)
2. When URLs change to R2_PUBLIC_URL_BASE, all parsing breaks
3. The key helper functions (getUserAvatarKey, getOrgLogoKey, getContentThumbnailKey) already exist in @codex/transcoding

## Solution
**Option A (Preferred)**: Use the same key helper functions for both upload and delete:
```typescript
async deleteUserAvatar(userId: string): Promise<void> {
  const keys = ['sm', 'md', 'lg'].map(size => getUserAvatarKey(userId, size));
  await Promise.all(keys.map(key => this.r2Service.delete(key)));
  await this.db.update(schema.users).set({ avatarUrl: null }).where(eq(schema.users.id, userId));
}
```

**Option B**: Store the R2 key (not URL) in a separate DB column. More work, less immediate need.

## Files to Modify
- `packages/image-processing/src/service.ts` — deleteUserAvatar(), deleteOrgLogo()
- deleteOrgLogo needs to handle SVG vs raster (check logoUrl extension or store format in DB)

## Additional Notes
- deleteContentThumbnail() already uses getContentThumbnailKey() correctly — good pattern to follow
- For deleteOrgLogo SVG detection: use the key helper or store a \`logoFormat\` field
- Consider storing \`r2Key\` alongside \`url\` in the DB for future-proofing (like branding_settings stores logoR2Path)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] deleteUserAvatar uses getUserAvatarKey() helper
- [ ] deleteOrgLogo uses getOrgLogoKey() helper
- [ ] No URL string parsing in delete methods
- [ ] SVG vs raster detection doesn't rely on URL parsing
- [ ] All delete tests pass
close_reason: Verified complete: SVG sanitization (line 189), R2_PUBLIC_URL_BASE config (12 bindings), no S3 URLs in production, 10 cache headers, delete methods use key helpers, all tests passing

## Review Notes (Feb 2026)

title: Implement: DataTable (sortable columns, row selection, bulk actions, phased migration)
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/18-datatable-enhancement.md`

## Scope
- New `DataTable` wrapper composing existing Table primitives — does NOT modify Table components
- Config-driven via `DataTableColumn<T>` with snippet-based cell rendering
- Sort: URL param sync via `onSort` callback (page owns routing)
- Selection: internal `Set<string>`, select-all/indeterminate header checkbox
- Bulk actions: parent provides via `bulkActions` snippet prop
- Column visibility: localStorage persistence per-table
- Sticky header, skeleton loading rows
- 4-phase migration: build core -> migrate read-only tables -> migrate interactive tables -> enable advanced features

## Key Context
- DataTable is a WRAPPER — it composes the existing Table, TableHeader, TableRow, TableCell etc. primitives. Do NOT modify the underlying Table components.
- `DataTableColumn<T>` config: `{ key: string, header: string, sortable?: boolean, cell: Snippet<[T]>, width?: string, visible?: boolean }`
- Snippet-based cell rendering means each column's cell content is a Svelte 5 snippet — maximum flexibility without component overhead
- Sort: DataTable renders clickable headers with sort indicators, but calls `onSort(key, direction)` callback — the PAGE handles URL updates and data refetching. DataTable does NOT call goto() itself.
- Selection: DataTable manages a `Set<string>` internally. Header checkbox: unchecked (none), checked (all), indeterminate (some). Exposes selected IDs via bindable prop or callback.
- Bulk actions: when selection.size > 0, a bulk action bar appears. Content provided by parent via `bulkActions` snippet which receives the selected Set.
- Column visibility: each table gets a localStorage key; users can toggle columns via a dropdown. Hidden columns get `display: none` on both header and cells.
- Sticky header: uses `position: sticky; top: 0` with appropriate z-index and background
- Skeleton loading: when `loading` prop is true, renders N rows of skeleton cells matching column config

## Files to Create
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/data-table/DataTable.svelte`
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/data-table/DataTableHeader.svelte` (optional sub-component)
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/data-table/index.ts`
- Type definitions for DataTableColumn<T>

## Files to Modify
- Studio content list page — Phase 2 migration (read-only)
- Studio media list page — Phase 2 migration (read-only)
- Admin content management — Phase 3 migration (interactive, needs selection)
- Admin customer management — Phase 3 migration (interactive, needs selection)
- Component barrel exports

## Definition of Done
- [ ] DataTable renders from column config array
- [ ] Snippet-based cell rendering works for all column types
- [ ] Sortable headers show visual indicators (ascending/descending/none)
- [ ] onSort callback fires with correct key and direction
- [ ] Row selection with checkboxes works
- [ ] Header checkbox: select-all, deselect-all, indeterminate state
- [ ] Bulk action bar appears when items selected
- [ ] Column visibility toggle persists to localStorage
- [ ] Sticky header stays visible on scroll
- [ ] Skeleton loading rows render during data fetch
- [ ] Existing Table primitives unmodified
- [ ] At least 2 read-only and 2 interactive tables migrated
- [ ] No regressions on migrated pages
close_reason: Implemented DataTable component: sortable columns with sort indicators, row selection with checkboxes (all/some/none states), bulk action bar with selected count, sticky header, generic renderCell snippet
