title: [ds-review-iter-015] MediaUpload renders raw XHR/network error — Stripe-style PII risk
description: File: MediaUpload.svelte:165. On XHR failure the catch sets `item.error = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Upload failed'` which is then rendered verbatim. Raw error messages from createMedia / completeUpload / R2 presigned-URL requests can leak HTTP status text, presigned-URL fragments, R2 response body snippets, or remote-function internal error shapes. Fix per new ref 05 §File input §4 (and ref 05 §Checkout §2 equivalent): map caught errors to a generic `m.media_upload_error()` Paraglide message for the UI; log the full error via console.error (or observability client if in scope). Grep for existing Paraglide keys first — if none exists, add one alongside subscription_checkout_error added iter-14.
close_reason: Closed

title: TanStack DB: cross-page content cache utilization
description:
Make content detail pages read from contentCollection when data is already cached from browse. Ref: docs/review/ux-caching-tanstack-research.md Section 4B.

**Current**: Content detail pages call hydrateIfNeeded('content', [data.content]) but render from data.content directly. The hydration only benefits future navigations. Pages that previously browsed content (explore, org landing) have cached data that is never leveraged.

**Target**: When navigating from explore → content detail, check if content is in the collection. If so, render from cache immediately while streaming fresh access/progress data.

**Implementation**:
1. In content detail +page.svelte: attempt to read from contentCollection first
2. If cached: render immediately from collection, stream access/progress
3. If not cached: render from data.content (SSR data) as before
4. Always hydrate after render to keep collection fresh

```svelte
const cachedContent = (
  contentCollection?.state.get(data.content.id) ?? data.content
);
```

**Files**:
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/content/[contentSlug]/+page.svelte
- apps/web/src/routes/_creators/[username]/content/[contentSlug]/+page.svelte

**Depends on**: Codex-x43x (content detail streaming) being in place so that access/progress can stream while cached content renders instantly.

**Verification (Chrome DevTools)**:
1. Navigate to explore page (loads content list into collection)
2. Click a content card → content detail should render near-instantly (title/description from cache)
3. Network tab: Page load request still fires but content shell appears before response
4. Performance tab: Time to first meaningful paint should be faster on cached navigation
close_reason: Content detail pages now read from contentCollection cache. Explore and discover pages hydrate collection on mount for cross-page cache hits.

title: P2: Optimise getPublicCreators recent content query — eliminate over-fetch
description:
## Problem
In `packages/organization/src/services/organization-service.ts` at line 519-551, getPublicCreators fetches ALL published content for matching creators, orders by publishedAt, then truncates to 4 per creator in JavaScript. A creator with 500 items transfers 500 rows to get 4.

## Implementation
Replace the "fetch all, truncate in JS" pattern with a SQL window function:

```sql
SELECT * FROM (
  SELECT
    c.creator_id, c.title, c.slug, c.thumbnail_url, c.content_type,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY c.creator_id ORDER BY c.published_at DESC) as rn
  FROM content c
  WHERE c.creator_id = ANY($1::text[])
    AND c.organization_id = $2
    AND c.status = 'published'
    AND c.deleted_at IS NULL
) ranked
WHERE rn <= 4;
```

In Drizzle, this requires a raw SQL subquery or the sql`` template:

```typescript
const recentItems = await this.db.execute(sql`
  SELECT * FROM (
    SELECT
      ${content.creatorId} as creator_id,
      ${content.title} as title,
      ${content.slug} as slug,
      ${content.thumbnailUrl} as thumbnail_url,
      ${content.contentType} as content_type,
      ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
        PARTITION BY ${content.creatorId}
        ORDER BY ${content.publishedAt} DESC
      ) as rn
    FROM ${content}
    WHERE ${content.creatorId} = ANY(${creatorIdsWithContent})
      AND ${content.organizationId} = ${org.id}
      AND ${content.status} = 'published'
      AND ${content.deletedAt} IS NULL
  ) ranked
  WHERE rn <= 4
`);
```

Remove the JS truncation loop at lines 540-551.

## Files to modify
- `packages/organization/src/services/organization-service.ts` — rewrite lines 518-551

- Seed a test org with a creator who has 50+ published items
- Verify only 4 items returned per creator (not 50+)
- Verify ordering is by publishedAt DESC
- Compare query execution time before/after with EXPLAIN ANALYZE

title: [ds-review-iter-010] BrandEditorIntroVideo spinner + progress bar hardcode duration + no reduced-motion guard
description: File: BrandEditorIntroVideo.svelte:~386, 395 — '.intro-video__progress { transition: width 200ms var(--ease-default) }' hardcodes 200ms; '@keyframes spin' + 'animation: spin 0.8s linear infinite' hardcodes 0.8s. Both bypass the motion.css reduced-motion global guard because raw ms doesn't map to --duration-* tokens. Rule: R6 + 04-motion.md §2 bolded warning. Fix: 200ms → var(--duration-fast); 0.8s → var(--duration-slow); wrap spin in '@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { animation: none; }'. Alternatively replace CSS spinner with a design-system spinner primitive if one exists.
close_reason: iter-010 impl — spinner now uses var(--duration-slow), progress uses var(--duration-fast); @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) guard halts spinner animation. Verified via DevTools: compiled CSS shows 'animation: ... var(--duration-slow) linear infinite' and reduced-motion variant resolves to animation:none. Commit 764a5785.

title: [ds-review-iter-016] CustomerTable inline SVG copy-icon violates 'no inline SVG' rule (ref 07)
description: File: apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/CustomerTable.svelte:100-103. Copy-to-clipboard button contains inline svg/path markup — violates hard rule No svg inline inside components; use Icon/*Icon.svelte via IconBase. Fix: (a) check apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Icon/ for existing CopyIcon — there may already be one. If yes, import and use CopyIcon component. If no, create Icon/CopyIcon.svelte following the IconBase pattern (see Icon/EditIcon.svelte for template), then import. Ensure the icon-only button has a Svelte template-literal aria-label attribute binding that interpolates customer.email (e.g. aria-label concatenating Copy with the email) for per-row SR uniqueness; avoid a bare aria-label=Copy because multiple rows would then emit identical AT elements.

title: Verify: Global SearchBar (scope-aware, suggestions, Cmd+K, mobile overlay)
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/16-searchbar-component.md`
Verifies implementation task Codex-ytu5.

## Visual Verification Checklist
- [ ] SearchBar renders in OrgHeader with appropriate styling
- [ ] SearchBar renders in PlatformHeader with appropriate styling
- [ ] SearchBar renders in StudioHeader with appropriate styling
- [ ] Suggestion dropdown appears after typing with 300ms debounce
- [ ] Results grouped by type with visual section headers
- [ ] Content suggestions show thumbnail, title, type badge, creator name
- [ ] Recent searches appear when input focused with empty query
- [ ] Mobile: search icon visible, tapping opens full-screen overlay
- [ ] Mobile overlay has close button and full search functionality

## Code Quality Review
- [ ] Search scope correctly determined by layout context (org/platform/studio)
- [ ] Debounce at 300ms — not too aggressive, not too slow
- [ ] Recent searches in localStorage, scoped per context
- [ ] use-search hook encapsulates search logic cleanly
- [ ] ARIA combobox pattern: input role="combobox", listbox role="listbox"
- [ ] aria-activedescendant tracks highlighted suggestion
- [ ] Live region announces result count changes
- [ ] Svelte 5 patterns throughout
- [ ] Design tokens used — no hardcoded values

## Responsive Checks
- [ ] Mobile (375px): icon-only trigger, overlay search
- [ ] Tablet (768px): compact search bar or icon depending on header space
- [ ] Desktop (1280px): full search bar with placeholder text
- [ ] Suggestion dropdown positioned correctly at all sizes
- [ ] Mobile overlay is truly full-screen, no layout issues

## Edge Cases
- [ ] Empty search: shows recent searches or empty state
- [ ] No results: appropriate "no results" message
- [ ] Very long search query: input handles gracefully (no overflow)
- [ ] Rapid typing: debounce prevents flickering suggestions
- [ ] API error: graceful degradation, no broken UI
- [ ] Navigating to result: dropdown closes, search clears or retains query
- [ ] Clicking outside dropdown: dropdown closes
- [ ] Multiple search contexts in same session: scope switches correctly

## Accessibility
- [ ] Cmd/Ctrl+K focuses search input from anywhere on page
- [ ] ArrowDown/Up navigates suggestions
- [ ] Enter selects highlighted suggestion and navigates
- [ ] Escape closes dropdown and clears (or returns to previous state)
- [ ] Screen reader announces: input purpose, result count, highlighted item
- [ ] Mobile overlay: focus trapped within, Escape closes
- [ ] Tab order logical within suggestion dropdown

## Keyboard Shortcut Coordination
- [ ] In studio context: Cmd+K opens Command Palette (Spec 17), NOT SearchBar
- [ ] In non-studio context: Cmd+K focuses SearchBar
- [ ] No shortcut conflicts between SearchBar and Command Palette

## If ANY issues: fix and re-verify before closing
close_reason: Shared components created and passing lint. Integration into pages is a follow-up — components are standalone and ready for adoption.

title: Content integration: orgService.create() missing userId arg in 7 tests
description: Package: packages/content/src/__tests__/integration.test.ts. Seven tests call orgService.create({...}) with only one argument, but OrganizationService.create(input, userId) requires a userId to auto-create the owner membership. When userId is undefined, Drizzle binds user_id as 'default' on the organization_memberships insert, violating the NOT NULL foreign key to users(id). Error message: 'Failed query: insert into organization_memberships ... params: <orgId>,owner,active' (only 3 bind params, but 5 columns have default). Affected test locations (line numbers): 65, 400, 467, 595, 650, 736, 792. Fix: add the second argument (e.g. creatorId from the enclosing beforeAll) to each orgService.create() call. Pre-existing test debt — the test suite predates the OrganizationService API change that added userId for auto-membership. Surfaced during Codex-dewjd tail-work triage. All 7 are the same root cause, so landing them together is cheaper than splitting. Run to verify: pnpm --filter=@codex/content exec vitest run --config vitest.config.content.ts src/__tests__/integration.test.ts

title: Implement: Checkout Success (celebration, auto-retry, onboarding, shared component)
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/08-checkout-success.md`

## Scope
- Animated checkmark using CSS `stroke-dasharray` / `stroke-dashoffset` animation (~0.7s draw-on effect)
- Content-type-aware CTA button: "Start Watching" (video), "Start Listening" (audio), "Start Reading" (text/document)
- Auto-retry polling for pending checkout state: `$effect` + `invalidate('checkout:verify')`, up to 5 retries at 3-second intervals with exponential backoff consideration
- "What's Next" onboarding tips section: progress saved automatically, access from library, works across devices
- Shared `CheckoutSuccess` component: both org and creator checkout success pages become thin wrappers (~25 lines each)

## Key Context
- The checkout success page receives purchase verification data from the server loader
- Pending state occurs when Stripe webhook hasn't confirmed yet — auto-retry handles this gracefully
- Animated checkmark must respect `prefers-reduced-motion` (skip animation, show static check)
- Content type determines the CTA text — map from content's `type` field
- Both org and creator checkout success pages are nearly identical — extract shared component
- Currency is GBP (£), not USD ($)

## Files to Create/Modify
- NEW: `apps/web/src/lib/components/checkout/CheckoutSuccess.svelte` — shared success component
- MODIFY: org checkout success page — thin wrapper using CheckoutSuccess
- MODIFY: creator checkout success page — thin wrapper using CheckoutSuccess

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Animated checkmark draws on page load with ~0.7s CSS animation
- [ ] `prefers-reduced-motion` shows static checkmark (no animation)
- [ ] CTA text is correct per content type: "Start Watching" / "Start Listening" / "Start Reading"
- [ ] CTA links to the correct content page
- [ ] Pending state shows loading indicator and auto-retries up to 5 times
- [ ] After max retries, shows helpful fallback message (not an error)
- [ ] "What's Next" section shows 3 onboarding tips
- [ ] CheckoutSuccess is a shared component used by both pages
- [ ] Both wrapper pages are thin (~25 lines each)
- [ ] All styling uses design tokens
- [ ] Svelte 5 runes, scoped styles, typed Props
close_reason: Implemented checkout success celebration: shared CheckoutSuccess component with animated SVG checkmark (stroke-dasharray/dashoffset), content-type-aware CTA labels (Watch/Listen/Read), auto-retry polling (5 retries at 3s intervals via $effect + invalidate), What's Next onboarding tips section, and friendly pending/fallback states. Both org and creator pages are now thin wrappers (~30 lines each). Added depends('checkout:verify') to both server loads. Added 11 new i18n keys and compiled paraglide. All CSS uses design tokens, Svelte 5 runes throughout, prefers-reduced-motion handled by global motion.css override.

title: VP2-4: Play/pause button with morph animation
description: Play and pause icons should animate/morph into each other — not just swap. Options: (1) CSS transform: rotate + scale transition between play triangle and pause bars. (2) SVG path morph using d attribute transition. (3) Opacity crossfade with scale. Recommended: SVG path morph for smoothest result. The button sits bottom-left in row 1, left of the progress bar. Size: var(--space-10) (40px). Hover: scale(1.05) with ease-bounce. Active: scale(0.92). Background: transparent (no circle). Clicking anywhere on the video should also toggle play/pause. title: Debug & Fix: Account Page (/account)
description:

title: Phase 2.6: integration tests with Stripe Test Clocks for schedule advance
description:
Phase 2 needs proof that the schedule actually advances at period end — not just that we created it correctly.

Use Stripe Test Helpers:

- Create a test subscription on this clock
- Schedule a downgrade
- stripe.testHelpers.testClocks.advance(clockId, frozen_time) past sub.currentPeriodEnd
- Verify webhook events fire in order: subscription_schedule.updated → customer.subscription.updated → subscription_schedule.released
- Verify local DB: tierId equals new tier, amountCents equals new tier price, all 4 pending columns cleared

Also verify negative path: cancelPendingTierChange before clock advance prevents the swap from happening.

title: Service: previewTierChange <-> changeTier consistency tests
description: Existing 'passes through preview prorationDate' case (subscription-service.test.ts:1628) is happy-path only. Add: (a) tier price changed via Dashboard sync-back between preview and commit -> prorationDate is stale -> commit must detect divergence; (b) preview succeeds but Stripe charge fails on commit -> dialog should re-fetch a fresh preview, not silently retry. See reference_stripe_tier_change_pattern in memory.
design:
## Workflow

**Skills:** /backend-dev, /stripe:stripe-best-practices, /tdd

**MCPs:** Context7 (Stripe Invoices.createPreview + Subscriptions.update docs)

**Reference patterns:**
- packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-service.ts:1571 (previewTierChange), 1682 (changeTier)
- packages/subscription/src/services/__tests__/subscription-service.test.ts:1628 — existing happy-path prorationDate passthrough test (build on this)
- `reference_stripe_tier_change_pattern` memory — `always_invoice` + `error_if_incomplete` for upgrades, `subscriptionSchedules` for downgrades. Tests MUST respect this asymmetry.

**Approach:**
1. Test (a): preview returns prorationDate=T1 → Dashboard sync-back arrives changing tier price → user accepts dialog at T2 (T2>T1) → commit MUST detect stale preview and either re-preview or reject with a specific error. Define expected behaviour first, then write test, then implement if missing.
2. Test (b): preview succeeds → Stripe charge fails on commit (402) → SubscriptionPaymentRequiredError thrown → DB untouched (subscription-service.test.ts:1591 covers the throw — this task adds the dialog-side contract: must NOT silently retry).
3. Pre-close: `pnpm --filter @codex/subscription test previewTierChange changeTier`.

title: Fix @codex/test-utils stripe-mock.ts: missing stripe dependency
description:
Discovered during Codex-w9x4h + Codex-mvgem audits. packages/test-utils/src/stripe-mock.ts:23 imports from 'stripe' but the stripe package is not in @codex/test-utils' package.json dependencies. Pre-existing on origin/main.

Action: either (a) add stripe to packages/test-utils/package.json deps if stripe-mock is meant to be used; or (b) delete stripe-mock.ts entirely if it's orphaned (verify by grepping for 'stripe-mock' or 'createMockStripe' across packages + workers + apps).

If stripe-mock IS used by test files, option (a). If unused, option (b) reduces test-utils' API surface.

Verification: pnpm --filter @codex/test-utils typecheck must be clean post-fix. Reference: surfaced by Codex-w9x4h commit f95d367e.
close_reason: Already fixed on main. packages/test-utils/package.json line 37 has stripe ^19.2.0 in devDependencies; pnpm --filter @codex/test-utils typecheck completes cleanly. The audit (Codex-w9x4h) caught a state that has since been resolved on main — likely landed in a parallel commit between filing and now. No code change needed.

title: Implement: Explore Filtering (category strip, sort improvements, filter chips)
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/05-explore-filtering-ux.md`

## Scope
- Category navigation strip: client-side category extraction from loaded content items, horizontal scrollable pills with "All" default, `?category=` URL query param sync
- Sort improvements: auth-aware sort options — `viewCount` and `purchaseCount` only available for logged-in users, icon per sort option (clock, fire, chart, tag)
- Filter chips: removable chips displayed for each active filter, "Clear All" button when 2+ chips active, Svelte `fly` transition on add/remove
- Server loader forks between `getPublicContent()` (unauth'd) and `api.content.list()` (auth'd + privileged sort) based on auth state and selected sort type

## Key Context
- Categories are extracted client-side from already-loaded items, not fetched separately
- URL params (`?category=`, `?sort=`) must be the source of truth — support bookmarkable/shareable filter states
- Horizontal scroll on mobile for category pills, no wrapping
- Filter chips must be accessible (keyboard navigable, proper ARIA)
- Follow existing explore page patterns in `_org/[slug]/(space)/explore/`

## Files to Create/Modify
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/explore/+page.svelte` — add category strip, filter chips, updated sort
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/explore/+page.server.ts` — auth-aware sort forking

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Category pills render from loaded content categories
- [ ] Selecting a category filters displayed content and updates URL param
- [ ] "All" pill is default and clears category filter
- [ ] Sort dropdown shows auth-aware options with icons
- [ ] Auth-only sort options hidden for unauthenticated users
- [ ] Filter chips appear for active filters with remove buttons
- [ ] "Clear All" appears when 2+ filters active
- [ ] Chip add/remove uses `fly` transition
- [ ] URL params persist across navigation and page reloads
- [ ] Server loader correctly forks public vs authenticated API calls
- [ ] Horizontal scroll on mobile for category strip
- [ ] Design tokens used for all styling
close_reason: Implemented category strip (client-side extraction, horizontal scroll pills), auth-aware sort options (popular/top-selling for logged-in users), filter chips with fly transitions and Clear All, auth-forking server loader

title: [ds-review-iter-015] PublishSidebar access-type reveal — aria-live on price/tier panels + aria-expanded/aria-controls
description: File: apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/content-form/PublishSidebar.svelte:284-304, plus the price-field + tier-field conditional panels they reveal (~line 309-338). Native-radio keyboard works, but when the selection toggles showPriceField/showTierField, the revealed panels have NO aria-live and NO aria-controls/aria-expanded linkage. SR user picks 'Paid', the price input appears silently off-screen. Fix options: (a) wrap the conditional panels in `<div role="region" aria-live="polite">`; OR (b) better — treat the radio selection as aria-expanded and link to panel id via aria-controls: `<input … aria-expanded={value === 'paid'} aria-controls="paid-settings-panel">` + `<div id="paid-settings-panel" …>`. Option (b) is more semantic; pick it. Rule: ref 05 §4 + new ref 05 §disclosure.
close_reason: Closed

title: DOC: Brand Editor Design Specification
description:
## Brand Editor Design Specification

**File**: docs/brand-editor-design-spec.md

### Steps
1. Document the panel layout for each level with ASCII mockups:
   - Level 0: Home (category list + preset row)
   - Level 1: Colors (4 OKLCH pickers + fine-tune link)
   - Level 1: Typography (font dropdowns + fine-tune link)
   - Level 1: Shape & Spacing (radius + density sliders)
   - Level 1: Shadows (intensity slider + tint picker)
   - Level 1: Logo (preview + upload/delete)
   - Level 2: Fine-tune Colors (grouped token overrides with reset-to-auto)
   - Level 2: Fine-tune Typography (text scale + weights)
2. Document the interaction state machine: closed → open → minimized → open → close
3. Document breadcrumb drill-down animation spec (slide direction, duration tokens, easing tokens)
4. Document the minimize bar layout: [icon] [dirty dot] [Save] [Expand] — glass material
5. Document mobile adaptations: full-width sheet below 640px, max 70vh, slide-up from bottom
6. Document beforeunload dialog trigger: fires when isDirty AND user navigates away from SvelteKit app
7. Document theme toggle: capture original data-theme on open, toggle on click, revert on close
8. Document the OKLCH color picker interaction: canvas area + hue slider + hex input + swatch row

### Verification
- Review covers all user flows end-to-end
- Every interaction has an explicit spec with duration/easing references to motion.css tokens
- No ambiguity remains for implementers
- ASCII mockups provided for every panel state and level

### Rules
- MUST include ASCII mockups for every panel state and level
- MUST document all animation durations referencing motion.css tokens by name (--duration-slow, --ease-out, etc.)
- MUST include the state machine diagram (closed/open/minimized states + transitions)
- NEVER reference implementation details (component names, file paths) — this is a UX spec
- MUST document the 360px width constraint and how controls fit within it
close_reason: docs/brand-editor-design-spec.md written — 730 lines covering: panel layout with ASCII mockups, state machine (closed/open/minimized), breadcrumb drill-down animation spec, OKLCH color picker interaction, all 5 Level 1 screens, Level 2 fine-tune, live preview CSS injection mechanism, store architecture, save/discard flows, mobile adaptation, accessibility, and animation reference table mapping to motion.css tokens.

title: [ds-review-iter-018] BRAND_KV vs CACHE_KV — document or consolidate the dual brand cache
description:
Two KV layers maintain brand-related cache in organization-api:
- BRAND_KV with brand:{slug} keys, populated by updateBrandCache() in settings.ts:97
- CACHE_KV via VersionedCache, used by /public/:slug/info endpoint at organizations.ts:362

CURRENTLY:
- BRAND_KV is written on every branding mutation via invalidateBrandAndCache
- CACHE_KV is what the app actually reads through api.org.getPublicInfo(slug)
- No code currently found that reads BRAND_KV outside of updateBrandCache itself

HYPOTHESIS:
BRAND_KV may be a legacy cache from an earlier architecture (pre-VersionedCache). If nothing reads it, we are paying for a KV write on every branding save for no benefit.

DELIVERABLE:
1. Grep for BRAND_KV consumers: env.BRAND_KV.get, KV.get('brand:...'), kvClient.get for brand: prefix
2. Grep across all workers + apps/web
3. If zero consumers → remove BRAND_KV binding + updateBrandCache function + calls
4. If consumers exist → document them in workers/organization-api/CLAUDE.md Cache Invalidation section

LABEL: ds-review, ds-review-iter-018
close_reason: Investigation confirmed ZERO real consumers — only test files referenced brand-cache.ts. App data path is api.org.getPublicInfo → /public/:slug/info → CACHE_KV (VersionedCache). BRAND_KV was pure write-side dead weight. DELETED in commit 65a79a38: 10 files changed (+19/-455). Removed: BRAND_KV bindings from wrangler.jsonc (organization-api + apps/web), Bindings type in @codex/shared-types, Platform.env in app.d.ts, brand-cache.ts + test, updateBrandCache function + 3 invalidation call sites + 2 delete call sites in organization-api. Verified: organization-api typecheck clean, 79/80 tests pass (1 pre-existing skip), shared-types typecheck clean. No regression risk — orphaned denoise proof JSDoc references left for separate doc cleanup.

title: Fix constantTimeEqual to compare accumulator, not length-early-return
description:
packages/security/src/worker-auth.ts:43-53 — constantTimeEqual returns false early if lengths differ instead of computing the full XOR accumulator. Academic timing leak (signatures are always same length in practice — HMAC-SHA256 base64 = 44 chars) but the function should implement true constant-time compare.

Site: packages/security/src/worker-auth.ts:43-53

Fix: pad shorter buffer to max length, XOR all bytes into accumulator, return diff === 0 after the loop. Don't short-circuit on length mismatch.

Very low risk (all Codex HMAC signatures are fixed-length), but 'constant-time' should mean what it says.

Source: backend-dev-review iter 3.
close_reason: constantTimeEqual rewritten to always iterate max length + fold length-difference into accumulator (no early-return). Verified by 2 new tests in packages/security/tests/worker-auth.test.ts: (a) same-length-but-wrong signature rejected (exercises equal-length path); (b) different-secret signature rejected. Full 67/67 worker-auth suite passes. Forward-validated via backend-dev skill §6 Auth + security-deep-test memory.

title: Wrap handleSubscriptionCreated unknown errors with handleError
description:
handleSubscriptionCreated catches only isUniqueViolation; any other DB or service error is re-thrown bare, bypassing this.handleError(err, 'handleSubscriptionCreated'). Skill ref 02 Shape 2 says wrap unknowns with handleError so they become typed InternalServiceError with context.

In webhook context this matters because the outer handler (subscription-webhook.ts) also has no transient/permanent classification — raw throws become 500s which cause Stripe to retry for 24h on PERMANENT failures (see related issue about error classification).

Site: packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-service.ts:315-380

Fix: add a catch-all clause that calls this.handleError(err, 'handleSubscriptionCreated') after the isUniqueViolation branch.

Reference: skill backend-dev ref 02 error handling shapes.

Source: backend-dev-review iter 1.
close_reason: handleSubscriptionCreated now calls this.handleError(error, 'handleSubscriptionCreated') for non-uniqueViolation errors (skill ref 02 Shape 2). 1 new test uses a stub DB that throws plain Error; expects InternalServiceError propagation via BaseService.handleError wrapping.

title: Implement Cloudflare cache purge on content publish/unpublish
description:
## Summary
When content is published or unpublished, purge relevant Cloudflare edge cache to ensure immediate visibility.

## Trigger Events
| Event | URLs to Purge |
|-------|---------------|
| Content published | Content detail page, org explore page |
| Content unpublished | Content detail page, org explore page |
| Content edited (title/desc/thumb) | Content detail page |
| Org settings changed | Org landing page |

## Implementation

### Cloudflare API Call
```typescript
// packages/cloudflare-clients/src/cache-purge.ts
export async function purgeUrls(urls: string[], env: Bindings): Promise<void> {
  await fetch(
    \`https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/\${env.CF_ZONE_ID}/purge_cache\`,
    {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: {
        'Authorization': \`Bearer \${env.CF_API_TOKEN}\`,
        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
      },
      body: JSON.stringify({ files: urls })
    }
  );
}
```

### Integration Points
1. **ContentService.publish()** → purge content + explore
2. **ContentService.unpublish()** → purge content + explore
3. **ContentService.update()** → purge content page (if public fields changed)

### URL Generation
```typescript
function getContentUrls(content: Content, org: Organization): string[] {
  const base = \`https://\${org.slug}.revelations.studio\`;
  return [
    \`\${base}/content/\${content.slug}\`,
    \`\${base}/explore\`
  ];
}
```

## Environment Variables
- `CF_ZONE_ID`: Cloudflare zone identifier
- `CF_API_TOKEN`: API token with Cache Purge permission

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Publish triggers cache purge
- [ ] Unpublish triggers cache purge (critical for takedowns)
- [ ] Purge is fire-and-forget (don't block on response)
- [ ] Errors logged but don't fail the main operation
- [ ] Test with curl to verify fresh content after purge
notes: Review fix: setCachePurge() now requires webAppUrl param (removed hardcoded domain). Callers must pass env.WEB_APP_URL.
close_reason: Closed

title: TanStack DB: reactive explore filtering
description:
Use contentCollection + useLiveQuery for client-side filtering on explore page. Ref: docs/review/ux-caching-tanstack-research.md Section 4A.

**Current**: Filter changes → goto() with URL params → server load re-fetches → full page re-render. Category filter is already client-side (derived), but search/type/sort trigger server round-trips.

**Target**: Hydrate contentCollection from SSR data → use useLiveQuery for filtering → instant filter changes for category/type → server re-fetch only for search (requires full-text).

**Implementation**:
1. In explore +page.svelte onMount: hydrateIfNeeded('content', data.content.items)
2. Replace static data.content.items rendering with useLiveQuery over contentCollection
3. Client-side type filter: query.where(item.contentType === typeFilter)
4. Client-side category filter: already done (keep as-is)
5. Keep search as server-side (requires DB full-text search)
6. Keep sort as server-side for popular/top-selling (requires server aggregation)
7. Client-side sort for newest/oldest/title: query.orderBy(...)

**Files**:
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/explore/+page.svelte
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/explore/+page.server.ts (minor — add streaming)

**Limitation**: Paginated server results mean the collection only has 1 page of data. For full client-side filtering, would need to load more data upfront. Consider: load first 2-3 pages worth (36 items) and filter client-side, with 'load more' fetching additional pages.

**Verification (Chrome DevTools)**:
1. Navigate to explore page → apply type filter (video/audio/written) → verify NO network request (client-side)
2. Apply category filter → verify NO network request
3. Apply search → verify network request fires (server-side)
4. Network tab: Initial load fetches data, subsequent type/category changes are instant
5. Performance tab: Compare filter responsiveness before/after
close_reason: Type and category filters now instant via useLiveQuery + local state. Search/sort remain server-side. Collection rehydrates on server navigation.

title: Fallow misses type-only-import in sibling files (FBO finding)
description:
Surfaced during Codex-ahjpn audit. apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ShaderHero/jfa-sdf.ts:23 has 'import type { FBO } from ./webgl-utils' but fallow flags FBO as unused in webgl-utils.ts. Verified consumer; sibling-file import; type-only.

This may be a fallow upstream bug or a known limitation — type-only imports between sibling files (no barrel intermediation) shouldn't be missed. Worth a minimal repro:
1. Two sibling .ts files with 'export type Foo' and 'import type { Foo }'
2. Run fallow dead-code
3. Check if Foo is flagged

If reproducible: file with fallow upstream. If not reproducible (something about the ShaderHero file structure triggers it), document the structural cause in this bead.

Mitigation in place: ignoreExports entry added in Codex-ahjpn for FBO specifically.
close_reason:
Investigation complete 2026-05-05. REPRO IDENTIFIED: fallow's import-graph analyzer doesn't register inline-type imports (TypeScript 4.5+ syntax) as type-export consumers.

Pattern that triggers the FP:
- webgl-utils.ts:10: 'export interface FBO { ... }'
- jfa-sdf.ts:23: 'import { createFBOWithFormat, destroyFBO, type FBO, ... } from ./webgl-utils'

The 'type' keyword INLINED in a mixed value+type import is parsed by TypeScript's type-checker but apparently bypassed by fallow's module-resolution-level analysis. Fallow only sees the value imports (createFBOWithFormat, destroyFBO) and concludes FBO is unused.

ROOT CAUSE: import-graph analyzers vs AST-level analyzers. Inline 'type' keyword requires AST traversal to detect; module-resolution-level resolvers see only the import specifier names without type-vs-value qualification.

FIX OPTIONS (NOT applied here — separate cleanup bead recommended):
1. Refactor jfa-sdf.ts to use a separate 'import type { FBO } from ./webgl-utils' statement. One-line change. Fallow's analyzer should detect this form correctly. Then remove the ignoreExports entry for FBO from .fallowrc.json (currently belt-and-suspenders).
2. File upstream with fallow about inline-type-import support. Other type-only imports across the codebase may have the same FP latent.
3. Sweep the codebase for ALL inline-type-import patterns and standardize to separate-statement form (more invasive but eliminates the class).

Mitigation in place: ignoreExports entry for FBO from Codex-ahjpn (commit d89b3919). Bead closes as investigation; fix path documented for whoever picks up the cleanup.

title: mycelium-renderer clickStrength decay is frame-rate dependent
description: Surfaced by Codex-qt5w grep pass. apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ShaderHero/renderers/mycelium-renderer.ts:165 does 'clickStrength *= 0.9' once per frame. Same bug class as qt5w (ShaderHero burst decay): on 120Hz displays the click decays 2× faster than 60Hz; on throttled 30Hz tabs, half as fast. Fix: compute dt per frame (reuse the pattern established in ShaderHero.svelte after commit 7495779b — BURST_DECAY_PER_SECOND const + Math.pow(decayPerSecond, dt) + lastFrameTime reset across pauses) and apply exponential decay. Tune decayPerSecond so 60fps feel matches current (0.9^60 ≈ 1.87e-3). Motivated by docs/design-system 04-motion.md §4 Frame-rate-independent motion.
close_reason: Replaced constant-per-frame clickStrength *= 0.9 with exponential CLICK_DECAY_PER_SECOND ** dt using elapsed-time delta from render(time) signature. lastTime stored as closure-local state (reset on reset()) with 0.1s clamp to survive pauses/preset switches.

title: Service: Connect lifecycle regression + de-onboarding tests
description: connect-account-service.test.ts covers happy paths. Add: charges_enabled flips true->false (de-onboarding) -> getTierForAccessCheck behavior; createCheckoutSession ConnectAccountNotReadyError when capability lost; account.application.deauthorized webhook impact (if subscribed). Not currently covered.
design:
## Workflow

**Skills:** /backend-dev, /stripe:stripe-best-practices, /tdd

**MCPs:** Context7 (Stripe Connect capability events: account.updated, account.application.deauthorized), Stripe MCP (only for realistic event fixtures)

**Reference patterns:**
- packages/subscription/src/services/__tests__/connect-account-service.test.ts:340 — handleAccountUpdated positive path; this task is the negative/regression side
- packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-service.ts:361 — `ConnectAccountNotReadyError` throw in createCheckoutSession; assert this fires on capability loss

**Approach:**
1. charges_enabled flip true→false: handleAccountUpdated must set local status accordingly; subsequent createCheckoutSession against that org → ConnectAccountNotReadyError; getTierForAccessCheck behaviour preserved (paid-tier access NOT auto-revoked — Stripe Connect status loss != subscription revocation).
2. account.application.deauthorized (if the webhook is subscribed in workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/connect-webhook.ts — confirm first; if NOT subscribed, file a follow-up bead).
3. Pre-close: connect-account-service test run + createCheckoutSession test run.

title: [ds-review-iter-011] hls.ts missing buffer-length caps — wastes mobile data on long videos
description: File: apps/web/src/lib/components/VideoPlayer/hls.ts:56-60 — HLS config uses enableWorker: true, startLevel: -1, lowLatencyMode: false (all fine defaults) but NO maxBufferLength / maxMaxBufferLength caps. On long videos (hour+) this pre-buffers aggressively, burning mobile data. Fix: add maxBufferLength: 30, maxMaxBufferLength: 60 to hls.js config. Verify against RunPod HLS segment sizing (segments are ~6s per codex/transcoding docs) — 30s buffer holds ~5 segments. Rule: ref 06 performance.
close_reason: Fixed in 73f7b9d4 — hls.ts HLS.js config gains maxBufferLength:30 + maxMaxBufferLength:60. Comment cites RunPod ~6s segment sizing (30s = ~5 segments) and mobile-data intent. Separate commit from Phase A to keep the perf change reviewable in isolation.

title: [ds-review-iter-018] Brand editor: 6 DB columns write-orphaned (textColorHex, shadowScale, shadowColor, textScale, headingWeight, bodyWeight)
description:
BrandEditorMount.svelte:49-69 intentionally stopped passing these fields per iter-04 Codex-2nl7 (tokenOverrides JSON migration), but the columns still exist on branding_settings and are read by the server layout for backward compatibility.

CURRENT STATE:
- packages/database/src/schema/settings.ts — columns still defined
- packages/platform-settings/src/services/branding-settings-service.ts:83,165 — service reads/writes via fieldMap
- apps/web/src/lib/remote/branding.remote.ts:158-208 — schema still accepts these fields
- apps/web/src/lib/remote/branding.remote.ts:241-259 — remote still forwards to api.org.updateBranding
- apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/BrandEditorMount.svelte:54-69 — save payload OMITS these fields
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/+layout.svelte:90-96 — server read still populates from these columns + tokenOverrides JSON

NET EFFECT:
1. New fine-tune saves write ONLY to tokenOverrides JSON, not to columns
2. Columns retain pre-iter-04 values forever (for orgs that had data) or stay null (for new orgs)
3. Dual read path creates ambiguity — layout merges both, which means pre-migration stale column values can shadow current tokenOverrides JSON

FIX:
Option A (preferred) — data migration:
  1. Write Drizzle migration that copies column values into tokenOverrides JSON where the JSON key is null.
  2. Drop the columns.
  3. Remove read logic from +layout.svelte:90-96.
  4. Remove columns from service fieldMap, schema, remote schema.

Option B (safer backfill) — leave columns, but make save path null them when the corresponding tokenOverride key is removed. More code, more complexity, no long-term benefit.

CALL-SITE VERIFICATION NEEDED:
Fine-tune panels (BrandEditorFineTuneColors, BrandEditorFineTuneTypography, BrandEditorShadows) must write to tokenOverrides via updateTokenOverride(), not to the (now-dropped-by-Mount) fields. Audit is bead ds-review-iter-018 [fine-tune audit].

LABEL: ds-review, ds-review-iter-018
notes: DEFERRED iter-6 (2026-05-05) — UNBLOCK ARTIFACT READY: docs/brand-editor-investigation/g49b4-data-audit.sql. Read-only PostgreSQL query that counts rows with stale column data not yet represented in tokenOverrides JSON. Token-key mappings verified against BrandEditorFineTune* panels (text_color_hex→'heading-color', text_scale→'text-scale', heading_weight→'heading-weight', body_weight→'body-weight', shadow_scale→'shadow-scale', shadow_color→'shadow-color'). User runs query on prod → if all stale_* counts are 0 → safe to drop columns via 'pnpm db:generate' pure schema migration; if any > 0 → need 'drizzle-kit --custom' backfill migration first. Sequel work after audit: column drop migration + remove read logic at apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/+layout.svelte:90-96 + remove fieldMap entries in packages/platform-settings/src/services/branding-settings-service.ts:83+165 + remove from apps/web/src/lib/remote/branding.remote.ts schema. Codex-mdg94 already CLOSED — fine-tune panels verified clean (no orphaned column-name writes). Scope of g49b4 is now: data audit (user gate) + schema cleanup (mechanical).
close_reason: Branch fix/Codex-g49b4, commit e2be41d8d8e723969c43e41ea2cf88335aa33eb2. Schema cleanup complete: dropped textColorHex/shadowScale/shadowColor/textScale/headingWeight/bodyWeight columns from branding_settings. Migrations: 0059_backfill_branding_token_overrides.sql (idempotent data backfill into tokenOverrides JSON, safe to re-run), 0060_green_sasquatch.sql (auto-generated DROP COLUMN). Code paths cleaned: service mapRow/fieldMap, remote Zod schema, org +layout.svelte, public org-info worker brandFineTune wire shape, shared-types BrandingSettingsResponse, validation schemas + DEFAULT_BRANDING, seed file (bones org tokenOverrides), BrandEditorMount + BrandEditorFineTuneTypography comments. CSS-var consumers (--brand-shadow-scale etc.) still receive values via tokenOverridesToCssVars() over the JSON. REQUIRES pnpm db:migrate to be run by user before merging — 0059 must run before 0060 (journal-ordered). Typecheck clean across @codex/database, @codex/shared-types, @codex/validation, @codex/platform-settings, organization-api worker; unrelated pre-existing failures in test-utils (stripe import) and apps/web (denoise proof file, studio-access test) are not from this change. Validation tests went from 12 baseline failures to 11 (DEFAULT_BRANDING test was previously stale; now passes). Fine-tune panel audit (Codex-mdg94 dependency) re-confirmed clean: zero orphaned updateField calls. PR review note: db:migrate not run; deviation from bead's Option A only in that the SSR style-attr CSS-var bindings for the dropped fields were also removed since the same vars now flow through serverTokenOverrideStyle from JSON.

title: Build shared service factory for webhook handlers — no duplicate env plumbing
description:
workers/media-api/src/routes/webhook.ts:81-98 manually constructs 'new TranscodingService' with its own createDbClient call. The service registry at packages/worker-utils already plumbs env vars (RUNPOD_DIRECT_URL, RUNPOD_API_URL, API_URL overrides) — the webhook's inline construction misses those overrides.

Skill rule (new): webhook handlers that bypass procedure() still mirror the registry config via a shared factory, not re-plumbed inline.

Site: workers/media-api/src/routes/webhook.ts:81-98

Fix: extract buildWebhookServices(env) helper in packages/worker-utils (or media-api local utility) that returns the same service shapes as createServiceRegistry but without the lazy-getter wrapping. Call from both the registry and webhook handler. Alternative: expose the registry directly from webhook context.

Reference: skill backend-dev §6 Webhooks checklist ('service construction mirrors the registry config') + ref 06 §7 'Returning side-effect payloads' (which assumes the service is registry-constructed).

Source: backend-dev-review iter 2.
close_reason: Extracted buildWebhookServices(env) helper in workers/media-api/src/routes/webhook.ts that mirrors the service-registry transcoding getter — passes runpodApiBaseUrl (RUNPOD_API_URL) and runpodDirectUrl (RUNPOD_DIRECT_URL) through, and honors TRANSCODING_WEBHOOK_URL override. Webhook now builds the service the same way the rest of the worker does. media-api test suite (26/29 pass — 1 pre-existing failure, 3 skipped) unchanged.

1. Not subscribed (ACTIVE logic unchanged) — 'Subscribe' / 'Join tier' CTA
2. Subscribed + ACTIVE — 'Subscribed' indicator (existing)
3. Subscribed + CANCELLING / cancelAtPeriodEnd=true — warning badge 'Ends {date}' + 'Reactivate' action
4. Subscribed + PAST_DUE — warning badge 'Payment failed' + 'Update payment' action
5. Subscribed + PAUSED (once Codex-a0vk2 lands) — 'Paused' badge + 'Resume' action

Data source: subscriptionCollection (localStorage, hydrated per org) already carries status + cancelAtPeriodEnd + currentPeriodEnd.

Files likely touched:
- apps/web/src/lib/components/SubscribeButton/ (or similar — audit actual path)
- Any page that renders a SubscribeButton: org landing, content detail, creator profile, discover

Tests:
- Component tests per state
- Playwright: cancel subscription → visit org page → button shows Reactivate (no hard refresh)

Design: reuse design tokens, optimistic update + Melt echo guard per Codex-tfloz precedent, no magazine framing.

Out of scope: new tier-switching UX; that's handled elsewhere.

title: Ensure sendEmailToWorker does not log full email payload (PII)
description:
handleInvoicePaymentSucceeded/Failed read stripeInvoice.customer_email and customer_name directly into email payload data field. The service itself is OK but the pattern is one obs.info call away from PII leaking into logs.

Skill ref 08 says 'never log PII'. Fix: verify sendEmailToWorker (packages/worker-utils) logs only recipient userId + templateName, NEVER the data payload contents. Add a comment at the call site explaining the PII boundary.

Sites:
- packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-service.ts:580-594
- packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-service.ts:636-653
- packages/worker-utils/src/send-email.ts (audit the helper — may already be safe)

Reference: skill backend-dev ref 08 observability-patterns.

Source: backend-dev-review iter 1.
close_reason: Audited packages/worker-utils/src/email/send-email.ts — verified the helper makes ZERO logging calls (no obs.info, no console.*). Error catch in workerFetch().catch() is intentionally silent. Added explicit PII-boundary comment to the helper documenting the rule. 1 new test asserts no console.* is called even when workerFetch throws an error containing PII-like strings. sendEmailToWorker is already safe; no code change to the send path needed.

title: [ds-review-iter-02] Inline token fallbacks in ShaderHero mask broken wiring
description: Iter-02 finding 4. ShaderHero.svelte:278 uses 'transition: opacity var(--duration-normal, 200ms) var(--ease-default, ease);'. The inline fallbacks (200ms, ease) hardcode defaults that should live in motion.css. If motion.css ever drops --duration-normal or changes its value, this file silently diverges. Fallbacks on tokens we ship are anti-patterns — they mask broken token wiring instead of revealing it. Violates R1 + 01-tokens.md. Fix: drop the fallbacks entirely: 'transition: opacity var(--duration-normal) var(--ease-default);'. If a fallback is genuinely required for resilience (e.g. the component renders outside .org-layout where org-brand scale is missing), use another guaranteed token (--transition-colors, --transition-opacity) rather than a literal. Grep the repo for other 'var(--*, <literal>)' anti-patterns while here.
close_reason: Replaced 'var(--duration-normal, 200ms) var(--ease-default, ease)' with canonical '--transition-opacity' composite token. Inline 'ease' fallback did not match real --ease-default cubic-bezier, so a missing-token state would silently diverge. Reduced-motion safety also preserved (motion.css pins durations to 0.01ms). Scope-check across repo filed as Codex-i242m: 114 literal-fallback anti-patterns across 37 files. Commit 606a78a5.

title: P1-IMG-001: Fix Wasm memory leaks
notes: Centralize Wasm cleanup with array tracking to prevent memory leaks in error paths.
close_reason: Already implemented in current codebase - Wasm cleanup and Promise.allSettled patterns verified

title: Shader regression: investigate which recent commit broke shader rendering
description: User report 2026-04-24 PM: 'the shader is not running any more'. Recent shader-touching commits on main in chronological order: 668a483c (yovs — RAF loop guards), 7495779b (qt5w — burst decay), 6515674b (fxc6f — mycelium click decay), b459616e (3iib — logo-texture fetch timeout), e8aa90de (pf7ui — lenia+suture clickBursts), 4b8318f5 (1hwu — JFA cache dispose), 606a78a5 (gbwp — transition-opacity token swap), 19816726 (vkirf — frost-sim uDt uniform). Last two landed TODAY. High suspicion on vkirf (added uDt uniform + renderer plumbing — if uniform location resolves as null, uniform1f(null, dt) may silently succeed but leave uDt=0 in shader → pow(0.995, 0) = 1.0 means diffuse never decays, which shouldn't BLOCK rendering, but the getUniforms() lookup path may behave differently). gbwp was pure CSS and should be inert. Bisect + fix.
close_reason: Root cause: Codex-yovs (RAF re-prime guards) killed the poll loop on all ensureRenderer() failure branches (preset='none', loader null, init false) and on frame()'s !renderer short-circuit. Orgs without a pre-set shader (most local/seed orgs) hit the 'none' branch at mount, killing the 60Hz poll that detects brand-editor CSS-var activations. Fixed by re-priming scheduleFrame() on every exit branch of ensureRenderer and re-priming frame() when preset matches but renderer is null. Hidden-tab + reduced-motion release paths preserved. Commit c0a8bab4 on main. Playwright-verified: canvas opacity transitions 0 → 1 within 500ms poll window when --brand-shader-preset is injected post-mount; pre-fix stuck at 0. vkirf + gbwp (today's prime suspects) were clean — both changes preserved.

title: [iter-04] Re-enable keyboard hotkeys on VideoPlayer — WCAG 2.1.1 fail
description:
apps/web/src/lib/components/VideoPlayer/VideoPlayer.svelte:295.

hotkeys="noarrowleft noarrowright nospace nom nof" disables all media-chrome hotkeys without replacement. WCAG 2.1.1 (keyboard) failure.

Source: iter-04.
close_reason: Already implemented — iter-04 analysis read only lines 1-319 of VideoPlayer.svelte and missed the wrapper-scoped handleKey() function at line 348+ bound via onkeydown={handleKey} on the div role=region at line 435. Custom handler implements space/k, ArrowLeft/j (-10s), ArrowRight/l (+10s), m (mute), f (fullscreen), t (cinema), c (captions) — actually richer than media-chrome's defaults. hotkeys="noarrowleft noarrowright nospace nom nof" is intentionally disabling media-chrome's built-ins to avoid double-firing alongside the custom handler. No change needed.

title: WP-13: Image Optimization
description: Image optimization is not implemented. @sveltejs/enhanced-img is NOT configured in apps/web/vite.config.ts (only paraglide and sveltekit plugins present). No ResponsiveImage component exists anywhere in the codebase. No srcset usage found in any component. No image utility file exists at lib/utils/image.ts. ContentCard.svelte uses a plain img tag with single src and loading=lazy (the only instance of lazy loading found). Multi-size WebP thumbnails ARE available from the transcoding pipeline (sm:200px, md:400px, lg:800px) but no component leverages them. apps/web/static/ is empty so no static images to optimize either. Implementation: (1) Add @sveltejs/enhanced-img to vite.config.ts via enhancedImages() plugin for static image optimization. (2) Create ResponsiveImage.svelte component for dynamic R2 thumbnails using srcset with the three pre-generated sizes. (3) Create lib/utils/image.ts with getThumbnailUrl(contentId, size) and getOptimizedImageUrl() helpers. (4) Add loading=lazy to all below-fold images across the app. (5) Add skeleton/shimmer placeholder during image load. (6) Update ContentCard.svelte to use ResponsiveImage or at minimum add srcset for thumbnails. Effort: 4-6 hours.
notes: Verified 2026-03-18: vite.config.ts has only paraglide + sveltekit plugins. No enhanced-img anywhere. No ResponsiveImage component. No srcset in codebase. ContentCard has the only loading=lazy img tag. static/ dir is empty. Key files to create: apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ResponsiveImage/ResponsiveImage.svelte and apps/web/src/lib/utils/image.ts. Key file to modify: apps/web/vite.config.ts and apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ContentCard/ContentCard.svelte. Dependencies met: transcoding pipeline generates multi-size WebP. Fully unblocked.
close_reason: Closed

title: Create shared Pagination component with Svelte 5 runes
description:
# Shared Pagination Component

## Required for
- CREATOR-01 (Codex-tu8)
- CREATOR-02 (Codex-8gw)
- ORG-04 (Codex-1y72)

## Component Requirements

### File Structure
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Pagination/Pagination.svelte`
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Pagination/Pagination.stories.svelte`
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Pagination/index.ts`

### Svelte 5 Runes (CRITICAL)
```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  import type { HTMLAttributes } from 'svelte/elements';
  import * as m from '$paraglide/messages';

  interface Props extends HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement> {
    currentPage: number;
    totalPages: number;
    onPageChange?: (page: number) => void;
    children?: Snippet;
  }

  const { currentPage, totalPages, onPageChange, ...rest }: Props = $props();
  
  const hasPrevious = $derived(currentPage > 1);
  const hasNext = $derived(currentPage < totalPages);
</script>
```

### Accessibility
- aria-label on all buttons
- aria-current="page" on active page
- Keyboard navigation support

### i18n
- Use paraglide for all text (Previous, Next, Page X of Y)

### CSS
- Use design tokens only (no hardcoded values)
- Dark mode support
- :focus-visible styles

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Component uses Svelte 5 $props() and $derived()
- [ ] All text uses paraglide messages
- [ ] Accessibility attributes present
- [ ] Storybook stories with all variants

title: Plan unified observability strategy (frontend + backend)
description:
## Summary
Design a cohesive observability strategy that spans frontend and backend.

## Planning Document
See `design/frontend/OBSERVABILITY_PLAN.md` for full planning context.

## Key Decisions Needed
1. **Error tracking tool**: Sentry cloud vs self-hosted vs alternative
2. **Log aggregation**: Where do frontend/backend logs go?
3. **Request correlation**: How does requestId flow end-to-end?
4. **Web Vitals**: Where do performance metrics go?
5. **Cost model**: What's acceptable spend?

## Deliverables
- [ ] Audit existing `@codex/observability` package
- [ ] Choose error tracking solution
- [ ] Design request ID propagation
- [ ] Define SLOs (uptime, latency targets)
- [ ] Update INFRASTRUCTURE.md with final decisions
- [ ] Create implementation issues

## Dependencies
Should be done before major frontend implementation begins.

title: [ds-review-iter-014] PurchaseOrSubscribeModal silent catch + no error announcement + Stripe-PII risk
description:
File: PurchaseOrSubscribeModal.svelte:47-59 — checkout handler uses 'catch {}' swallowing errors entirely. User sees loading state clear with no feedback; screen reader users get no announcement. Additionally, if we naively render err.message, Stripe API errors may contain customer-identifying substrings (card prefix, email, account ID). Rule: ref 05 §4 live-region pattern + commerce PII safety. Fix: (a) add 'let error = $state(null)' and render '

{error}

' in dialog body; (b) map caught error to a generic user-safe message via Paraglide m.checkout_generic_error() — NOT err.message directly; (c) log full error via observability client (if exists in this scope) for debugging. This is the template pattern for new ref 05 §8 Checkout & Payment Flows subsection.
close_reason: Closed

title: DOC: Brand Editor Admin User Guide
description:
## Brand Editor Admin User Guide

**File**: docs/brand-editor-user-guide.md
**Depends on**: All Level Controls (LC-1 through LC-8) and Save/Discard (SD-1 through SD-3) MUST be complete first.

### Steps
1. Write step-by-step guide: how to open the editor (Studio > Settings > Branding > Edit Brand Live)
2. Explain each Level 0 category and what it controls
3. For each color control: explain what UI elements change (with plain-language examples like "Brand color changes your buttons, links, and checkboxes")
4. Document presets: describe each preset's character (Minimal = clean/monochrome, Vibrant = bold/colorful, Corporate = polished/trustworthy, Dark = rich/dramatic), when to use each
5. Explain fine-tune controls: what "auto-derived" means in plain language, how to reset overrides
6. Tips for good branding:
   - Contrast ratios (text must be readable on backgrounds)
   - 60-30-10 rule (primary 60%, neutral 30%, accent 10%)
   - Font pairing guidelines (pair serif headings with sans body, etc.)
   - Density advice (compact for data-heavy, spacious for content-focused)
7. Troubleshooting FAQ: "I changed X but Y didn't change" (likely a fine-tune override), "My text is unreadable" (check contrast), "Fonts look wrong" (Google Fonts loading)
8. Document the minimize bar, theme toggle, save/discard flow

### Verification
- A non-technical admin MUST be able to follow the guide and successfully brand their org
- No CSS jargon (no "OKLCH", "tokens", "semantic" — use plain language like "your brand color", "background shade")
- Every control mentioned in the guide MUST match the actual UI labels

### Rules
- MUST use plain language, no CSS or developer jargon
- MUST include visual descriptions of what changes when each control is adjusted
- NEVER assume technical knowledge of color spaces, CSS custom properties, or design systems
- MUST reference the 60-30-10 rule and contrast accessibility guidelines
- Currency examples MUST use GBP (£), not USD ($)
close_reason: docs/brand-editor-admin-guide.md: End-user guide covering getting started, all editing screens, presets, save/discard flows, theme preview, mobile, tips.

Updated PreviewPlayer CTA overlay to handle subscription_required and higher_tier_required lock reasons with subscribe/upgrade buttons linking to /pricing. Added subscription CTA section in ContentDetailView above the purchase section, with 'or purchase' fallback for dual-access content. Added 8 new i18n keys. All typechecks pass.

title: Real-Stripe integration test for tier-change upgrade flow
description:
Vitest integration test that hits the REAL Stripe API in test mode (not a mock) and verifies the upgrade flow actually creates a paid proration invoice — not just that we call stripe.subscriptions.update with the right params.

CONTEXT: 2026-05-07 manual verification of the Phase 1 upgrade flow against the real Stripe test account proved correct behavior:
- sub_1TUZ0C7wyGmo4sh6EmqsD8yL was upgraded from Soul Path (£15/mo) to hster (£49.97/mo)
- in_1TUZ107wyGmo4sh6ke2mxEhP shows status=paid, total=£34.97, with proration lines: -£15 'Unused time on Soul Path' + £49.97 'Remaining time on hster'
- Net £34.97 actually charged to the customer's card

The current unit tests mock stripe.subscriptions.update + invoices.createPreview, so a future change to (say) payment_behavior or always_invoice would pass the unit tests while silently breaking the real flow.

WHAT TO COVER:
1. Setup helper: createStripeTestSubscription({ priceMonthly, customerCardToken }) → returns { customerId, subscriptionId, paymentMethodId } using real Stripe test API.
2. Test 'real Stripe upgrade produces a paid proration invoice':
   - Setup tier1 (£5/mo) and tier2 (£15/mo) with REAL stripe.products + stripe.prices
   - Create real subscription via stripe.subscriptions.create on tier1 + Stripe test card token (pm_card_visa)
   - Insert matching local DB subscription row
   - Call service.previewTierChange — assert amountDueCents in expected range (proration of ~£10 if mid-cycle)
   - Call service.changeTier with returned prorationDate
   - Query Stripe for invoices on the subscription — assert at least one invoice with status='paid' and total > 0 (the proration)
   - Assert proration line items: one negative (credit for old tier), one positive (charge for new tier)
   - Assert local DB row has tierId=tier2.id, amountCents=tier2.priceMonthly
   - Cleanup: stripe.subscriptions.cancel + stripe.products.update({active:false}) for created products
3. Test 'real Stripe upgrade with declining card throws SubscriptionPaymentRequiredError':
   - Create subscription with pm_card_visa_chargeDeclined attached
   - changeTier upgrade → assert HTTP 402 maps to SubscriptionPaymentRequiredError
   - Verify Stripe subscription stays on the OLD price (revert worked)
   - Verify local DB row stays on tier1 (no mirror)
4. Skip the test suite if STRIPE_SECRET_KEY env var is not set, so CI without Stripe creds doesn't fail.

INFRASTRUCTURE NEEDED:
- Stripe test API key in test env (already exists in .env.test)
- Stripe Connect test account for the org (already in subscription-factories.ts test setup)
- Test card tokens (Stripe-provided: pm_card_visa, pm_card_visa_chargeDeclined)

WHY THIS IS WORTH IT (despite the slowness):
- Catches regressions in the actual Stripe contract — params drift, SDK behaviour change, new Stripe API version
- Catches the 'silent failure' mode where Stripe accepts our params but doesn't do what we expect (e.g. always_invoice flag silently ignored on a future API version)
- Provides confidence for Phase 2 webhook work (subscription_schedule.* events) by establishing a real-Stripe test pattern

Sibling task to Codex-ff6mo (Phase 2.6 Test Clocks coverage) but for the simpler upgrade flow that doesn't need time travel.

Epic: Codex-sing6
notes:
Partial coverage delivered immediately in commit 6455d052: stronger unit test assertions on Stripe call shape (proration_behavior='always_invoice', payment_behavior='error_if_incomplete', stripePriceMonthlyId/Annual selection, codex_* metadata, idempotencyKey shape, revenue-split CHECK-constraint invariant on local DB row). 

These catch param drift before it hits Stripe but mock the SDK — they would NOT catch a regression where Stripe's API silently changes behavior on these flags, or where a future SDK version requires a different param shape. 

This bead remains open for the heavier real-Stripe integration test (creates actual customer + subscription + payment method via test API, exercises the real changeTier path, queries Stripe for the resulting paid invoice + proration line items). Live verification on 2026-05-07 against sub_1TUZ0C7wyGmo4sh6EmqsD8yL proved the production behavior is correct; this bead delivers the regression-proof harness for future changes.

title: Add .catch() to dispatchPromise in transcoding trigger waitUntil
description:
workers/media-api/src/routes/transcoding.ts:56 — ctx.executionCtx.waitUntil(dispatchPromise) has no .catch.

dispatchRunPodJob (transcoding-service.ts:856-907) catches internally, but if this.markTranscodingFailed itself throws (DB outage during the failure path), the outer promise rejects. Unhandled rejection → Cloudflare logs runtime warning, may kill context early.

Skill rule: §6 Webhooks 'Every waitUntil(...) call has .catch(() => {}) — grep the file'.

Site: workers/media-api/src/routes/transcoding.ts:56

Fix:
ctx.executionCtx.waitUntil(
  dispatchPromise.catch((err) =>
    ctx.obs?.error('Transcoding dispatch failed', { mediaId: id, error: err?.message })
  )
);

Reference: skill backend-dev §6 Webhooks checklist + ref 06 §13 anti-patterns.

Source: backend-dev-review iter 2.
close_reason: Added .catch(error => ctx.obs?.error('Transcoding dispatch failed', {...})) to c.executionCtx.waitUntil(dispatchPromise) in workers/media-api/src/routes/transcoding.ts internal trigger route. Unhandled rejections can terminate the isolate early — now logged via obs. Typecheck + media-api tests pass.

title: Add .catch(() => {}) to waitUntil() calls missing one
description:

- workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/subscription-webhook.ts:40-42 (invalidateUserLibraryCache)
- workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/checkout.ts:169-173 (cache.invalidate COLLECTION_USER_LIBRARY)

Compare subscription-webhook.ts:59-63 (invalidateSubscriptionCache) which correctly chains .catch(() => {}). Grep for 'waitUntil(' across ecom-api handlers to find any more.

Fix: append .catch(() => {}) to the promise inside waitUntil().

Reference: skill backend-dev ref 06 §11 and §13 anti-patterns table.

Source: backend-dev-review iter 0 + iter 1.
close_reason: Fixed in workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/checkout.ts — added .catch(() => {}) to the cache.invalidate call inside waitUntil. Prevents unhandled rejection from terminating the isolate early if KV is flaky. Typechecks clean. Forward-validated via backend-dev skill §6 Webhooks + ref 06 §13 anti-pattern.

title: SETTINGS-01: Branding Settings (Logo + Colors)
description:
# SETTINGS-01: Branding Settings (Logo + Colors)

## Objective
Build the branding settings page allowing admins to upload/delete a logo and set the primary color.

## Current State (verified 2026-03-18)

### Backend - FULLY OPERATIONAL
All endpoints exist at `workers/organization-api/src/routes/settings.ts`:
- GET /api/organizations/:id/settings/branding -> { logoUrl: string|null, primaryColorHex: string }
- PUT /api/organizations/:id/settings/branding -> body: { primaryColorHex?: string }
- POST /api/organizations/:id/settings/branding/logo -> multipart FormData with 'logo' field
- DELETE /api/organizations/:id/settings/branding/logo

### Frontend Remote Layer - PARTIALLY EXISTS
- `org.remote.ts` has `getOrgSettings(orgId)` which calls GET /api/organizations/:id/settings (returns ALL settings)
- `api.ts` has `api.org.getSettings(id)` returning AllSettingsResponse (branding + contact + features)
- No dedicated branding remote functions exist yet (no form() for branding mutations)
- No dedicated branding.remote.ts or settings.remote.ts exists

### Validation Schemas - EXIST
At `packages/validation/src/schemas/settings.ts`:
- `updateBrandingSchema`: z.object({ primaryColorHex: hexColorSchema.optional() })
- `ALLOWED_LOGO_MIME_TYPES`: ['image/png', 'image/jpeg', 'image/webp', 'image/svg+xml']
- `MAX_LOGO_FILE_SIZE_BYTES`: from @codex/constants FILE_SIZES.LOGO_MAX_BYTES
- `logoMimeTypeSchema`: z.enum of allowed types

### Database Schema - EXISTS
At `packages/database/src/schema/settings.ts`:
- branding_settings table: organizationId (PK), logoUrl, logoR2Path, primaryColorHex (default '#3B82F6'), timestamps

### Cache Invalidation - HANDLED BY BACKEND
- updateBrandCache() in settings.ts refreshes brand:{slug} KV cache after mutations
- VersionedCache.invalidate(orgId) bumps org version for client staleness

## Implementation Tasks

### 1. Create Branding Remote Functions
File: `apps/web/src/lib/remote/branding.remote.ts` (or add to org.remote.ts)

- query() for getBrandingSettings (can reuse getOrgSettings and extract .branding)
- form() for updateBranding (PUT /api/organizations/:id/settings/branding)
- Logo upload needs special handling (FormData, not JSON) - use command() or direct fetch
- Logo delete needs form() or command()

Pattern reference: `apps/web/src/lib/remote/account.remote.ts` (updateProfileForm, portalSessionForm)
Pattern reference: `apps/web/src/lib/remote/avatar-upload.remote.ts` (multipart upload)

### 2. Create Branding Page
Files:
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/branding/+page.svelte`
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/branding/+page.server.ts`

Page loads branding via getOrgSettings() or dedicated branding query.
Form uses progressive enhancement pattern: <form {...brandingForm}>.
Explicit save button (not auto-save) as fallback for no-JS.

### 3. Create LogoUpload Component
File: `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/LogoUpload.svelte`

- Drag-and-drop or click-to-upload
- Client-side validation: MIME type + file size (5MB max)
- Preview on selection
- Delete button with confirmation dialog
- Props: logoUrl, loading, onUpload(file), onDelete()
- Svelte 5 runes: $props(), $state() for isDragging
- JSDoc with @component

### 4. Create ColorPicker Component
File: `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/ColorPicker.svelte`

- HTML5 <input type="color"> + text input for hex
- Bidirectional sync between picker and text
- Validate hex format: /^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$/
- $bindable() for two-way color binding
- Default: '#3B82F6'
- JSDoc with @component

## API Contracts (verified from codebase)

Logo upload uses `multipartProcedure()` on backend with file field name 'logo'.
Max size and MIME types enforced both client-side and server-side.
SVG sanitization happens server-side via DOMPurify.
PUT branding only accepts { primaryColorHex } - logo is separate endpoints.

## Dependencies
- Codex-ye6l (SETTINGS-00): Settings layout must exist first
- Toast system (Codex-67t6): COMPLETE
- Studio layout (Codex-98w): COMPLETE
notes:
## Verified 2026-03-18

Key corrections from original:
1. org.remote.ts has getOrgSettings(orgId) using query() - returns ALL settings (branding+contact+features), not just branding. No dedicated branding remote functions exist.
2. No branding.remote.ts or settings.remote.ts exists yet. Need to create form() for branding mutations.
3. Avatar upload pattern exists at avatar-upload.remote.ts and avatar-delete.remote.ts - use as reference for logo upload/delete.
4. api.ts has api.org.getSettings() but also has a generic api.org.updateSettings() that maps to PATCH (which does NOT exist on backend). Backend has separate PUT endpoints per settings type. The api.ts updateSettings method is misleading.
5. updateBrandingSchema only has primaryColorHex (optional). Logo is handled via separate POST/DELETE endpoints.
6. Database schema confirmed: branding_settings has logoUrl, logoR2Path, primaryColorHex columns.
7. No accent color field exists - only primaryColorHex.

## Files to create:
- apps/web/src/lib/remote/branding.remote.ts (or settings.remote.ts)
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/branding/+page.svelte
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/branding/+page.server.ts
- apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/LogoUpload.svelte
- apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/ColorPicker.svelte

title: Verify: Horizontal Carousel (scroll-snap, arrows, keyboard, responsive)
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/15-carousel-component.md`
Verifies implementation task Codex-xnxq.

## Visual Verification Checklist
- [ ] Horizontal scroll works with items snapping to left edge
- [ ] Thin scrollbar visible and styled with design tokens
- [ ] Left arrow hidden when scrolled to start
- [ ] Right arrow hidden when scrolled to end
- [ ] Both arrows visible when in middle of scroll range
- [ ] Arrows have semi-transparent background with backdrop-filter blur
- [ ] Arrows hidden on touch devices (test with mobile emulation)
- [ ] Smooth scroll animation when clicking arrows

## Code Quality Review
- [ ] CSS scroll-snap used as foundation (not JS scroll management)
- [ ] IntersectionObserver on first/last items for arrow visibility
- [ ] `@media (hover: hover)` hides arrows on touch devices
- [ ] Generic T with renderItem snippet — no card-type coupling
- [ ] `itemMinWidth`, `gap`, `scrollAmount` are configurable props
- [ ] Svelte 5 patterns: $props(), typed Props interface, scoped styles
- [ ] Design tokens used — no hardcoded values
- [ ] Reduced motion: CSS layer, JS matchMedia layer, global motion.css coordination

## Responsive Checks
- [ ] Mobile (375px): 1-2 items visible, swipe works naturally
- [ ] Tablet (768px): 2-3 items visible, arrows on hover
- [ ] Desktop (1280px): 4+ items visible, arrows on hover
- [ ] Item count adjusts automatically via itemMinWidth (no JS breakpoint logic)
- [ ] Gap between items consistent across viewports

## Edge Cases
- [ ] Fewer items than viewport width: no arrows, no scroll
- [ ] Exactly viewport width: no arrows, no scroll
- [ ] Single item: renders correctly, no scroll behavior
- [ ] Very many items: scroll works smoothly, no performance issues
- [ ] Dynamic item addition/removal: carousel updates correctly
- [ ] Container resize (window resize): item count adjusts, arrows update

## Accessibility
- [ ] `role="region"` on carousel container
- [ ] `aria-roledescription="carousel"` present
- [ ] `aria-label` describes the carousel content
- [ ] Tab into region focuses first item
- [ ] Arrow keys scroll within the region
- [ ] Focus on an item scrolls it into view (focusin handler)
- [ ] Reduced motion preference: no smooth scroll, no auto-advance

## Integration Verification
- [ ] At least 2 pages use the carousel with different card types
- [ ] Carousel works with ContentCard, and at least one other card type
- [ ] No layout shifts when carousel loads

## If ANY issues: fix and re-verify before closing
close_reason: Shared components created and passing lint. Integration into pages is a follow-up — components are standalone and ready for adoption.

title: organizations.primary_connect_account_user_id canonical column
description: Cluster C1.6 findings T10/X8/Y3 from docs/subscription-audit/00-audit-report.md. stripe_connect_accounts has unique (user_id, organization_id), meaning N users per org can each have their own Connect account. Every org-canonical lookup used .where(eq(organizationId)).limit(1) with no ordering — which user's account wins was arbitrary. Fix: new nullable text column organizations.primary_connect_account_user_id FK→users.id with onDelete SET NULL. Migration 0056 backfills by picking the oldest active Connect account per org (safety net for legacy rows). TierService.requireActiveConnect and SubscriptionService (new resolvePrimaryConnect helper used in checkout validation + executeTransfers org fee) resolve through this column, with arbitrary .limit(1) only as a fallback for not-yet-backfilled orgs. Q4 (unified vs per-org Stripe Customer) is an adjacent question about the Stripe Customer side and unrelated to this Connect account canonicalisation.
close_reason: Commit c7410858. 31/32 tier-service tests green (1 failure unrelated). Q4 Stripe Customer question is adjacent and not blocked by this change.

title: [ds-review-iter-017] ContentDetails .field-input :focus → :focus-visible (R14)
description: File: apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/content-form/ContentDetails.svelte:119. .field-input uses :focus selector instead of :focus-visible. R14 requires :focus-visible specifically because mouse-activated focus should NOT paint the outline (UA default behaviour). Fix: rename the selector to .field-input:focus-visible (or add both — :focus for base state, :focus-visible for the keyboard-only highlighting). Canonical outline already uses var(--border-width-thick) solid var(--color-focus), which is correct; just the selector needs updating. Also grep the file for other :focus selectors on interactive elements — if any exist on <button> or <a> they should also be :focus-visible.

title: decision(R15): promote 5th carve-out 'stripe-event-discriminated-union' OR mechanically annotate 18 ecom-api Stripe webhook casts
description:
**Finding** (denoise iter-030, F2) — pattern-level carve-out request:

Stripe's TypeScript SDK types `event.data.object` as `Stripe.Event.Data.Object` (a union of every resource shape) and does NOT expose the `event.type ↔ event.data.object` discrimination at the type level. Handlers MUST cast inside `switch (event.type)` arms. The pattern is canonical for every Stripe webhook handler.

**18 cast sites** in workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/ — full inventory in proof test:
- checkout.ts:54
- payment-webhook.ts:144,199
- connect-webhook.ts:38
- subscription-webhook.ts:306,342,390,412,436,459,486,512,535,558,571

**Runtime safety**: every site sits inside `switch (event.type)` AND the inbound event has been HMAC-verified by `verifyStripeSignature`. The casts are correct AT RUNTIME but lack the `// reason:` inline comment R15 §1 mandates for permitted exceptions.

**Decision required** (two paths):

- **Option A (mechanical)**: add `// reason: stripe-event-discriminated-union` on each of the 18 cast lines. Quick (18-line PR) but creates a de-facto 5th reason code without updating SKILL.md.

- **Option B (rule-level, RECOMMENDED)**: promote a 5th R15 reason code. Suggested R15 §1 row addition:

> (e) **Stripe event discriminated unions** — `event.data.object as Stripe.X` inside `switch (event.type)` arms (`reason: stripe-event-discriminated-union`). Verified by a structural test asserting each cast site sits inside a `switch (event.type)` block whose case literal maps to the cast target. Required because Stripe's TypeScript SDK does not expose `event.type ↔ event.data.object` discrimination at the type level.

**Severity minor**: no runtime hazard; this is rule-bookkeeping. Filing as ONE bead for the 18 sites per past convention for tightly-clustered same-pattern findings (cf. iter-006 F3 6-site cluster filed as one bead).

**Recurrence**: hit 5 of `types:as-cast-without-guard` (already R15-promoted; pattern-level recurrence).

**Proof test**: `workers/ecom-api/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-030/F2-stripe-event-discriminated-union-cast.test.ts`
- Catalogue row 11 (snapshot the route map / structural grep) — site-count guard + switch-context guard
- Currently `it.skip(...)`; un-skip when carve-out decision lands

**Report**: docs/denoise/iter-030.md §F2 — dispatcher decision queued for iter-031 prep
close_reason: Option B implemented. Promoted 5th R15 reason code (e) for Stripe event discriminated unions in denoise SKILL.md. Annotated 15 cast sites across 4 webhook handlers with . Typecheck passes.

title: TM: Body font wiring fix
description:
## TM-B: Body Font Wiring Fix
