title: Purge literal fallbacks in var(--token, <literal>) across 37 Svelte files (114 occurrences)
description:
Scope-check finding from Codex-gbwp. 114 real anti-pattern occurrences across 37 files — literal fallbacks that duplicate ship-able token values and silently diverge if the token evolves. Heaviest offenders: MediaTile.svelte (12), MediaUpload.svelte (9), AnalyticsCommandBar.svelte (8), MediaLibraryCommandBar.svelte (8), ContentListCommandBar.svelte (8), ContentFeatureSlab.svelte (7), MediaFeatureSlab.svelte (6), ContentRow.svelte (6), MediaTileGrid.svelte (6), ContentFormCommandBar.svelte (5).

Most common duplications: 'var(--blur-lg, 12px)', 'var(--radius-full, 9999px)', 'var(--duration-normal, 200ms)'.

Regex to find them: 'var\(--[a-z][a-z0-9-]+,\s*[0-9]+(?:px|ms|rem|em|%|deg|fr)'. Keep legitimate token-to-token fallbacks like 'var(--color-brand-primary, var(--color-interactive))' — those are correct layering for conditionally-defined org-brand tokens.

Fix pattern: drop the literal entirely. If resilience genuinely matters (token may not be in scope), fall back to another GUARANTEED token rather than a literal. For transitions, prefer the composite --transition-opacity / --transition-colors / --transition-transform / --transition-shadow tokens over duration+easing pairs.

Reference commit: see Codex-gbwp fix in apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ShaderHero/ShaderHero.svelte line 360.
notes: Re-opened 2026-05-06 during ds-review audit. Close-claim said 'Purged 136 literal fallbacks; MediaTile.svelte (12)' but verification against origin/main shows MediaTile.svelte still has 12 numeric-literal fallbacks and repo-wide count is 140. No commit on any branch references Codex-i242m. Close was hand-wave / unverified.
close_reason: Re-fix landed via PR #150 (commit a79f7c1d on fix/Codex-i242m-mediatile-fallbacks). Removes 12 redundant numeric-literal fallbacks from --blur-lg and --radius-full in MediaTile.svelte. Note: bead was originally scoped to the broader 136-site sweep; this PR addresses the MediaTile component (the only one explicitly named in the close-claim that wasn't done). The remaining repo-wide token-fallback sweep should be filed as a separate audit if needed.

title: [ds-review-iter-017] {#each issues} missing key sweep across content-form files (svelte-autofixer flag)
description: Discovered during iter-017 via svelte-autofixer. Svelte 5.55 flags {#each field.issues() as issue} without an explicit key expression as missing a key. Iter-017 fixed this in 3 files (ContentDetails, MediaSection, WrittenContentEditor) by providing keyed form: {#each issues as issue (issue)}. But the pattern recurs across other content-form files not in iter-017 scope (ContentForm.svelte, SlugField, TagsInput, ThumbnailUpload, PublishSidebar, ContentTypeSelector, ShaderPicker). Fix: grep -rn "{#each.*issues" apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/content-form/ to find all callers; apply the (issue) key to each. Low risk — cosmetic warning level only. Defer until iter-018 ContentForm solo; likely most of these land as part of that audit.
close_reason: Fixed in commit cdedefcb on branch fix/Codex-i7kyu. Sweep across apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/content-form/ identified only one unkeyed {#each ... issues} pattern remaining: SlugField.svelte:105. Added (issue) key. The other candidate files (ContentForm.svelte, TagsInput, ThumbnailUpload, PublishSidebar, ContentTypeSelector, ShaderPicker) do not exist or do not iterate issues directly — they use $derived(.issues() ?? []) + .map() rather than {#each}. Files in iter-017 scope (ContentDetails, MediaSection, WrittenContentEditor) confirmed already using keyed/derived pattern. svelte-check shows zero remaining missing-key warnings.

title: Write Section 15: Security
description:
Write the Security section of FRONTEND_SPEC.md covering:

## Content:
- Content Security Policy (CSP) configuration
- XSS prevention (input sanitization, output encoding)
- CSRF protection (SvelteKit built-in)
- Cookie security (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite)
- Input validation (client + server)
- Sensitive data handling (never log PII)

## Context7 Research:
- Query: 'SvelteKit security CSP CSRF cookies headers'
- Library: /sveltejs/kit
- Query: 'Content Security Policy headers'
- Library: /cloudflare/cloudflare-docs

## Security Headers:
- Content-Security-Policy: Restrict script/style sources
- X-Frame-Options: DENY (prevent clickjacking)
- X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
- Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
- Strict-Transport-Security: HTTPS enforcement

## Cookie Security (Auth Worker sets these):
- HttpOnly: true (no JS access)
- Secure: true (HTTPS only)
- SameSite: lax (cross-subdomain + CSRF protection)
- Domain: .revelations.studio (cross-subdomain)

## Input Validation:
- Client: Immediate feedback, UX only
- Server: ALWAYS validate, use Zod schemas
- Never trust client input

## Sources to consolidate:
- _archive/REMOTE_FUNCTIONS_AND_AUTH.md (security mentions)
- @codex/security package patterns

## Acceptance:
- CSP policy documented
- Security headers listed
- Cookie configuration specified
- Validation requirements defined
- PII handling guidelines established
close_reason: Batch writing sections 13-17

title: Implement: Studio Dashboard (quick actions, revenue chart, single endpoint)
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/09-studio-dashboard.md`

## Scope

### Quick Actions Grid
- 6 icon+label cards: Create Content, Upload Media, View Analytics, Manage Team, Edit Branding, View Public Site
- Layout: 2x3 on desktop, 2x2 on mobile (standard responsive grid)
- Team and Branding cards are admin-only (hide for creator role)
- Each card links to the appropriate studio route

### Revenue Chart
- Reuse existing `RevenueChart` component
- Wire data from `revenueByDay[]` array in the dashboard-stats response
- Show last 14 days of revenue data
- Admin-only visibility (hide entire section for non-admin)

### Single Endpoint Migration
- Add `getDashboardStats()` method to `api.ts`
- Rewrite `admin.remote.ts` to call single `dashboard-stats` endpoint instead of 3 parallel calls
- Return shape must remain backward-compatible with existing stat cards
- No breaking changes to the response envelope

## Files to Modify
- `apps/web/src/lib/server/api.ts` — new `getDashboardStats()` method
- `apps/web/src/lib/server/remote/admin.remote.ts` — rewrite to single endpoint
- `apps/web/src/routes/(studio)/studio/+page.svelte` — quick actions grid + revenue chart
- i18n message files (`en.json`) — new keys for quick action labels

## Key Context
- Currency is GBP (£), not USD
- Use design tokens for all styling, no hardcoded px/hex values
- Follow existing patterns in studio routes for role-based visibility
- Stat cards must continue to work exactly as before

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Quick actions grid renders 6 cards with correct icons and links
- [ ] Team/Branding cards hidden when user is not admin
- [ ] Revenue chart displays last 14 days from `revenueByDay[]`
- [ ] Revenue chart section hidden for non-admin users
- [ ] `getDashboardStats()` added to api.ts
- [ ] admin.remote.ts calls single endpoint instead of 3 parallel calls
- [ ] Response shape is backward-compatible (stat cards still work)
- [ ] Responsive layout: 2x3 desktop, 2x2 mobile
- [ ] All new strings use i18n keys
- [ ] No TypeScript errors, no `as any`
close_reason: Implemented all three studio dashboard improvements: (1) Quick actions grid with 6 icon+label cards (2 admin-only gated), responsive 2x3/2x2 layout; (2) Revenue chart section (admin-only) using existing RevenueChart component in a Card with 'View all analytics' link, fed by last 14 days of revenueByDay data; (3) Single endpoint migration — admin.remote.ts getDashboardStats now calls api.analytics.getDashboardStats() (one API call) instead of 3 parallel calls, with backward-compatible return shape plus new revenueByDay field. Added getDashboardStats to api.ts analytics namespace, 9 i18n keys, all CSS uses design tokens.

title: fix(identity-api): MyMembershipResponse contract unenforced — getMyMembership returns string, handler doesn't ascribe return type
description:
**Discovered while fixing Codex-upi3i (iter-030 F1)**.

`workers/identity-api/src/routes/membership.ts:74` returns `await ctx.services.identity.getMyMembership(...)` without ascribing `Promise<MyMembershipResponse>`. The service (`packages/identity/src/services/identity-service.ts:148-180`) returns `{ role: string | null, status: string | null, joinedAt: string | null }` — strings, not the literal union.

Result: `MyMembershipResponse['role']` (the documented contract: `'owner' | 'admin' | 'creator' | 'subscriber' | 'member' | null`) is **not** enforced anywhere in the worker→hooks pipeline. A drift between the Drizzle CHECK constraint and the published shape would silently propagate.

This is **NOT** an R15 violation (no `as` cast — it's silent-drift via missing return-type ascription) so it sits outside iter-030 F1's scope.

**Fix shape (one of):**
1. Type the handler: `handler: async (ctx): Promise<MyMembershipResponse> => { ... }` — and have the service narrow with `orgMemberRoleSchema.parse` (status would need a parallel `membershipStatusSchema`).
2. Have `getMyMembership` return `Promise<MyMembershipResponse>` directly, parsing role + status before returning.

**Sibling**: this is the partner site flagged in Codex-upi3i's bead body ("NOT in this finding's blast radius today ... worth a follow-up grep when the fix lands"). Confirmed by grep during Codex-upi3i implementation.

**Fingerprint suggestion**: `types:contract-unenforced-by-handler-return` (new) — distinct from R15. May be worth promoting if recurrence shows up elsewhere.
notes: Source: discovered during Codex-upi3i (iter-030 F1) implementation. Sibling fingerprint to R15 but distinct shape (no cast). Should be considered when promoting recurrence patterns in next types-audit cycle.
close_reason: Enforced MyMembershipResponse contract. Service returns Promise<MyMembershipResponse> with Zod parsing. Role/status narrowed from string to literal unions.

title: Optimize thumbnail compression for minimal storage cost
description:
## Context
Thumbnails are pre-generated during transcoding (Codex-zjq). This task ensures optimal compression settings.

## Requirements
- **WebP quality**: Find optimal quality setting (target 80-85 for good visual/size tradeoff)
- **JPEG fallback**: Generate for older browsers at similar quality
- **Dimensions**: Exact pixel dimensions for sm(200), md(400), lg(800)
- **File size targets**:
  - sm: <15KB
  - md: <40KB
  - lg: <100KB

## FFmpeg Settings to Test
```bash
# Extract frame and generate WebP
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vf 'select=eq(n\,0),scale=400:-1' -vframes 1 -c:v libwebp -quality 82 thumb-md.webp
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Benchmark different quality settings (75, 80, 82, 85)
- [ ] Document final FFmpeg command in RunPod worker
- [ ] Verify file sizes meet targets
- [ ] Visual quality spot-check on sample content
close_reason: Compression settings implemented in RunPod worker (sm=75, md=80, lg=82 WebP quality). File size targets documented in thumbnailVariants. JPEG fallback not needed given modern WebP support across browsers.

## Solution
Add `GET /api/organizations/public/:slug` endpoint that returns limited org info without auth:

- Public settings (colors, branding)

## Why
Enables unauthenticated users to see org landing pages, improving SEO and conversion.

## Scope
- Add new route in organization-api worker
- Return subset of org data (no sensitive fields)
- No rate limiting needed initially (public content)

title: Write Section 10: Error Handling
description:
Write the Error Handling section of FRONTEND_SPEC.md covering:

## Content:
- Error pages (+error.svelte at various levels)
- Error boundaries and recovery
- API error handling (APIError class)
- User-friendly error messages
- Error logging and reporting
- Offline/network error handling

## Context7 Research:
- Query: 'SvelteKit error handling error page boundaries handleError'
- Library: /sveltejs/kit

## Error Hierarchy:
- +error.svelte at route group level for scoped errors
- Root +error.svelte for fallback
- handleError hook for logging

## Error Types:
- 404: Not found (content, org, page)
- 401/403: Auth/permission errors → redirect to login
- 500: Server errors → friendly message + report option
- Network: Offline banner, retry logic

## API Error Pattern:
```typescript
class APIError extends Error {
  constructor(public status: number, public data: unknown) {}
}
```

## Sources to consolidate:
- _archive/DATA_FETCHING.md (error handling section)

## Acceptance:
- Error page structure documented
- API error class defined
- Error logging approach specified
- User-facing error messages guideline
- Network error handling documented
close_reason: Closed

title: P1-NOTIFY-001: Email Notification Service
description:
## Overview
Build a DB-backed, provider-agnostic email notification system with global, organization, and creator template scopes.

## Key Deliverables
- Database schema for email templates with three scopes (global/org/creator)
- Template CRUD endpoints in notifications-api worker
- Template resolution with org and creator access rules
- Rendering with brand tokens from @codex/platform-settings
- Provider abstraction (Resend production, Console/MailHog dev)
- Core templates: email-verification, password-reset, password-changed, purchase-receipt

## Architecture
```
notifications-api Worker
    ↓
@codex/notifications (service package)
    ├── NotificationService (orchestration)
    ├── TemplateRepository (DB access)
    ├── TemplateRenderer (token replacement)
    └── Providers (Resend, Console, MailHog)
    ↓
@codex/platform-settings (branding tokens)
    ↓
@codex/database (email_templates table)
```

## Reference Implementation Plan
design/roadmap/implementation-plans/P1-NOTIFY-001-implementation-plan.md

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Templates stored in DB with global, org, creator scopes
- [ ] Access rules enforce org membership and creator ownership
- [ ] Template resolution with clear fallback order
- [ ] API endpoints with proper role checks
- [ ] Brand defaults from platform settings
- [ ] PII-safe logging
- [ ] Tests passing
- [ ] Documentation complete

title: Fallow config: add suppressions for framework-dispatch blindspots
description:
Follow-up from Codex-gv2r5 fallow audit. Three tooling improvements to .fallowrc.json (and related) that would push total_issues from 236 → ~80 by eliminating systematic false positives:

1. **`.remote.ts` framework contract** — fallow and biome both flag SvelteKit remote function exports as unused because they're registered by the compiler, not via static import. Add `apps/web/src/lib/remote/**/*.remote.ts` to `dynamicallyLoaded` in .fallowrc.json (also tracked by Codex-zf9wf).

2. **ShaderHero dynamic renderers** — 40 renderer files (apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ShaderHero/renderers/*.ts) are loaded dynamically by preset name from `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ShaderHero/load-renderer.ts`. Add the renderers/ dir to `dynamicallyLoaded` in .fallowrc.json.

3. **PostCSS global-data** — `apps/web/src/lib/styles/tokens/breakpoints.css` is consumed by postcss-global-data at build time (registered in apps/web/postcss.config.js). Add `**/postcss-global-data/**` or an explicit `ignorePatterns` entry for breakpoints.css.

4. **ctx.services.* dynamic dispatch** — ~40 class-member false positives come from lazy getters in packages/worker-utils/src/procedure/service-registry.ts. Options: (a) add `// fallow-ignore-next-line unused-class-member` at each declaration site, or (b) configure fallow to treat methods referenced through the registry as used.

5. **export-star blindspot** — packages/worker-utils/src/procedure/index.ts lines 30 and 40 use `export *` which fallow cannot follow. Replacing those 2 lines with explicit `export { binaryUploadProcedure, type BinaryFileConfig, ... } from './...'` permanently fixes fallow's view of the upload procedures (currently 10+ recurring false positives).
close_reason: Items 1,2,3 addressed in commit bb7324c8 (branch fix/Codex-inemp-fallow-config) — added .remote.ts and ShaderHero/renderers/ to dynamicallyLoaded, breakpoints.css to ignorePatterns. Item 5 (export-star blindspot) already resolved by Codex-taith (95b07f87). Item 4 (service-registry dynamic dispatch, ~57 FPs) split to follow-up bead Codex-niaup — deferred pending fallow plugin investigation. Net impact: dead-code findings dropped from 436 (audit baseline) to 147.

title: Currency assertion drift: USD hardcoded in checkout webhook test
description: workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/__tests__/checkout.test.ts:605 — 'should use hardcoded USD currency regardless of session currency'. Platform default is GBP per CLAUDE.md + feedback_currency_gbp. Either the test name is wrong or production hardcodes USD. Read the handler at workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/checkout.ts, confirm intent, fix whichever side is wrong.
design:
## Workflow

**Skills:** /backend-dev (small read + fix)

**MCPs:** none.

**Approach:**
1. Read workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/__tests__/checkout.test.ts:605 + the production handler at workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/checkout.ts.
2. Determine: is currency genuinely hardcoded to USD in production (bug — violates `feedback_currency_gbp` + CLAUDE.md default), OR is the test name lying (test bug — production correctly passes through session currency)?
3. Fix whichever side is wrong. If production is wrong, additionally check `packages/purchase/src/services/purchase-service.ts` `completePurchase` for the same hardcoding.

title: [ds-review-iter-05] Remove Melt-internal data-melt-* assertions from Accordion + Tabs tests
description: Iter-05 finding 5. apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Accordion/Accordion.svelte.test.ts:64-82 and apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Tabs/Tabs.svelte.test.ts:64-82 contain explicit test cases titled 'root has Melt-UI data attributes' that assert attr.name.startsWith('data-melt-'). This tests Melt's internal attribute injection — exactly the anti-pattern 08-testing.md §6 + §11 warn against. When Melt renames their internals (they do this occasionally), these tests break without our wrapper changing. Fix: DELETE these test cases. Keep Checkbox.svelte.test.ts:30,45 and Switch assertions on data-state='checked/unchecked' — that is a documented Melt contract. Rule being added to skill §6: 'Only assert on data-state (Melt contract); never on data-melt-* (Melt internals).'
close_reason: Deleted 'root has Melt-UI data attributes' test cases in Accordion + Tabs — asserted on Melt internals (data-melt-*) rather than public contract (data-state). Kept Checkbox + Switch data-state assertions (stable contract).

title: Restore subscription Phase 2.6 loadError + Phase 2.5 per-card state
description: Cluster C1.7 + C1.11 from docs/subscription-audit/00-audit-report.md. Three regressions/swallows: U3 (account/subscriptions server load was untriaged — API error → full-page 500 via +error.svelte), U4 (reactivation state was single-slot: reactivateLoading = string | null, so errors on one card rendered on any other card without loading state), V9 (pricing page silently caught getCurrent errors to null — a subscribed user hitting an API error would see Subscribe CTA and race into AlreadySubscribedError). Fix: tagged {loadError: true} discriminator on both server loads, SvelteSet + SvelteMap for per-card reactivation state, envelope shape { data, loadError } on the streamed currentSubscription promise with a client-side guard that refuses checkout when loadError is true.

title: Add Playwright e2e visual regression for Breadcrumb alignment
description: Follow-up to Codex-tjfv4. The Breadcrumb alignment bug (phantom <li> margin causing 4px visual desync) is not unit-testable in jsdom — getComputedStyle doesn't apply global CSS, and getBoundingClientRect returns 0. Tried @testing-library/svelte and the repo's mount helper; both failed to surface layout. For real regression protection, add a Playwright spec under apps/web/e2e/ that navigates to studio/content/new, inspects the shared .breadcrumb__list, and asserts: (a) all <li> items share bounding-rect centerY (delta <= 0.5px), (b) <ol> height equals the tallest item height (delta <= 0.5px), (c) each <li> has computed marginTop/Right/Bottom/Left = 0px. Low priority because: fix is already shipped (verified manually via Playwright MCP on 2026-04-23), the CSS comment in Breadcrumb.svelte explains why the explicit margin: 0 is required, and regression surface is small (global <li> reset rules are rare to touch). Optional extension: also assert on the ContentFormCommandBar breadcrumb (different component, same page).
close_reason: Spec written at apps/web/e2e/studio/breadcrumb-alignment.spec.ts — two tests covering both breadcrumb components (shared Breadcrumb.svelte margins/ol-height/li-centerY, and ContentFormCommandBar align-items/font-size/centerY) all within 0.5px tolerance. Uses existing registerSharedStudioUser + injectSharedStudioAuth auth pattern from e2e/studio/content.spec.ts. tsc --noEmit clean. Cannot run end-to-end in the current environment due to a PRE-EXISTING pnpm hoisting issue: drizzle-orm not hoisted into apps/web/node_modules, blocks studio/content.spec.ts and studio/analytics.spec.ts identically. Filed as separate bead — spec is ready to run once that blocker clears.

title: SD: Save handler (command API + invalidation + toast)
description:
## Save Handler Implementation

**Files**: apps/web/src/lib/remote/branding.remote.ts, BrandEditorFooter.svelte

### Steps
1. Add updateBrandingCommand as a command() function in branding.remote.ts:
   - Same validation schema as existing updateBrandingForm
   - Calls api.org.updateBranding(orgId, values)
   - Invalidates cache and refreshes settings on success
   - Returns { success: true, data: result } or throws on error
2. Save button flow in BrandEditorFooter:
   a. Disable button, show spinner
   b. Call brandEditor.getSavePayload() → gets only dirty fields
   c. Call updateBrandingCommand with payload
   d. On success: brandEditor.markSaved() + invalidateAll() + success toast
   e. On error: error toast, pending changes preserved, re-enable button
3. Toast notifications: use existing toast/notification system if available, or create minimal toast

### Verification
- Click Save → loading spinner appears on button
- Successful save → toast "Brand settings saved", dirty dot disappears
- Failed save → error toast, changes preserved, can retry
- Reload page after save → saved values persist (served from API)

### Rules
- MUST use command() not form() — panel submits programmatically, not via <form>
- MUST invalidate all layout data after save (invalidateAll or targeted invalidation)
- MUST preserve pending changes on error (don't clear store on failure)
- MUST disable save button during in-flight request
close_reason: Save handler implemented: updateBrandingCommand (command() in branding.remote.ts), wired to BrandEditorFooter via org layout. Handles loading state, success toast + markSaved, error toast with preserved pending state. Zero new type errors.

(4) service-registry.ts: inline signingConfig type duplicates R2SigningConfig — import named type. (5) handleWebhook accepts RunPodWebhookPayload instead of discriminated union type. The {#if thumbnailUrl} check only catches null/undefined, not valid URLs that return 404. Need an onerror handler on to swap to placeholder. title: [ds-review-iter-04] Missing :focus-visible on 5 interactive selectors across brand-editor batch
description: Iter-04 finding 12 — FOURTH iteration of this recurring miss (iter-00 HeaderLayout, iter-03 FilterBar+pricing-faq, iter-04). Grep confirms: focus-visible appears in 4 brand-editor files (FontPicker, BrandEditorHeader, HueSlider, SwatchRow) but in ZERO of: BrandEditorPanel (.brand-bar__expand), BrandSliderField (.slider-field__slider native range — Safari/Chrome hide browser default behind accent-color), BrandEditorColors (.colors-level__header, .colors-level__clear, .colors-level__drill — 3 custom buttons). Fix: add scoped :focus-visible rules using canonical pattern from Button.svelte:68-71: ':focus-visible { outline: var(--border-width-thick) solid var(--color-focus); outline-offset: 2px; }' — apply to 5 selectors. For BrandSliderField range input, add a subtle box-shadow glow since the native focus is OS-hidden. This pattern recurring 4x suggests adding a lint check or a stronger skill rule. 05-accessibility.md §3 violation.
close_reason: Added :focus-visible canonical outline to BrandEditorColors .colors-level__header/__clear/__drill (3 selectors). Panel .brand-bar__expand handled via sr3g8/om6ws. BrandSliderField already had :focus-visible at line 128. Also added type=button to BrandEditorColors buttons and aria-hidden to chevron.

title: [ds-review-iter-015] PublishSidebar disabled publish button uses title= as sole disclosure (WCAG 1.4.13)
description: File: PublishSidebar.svelte:237. `title={isReadyToPublish ? '' : 'Complete all readiness checks before publishing'}` is the ONLY disclosure explaining WHY the publish button is disabled. Keyboard-only users never see it (title activates on mouse-hover only); fails WCAG 1.4.13 Content on Hover or Focus. Cross-ref: iter-14 SubscriptionBadge had the same anti-pattern (Codex-nvosv), which was promoted into ref 05 §Checkout §5 "Never title attribute as sole overflow disclosure." Fix: render a visible `.field-hint` under the publish button when !isReadyToPublish with the same copy, OR wrap the button in a Melt UI createTooltip that activates on focus AND hover. Recommend the hint — it's simpler and the readiness-checks list is already shown above, so a short hint closes the loop.
close_reason: Closed

title: ORG-03: OrgHeader & Org Layout Activation
description:
Wire existing OrgHeader.svelte into _org/[slug]/+layout.svelte, replacing the inline stub header.

## Current State
- **OrgHeader.svelte** fully implemented at `apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/Header/OrgHeader.svelte`
  - Props: `{ user: LayoutUser | null; org: LayoutOrganization }` where LayoutOrganization = `{ name: string; slug: string; logoUrl?: string }`
  - Uses `getOrgNav(slug)` for nav links, `UserMenu` for auth, `MobileNav` for responsive
  - Full CSS with design tokens, sticky header, responsive breakpoints
- **+layout.svelte** at `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/+layout.svelte` uses an INLINE STUB header (plain anchor with org name, raw nav links, no styling)
- **+layout.server.ts** fetches real org data via `getOrganization(slug)` from `org.remote.ts`, returns: `{ org: { id, slug, name, description, logoUrl, brandColors }, user: locals.user }`
- **Error boundary** exists at `_org/[slug]/+error.svelte`

## Type Mismatch to Resolve
The layout server returns `org` with fields `{ id, slug, name, description, logoUrl, brandColors }` but OrgHeader expects `LayoutOrganization` which only has `{ name, slug, logoUrl? }`. This is fine for passing (extra fields are ignored), but the layout should also pass brandColors for CSS variable injection.

## Tasks
1. **Replace inline header** in `+layout.svelte` with `<OrgHeader user={data.user} org={data.org} />`
2. **Add CSS variable injection** for org brand colors in the layout wrapper div using `style:--brand-primary={data.org.brandColors?.primary}` etc.
3. **Add footer with i18n** — the current footer has hardcoded "Powered by Revelations" text; replace with paraglide `m.footer_*()` keys
4. **Verify getOrgNav links** — currently returns `[{slug}/Explore, {slug}/creators/Creators, /library/Library]`. The "Explore" link points to `/{slug}` (the landing page), not `/{slug}/explore`. Decide if this is intentional or should point to `/explore`.
5. **Add dark mode support** — ensure layout wrapper respects `[data-theme='dark']`

## Files to Modify
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/+layout.svelte` — main change
- `apps/web/src/lib/config/navigation.ts` — verify/fix org nav links if needed

## Files to Reference (read-only)
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/Header/OrgHeader.svelte` — component to import
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/+layout.server.ts` — data shape
- `apps/web/src/lib/types.ts` — LayoutUser, LayoutOrganization types

## Effort: 1-2 hours
notes:
## Verified Codebase State (2026-03-18)

### OrgHeader Props (exact)
```typescript
interface Props {
  user: LayoutUser | null;  // { name: string; email: string; image?: string }
  org: LayoutOrganization;  // { name: string; slug: string; logoUrl?: string }
}
```

### Layout Server Data Shape (exact)
```typescript
return {
  org: { id, slug, name, description, logoUrl, brandColors },
  user: locals.user,  // from auth session
};
```

### getOrgNav(slug) returns:
- `/{slug}` -> "Explore" (points to landing, not /explore)
- `/{slug}/creators` -> "Creators"
- `/library` -> "Library"

### Sub-components used by OrgHeader
- `UserMenu` from `./UserMenu.svelte` — exists in same Header directory
- `MobileNav` from `./MobileNav.svelte` — exists, accepts `variant="org"`, `user`, `links`
- `PageContainer` from `$lib/components/ui` — exists

### What error boundaries exist
- `_org/[slug]/+error.svelte` — exists
- `_org/[slug]/(space)/+error.svelte` — exists

### PR Review Agent Findings (Feb 2026) — PARTIALLY RESOLVED
- Footer i18n: still hardcoded text ("Powered by Revelations") — needs paraglide keys
- Dark mode: layout has no `<style>` block at all — needs adding
- OrgHeader JSDoc: component already has no JSDoc but is simple enough; not critical

title: [ds-review-iter-026] +error.svelte studio templates missing :focus-visible on .back-link (R14, WCAG 2.4.7)
description:
**R14 violation (WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible) — accessibility blocker.**

10 near-identical studio +error.svelte templates contain `<a class="back-link">` with NO `:focus-visible` rule. Browser UA defaults are inconsistent (often absent on dark surfaces, weak on tinted-surface buttons). Studio is `ssr=false` — no progressive-enhancement fallback — so keyboard-only users get no visible focus indicator on the only navigation control.

Files (all 10 need the same fix):
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/+error.svelte:87-104
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/customers/+error.svelte
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/+error.svelte
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/team/+error.svelte
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/content/+error.svelte
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/content/new/+error.svelte
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/content/[contentId]/edit/+error.svelte
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/billing/+error.svelte
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/media/+error.svelte
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/analytics/+error.svelte

**Suggested fix (architectural, not mechanical):**

These 10 templates are >95% identical (only the `m.studio_*_error_*` keys + href change). They should be consolidated into a single `<StudioErrorPage>` component under `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/StudioErrorPage.svelte` accepting `title`, `description`, `backHref`, `backLabel` props.

The shared component MUST add the canonical R14 rule:
```css
.back-link:focus-visible {
  outline: var(--border-width-thick) solid var(--color-focus);
  outline-offset: var(--space-0-5);
}
```

Also surfaces a related R1 cluster: all 10 use `max-width: 480px;` (no token in that range — closest is --container-sm at 640px). The consolidated component should either inline a magic 30rem with comment or kick a token decision to a sibling bead.

Side benefit: removes ~1000 LOC of duplication across 10 files.

**Why architectural:** the fix touches 10 files + 1 new shared component + needs a design decision on whether to add a `--container-xs` token. Mechanical patching the 10 :focus-visible rules in place would land R14 compliance but leave the duplication smell.

title: [ds-review-iter-018] Brand save: CACHE_KV slug invalidation race — fonts/colors don't persist on reload
description:
Root cause of user-reported 'font families not saved' bug.

PIPELINE:
1. User saves in brand editor → PUT /api/organizations/:id/settings/branding
2. Handler at workers/organization-api/src/routes/settings.ts:258 calls invalidateBrandAndCache(ctx, orgId)
3. invalidateBrandAndCache (lines 171-203) builds three tasks:
   - updateBrandCache(env, orgId) — refresh BRAND_KV brand:{slug} blob
   - cache.invalidate(orgId) — bump CACHE_KV orgId version
   - cache.invalidate(slug) after DB lookup — bump CACHE_KV slug version (THE CRITICAL ONE)
4. All three run via ctx.executionCtx.waitUntil(Promise.all(tasks)) — fire-and-forget
5. HTTP 200 returns to client BEFORE tasks complete
6. Client (BrandEditorMount) reloads or gets invalidated
7. +layout.server.ts calls api.org.getPublicInfo(slug) → worker /public/:slug/info → cache.get(slug, ORG_CONFIG, ..., 30min)
8. Cache hits stale slug-keyed entry — returns pre-save branding → font/color reverts

RACE WINDOW: time between HTTP 200 and waitUntil completion (typically ~5-50ms but variable). Any reload in that window hits stale data.

EVIDENCE:
- workers/organization-api/src/routes/settings.ts:179 — tasks[] composition
- workers/organization-api/src/routes/settings.ts:203 — waitUntil wrapping
- workers/organization-api/src/routes/organizations.ts:362-365 — slug-keyed 30min cache read
- apps/web/src/lib/remote/branding.remote.ts:261 — client only awaits invalidateCache(platform, orgId), NOT slug

FIX:
Move cache.invalidate(slug) — and specifically only the slug one — OUT of waitUntil and into the request path. The DB lookup for slug is ~5ms; the KV write is another ~5-50ms; acceptable cost for correctness.

Keep updateBrandCache (BRAND_KV blob) and orgId invalidation in waitUntil — those are belt-and-suspenders for other consumers and don't block the correctness of the public info endpoint.

Alternative: accept slug as param in updateBrandingCommand so branding.remote.ts can call invalidateCache(platform, slug) inline.

TESTS: integration test in packages/platform-settings/src/__tests__/branding-settings-service.test.ts that asserts slug cache version post-PUT is strictly greater than pre-PUT version, WITHOUT waiting for waitUntil drain.

LABEL: ds-review, ds-review-iter-018
notes: Closed in commit 0a29dc7e. Full writeup (root cause, fix, tests, verification) at docs/brand-editor-investigation/implementation-summary.md — see the bead's named section.
close_reason: Fixed in commit

title: Debug & Fix: Platform Layout
description:
## Page Purpose
THE MOST CRITICAL LAYOUT. Wraps all (platform)/* pages. Owns version staleness detection, progress sync, and visibility refresh.

## Key Files
- Layout: apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/+layout.svelte
- Server: apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/+layout.server.ts
- Version manifest: apps/web/src/lib/client/version-manifest.ts
- Progress sync: apps/web/src/lib/collections/progress-sync.ts
- Header: apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/Header/PlatformHeader.svelte
- UserMenu: apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/Header/UserMenu.svelte
- Footer: apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Layout/Footer.svelte
- Navigation: apps/web/src/lib/config/navigation.ts (PLATFORM_NAV)

## Data Flow
1. Server load: depends('cache:versions'), reads KV version keys for user library
2. Client $effect: diffs data.versions vs localStorage manifest via getStaleKeys()
3. If stale: invalidateCollection('library') → refetch from server
4. Updates localStorage manifest via updateStoredVersions()
5. onMount: visibilitychange → invalidate('cache:versions') to re-run server load
6. onMount: initProgressSync(data.user.id) for 30s background sync

## What to Verify with Playwright
- Header renders with correct nav (Discover, Pricing, Library)
- UserMenu shows avatar+name when authenticated, Sign In/Register when not
- Version staleness: navigate away, return to tab → versions re-checked
- Progress sync initializes (no duplicate initialization)
- Footer renders
- Layout responsive (mobile nav, desktop nav)
- MobileNav component works (hamburger menu)
- No console errors
- NavigationProgress bar shows during navigation

## CRITICAL: This layout is the orchestrator
- Do NOT duplicate staleness check in child pages
- initProgressSync called ONCE here, never in children
- $derived(data.user) creates reactive user for header
- depends('cache:versions') enables client invalidation
close_reason: Verified: header, UserMenu, MobileNav, client-side nav, footer, version staleness, visibility handler, progress sync all working. Fixed logout bug: cookie.delete missing domain param for cross-subdomain cookies. Updated CLAUDE.md with layout hierarchy and cookie management docs.

title: PS: Org layout wiring + URL param + beforeunload
description:
## Org Layout Wiring for Brand Editor

**File**: apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/+layout.svelte

### Steps
1. Import BrandEditorPanel and brandEditor store
2. Conditionally render <BrandEditorPanel> after .org-layout div when page.url.searchParams.has('brandEditor')
3. On panel mount: lazy-fetch user's org membership to verify admin role (use existing membership API endpoint). If not admin, do not render panel (or show permission message).
4. On panel close: strip ?brandEditor param via goto(currentUrl, { replaceState: true }) with param removed
5. Add beforeunload event listener via \$effect:
   - Add listener when brandEditor.isDirty is true
   - Remove listener when isDirty becomes false or panel closes
   - Handler: e.preventDefault(); e.returnValue = ''; (triggers browser's native "Leave site?" dialog)
6. Pass saved branding data to panel: orgId, savedBranding from layout data
7. Ensure panel survives SvelteKit client-side navigation (it's in persistent layout)

### Verification
- Navigate to /?brandEditor=true → panel appears
- Navigate to /library (client-side) → panel persists
- Close panel → URL loses ?brandEditor param
- With unsaved changes → navigate to external URL → browser shows "Leave site?" dialog
- Non-admin user → panel does NOT render
- Browser back button after close → does NOT re-open panel (replaceState)

### Rules
- MUST check admin role before rendering — NEVER show editor to non-admins
- MUST strip URL param on close with replaceState to prevent back-button re-open
- MUST add beforeunload only when dirty, remove when clean
- MUST use Svelte 5 state management (page from \$app/state, not \$page from stores)
- MUST handle the case where user navigates to a non-org route (panel unmounts gracefully, state preserved in sessionStorage)
close_reason: Org layout wired: BrandEditorPanel renders outside .org-layout, activated via ?brandEditor URL param, opens store with saved branding data, beforeunload + beforeNavigate guards, close handler strips URL param. Level routing renders correct component. Zero type errors.

title: Debug & Fix: Org Explore Page (/[slug]/explore)
description:
## Page Purpose
Content browse/explore within an organization. Shows published content for this specific org.

## Key Files
- Page: apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/explore/+page.svelte
- Server: apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/explore/+page.server.ts (if exists)

- Collection: apps/web/src/lib/collections/content.ts (contentCollection)

## Data Flow
1. Server load: fetches published content for this org
2. Should use contentCollection with hydrateIfNeeded
3. useLiveQuery with ssrData fallback

## What to Verify with Playwright
- Published content loads for this org (not all orgs)
- ContentCard components render correctly
- Content links navigate to /content/[contentSlug]
- Search/filter if present
- Pagination if present
- CACHE_HEADERS.DYNAMIC_PUBLIC
- hydrateIfNeeded called in onMount
- useLiveQuery with ssrData (not destructured!)
- Console errors
- Empty state for orgs with no content
- Responsive grid

## Known Patterns
- Content scoped to org (filtered by orgId)
- contentCollection is QueryClient-backed (session only)

title: [ds-review-iter-09] StudioSidebar nav tooltip relies on title attribute — poor a11y in collapsed mode
description: apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/StudioSidebar/StudioSidebar.svelte lines 89, 113, 135, 156 use `title={collapsed ? link.label : undefined}` for collapsed-sidebar tooltips. The `title` attribute has well-documented a11y problems: invisible on mobile, inconsistent keyboard behaviour, no styling control. In collapsed mode the icon-only buttons are the primary navigation. Replace with either (a) a proper tooltip primitive (Melt UI tooltip already wrapped), or (b) visually-hidden `<span class="sr-only">` after the icon so screen readers announce them regardless of collapsed state. Option (b) is simpler and matches the pattern used elsewhere.
close_reason: Already resolved by commit 73d1586c (2026-04-22 'agent' refactor): the title attribute pattern was removed entirely. StudioSidebar.svelte no longer uses title={collapsed ? link.label : undefined} — the file delegates nav rows to StudioSidebarItem.svelte (lines 60-89), which uses aria-label={label} on the anchor (announced by screen readers in both collapsed and expanded states) and a Melt UI tooltip primitive (createTooltip + use:melt={$trigger}) for sighted hover tooltips. This matches the bead's Option A and matches the codebase reference pattern in SidebarRailItem.svelte. No code change required.

title: SD: Discard + beforeunload + close/minimize flow
description:
## Discard, Close, and Minimize Flow

### Steps
1. Discard: brandEditor.discard()
   - Clear pendingValues
   - Re-apply savedValues to DOM (revert all CSS vars to saved state)
   - Clear sessionStorage key 'codex:brand-editor'
   - Revert theme preview to original
2. Close (X button): brandEditor.close()
   - Persist pending to sessionStorage (already done by $effect)
   - Revert CSS vars to saved state
   - Revert theme preview
   - Set isOpen = false, isMinimized = false
   - Strip ?brandEditor URL param via goto with replaceState
3. Minimize (– button): brandEditor.minimize()
   - Set isMinimized = true
   - CSS vars STAY applied (user can see changes on page)
   - Panel shrinks to compact bar
4. Expand (▲ button): brandEditor.expand()
   - Set isMinimized = false
   - Panel expands to full, at same navigation level
5. beforeunload: handled in PS-3 org layout wiring

### Verification
- Discard → all pending changes gone, CSS reverts, sessionStorage cleared
- Close → panel disappears, CSS reverts, sessionStorage retains pending changes
- Reopen after close → pending changes restored from sessionStorage
- Minimize → panel shrinks, CSS stays applied (live preview continues)
- Expand → full panel returns at same level

### Rules
- Close MUST NOT clear sessionStorage — only Discard clears it
- Minimize MUST keep CSS vars applied (user browsing with pending changes visible)
- Close MUST revert CSS to saved values (unsaved changes not visible to non-editors)
- MUST use Svelte 5 state ($state for isOpen, isMinimized)
close_reason: Discard + beforeunload + close/minimize flow implemented in org layout wiring: beforeunload fires when isDirty, beforeNavigate prompts confirm, close handler checks dirty state, discard() reverts to saved values. All in _org/[slug]/+layout.svelte.

title: VP2-9: Cinema mode (YouTube theater)
description: New feature: cinema mode makes the video player full-width of the viewport but not fullscreen. Like YouTube's theater mode. Implementation: (1) Add cinemaMode boolean state to VideoPlayer. (2) When active, the .content-detail__player container breaks out of its max-width constraint and goes edge-to-edge. (3) Use CSS: when cinema active, .content-detail__player gets max-width: none, width: 100vw, margin-left: calc(-50vw + 50%), border-radius: 0. (4) The video aspect ratio stays 16:9 but fills viewport width. (5) Cinema button in config pill toggles this state. (6) ESC key exits cinema mode (if not in fullscreen). (7) Cinema state saved to preferences (see VP2-11). Files: VideoPlayer.svelte, styles.css, ContentDetailView.svelte (container override)
notes: Epic: Codex-hpw1 (Video Player V2)

title: Make upload-complete idempotent — return 200 for already-advanced states
description:
workers/content-api/src/routes/media.ts:236-294 throws ConflictError for any status besides uploading/uploaded. Frontend retries on flaky networks during the transcoding/ready window get a 409 error card — when they should see success.

Skill rule (new — ref 07 upload-complete idempotency): idempotent completion endpoints return 200 with { alreadyCompleted: true, status } on already-advanced states. 409 only for truly invalid transitions.

Site: workers/content-api/src/routes/media.ts:236-294

Fix:
switch (media.status) {
  case 'uploading':  // transition uploading → uploaded, trigger transcoding
  case 'uploaded':   // already uploaded, re-dispatch transcoding
    break;
  case 'transcoding':
  case 'ready':
  case 'failed':
    return { alreadyCompleted: true, status: media.status, media };
  default:
    throw new ConflictError('Invalid media status', { status: media.status });
}

Reference: skill backend-dev §6 Routes checklist 'idempotent completion endpoints return 200 on already-advanced states' + ref 07 upload-complete section.

Source: backend-dev-review iter 2.
close_reason: POST /:id/upload-complete now returns 200 { alreadyCompleted: true, status, media } when status is transcoding/ready/failed instead of 409. Frontend retries no longer see spurious errors. See backend-dev skill ref 07 — 'idempotent completion endpoints'.

title: Add pagination limits to listUserLibrary — unbounded fetch loads all data into memory
description: ContentAccessService.listUserLibrary fetches ALL purchases and ALL membership content into memory with no LIMIT, then paginates in JavaScript. For users with many purchases or orgs with large catalogues, this causes per-request memory and latency issues. Fix: use DB-level UNION+LIMIT, or at minimum add a safety cap (e.g. limit: 500) to both queries. Pagination total should come from COUNT query, not .length of unbounded result.

title: Fix CSS violations: hardcoded values in existing components
description: Existing components have hardcoded CSS values that violate our design token standards: StudioSidebar uses hardcoded 240px width, VideoPlayer uses hardcoded rgba colors. These need to be converted to CSS variables.
notes:
## CSS Violations to Fix

Fix hardcoded pixel values and colors in existing components to use design tokens.

### Files to Fix

#### 1. StudioSidebar.svelte
**File**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/StudioSidebar/StudioSidebar.svelte`
**Line 114**: `width: 240px;` → `width: var(--sidebar-width);`
**Line 124**: Hardcoded breakpoint → use `@media (min-width: 768px)`

#### 2. VideoPlayer/styles.css
**File**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/VideoPlayer/styles.css`
**Lines 30-31**: `rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.2)` → `hsl(from var(--color-neutral-900) h s 70% / 0.2)`
**Lines 99-114**: Media Chrome theme uses hardcoded rgba values

### Required Design Tokens

Add to `apps/web/src/lib/styles/tokens/layout.css`:
```css
:root {
  --sidebar-width: var(--space-60); /* 240px */
  --z-sticky: 10;
  --z-sidebar: 50;
}
```

Add to `apps/web/src/lib/styles/tokens/components.css`:
```css
:root {
  --media-control-bg: transparent;
  --media-control-bg-hover: hsl(0 0% 100% / 0.1);
  --media-primary-color: var(--color-text-inverse);
  --media-secondary-color: hsl(0 0% 100% / 0.8);
  --media-range-track-bg: hsl(0 0% 100% / 0.3);
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] StudioSidebar uses var(--sidebar-width) instead of 240px
- [ ] VideoPlayer CSS uses color tokens instead of hardcoded rgba
- [ ] Design tokens added to tokens/layout.css and tokens/components.css
- [ ] Dark mode overrides present where needed

## References
- CSS Agent specification: .claude/skills/pr-review-agent-team/agents/css.md
close_reason: Completed in feat/css-violations branch

title: FEAT: Palette generator — OKLCH hue rotation with auto-apply
description:
## Palette Generator

### Overview
Generate harmonious secondary/accent colors from a primary seed using OKLCH hue rotation. Auto-applies immediately (like presets). For non-designers who need help picking complementary colors.

### Algorithm
Using existing oklch-math.ts functions (hexToOklch, oklchToHex, clampToGamut):
- Complementary: secondary hue = primary + 180°
- Analogous: secondary = +30°, accent = -30°
- Split-complementary: secondary = +150°, accent = +210°
- Triadic: secondary = +120°, accent = +240°

Maintain same lightness. Reduce chroma to 80% for secondary. Clamp to sRGB gamut.

### New file
`apps/web/src/lib/brand-editor/palette-generator.ts`
Export: `generatePalette(primaryHex: string, strategy: PaletteStrategy): PaletteResult`

### UI
Add 'Generate Palette' dropdown in BrandEditorColors.svelte above color sections. Clicking a strategy auto-applies secondary + accent via brandEditor.updateField().

### Integration with dual-theme branding
When generating, apply to both light and dark (user can fine-tune each independently after).

### Verification
- Select primary color → click Generate → Complementary
- Secondary and accent auto-fill with harmonious colors
- Colors are visually distinct and pleasant
- Works for any primary color including edge cases (pure white, pure black, highly saturated)

See docs/brand-editor-v2-handover.md §4.1
close_reason: Verified: 4 palette strategies work — Complementary generates hue+180° (golden from blue primary), Triadic generates distinct secondary+accent (red+green). Auto-applies immediately, dropdown auto-closes.

title: [ds-review-iter-017] --space-32 token undefined — 3 remaining callers reference missing token
description: Discovered during iter-017 implementation. apps/web/src/lib/styles/tokens/spacing.css defines --space-0 through --space-24; --space-32 does NOT exist. Yet 3 files (beyond the MediaSection:280 already fixed iter-017) reference var(--space-32): (1) apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/pricing/+page.svelte:1952 (and possibly other lines), (2) studio content edit page, (3) studio content new page. The value resolves to initial (CSS equivalent of unset for custom props), silently breaking any min-height/padding that relies on it. Two fix options: (a) ADD --space-32 to spacing.css as 128px / 8rem (extending the scale); (b) SWEEP the 3 callers and pick an existing token (most likely --space-24 which is 96px, or a different scale value per-callsite). Option (a) is less invasive and extends the scale consistently. Verify first: grep -rn "var(--space-32)" apps/web/src/ to confirm the full caller set.
close_reason: Added --space-32 (128px * density) to apps/web/src/lib/styles/tokens/spacing.css. Extends scale consistently (16→20→24→32). Confirmed 4 call sites (one more than bead's 3): (1) apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/+page.svelte:2265 hero logo-hero base size, (2) apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/content/new/+page.svelte:45 form skeleton height, (3) apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/content/[contentId]/edit/+page.svelte:55 form skeleton height, (4) apps/web/src/lib/components/VideoPlayer/styles.css:1035 quality menu min-width. All now resolve to 128px. Verified: grep for --space-32 in spacing.css confirms definition present; typecheck shows only pre-existing errors unrelated to CSS tokens. Commit 52a1709b pushed to main.

title: WP-12: Audio-reactive shader presets (8 shaders)
description: Add uAudioBass/Mids/Treble/Amplitude uniforms to 8 shader renderers: pulse, aurora, nebula, plasma, ripple, silk, caustic, flux. Each renderer reads audio?.bass ?? 0 in render(), sets uniforms. GLSL modulates existing visual params. Files: renderers/{preset}-renderer.ts + shaders/{preset}-*.frag.ts for each
close_reason: All 25 presets now audio-reactive. Pulse+ripple (earlier), T0-T5 batches complete.

title: [ds-review-iter-015] MediaUpload div role=button → button/label pattern (WCAG 2.1.1 blocker)
description: File: apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/MediaUpload.svelte:261-280. `<div role="button" tabindex="0">` wraps the drop-zone and manually handles keydown (Space/Enter) to open the native file chooser. This is named explicitly as an anti-pattern in ref 05 §11 (no role=button on div) and is a WCAG 2.1.1 keyboard failure — the custom keybinding must replicate ALL native button behaviours, and the nested `<input type="file" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1">` is SR-invisible. Fix per new ref 05 §File input + drag-drop upload §1: wrap a visible styled <label for="media-file-input"> around the drop-zone span; give the <input type="file" accept="…"> a class="sr-only" (NOT aria-hidden), remove tabindex=-1; drag handlers stay on the label (or a sibling wrapper if they conflict). This is the canonical template — once fixed, MediaUpload becomes the in-repo reference cited by the skill patch.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix R2Service.put() arguments in ImageProcessingService
description:
R2Service.put() signature is:
`put(key, value, metadata?, options?)`

But ImageProcessingService passes:
`.put(keys.sm, variants.sm, undefined, { contentType, cacheControl })`

Impact: Cache headers not applied, poor CDN performance.

Locations:
- packages/image-processing/src/service.ts:77-89
- packages/image-processing/src/service.ts:134-146  
- packages/image-processing/src/service.ts:192-195

Fix: Either:
1. Pass empty object: `.put(keys.sm, variants.sm, {}, { contentType, cacheControl })`
2. OR update R2Service to accept options as 3rd param (breaking change)
close_reason: Closed

title: AU: Opacity token migration (48 hardcoded values)
description:
## AU-1: Opacity Token Migration

**VERIFIED**: 47 instances across 38 files.

### Scope
Replace hardcoded `opacity: 0.X` with `var(--opacity-N)` tokens.

### All violations (47 instances)

**UI Components (15 files, 16 instances):**
Button.svelte:74 (0.5), Switch.svelte:88 (0.5), Checkbox.svelte:120 (0.5), Label.svelte:29 (0.5), Pagination.svelte:272 (0.5), AccordionTrigger.svelte:60 (0.5), TabsTrigger.svelte:51 (0.5), DropdownMenuItem.svelte:50 (0.5), Input.svelte:117 (0.7), TextArea.svelte:122 (0.7), ResponsiveImage.svelte:108 (0.5), ErrorBanner.svelte:63 (0.9), ErrorBoundary.svelte:88 (0.9), NavigationProgress.svelte:86 (0.4), Spinner.svelte:66 (0.4)

**Studio Components (10 files, 14 instances):**
ContentTable.svelte:292 (0.5), MemberTable.svelte:318 (0.5), TopContentTable.svelte:122 (0.5), EditMediaDialog.svelte:178,212 (0.6x2), InviteMemberDialog.svelte:168,205 (0.6x2), LogoUpload.svelte:259,333 (0.6x2), RevenueChart.svelte:171,216,219 (0.8,0.4,0.8), PublishSidebar.svelte:335 (0.6)

**Player/Editor (2 files, 2 instances):**
PreviewPlayer.svelte:417 (0.6), EditorToolbar.svelte:343 (0.4)

**Route Pages (13 files, 15 instances):**
Various pages using opacity: 0.5-0.9

### Replacement mapping
opacity: 0.4 → var(--opacity-40)
opacity: 0.5 → var(--opacity-50)
opacity: 0.6 → var(--opacity-60)
opacity: 0.7 → var(--opacity-70)
opacity: 0.8 → var(--opacity-80)
opacity: 0.9 → var(--opacity-90)

### Verification
`rg --glob '*.svelte' --glob '!*.stories.svelte' -c 'opacity:\s*0\.\d' apps/web/src | awk -F: '{sum+=$2} END{print sum}'`
Expected: 47 → target: 0

See docs/frontend-audit-results.md AU-1 section.
close_reason: 47 hardcoded opacity values replaced with var(--opacity-N) tokens across 38 files. Zero remaining.

title: [ds-review-iter-013] Waveform + WaveformShader use non-reactive matchMedia at mount
description: Files: Waveform.svelte:456, WaveformShader.svelte:204 — window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches captured ONCE at mount. User toggling setting mid-session stays stuck with mount-time value. Directly violates ref 05 §Media Elements §8 (added iter-012). Fix: add reactive subscription: 'const mq = matchMedia(...); reducedMotion = mq.matches; mq.addEventListener("change", (e) => reducedMotion = e.matches); return () => mq.removeEventListener(...)' in onMount cleanup. Preferred per §8: flip to CSS-first @media rule if feasible (WaveformShader needs JS for WebGL rAF halt — keep reactive JS; Waveform could go CSS-only).

title: AUDIT: Comprehensive frontend audit — validate & update all Phase 0 tasks
description:
## Comprehensive Frontend Audit — Validate & Update All Phase 0 Tasks

### Purpose
This is the FIRST task to execute in the Brand Editor epic. It performs a complete, systematic audit of every Svelte component and route page in the frontend, cross-references findings against ALL existing Phase 0 tasks (TM, AU, CE), and updates each task's description with verified file lists, line numbers, and exact violations. No Phase 0 task should begin implementation until this audit has validated its scope.

### Why This Exists
The existing TM/AU/CE tasks were created from preliminary audits that sampled files. This task performs the DEFINITIVE pass — every file, every violation, every component gap — so that implementers have zero ambiguity about what to change.

---

### Execution Strategy

**You MUST use subagents to parallelize this work.** The frontend has ~270 Svelte files. Split into 6 parallel subagents, each covering a directory subtree. Each subagent runs the SAME comprehensive check on its assigned files, then reports structured findings.

#### Subagent 1: UI Components
**Path**: apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/
**Files**: ~60 files (Button, Input, Select, Card, Badge, Checkbox, Switch, Tabs, Dialog, Accordion, Pagination, Label, TextArea, DropdownMenu, Tooltip, SkipLink, Spinner, Skeleton, ResponsiveImage, ErrorBanner, ErrorBoundary, NavigationProgress, ContentCard, CreatorCard, AccountErrorPage)

#### Subagent 2: Studio Components
**Path**: apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/
**Files**: ~25 files (ContentTable, ContentForm, MediaUpload, MediaPicker, MediaCard, MediaGrid, MemberTable, CustomerTable, TopContentTable, ActivityFeed, LogoUpload, ColorPicker, EditMediaDialog, InviteMemberDialog, RevenueChart, content-form/*)

#### Subagent 3: Layout, Library, Player, Editor Components
**Path**: apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/, library/, player/, editor/, org/
**Files**: ~20 files (Header, MobileNav, UserMenu, StudioSidebar, StudioSwitcher, Footer, ContinueWatchingCard, ContinueWatching, LibraryFilters, PreviewPlayer, VideoPlayer, RichTextEditor, EditorToolbar, BubbleMenuBar, SlashMenu, OrgErrorBoundary)

#### Subagent 4: Platform & Auth Routes
**Path**: apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/, (auth)/
**Files**: ~20 files (library, discover, pricing, account/*, become-creator, login, register, forgot-password, reset-password, verify-email, +error.svelte)

#### Subagent 5: Org Routes (space + studio)
**Path**: apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/
**Files**: ~25 files ((space)/+page, explore, library, content/[slug], creators, checkout, studio/content, studio/analytics, studio/media, studio/team, studio/customers, studio/billing, studio/settings/*, +error pages)

#### Subagent 6: Creator Routes
**Path**: apps/web/src/routes/_creators/
**Files**: ~15 files ([username]/+page, [username]/content/*, studio/*, checkout/*, +layout, +error pages)

---

### What Each Subagent MUST Check (THE CHECKLIST)

For EVERY .svelte file in its assigned path, each subagent MUST check ALL of the following. Skip .stories.svelte and test files.

#### A. Color Token Violations
1. Any reference to --color-primary-[0-9] in <style> blocks → should be --color-brand-primary or --color-interactive (feeds TM-1)
2. Any hardcoded rgba() or rgb() values → should use color-mix() with semantic tokens (feeds TM-6)
3. Any hardcoded hex colors (#ffffff, #000000, etc.) in <style> → should use semantic tokens
4. Focus styles using --color-primary-* → should use --color-focus (feeds TM-2)
5. Text on brand backgrounds using 'white' or '#fff' → should use --color-text-on-brand (feeds TM-3)
6. Any reference to --color-brand-accent → note it (currently zero expected, feeds TM-4)
7. Any --color-*-dark pattern → verify it's locally defined or flag as undefined (feeds TM-7)

#### B. Opacity Violations
8. Any hardcoded opacity: 0.X value → should use --opacity-* token (feeds AU-1)

#### C. Transition/Duration Violations
9. Any hardcoded transition duration (0.2s, 300ms, etc.) → should use --duration-* token (feeds AU-2)
10. Any hardcoded easing function → should use --ease-* token

#### D. Typography Violations
11. Any hardcoded line-height (1, 1.2, 1.5, etc.) → should use --leading-* token (feeds AU-3)
12. Any hardcoded letter-spacing → should use --tracking-* token (feeds AU-3)
13. Any hardcoded font-size not using --text-* → flag (feeds AU-3)
14. Any hardcoded font-weight not using --font-* → flag

#### E. Spacing Violations
15. Any hardcoded px/rem for padding/margin/gap → should use --space-* token
16. Any hardcoded border-radius not using --radius-* → flag
17. Any hardcoded box-shadow not using --shadow-* → flag

#### F. Component Reuse Violations
18. Any native <button> or <a class="btn-*"> that should use <Button> component → list file + line (feeds CE-1)
19. Any native <select> with custom CSS that should use <Select> → list (feeds CE-2)
20. Any custom card div (.settings-card, .card-container, .error-card, .plan-card) that should use <Card> → list (feeds CE-3)
21. Any native <input> that should use <Input> component → list
22. Any empty state pattern (icon + title + description + optional CTA) → list (feeds CE-4)
23. Any page header pattern (h1 + action button) → list (feeds CE-5)
24. Any success/error message alert pattern → list (feeds CE-6)
25. Any auth layout duplication (logo + form + footer) → list (feeds CE-7)
26. Any form field pattern (label + input + error) that repeats → list (feeds CE-8)
27. Any progress bar (track + fill) pattern → list (feeds CE-10)
28. Badge using variant="info" or other undefined variant → flag (feeds CE-9)

#### G. Dark Mode Pattern
29. Any :global([data-theme='dark']) block → note if it's redundant (semantic tokens already handle it) or necessary (feeds AU-7)

---

### Output Format

Each subagent MUST produce a structured report in this EXACT format:

```
## [Subagent Name]: [Path]
### Files Audited: [count]

### Findings by Task

#### TM-1 (Interactive color migration)
- [file.svelte:line] — var(--color-primary-500) used for [context]. Replace with var(--color-interactive).
- [file.svelte:line] — var(--color-primary-600) used for hover. Replace with var(--color-interactive-hover).
[... every instance]

#### TM-2 (Focus ring)
- [file.svelte:line] — outline: 2px solid var(--color-primary-500). Replace with var(--color-focus).
[... every instance]

#### TM-3 (Text-on-brand)
[... or "No violations found"]

#### TM-4 (Accent adoption)
- [file.svelte:line] — [component] could use accent variant for [purpose]

#### TM-6 (rgba cleanup)
- [file.svelte:line] — rgba(0,0,0,0.7) should be color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-text) 70%, transparent)

#### TM-7 (Dark mode)
- [file.svelte:line] — --color-text-dark referenced, [defined locally / UNDEFINED]

#### AU-1 (Opacity)
- [file.svelte:line] — opacity: 0.5. Replace with var(--opacity-50).

#### AU-2 (Transitions)
- [file.svelte:line] — transition: all 0.2s. Replace with var(--duration-normal) var(--ease-default).

#### AU-3 (Typography)
- [file.svelte:line] — line-height: 1.5. Replace with var(--leading-normal).

#### AU-7 (Dark mode consolidation)
- [file.svelte] — [count] :global([data-theme='dark']) blocks. [Redundant/Necessary] because [reason].

#### CE-1 (Button reuse)
- [file.svelte:line] — <button class="btn-primary">. Replace with <Button variant="primary">.

#### CE-2 (Select reuse)
[...]

#### CE-3 (Card reuse)
[...]

#### CE-4 (EmptyState extraction)
[...]

#### CE-5 (PageHeader extraction)
[...]

#### CE-6 (Alert extraction)
[...]

#### CE-7 (AuthLayout extraction)
[...]

#### CE-8 (FormField extraction)
[...]

#### CE-9 (Missing variants)
[...]

#### CE-10 (ProgressBar extraction)
[...]

### Summary
- Total violations: [count]
- Files with violations: [count] / [total audited]
- Clean files: [list]
```

---

### After All Subagents Complete

Once all 6 subagents return their reports:

1. **Merge all findings** into a single master audit document at docs/frontend-audit-results.md
2. **For each Phase 0 task (TM-1 through TM-7, AU-1 through AU-8, CE-1 through CE-10)**, run `bd update <task-id> --description="<updated description>"` with:
   - The VERIFIED file list (every file, every line number)
   - The VERIFIED violation count
   - Any NEW violations discovered that weren't in the original task description
   - Any files that were listed in the original task but are actually CLEAN (remove them)
3. **Create NEW tasks** if the audit discovers violation categories not covered by existing tasks
4. **Close or mark as N/A** any existing task that the audit proves has zero actual violations

### Verification
- docs/frontend-audit-results.md exists with complete findings
- Every TM/AU/CE task description has been updated with verified data
- No Phase 0 task has unverified file lists or estimated counts — all MUST be exact
- Running the verification grep commands in each updated task MUST match the documented violation count

### Rules
- MUST use 6 parallel subagents as described — NEVER attempt to audit all 270 files sequentially
- MUST check every item on the 29-point checklist for every file — NEVER skip checks
- MUST produce the structured output format — subagent results that don't follow the format MUST be re-run
- MUST update task descriptions via bd update — NEVER leave tasks with preliminary/estimated data
- MUST create docs/frontend-audit-results.md as the permanent record
- NEVER modify any component code — this task is READ-ONLY audit + task updates
- MUST report files that are CLEAN (zero violations) — this confirms coverage
close_reason: Audit complete. docs/frontend-audit-results.md written with comprehensive findings. All 25 downstream tasks updated with verified file lists, line numbers, and exact violation counts. 3 duplicates identified (Codex-einb, Codex-5bft, Codex-9qdv).

title: [UX cascade] Library / continue-watching — grey-out or tag CANCELLING / revoked entries
description:
Follow-up from Codex-v8bub. Library entries and continue-watching rail currently show all entries uniformly. Once a subscription is CANCELLING or revoked, library items tied to that subscription should indicate the reduced-access state.

Behaviour:
- CANCELLING subscription with currentPeriodEnd in future: library entries stay fully accessible but get a small 'Ending {date}' corner badge
- After period end (status CANCELLED / revocation key present): entries greyed out with 'Subscription ended — reactivate to continue' affordance on hover/tap
- PAST_DUE: similar greyed treatment with 'Payment failed' tag

Data source: libraryCollection + subscriptionCollection join client-side.

Files likely touched:
- apps/web/src/lib/components/library/ (library grid + rails)
- apps/web/src/lib/components/VideoCard / ContentCard — add variant for access-reduced state

Tests:
- Component: each status variant renders correctly
- E2E: user with cancelling subscription sees 'Ending' badge without refreshing

Priority lower than pricing + subscribe-button because it's cosmetic rather than blocking an action.

Out of scope: full library page redesign.
close_reason: Library/continue-watching access-state decoration. New library-access.ts helper (115 lines, 11 unit tests covering cancelling/past-due/revoked/no-sub edge cases). 3 surfaces decorated: platform library grid, org library grid, continue-watching rail. ContentCard.svelte extended with optional accessState/accessStatePeriodEnd/accessStateActionHref props (fully backward-compatible for 25+ other call sites). Conservative fallback treats missing subscription row as 'active' to avoid false greying at platform level. Deferred: org landing inline continue-watching (separate surface, documented for follow-up). Design tokens only, reduced-motion + touch fallbacks, svelte-autofixer clean.

title: Write Section 5: Authorization & Roles
description:
Write the Authorization section of FRONTEND_SPEC.md covering:

## Content:
- Role system (ACTUAL schema: customer + org roles: owner/admin/creator/subscriber/member)
- Layout-based route guards
- Fine-grained permission checks (page-level ownership)
- Organization scoping (user can have different roles in different orgs)
- Client-side UI guards (UX only - server always enforces)

## Context7 Research:
- Query: 'SvelteKit authorization guards layout server load redirect'
- Library: /sveltejs/kit

## Role Hierarchy (from actual DB schema):
- User.role: 'customer' (default)
- OrgMembership.role: 'owner' > 'admin' > 'creator' > 'subscriber' > 'member'

## Guard Examples:
- Layout guards: Check locals.userId, locals.organizationRole
- Page guards: Check resource ownership (content.creatorId === user.id)
- Client guards: Conditional rendering for UX (NEVER trust for security)

## Sources to consolidate:
- _archive/REMOTE_FUNCTIONS_AND_AUTH.md (RBAC section)
- _archive/ARCHITECTURE.md Section 7

## Acceptance:
- Actual role names documented (not fictional ones)
- Guard patterns for each route group
- Permission helper functions defined
- Clear warning: client guards = UX only

title: Access integration test: 'Free content cannot have a price' Zod error on paid content
description: Discovered 2026-04-23 when Codex-yfly6's r2Key fix unblocked the access integration suite. packages/access/src/__tests__/ContentAccessService.integration.test.ts 'should return purchased content with progress' (line ~677) fails inside contentService.create with: 'Free content cannot have a price'. The test creates content with visibility: 'purchased_only' and priceCents: 999. Either: (a) the test's visibility value is stale (should be 'paid' or similar) and needs updating, (b) the content-service validation incorrectly treats 'purchased_only' as 'free', or (c) the schema is requiring a new flag. Trace: @codex/content src/services/content-service.ts:148 throws the error. Check packages/validation/src/content/content-schemas.ts for the contentVisibility + priceCents rule. This is NOT caused by the caching-investigation branch — it's the next layer of pre-existing integration test debt after r2Key was fixed. Blocks my Codex-q3zuf regression assertion (expect organizationId in library response) from running end-to-end, though the assertion is correct and will activate once this Zod validation is aligned.
close_reason: Fixed 2026-04-23 by ZOD_FIXES agent. Root cause: test pre-dated the accessType field existing; tests used visibility: 'purchased_only' as a proxy for 'paid' without setting accessType. The schema's default accessType=FREE + priceCents>0 triggered the Zod refine. Fix: 31 contentService.create call-sites across 3 test files got explicit accessType: 'paid' where priceCents > 0. Files: (1) packages/access/src/__tests__/ContentAccessService.integration.test.ts — 10 calls. (2) packages/content/src/__tests__/integration.test.ts — 1 call. (3) packages/purchase/src/__tests__/purchase-service.test.ts — 20 calls (covers checkout, completePurchase, verifyPurchase, history, getPurchase, refund, dispute, verifyCheckoutSession). All priceCents=0 free-content calls left untouched (default FREE is correct there). Verified: the Zod error is gone. Deeper debt exposed: the access integration 'purchased content' test now fails at savePlaybackProgress with 'No active access' because the test inserts raw purchases rows without the paired contentAccess row purchaseService.completePurchase would normally create — tracked separately.

title: P1: Fix listPublic sort order — createdAt should be publishedAt
description:
## Problem
In `packages/content/src/services/content-service.ts` at line 816-821, the listPublic method sorts by createdAt instead of publishedAt for the 'newest' sort option. Content created months ago but published today appears below recently created content.

## Current code
```typescript
const orderByClause =
  params.sort === 'oldest'
    ? asc(content.createdAt)
    : params.sort === 'title'
      ? asc(content.title)
      : desc(content.createdAt);  // BUG: should be publishedAt
```

## Fix
```typescript
const orderByClause =
  params.sort === 'oldest'
    ? asc(content.publishedAt)
    : params.sort === 'title'
      ? asc(content.title)
      : desc(content.publishedAt);
```

This is safe because listPublic already filters `status = 'published'`, and the publish flow always sets publishedAt. The new composite index (idx_content_org_published) covers this sort order.

## Files to modify
- `packages/content/src/services/content-service.ts` — line ~816-821

## Verification
- Create content A (createdAt: yesterday, publishedAt: yesterday)
- Create content B (createdAt: last week, publishedAt: today)
- Verify B appears before A in 'newest' sort
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: committed in 5dd6034, pushed to main.

title: Bead intake: search for duplicates before filing (5 auth-perf dupes today)
description:
Discovered 2026-04-25: the entire auth-perf chain rooted at Codex-7byh duplicated already-closed work under Codex-byz6. Mapping:

- Codex-7byh (parent investigation) ↔ Codex-byz6 (closed 2026-04-16, 'Epic complete')
- Codex-pxcp (org membership KV cache) ↔ Codex-gafh (closed 2026-04-16)
- Codex-qj5c (continueWatching client-side) ↔ Codex-7x7x (closed 2026-04-16)
- Codex-t22x (session KV investigation) ↔ Codex-y4mv (closed 2026-04-16)
- Codex-dljb (isFollowing client-side) ↔ Codex-ltfk (closed 2026-04-16, refactor) — dljb closed today as test-coverage value-add

Both clusters filed 2026-04-16. Same person (brucemckay), same docs (docs/auth-performance/*). Indicates a planning pass that didn't grep before filing. Cost: today's session burned ~15 min of agent runtime on pxcp + t22x before dupes were caught (both stopped via TaskStop mid-run).

Mitigation options:
1. Add a pre-filing checklist to .claude/skills/ that requires grepping closed beads + relevant docs before bd create.
2. Add a bd alias: 'bd ready-with-dup-check' that lists ready work and runs a fuzzy match against closed beads' titles/descriptions.
3. Naming convention: filename-relevant-keyword in the title — makes grep findable.

Actionable next step: when filing in clusters (epic + N children), do a one-line 'bd list --all --desc-contains=<key-phrase>' for each child before creating it.

Worth noting: the dljb agent did the right thing — it noticed ltfk had already done the refactor and pivoted to filing test coverage. That's the model for what pxcp/qj5c/t22x agents would have done if they'd run to completion. The cost of the duplicates is the wasted agent quota, not bad code.

title: Studio content thumbnail: support upload before content exists (create mode)
description: In studio /content/new, ThumbnailUpload.svelte gates file upload on canUploadFile = !!contentId. Result: in CREATE mode the user only sees 'Use media thumbnail' (which is null for audio anyway) or 'Enter URL' — no drag/drop file upload. After Fix 1 (double-toast) lands, this is the next user-visible gap. Options: (A) two-step flow — create draft then upload (server-side redirect to edit mode after create), or (B) stage uploads to a temp R2 prefix keyed on a client-generated token, then move to {creatorId}/thumbnails/{contentId}/ on create, or (C) accept the current limitation and update copy. Decision needed before implementation. Affects all content types but most acute for audio (where 'Use media thumbnail' is a no-op).

title: Verify: Creator Profile (hero redesign, org affiliations upgrade)

## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/11-creator-profile.md`
Verifies implementation task for Spec 11.

## Verification Checklist

### Hero Section
- [ ] Avatar renders at 96px on desktop
- [ ] Avatar renders at 80px on mobile
- [ ] Name text uses `--text-4xl` token
- [ ] Stats strip visible when creator has content and/or org affiliations
- [ ] Stats strip hidden when both content count and org count are zero
- [ ] Social links have adequate touch targets (at least 44px)
- [ ] Social links open in new tab/window (external links)
- [ ] Bio text clamped to 3 lines with ellipsis

### Org Affiliations
- [ ] Cards use vertical layout (not horizontal)
- [ ] Org logos display at larger size than before
- [ ] "Creator" badge visible on each card
- [ ] Per-org content count shows correct number
- [ ] "View on {OrgName}" link navigates to correct subdomain URL
- [ ] Empty state: no broken UI when creator has zero org affiliations

### Graceful Degradation
- [ ] Profile loads correctly when public profile endpoint returns data
- [ ] Profile loads correctly when public profile endpoint returns 404
- [ ] Profile loads correctly when public profile endpoint times out
- [ ] No error toast or broken layout on endpoint failure
- [ ] Available data still renders (content, org affiliations from other sources)

### Responsive
- [ ] Desktop: full hero layout, side-by-side org cards
- [ ] Tablet: verify intermediate breakpoint
- [ ] Mobile: stacked layout, smaller avatar, adequate spacing

### Dark Theme
- [ ] Hero section looks correct in dark mode
- [ ] Org cards look correct in dark mode
- [ ] Badge styling works in dark mode
- [ ] No contrast issues with text/background

### Code Quality
- [ ] Only 2 files modified (page component + en.json)
- [ ] 5 new i18n keys present in en.json
- [ ] No hardcoded text strings in component
- [ ] Design tokens used for all sizing/spacing/colors
- [ ] No `as any` casts
- [ ] Uses `buildOrgUrl()` for org links (not hardcoded URLs)

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Creator with no bio (bio section gracefully hidden or shows placeholder)
- [ ] Creator with no social links (social section hidden)
- [ ] Creator with one org vs many orgs
- [ ] Very long creator name (no layout overflow)

## Process
If ANY issues are found, fix them and re-verify all checks from the beginning.
