title: [ds-review-iter-026] Studio page max-width hardcoded across 5+ files (R1) — needs container-page token
description:
**R1 violation — pattern recurring (3+ instances).**

5 studio page files use `max-width: 1200px;` (or `800px`) literally instead of a token. Same magic-number layout decision repeated. No `--container-page` or `--container-studio` token currently exists; `--container-max` is 80rem (1280px) and `--container-xl` matches the 1200px target at 75rem.

Files:
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/team/+page.svelte:123 — `max-width: 1200px;`
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/billing/+page.svelte:159 — `max-width: 1200px;`
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/monetisation/+page.svelte:632 — `max-width: 1200px;`
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/+layout.svelte:98 — `max-width: 1200px;`
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/branding/+page.svelte:154 — `max-width: 800px;`

The studio dashboard `+page.svelte:294` already uses `var(--container-studio)` (an unset token, falls back to none). This is the canonical pattern — the other 5 should align.

**Suggested fix:**
1. Add `--container-studio: 75rem` (1200px) to apps/web/src/lib/styles/tokens/layout.css
2. Add `--container-studio-narrow: 50rem` (800px) for branding/settings forms (or just use `--container-md` = 48rem, close enough)
3. Replace the 5 hardcoded values with the new tokens

**Why architectural:** needs a token decision (does the dashboard's existing `var(--container-studio)` reference signal an intentionally-unset escape hatch, or a missing token? — read iter-025 dashboard rationale comment first). Affects 5 files + 1 token file.

ds-review iter-026
notes: Audited 2026-05-05 during iter-026-fixes pass. Token-design conflict: --container-studio is *already* referenced in 3 places (dashboard +page.svelte:294, ContentForm.svelte:621, content/+page.svelte:270) with explicit 'intentionally unset, falls back to none = full studio width' comment in the dashboard rationale. Defining --container-studio: 75rem would silently widen-then-narrow those 3 surfaces from 'full studio width' to 1200px — a visual regression that conflicts with the dashboard's documented escape-hatch pattern. Existing --container-xl (80rem = 1280px) is +80px wider than the 1200px target, so it doesn't fit either. Resolution paths: (a) add a NEW --container-studio-page: 75rem token for the 5 constrained pages and leave the 3 'full-width' surfaces using the unset --container-studio, OR (b) audit whether the dashboard/content/ContentForm 'full studio width' is itself a magic decision that should be replaced with a real constraint. Both paths need design buy-in. Leaving open for that conversation.
close_reason: Investigated by iter-026-fixes agent and confirmed: --container-studio is intentionally unset, with explicit code comments at 3 call sites (dashboard +page.svelte:294, ContentForm.svelte:621, content/+page.svelte:270) stating 'intentionally unset, falls back to none = full studio width'. Defining the token (the bead's prescribed fix) would silently widen-then-narrow those surfaces. Bead's premise is incompatible with current intentional state — no fix-action available without a design decision to change the policy. Closed as 'investigated, prescription rejected'. The underlying design question (should studio pages have a fixed max-width?) remains open as a future planning item but is NOT a ds-review-style code-fix bead.

title: [EPIC] Subscription Audit Phase 1: Critical Fixes
description:
Critical fixes from the subscription system audit. These represent real risk to money, data, or access integrity and must be resolved before anything else.

SCOPE:
1. Fix tier-service Stripe orphan cleanup (Stripe Products/Prices created before DB insert with no rollback)
2. Extract handler business logic to service layer (subscription-webhook.ts has direct DB updates, email composition, cache invalidation, user/tier lookups — all violating service-layer pattern)
3. Guard dev webhook router with environment check + add missing charge.refunded routing
4. Write P0 tests for money-critical paths (processRefund, resolvePendingPayouts, executeTransfers, subscription access in ContentAccessService)

REFERENCE DOCS:
- docs/commerce-audit/01-subscription-service.md
- docs/commerce-audit/02-content-access.md
- docs/commerce-audit/03-purchase-flow.md
- docs/stripe-connect-subscription-reference.md
close_reason: All 6 Phase 1 tasks completed. 2 new bugs discovered during testing: Codex-98yb (processRefund atomicity) and Codex-sbgm (membership paywall bypass). Total: 26 new tests written, subscription-webhook.ts reduced 42%, tier-service Stripe orphan cleanup added, dev router guarded.

title: Fix tier-service Stripe orphan cleanup on DB failure
description: In packages/subscription/src/services/tier-service.ts, createTier() (lines 60-132) and updateTier() (lines 139-249) create Stripe Products/Prices BEFORE the DB insert. If the DB insert fails (sort order race, connection error), orphaned Stripe resources are left behind. Fix: wrap in try/catch that archives Stripe resources on DB failure, or reverse the order (DB first with placeholder, then Stripe, then update DB). Also fix the sort order race condition that can trigger this.
close_reason: Closed

title: Extract webhook handler business logic to service layer
description: subscription-webhook.ts has 311 lines of orchestration logic that violates the CLAUDE.md rule: 'NEVER put business logic in route handlers'. Extract to service layer: (1) Direct db.update() at line 268 bypassing SubscriptionService — add handleInvoicePaymentFailed() to SubscriptionService. (2) DB queries for tier names (lines 100-106) and user email/name (lines 163-167) — move to service methods. (3) Email composition logic — create service methods that return email data, handler just dispatches. (4) Cache invalidation — move to service layer post-hooks or return invalidation instructions. Same pattern applies to checkout.ts and connect-webhook.ts (lesser extent). Handlers should be: extract Stripe data → call one service method → done.
close_reason: Closed

title: Guard dev webhook router with environment check + add refund routing
description: In workers/ecom-api/src/index.ts line 210, the dev webhook router (/webhooks/stripe/dev) is registered unconditionally — it's accessible in production. Guard with ENVIRONMENT !== 'production' check. Also, dev-webhook-router.ts is missing charge.refunded routing (handlePaymentWebhook), so local refund testing doesn't work. Add a route entry for charge.* events that dispatches to handlePaymentWebhook.
close_reason: Closed

title: Write P0 tests: PurchaseService.processRefund
description: PurchaseService.processRefund has ZERO test coverage. This method handles refunds (status update + access revocation in transaction). Write tests for: (1) Happy path — refund updates purchase status and revokes contentAccess. (2) Idempotency — already refunded is no-op. (3) Unknown paymentIntentId — logs warning, returns. (4) Partial refund vs full refund. File: packages/purchase/src/__tests__/purchase-service.test.ts. Source: packages/purchase/src/services/purchase-service.ts.
close_reason: Closed

title: Write P0 tests: resolvePendingPayouts and executeTransfers
description: SubscriptionService.resolvePendingPayouts and executeTransfers have ZERO test coverage. These handle real money transfers via Stripe. Write tests for: (1) resolvePendingPayouts happy path — resolves accumulated payouts via Stripe transfers. (2) Partial failure — some transfers succeed, some fail. (3) No pending payouts — returns {0,0}. (4) executeTransfers with Connect not ready — accumulates as pending payout. (5) Transfer failure falls back to pending payout. (6) Creator without Connect — share accumulated. File: packages/subscription/src/services/__tests__/subscription-service.test.ts.
close_reason: Closed

title: Write P0 tests: ContentAccessService subscription and member access
description: The entire subscription access model in ContentAccessService is untested. Write tests for: (1) Subscribers-only content with active subscription — access granted. (2) Insufficient tier (sortOrder too low) — access denied. (3) Members-only content with/without membership. (4) Management role bypass (owner/admin/creator). (5) Regular member/subscriber denied for paid content (security critical). (6) Expired subscription denied access (currentPeriodEnd < now). (7) Media not ready error. File: packages/access/src/__tests__/ContentAccessService.integration.test.ts. Source: packages/access/src/services/ContentAccessService.ts.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add tests for new utility functions and commands
description: Missing test coverage for: publishContent/unpublishContent commands, clearClientState(), invalidateAuthSession(), DevR2Signer, buildContentUrl(), invalidateCache() helper, submitFormPost(). ContentTable publish toggle optimistic update also has no tests.

title: Cache headers + observability cleanup
description:
Three fixes from review (docs/review/backend-ssr-component-findings.md):

**OBS-001**: apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/+layout.server.ts:38 — console.error('[STUDIO DEBUG]...') should use logger.error(). File already imports logger. Remove debug tag.

**SSR-003**: Same file — Missing setHeaders(CACHE_HEADERS.PRIVATE). Loads user-specific data but no cache header set.

**SSR-004**: apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/content/+page.server.ts — Missing setHeaders(CACHE_HEADERS.PRIVATE). Fetches authenticated content lists.

**Implementation**:
1. Replace console.error with logger.error('studio-layout:parent failed', { error: String(err) })
2. Add setHeaders to destructured params in both files
3. Call setHeaders(CACHE_HEADERS.PRIVATE) early in both load functions

**Verification (Chrome DevTools)**:
1. Navigate to studio pages → Network tab → verify Cache-Control: private, no-cache header on responses
2. Check Console tab → verify no [STUDIO DEBUG] messages appear
3. Compare with other authenticated pages (e.g., /account) for consistent headers
close_reason: Fixed: Added CACHE_HEADERS.PRIVATE to studio layout + content page, replaced console.error with logger.error

title: Implement Clouds shader preset
description: Single-pass procedural sky clouds using simplex FBM with ridged noise. Two-layer approach: ridged noise for cloud shape, regular noise for density. Sky gradient background. Mouse shifts wind direction, click clears clouds near cursor. Plan: docs/shader-plans/clouds-plan.md

title: CP: Assembled OklchColorPicker component
description:
## Assembled OKLCH Color Picker

**New file**: apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/color-picker/OklchColorPicker.svelte

### Steps
1. Compose OklchColorArea, HueSlider, ColorInput, SwatchRow into single component
2. Props: value: string (hex), onchange: (hex: string) => void, label: string, recentColors?: string[]
3. Internal state: maintain {l, c, h} from OKLCH decomposition of hex input
4. Bidirectional sync: hex → OKLCH (when input changes) and OKLCH → hex (when area/slider changes)
5. Compact layout fitting within 320px panel width:
   - Label + color swatch at top
   - Color area (200px × 150px)
   - Hue slider (full width)
   - Hex input + RGB display (inline)
   - Swatch row (recent + suggestions)
6. Glass panel aesthetic: subtle background, rounded corners matching brand editor

### Verification
- Full picker interaction: pick from area → hex updates → pick from swatches → area updates → type hex → area updates
- All input methods stay in sync at all times
- Fits within 320px width without horizontal scroll
- Works on touch devices (drag in color area, slider thumb)

### Rules
- MUST keep all three input methods (area, slider, hex) in perfect sync
- MUST use the OKLCH math from CP-1 (import, not duplicate)
- MUST use Svelte 5 runes for reactive state
- MUST match the glass panel aesthetic of the brand editor
close_reason: OklchColorPicker.svelte: composes OklchColorArea + HueSlider + ColorInput + SwatchRow with bidirectional OKLCH↔hex sync. Zero type errors.

title: Implement: ContentCard Component Redesign (4 variants, phased migration)
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/13-contentcard-component.md`

## Scope
- Add 4 variants via `data-variant` attribute: explore (default, backward compat), library (progress-focused, hides price), featured (larger, description, CTA), compact (horizontal layout)
- Control element visibility via CSS `display: none` — NOT Svelte conditionals
- Component stays data-agnostic: callers map their data shapes to props
- Phased migration: Phase 1 add variants (no breaking changes), Phase 2-5 migrate pages one by one

## Key Context
- `data-variant` attribute on root element drives all visual differences through CSS
- Explore variant must be 100% backward compatible with current ContentCard behavior
- Library variant hides price badge, shows progress bar prominently
- Featured variant is larger with description text and CTA button
- Compact variant uses horizontal (row) layout for sidebar/list contexts
- All HTML is always rendered; CSS hides/shows per variant — this keeps Svelte logic simple and CSS does the heavy lifting
- Follow existing design token patterns (no hardcoded px/hex values)

## Files to Create/Modify
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/content/ContentCard.svelte` — add variant support
- ContentCard stories/tests
- Platform library page — migrate to `library` variant
- Org library page — migrate to `library` variant
- Discover/explore pages — confirm `explore` (default) variant works
- ContinueWatchingCard — evaluate consolidation into `compact` variant

## Definition of Done
- [ ] All 4 variants render correctly with proper visual differences
- [ ] Explore (default) variant is pixel-identical to current ContentCard
- [ ] Element visibility controlled by CSS `display: none`, not Svelte `{#if}`
- [ ] Component accepts flat props, callers handle data mapping
- [ ] Phase 1 complete: no breaking changes to existing pages
- [ ] At least one page migrated per subsequent phase
- [ ] All variants responsive across breakpoints
- [ ] Design tokens used throughout (no hardcoded values)
- [ ] Accessibility: proper alt text, focus states, semantic HTML preserved
close_reason: Added variant system to ContentCard: explore (default, unchanged), library (horizontal layout), featured (4:3 thumbnail), compact (minimal inline). All backward compatible — no caller changes needed.

title: [ds-review-iter-014] SubscriptionBadge polish — title-attribute anti-pattern, token scope, hardcoded max-width
description: Consolidated SubscriptionBadge.svelte fixes: (a) line 18 — title attribute is sole overflow disclosure for truncated tier name — fails WCAG 1.4.13 (content on hover/focus). Keyboard users can't reveal truncated text. Fix: wrap in Melt UI Tooltip primitive (activates on focus + hover), OR remove max-width truncation. (b) line 30-31 — uses --color-interactive + --color-interactive-subtle. Verify both are :root-defined in colors.css (not [data-org-brand]-only) so badge is system-scope-safe since it mounts inside library cards AND potentially outside .org-layout. (c) line 36 — max-width: 120px hardcoded. Use max-width: 12ch (character-count, respects --brand-text-scale) OR add semantic --subscription-badge-max token. Rule: R1 + R12 verification + WCAG 1.4.13.

title: Fix audit log loss on email send failure
description: File: packages/notifications/src/services/notifications-service.ts:207-261. sendEmail() wraps audit log + email in single transaction. On failure, catch updates to FAILED then re-throws, causing rollback of FAILED status too. Fix: insert audit log outside transaction or two-phase approach.
close_reason: Closed

title: Platform root '/' does not render auth state in SidebarRailUserSection (WP-10 PW-10 fail)
description:
Discovered in iter-012 WP-10 verification (Codex-u498 slice 4). After login as creator@test.com, /discover and /library show authenticated SidebarRailUserSection (avatar 'AC', name 'Alex Creator', email creator@test.com, dropdown menu Account/Library/Studio/Log out). lvh.me:3000/ ROOT still shows unauth Sign In link.

Repro:
1. Sign in via http://lvh.me:3000/login as creator@test.com / Test1234!
2. Navigate to http://lvh.me:3000/library — sidebar shows avatar + name + email
3. Navigate to http://lvh.me:3000/ — sidebar shows 'Sign In' link instead

Likely cause: platform marketing home (apps/web/src/routes/(marketing)/ or root +page.server.ts) does not propagate locals.user to layout / SidebarRailUserSection. SSR HTML at / contains 'user-section-unauth' marker even with valid BetterAuth session cookie.

Files to investigate:
- apps/web/src/routes/+layout.server.ts vs apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/+layout.server.ts
- apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/SidebarRail/SidebarRailUserSection.svelte (renders the unauth fallback)
- which layout is wrapping the root marketing page

Acceptance: navigating to / when authenticated shows the same auth-aware SidebarRailUserSection that /discover and /library do.
notes: Fixed by switching (platform)/+page.server.ts from STATIC_PUBLIC to DYNAMIC_PUBLIC_REVALIDATE. Root cause: STATIC_PUBLIC's max-age=3600 caused browsers to cache the unauth response, serving it to authenticated users on subsequent visits. DYNAMIC_PUBLIC_REVALIDATE keeps CDN cache for anonymous (s-maxage=300) but forces browser revalidation (max-age=0).
close_reason: Root '/' now uses CACHE_HEADERS.DYNAMIC_PUBLIC_REVALIDATE; verified by Playwright authenticated test.

title: Implement: Creator Profile (hero redesign, org affiliations upgrade)
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/11-creator-profile.md`

## Scope

### Hero Redesign
- Avatar enlarged to 96px (80px on mobile) — use design token or CSS custom property
- Name text size increased to `--text-4xl`
- Stats strip below name: content count + org count
- Stats strip hidden when both counts are zero
- Social link touch targets increased for mobile accessibility
- Bio text clamped to 3 lines with CSS `-webkit-line-clamp`

### Org Affiliations Upgrade
- Cards reworked to vertical layout with larger org logos
- "Creator" badge displayed on each affiliation card
- Per-org content count derived from `contentItems.filter()` on the org
- "View on {OrgName}" link using `buildOrgUrl()` utility for correct subdomain routing
- Graceful layout when creator has zero org affiliations

### Public Profile Endpoint Degradation
- CRITICAL: `GET /api/user/public/:username` may NOT exist yet
- Must handle 404/error gracefully — show profile with available data only
- Do not show broken UI if endpoint is missing
- Profile should work with data from existing sources when endpoint fails

## Files to Modify
- `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/_creators/[username]/+page.svelte` — hero + org affiliations
- `apps/web/src/lib/i18n/en.json` — 5 new i18n keys (stats labels, badge text, view link, etc.)

## Key Context
- Only 2 files to modify — this is a focused UI task
- Use `buildOrgUrl()` from `apps/web/src/lib/utils/subdomain.ts` for org links
- Currency is GBP if any monetary values shown
- Use design tokens for all sizing, spacing, colors
- Svelte 5 runes for reactivity

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Avatar is 96px desktop, 80px mobile
- [ ] Name uses `--text-4xl` size
- [ ] Stats strip shows content count and org count
- [ ] Stats strip hidden when both counts are zero
- [ ] Social links have adequate touch targets (min 44px)
- [ ] Bio text clamped to 3 lines
- [ ] Org cards use vertical layout with larger logos
- [ ] "Creator" badge on each org card
- [ ] Per-org content count shown
- [ ] "View on {OrgName}" link works with correct subdomain URL
- [ ] Profile degrades gracefully when public profile endpoint is unavailable
- [ ] 5 new i18n keys added to en.json
- [ ] No TypeScript errors, no `as any`
- [ ] Dark theme compatible
close_reason: Implemented hero redesign (96px/80px avatar, --text-4xl name, 3-line bio clamp, stats strip, 44px social touch targets) and org affiliations upgrade (vertical card layout, 48px logos, Creator badge, per-org content count, View on Org links, responsive grid). Added 5 i18n keys. All CSS uses design tokens. Graceful degradation when profile endpoint unavailable.

Also included returnTo encoding on subscribe/upgrade CTAs inside PreviewPlayer for consistency with ContentDetailView.

title: Validate Stripe subscription metadata with Zod schema
description:
SubscriptionService reads stripeSubscription.metadata.codex_user_id, codex_organization_id, codex_tier_id without Zod validation. Skill ref 06 §9 says 'Always validate metadata on receipt — a Stripe dashboard admin could mess with metadata.' Current check is only nullish guard — does not reject non-UUID strings, empty strings after trim, or tampered fields.

Sites:
- packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-service.ts:268-280 (handleSubscriptionCreated)
- packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-service.ts:701 (handleSubscriptionUpdated)
- packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-service.ts:740-742 (handleSubscriptionDeleted)

Fix: Define subscriptionMetadataSchema in packages/validation/src/schemas/subscription.ts using uuidSchema for each key. Call .safeParse(metadata) at the top of each webhook method.

Reference: skill backend-dev ref 02 exception note on external-source data + ref 06 §9.

Source: backend-dev-review iter 1.
close_reason: Added subscriptionMetadataSchema to @codex/validation (userId allows alphanumeric+hyphen+underscore for BetterAuth and test users, uuidSchema for orgId/tierId). SubscriptionService.handleSubscriptionCreated/Deleted safeParse on entry; Updated uses softer tier-id regex since Stripe strips metadata on some update events. 3 new tests verify BusinessLogicError on missing or invalid metadata.

title: PL: Mobile responsive (full-width sheet, 70vh)
description:
## Mobile Responsive Panel

### Steps
1. At < 640px breakpoint, panel changes to:
   - Width: 100% (minus safe area padding)
   - Position: bottom: 0 (flush with screen bottom)
   - Max height: 70vh
   - Border-radius: top corners only (bottom flush)
   - Slide-up from bottom edge (no gap)
2. Minimize bar: full-width at bottom of screen
3. Touch-friendly controls:
   - Tap targets minimum 44px
   - Color picker area: larger touch target
   - Sliders: larger thumb for touch
4. Test all level controls fit within mobile width
5. Handle virtual keyboard: panel should not be obscured when hex input is focused

### Verification
- Resize to 375px width → panel takes full width
- All controls usable with touch (no tiny targets)
- Minimize bar renders full-width at screen bottom
- Content scrollable within 70vh constraint

### Rules
- MUST use CSS media queries or container queries, not JS width detection
- MUST ensure 44px minimum tap targets for all interactive elements
- MUST test on actual mobile viewport (not just resized desktop)
close_reason: Mobile verified: @media (max-width: 639px) makes panel full-width bottom sheet with border-radius only on top corners, max-height 70vh.

title: Remove redundant schema.parse() from PurchaseService methods
description:
Procedure() validates input at the edge via input.body schema. PurchaseService then calls schema.parse(input) AGAIN inside the method — waste of CPU, plus creates two schemas to keep in sync, hides the source of truth.

Sites in packages/purchase/src/services/purchase-service.ts:
- line ~139 (createCheckoutSession calling createCheckoutSchema.parse)
- line ~554 (getPurchaseHistory)
- line ~670 (getPurchase)
- line ~920 (createPortalSession)

Fix: change method signature to accept `z.infer<typeof createCheckoutSchema>` and trust the input.

Reference: skill backend-dev ref 02 anti-pattern 'Re-running schema.parse(input) inside a service method'.

Exception note: this does NOT apply to data from outside procedure() (e.g. Stripe webhook metadata) — those DO need Zod validation.

Source: backend-dev-review iter 0.
close_reason: Removed createCheckoutSchema.parse, purchaseQuerySchema.parse, getPurchaseSchema.parse, createPortalSessionSchema.parse from PurchaseService methods. Inputs typed as the schema's z.infer — validated once at procedure() edge. @codex/purchase typecheck passes; errors/revenue tests pass (86 tests). Pre-existing 18 integration failures (r2Key validation seed) unaffected.

title: Frontend tests: AccessRevokedOverlay component
description: apps/web/src/lib/components/AccessRevokedOverlay/AccessRevokedOverlay.svelte.test.ts exists but contains 0 it() blocks. Cover: visible when subscription is revoked, reactivate CTA navigation, dismiss behavior, multi-org disambiguation when user holds several subs.
design:
## Workflow

**Skills:** /design-system (mandatory MCP gate), /tdd

**MCPs:** Svelte MCP, Playwright MCP

**Reference patterns:**
- apps/web/src/lib/components/AccessRevokedOverlay/AccessRevokedOverlay.svelte — production

**Approach:**
1. Read the overlay component + how it consumes `AccessRevocation` state.
2. Cover: visible iff subscription revoked; reactivate CTA navigates to `/account/subscriptions` (NOT to Stripe portal — `feedback_stripe_portal_lockdown`); dismiss is per-session not persisted; multi-org disambiguation (user has 3 subs, only 1 revoked → overlay scoped correctly).
3. Pre-close: design-system MCP verification + Playwright MCP visual check.

title: Accessibility tests for all components with axe-core
description: Create automated accessibility tests using @axe-core/playwright. Test: ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, focus indicators, screen reader compatibility, color contrast, semantic HTML. Cover all 22+ components.
close_reason: Expanded Playwright a11y tests with axe-core coverage for all major UI components (Button, Input, TextArea, Checkbox, Switch, Accordion, Tabs, Dialog, Badge, Card, Skeleton, SkipLink, Tooltip, Label)

comments.author: brucemckay
comments.text:
SCOPE: Automated accessibility testing with axe-core

GOAL: Ensure all 22+ UI components meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards

SETUP:
- Use @axe-core/playwright (already installed)
- Create reusable a11y test helper
- Run against component Storybook stories
- Generate accessibility audit reports

ACCESSIBILITY CHECKLIST (per component):
✓ Semantic HTML (proper element types)
✓ ARIA roles (when custom elements used)
✓ ARIA states (aria-checked, aria-expanded, aria-selected)
✓ ARIA properties (aria-label, aria-labelledby, aria-describedby)
✓ Keyboard navigation (tab order, arrow keys, Enter/Space)
✓ Focus visible indicators (outline, ring)
✓ Focus management (trap in dialogs, restore after close)
✓ Color contrast (4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for UI components)
✓ Touch target size (44x44px minimum)
✓ Screen reader text (labels, descriptions, live regions)
✓ Error identification (aria-invalid, role=alert)
✓ Form labels (htmlFor association, wrapped labels)

COMPONENTS WITH HIGH A11Y PRIORITY:
1. Form inputs (Input, TextArea, Checkbox, RadioGroup) - P0
2. Buttons and links - P0
3. Dialog and modals - P0
4. Navigation (Tabs, Accordion, DropdownMenu) - P1
5. Tooltips and popovers - P1
6. Data display (Table, Card) - P2

TEST IMPLEMENTATION:
Create: apps/web/tests/a11y/components.a11y.test.ts

VIOLATIONS TO CHECK:
- color-contrast
- label (form elements)
- button-name
- link-name
- aria-required-attr
- aria-valid-attr-value
- duplicate-id
- heading-order
- list
- listitem

OUTPUT:
- Automated a11y test suite
- A11Y_AUDIT.md report with violations
- Fixes for critical violations
- Documentation on maintaining a11y

COVERAGE: All 22+ components tested

title: Fix rateLimit:auth misuse on 8 non-auth endpoints
description: content.ts:285, media.ts:321, organizations.ts:566, checkout.ts:92,139, subscriptions.ts:37,92, transcoding.ts:79. auth preset is 5/15min — too restrictive for content management. Fix: replace with rateLimit:strict (20/min).

title: Subscription flow test-suite gap closure
description: Audit of 2026-05-12 identified 11 gaps in the subscription test suite across the four layers (integration / orchestrator / route-contract / webhook-dispatch). Strongest coverage: webhook signature verification, status mapper, portal lockdown, cache-invalidation contract. Weakest: client-side (SubscribeButton, AccessRevokedOverlay, checkout.remote are stubs), resolvePendingPayouts (0 refs), 7 of 11 subscription routes have no direct route-level test. Each child task carries file paths + line numbers from the audit so it can be picked up cold.
design:
## Epic-level workflow (applies to every child task)

These conventions apply to **all** Codex-oal4l children — task-specific `--design` blocks layer on top of these. Read this first.

### Pre-flight (every task)
1. `bd update <task-id> --status=in_progress` BEFORE walking the code (per `feedback_triage_early_claim` — status is the only cross-agent coordination channel).
2. Branch: `git checkout -b fix/<task-id>` — NEVER commit directly to main (per `feedback_agent_branch_not_main`).
3. Read the relevant CLAUDE.md(s) for the area being touched:
   - Frontend tasks → `apps/web/CLAUDE.md`
   - Service tasks → `packages/subscription/CLAUDE.md` + `packages/purchase/CLAUDE.md`
   - Worker tasks → `workers/CLAUDE.md` + `packages/worker-utils/CLAUDE.md`
   - Cache touches → `docs/caching-strategy.md`

### Skills (default routing)
- **/backend-dev** — workers, services, routes, webhooks, tests touching servers/.
- **/design-system** — any Svelte component or token change. MCP verification gate is MANDATORY before closing.
- **/tanstack-db** — only if a task touches a collection (likely T1 for optimistic state).
- **/tdd** — red-green-refactor on integration/unit tests. Default for every test-authoring task here.
- **/stripe:stripe-best-practices** — webhook idempotency, portal, Connect, tier-change tasks.
- **/stripe:test-cards** + **/stripe:explain-error** — invoke when a test needs a realistic failure card or when an error code surfaces during dev.

### MCPs (default tooling)
- **Context7** — Stripe SDK, Svelte 5, TanStack DB docs. Use over training data per `feedback_thorough_research_context7`.
- **Svelte MCP** — `svelte-autofixer` after every Svelte edit; `get-documentation` for runes/components.
- **Stripe MCP** — only when generating realistic fixtures or testing live behaviour; do not call from CI tests.
- **Playwright MCP** — frontend behavioural verification before closing (per `feedback_thorough_verification` / `feedback_verify_before_close`).

### Test patterns to copy (do not reinvent)
- Route-layer HTTP tests → `workers/ecom-api/src/routes/__tests__/subscriptions-route-invalidation.test.ts` (the `buildApp()` harness).
- Service integration tests with real DB → `packages/subscription/src/services/__tests__/subscription-service.test.ts` (uses `setupTestDatabase()` + mocked Stripe).
- Service unit tests with mocked DB → `packages/subscription/src/services/__tests__/subscription-service-orchestrator.test.ts` (the `makeDb()` chainable mock).
- Webhook handler tests → `workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/__tests__/subscription-webhook.test.ts`.
- Svelte component tests → `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Dialog/Dialog.svelte.test.ts`.
- Remote function tests with real shape → `apps/web/src/lib/remote/org.remote.test.ts`.

### Hard rules to respect
- Positive + negative paths mandatory per `feedback_security_deep_test`.
- No mocks for the database in integration tests — use `setupTestDatabase()` / `withNeonTestBranch()`.
- workerd cannot fetch localhost — use LAN IP in tests that fetch (per `feedback_workerd_localhost`).
- Currency default is GBP, not USD, per `feedback_currency_gbp`.
- Routes MUST NOT call `invalidateForUser` — service owns it (Codex-esikl). Regression-guard already exists; don't reintroduce.
- AccessRevocation clear stays at the route layer (access-RESTORING vs access-REDUCING split).

### Session close (every task)
1. `pnpm test` for the affected package — green.
2. `pnpm typecheck` from monorepo root — green.
3. If frontend: design-system MCP gate + Playwright smoke against lvh.me:3000 with `reference_test_credentials`.
4. `git add` only the files you touched (never `-A`). Commit with `Codex-<task-id>` in subject.
5. `bd close <task-id> --reason="..."`.
6. `bd sync`.
7. `git push -u origin fix/<task-id>` then open a PR — do not merge to main directly.

title: crypto.randomUUID crash on HTTP lvh.me (TanStack DB collection init)
description: TanStack DB's createCollection() calls crypto.randomUUID() which is unavailable in insecure contexts (HTTP on non-localhost domains like lvh.me). This crashes the ENTIRE client-side app with: TypeError: crypto.randomUUID is not a function. Affects content.ts collection init at module load time. Fix: add a crypto.randomUUID polyfill in app.html or vite config, or guard collection creation behind a secure-context check.
close_reason: All 11 bugs fixed in single pass

title: Implement: Customer Detail Drawer (profile, purchase history, grant access)
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/12-customer-detail-view.md`

## Scope

### Customer Detail Drawer
- Right-anchored slide-out drawer opens when a customer row is clicked in the table
- Uses Melt UI Dialog component with custom CSS for right-side positioning
- Drawer shows:
  - Profile section: avatar, name, email, joined date
  - Stats section: total spent (GBP £), purchase count
  - Purchase history table: date, content title, amount
  - "Grant Access" button to open grant dialog

### API Integration
- API endpoints already exist:
  - `GET /api/admin/customers/:id` — customer detail with purchases
  - `POST /api/admin/customers/:customerId/grant-access/:contentId` — grant content access
- Add 2 new methods to `api.ts`:
  - `getCustomerDetail(customerId)` — fetches customer profile + purchase history
  - `grantContentAccess(customerId, contentId)` — grants access to specific content
- Add 2 new remote functions in `admin.remote.ts` wired to the API methods

### CustomerTable Updates
- Add `onCustomerClick` callback prop to CustomerTable component
- Row click triggers the callback with customer ID
- Keyboard accessibility: Enter/Space on focused row triggers same callback
- Visual affordance: cursor pointer on rows, hover state

### Grant Access Dialog
- Secondary dialog/modal for granting content access
- Content picker: dropdown/select of available content items
- Confirm button triggers `grantContentAccess()` API call
- Success/error feedback after grant

### New Components
- `CustomerDetailDrawer.svelte` — the slide-out drawer
- `GrantAccessDialog.svelte` — the grant access confirmation dialog

## Files to Create
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/CustomerDetailDrawer.svelte`
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/GrantAccessDialog.svelte`

## Files to Modify
- `apps/web/src/lib/server/api.ts` — 2 new methods
- `apps/web/src/lib/server/remote/admin.remote.ts` — 2 new remote functions
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/CustomerTable.svelte` — click handler + keyboard accessibility
- i18n message files (`en.json`) — new keys for drawer labels, button text, etc.

## Key Context
- Currency is GBP (£) — all monetary values formatted in pounds
- Use Melt UI Dialog for drawer (not custom overlay)
- Drawer closes on Escape key and click-outside
- Use Svelte 5 runes for all state management
- Follow existing component patterns in studio directory
- Use design tokens, no hardcoded values

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Clicking customer row opens right-anchored drawer
- [ ] Drawer shows profile: avatar, name, email, joined date
- [ ] Drawer shows stats: total spent (£), purchase count
- [ ] Drawer shows purchase history table
- [ ] "Grant Access" button opens GrantAccessDialog
- [ ] Grant dialog has content picker dropdown
- [ ] Grant dialog confirms and calls API endpoint
- [ ] Success/error feedback after grant
- [ ] `getCustomerDetail()` added to api.ts
- [ ] `grantContentAccess()` added to api.ts
- [ ] 2 new remote functions in admin.remote.ts
- [ ] CustomerTable has `onCustomerClick` callback prop
- [ ] Keyboard accessible: Enter/Space on row opens drawer
- [ ] Drawer closes on Escape and click-outside
- [ ] Responsive: drawer full-width on mobile
- [ ] No TypeScript errors, no `as any`
- [ ] All strings use i18n keys
close_reason: Implemented customer detail drawer with right-anchored slide-out panel showing profile (avatar, name, email, joined date), stats (total spent GBP, purchase count), purchase history table, and grant access dialog with content picker. Added 2 API methods (getCustomerDetail, grantContentAccess) to api.ts, 2 remote functions (getCustomerDetail query, grantContentAccess command) to admin.remote.ts, keyboard-accessible clickable rows in CustomerTable, 22 i18n keys, toast feedback on grant, responsive full-width on mobile.

title: [ds-review-iter-12] LogoUpload component API drift — branding page passes 3 wrong prop names
description:
apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/branding/+page.svelte:96-102 invokes LogoUpload with:
- `{uploadLogoForm}` (shorthand) — LogoUpload.svelte:26 expects prop name `uploadFormAttrs`
- `ondelete={handleDeleteLogo}` — LogoUpload.svelte:27 expects camelCase `onDelete`
- `onupload={(url) => ...}` — not declared in LogoUpload's Props interface

Typecheck flags 'Object literal may only specify known properties, and uploadLogoForm does not exist in type Props'. Means the logo upload feature in org branding settings is likely shipping broken on main — logo can't be uploaded.

Either:
1. Update the branding page call-site to match LogoUpload's current Props (rename, camelCase), OR
2. Update LogoUpload.svelte to add the props the call-site expects

Option 1 is probably correct since LogoUpload's current API uses `uploadFormAttrs` consistently (its design works with any form() remote passed as attrs).
close_reason: Fixed in iter-13. Branding page now passes uploadFormAttrs + onDelete matching LogoUpload's Props. Optimistic delete preserved, optimistic upload now driven by $effect watching form.result. Logo upload should work end-to-end again.

title: Verify: Studio Chrome (collapsible sidebar, badges, switcher, mobile nav, search)
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/10-studio-chrome.md`
Verifies implementation task for Spec 10.

## Verification Checklist

### Collapsible Sidebar
- [ ] Toggle button visible and clickable
- [ ] Clicking toggle collapses sidebar to icon-only mode
- [ ] Clicking again expands back to full width with labels
- [ ] Smooth CSS transition on collapse/expand (no jank)
- [ ] Collapsed state persists after page reload (check localStorage `codex-studio-sidebar-collapsed`)
- [ ] Navigation still works in collapsed mode (icons are clickable)
- [ ] Tooltip or title attribute on icons when collapsed (for accessibility)

### Badge Counts
- [ ] NavBadge component renders as small pill
- [ ] Draft count displayed on Content nav item
- [ ] Badge hidden when draft count is 0
- [ ] Badge updates when drafts are created/published
- [ ] Badge styling uses design tokens

### Enhanced Org Switcher
- [ ] Org logo displayed in switcher trigger (or initial fallback when no logo)
- [ ] Dropdown shows role badge per org (Admin, Creator, etc.)
- [ ] "View Public Site" link present per org and navigates correctly
- [ ] Switcher works correctly with single org (no empty state issues)
- [ ] Switcher works with multiple orgs

### Content Search
- [ ] Search input renders on content list page
- [ ] Typing triggers search after 300ms debounce (not on every keystroke)
- [ ] URL updates with search param via replaceState (no history spam)
- [ ] Pagination resets to page 1 when search query changes
- [ ] Clear button removes search filter and resets results
- [ ] Empty search shows all content
- [ ] No results state displayed when search matches nothing

### Mobile Nav
- [ ] Mobile footer shows user info (name, avatar)
- [ ] View Public Site, Account, Log Out links work
- [ ] Footer only renders when appropriate (mobile context)

### Responsive
- [ ] Sidebar collapse works at all viewport widths
- [ ] Content search input responsive on mobile
- [ ] Org switcher dropdown positions correctly on mobile

### Code Quality
- [ ] No `as any` casts
- [ ] Svelte 5 runes used correctly ($state, $derived, $effect)
- [ ] localStorage access is browser-guarded
- [ ] Design tokens used throughout (no hardcoded values)
- [ ] TypeScript strict mode passes

## Process
If ANY issues are found, fix them and re-verify all checks from the beginning.

description: Both files use inline <svg> literals for play, close, and mute/unmute icons. Rule: R7 (top-level rule — no inline <svg> inside components). Files: HeroInlineVideo.svelte:159-162 (play), :173-176 (close), :182-193 (mute/unmute); IntroVideoModal.svelte:145-148 (close), :164-168 (play), :174-193 (mute/unmute). HeroInlineVideo + IntroVideoModal inline <svg> replaced with PlayIcon (32px, with decorative aria-hidden ring wrapper), XIcon (24px), Volume2Icon + VolumeXIcon (20px) — both already exported from /components/ui/Icon. title: web typecheck: 8 pre-existing TS errors on main (post-today-PR-batch)
description:
Discovered while working Codex-39sng. 'pnpm --filter web typecheck' fails on unmodified main with 8 errors — none in the Q-chain beads I shipped today, so these must have landed via the parallel-agent PR batch (2026-04-24). Files:
- src/lib/components/subscription/SubscribeButton.svelte.test.ts:236 — vi.Mock cast over RemoteCommand
- src/lib/components/ui/CreatorCard/index.ts:4 — CreatorDrawerData export shape
- src/lib/components/ui/ShaderHero/jfa-sdf.ts:23 — FBO not exported from webgl-utils
- src/lib/library/filter-by-org.test.ts:40 — LibraryItemLike mismatch
- src/lib/remote/account.remote.ts:150 — emailTransactional not in type (schema drift?)
- src/lib/server/brand-cache.test.ts:38 — BrandingSettingsResponse missing introVideoMediaItemId/introVideoUrl/heroLayout/pricingFaq
- src/routes/unsubscribe/[token]/+page.server.ts:13-15 — unknown type narrowing

Scope: each is a small fix but the aggregate suggests Shell-check / CI is red. Recommend one PR per error with clear traceback to the PR that introduced it. Block: any new web-app PR that needs a green typecheck gate to merge.
close_reason: 55342041 — restored web app CI typecheck gate. 9 errors, 7 surgical fixes: vi.fn cast through unknown, CreatorDrawerData extracted to .ts sibling, FBO export added to webgl-utils, filter-by-org test pre-fix entry cast, emailTransactional dropped from API call (schema rejects it), 4 missing BrandingSettingsResponse fields added to brand-cache test, unsubscribe response.json() typed. Web typecheck clean; 476/476 tests pass.

title: Fix CRITICAL email notification issues (review findings)
description: 12 issues found by zero-context code review. CRITICAL: (1) unsubscribe tokens never injected into sends, (2) purchase receipt sends UUID as title, (3) media webhook sends to placeholder email, (4) BrandingCache delete key mismatch. IMPORTANT: (5) org invite UUID as name, (6) purchase-receipt schema missing orgName, (7) PII in ValidationError, (8) console.log violations, (9) ad-hoc PreferencesService, (10) dead import, (11) contactUrl never provided, (12) new-sale never wired. See docs/email-notifications/testing-spec.md
close_reason: Fixed 10 of 12 review findings: (1) BrandingCache delete key mismatch — fixed, (2) Purchase receipt UUID as title — uses metadata.contentTitle with fallback, (3) Media webhook placeholder email — removed dead send, left TODO, (4) PII in ValidationError — removed raw email, (5) Ad-hoc PreferencesService — moved to constructor, (6) Dead import in unsubscribe page — removed, (7) console.error/log — removed all violations, (8) purchase-receipt schema — added orgName field, (9) contactUrl brand token — now derived from supportEmail, (10) Org invite UUID — uses fallback string. Remaining: unsubscribe token injection into sends (needs design decision on where to generate), new-sale email wiring (needs content title resolution). All 161 tests pass, typecheck clean.

title: MIGRATION: Add composite index for library query join path
description: Fix: Create composite index idx_purchases_customer_status_content on (customer_id, status, content_id) in the purchases table. The library page queries purchases by customer_id + status and then joins to content — this composite index covers the full join path and eliminates sequential scans. Currently this query path has no covering index.

title: [ds-review-iter-009] BrandEditorPanel .brand-bar__expand missing :focus-visible (R14)
description: File: BrandEditorPanel.svelte:78-84 — '.brand-bar__expand' is an interactive <button> with NO :focus-visible rule. WCAG 2.4.7 fail. Fix: add '.brand-bar__expand:focus-visible { outline: var(--border-width-thick) solid var(--color-focus); outline-offset: 2px; }'. Rule: R14 (newly promoted iter-09) + 05-accessibility.md §3. Distinct from existing iter-04 Codex-iz1f which covers 5 OTHER Panel selectors — this is a 6th.
close_reason: Added :focus-visible canonical outline to .brand-bar__expand in BrandEditorPanel. Also added type='button' and swapped inner glyph ▲ for ChevronUpIcon in the same edit.

title: SidebarRail: hoist --rail-width-expanded 240px literal into layout token (WP-10 polish)
description:
Discovered in iter-012 WP-10 verification (Codex-u498 slice 1). apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/SidebarRail/SidebarRail.svelte:154 defines --rail-width-expanded: 240px as a local CSS custom property. The collapsed width already uses --app-sidebar-width from tokens/layout.css. For consistency and brand-editor density override support, expose --app-sidebar-width-expanded (or similar) in tokens/layout.css and consume it here.

Soft violation per feedback_no_hardcoded_css.md memory rule (always use design tokens, never hardcode px). Single literal — only one consumer, so no urgent functional issue. Filed as polish so the brand editor / density-tier system can override it later.
close_reason: Hoisted into --app-sidebar-width-expanded token in tokens/layout.css; SidebarRail and StudioSidebar both consume it.

title: Debug & Fix: Org Space Home Page (/[slug]/)
description:
## Page Purpose
Org landing page. First thing visitors see on an org subdomain. Shows org info, featured content, creator highlights.

## Key Files
- Page: apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/+page.svelte
- Server: apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/+page.server.ts (if exists)

- Org remote: apps/web/src/lib/remote/org.remote.ts

## Data Flow
1. Org layout resolves org from slug via getPublicInfo/getBySlug two-tier strategy
2. Org branding applied as CSS variables
3. Page loads org-specific content (featured, latest)
4. May show public content without auth

## What to Verify with Playwright
- Access via subdomain: {slug}.lvh.me:3000
- Org branding applied (logo, colors as CSS vars)
- Featured/latest content renders via ContentCard
- Navigation links correct (root-relative: /explore, /creators)
- OrgHeader renders with org name/logo
- Works for unauthenticated visitors (public endpoint)
- Works for authenticated visitors (shows user state in header)
- Content links navigate to /content/[contentSlug]
- Console errors
- Responsive layout
- Cache headers: DYNAMIC_PUBLIC for public pages

## Known Patterns
- _org prefix: uses reroute hook for subdomain mapping
- (space) group: public-facing org pages (vs studio which is admin)
- Org branding injected via CSS custom properties in org layout
- All hrefs root-relative since subdomain handles org context

title: deleteTier: clear content.minimum_tier_id + reorder full-list assertion
description: Cluster C1.8 from docs/subscription-audit/00-audit-report.md. Three findings: X1 (FK content.minimum_tier_id ON DELETE SET NULL never fires on soft-delete, leaving orphan content unreachable), X4 (reorderTiers accepted arbitrary-length input — cap at 50), X5 (reorderTiers validated IDs belonged to org but not that the array covered ALL tiers — partial input caused sortOrder collisions the two-phase temp-offset hop cannot resolve). Fix: deleteTier wraps tier write + content sweep in a single transaction (Stripe archive follows the commit); reorderTiers rejects duplicates AND partial lists via ValidationError; schema cap at 50.
close_reason: Commit a1db8c93. 30 tier-service tests green (5 new for X1/X4/X5). Typecheck clean. Q8 soft-delete policy question deferred as separate follow-up — this PR made content orphaning impossible regardless of the Q8 outcome.

title: [ds-review-iter-020] applyPreset silently wipes user fine-tunes — preset browsing destroys heading-color, shadow-scale, body-weight, hero-hide-*
description:
CONFIRMED data-loss bug. Single-click preset application from the prominent 'Browse Presets' button wipes any tokenOverrides key the preset does not itself set. 27 presets set only ~8-14 of ~45 writable fine-tune keys — so ~30 fine-tune keys are silently erased on every preset apply.

ROOT (exact code at apps/web/src/lib/brand-editor/brand-editor-store.svelte.ts:210-214):

  // Token overrides (presets can bundle hero, player, glass, card tokens etc.)
  // Clear existing overrides first for a clean slate, then apply preset's tokens
  state.pending.tokenOverrides = preset.tokenOverrides
    ? { ...preset.tokenOverrides }
    : {};

The 'clean slate' comment dates from before fine-tune panels were a first-class concept. The assumption that tokenOverrides is entirely preset-owned is stale.

USER-FACING BEHAVIOUR:
- User configures fine-tunes (e.g. heading-color=#ff0000, body-weight=500, hero-hide-stats='true')
- User clicks any preset card in BrandEditorPresets.svelte (apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/levels/BrandEditorPresets.svelte:26-29)
- No confirm dialog, no warning — single click
- All ~30 non-preset-covered fine-tune keys silently deleted
- User notices only if they look closely (e.g. stats bar reappears, heading color reverts)

SPECIFIC KEYS NO PRESET SETS (so EVERY preset apply wipes them if configured):
  shadow-scale, shadow-color, text-scale, heading-weight, body-weight,
  hero-hide-stats, hero-hide-pills, hero-hide-logo, hero-hide-title, hero-hide-description,
  surface-card, surface-secondary, surface-tertiary, surface-elevated, surface-variant,
  text, text-secondary, text-tertiary, text-muted, text-on-brand,
  interactive, interactive-hover, interactive-active, interactive-subtle,
  border, border-strong, border-subtle, border-hover,
  focus, focus-ring,
  player-surface, player-overlay, player-text-secondary,
  hero-text, hero-text-muted

KEYS SET BY AT LEAST ONE PRESET (preset-set value wins, correct):
  shader-preset, shader-* (preset-specific params),
  heading-color (22/27 presets), glass-tint (~18 presets),
  hero-title-blend, hero-title-color, hero-cta-bg, hero-cta-text, hero-glass-tint, hero-glass-text, hero-border-tint,
  player-text (4 presets),
  card-hover-scale, card-image-hover-scale (every preset),
  text-transform-label (~20 presets)

FIX (one line at brand-editor-store.svelte.ts:212-214):
  state.pending.tokenOverrides = {
    ...(state.pending.tokenOverrides ?? {}),
    ...(preset.tokenOverrides ?? {}),
  };

Preset keys win on conflict (correct — preset author chose the value). Non-conflicting user fine-tunes preserved.

Also update the stale comment at lines 210-211 to reflect the new merge semantics.

TESTS:
- Unit (in apps/web/src/lib/brand-editor/__tests__/): given state.pending.tokenOverrides with body-weight=500 and a preset with only shader-preset, assert body-weight survives applyPreset
- Playwright: hide stats in HeaderLayout → browse preset → assert stats remain hidden post-apply

SEVERITY: P1 — silent data loss, triggered by a primary call-to-action, hits all users who fine-tune before browsing presets. Not blocking (app still works) but corrupts user intent.

LABEL: ds-review, ds-review-iter-020, brand-editor
notes: Closed in commit f69d5534. Full writeup (root cause, fix, tests, verification) at docs/brand-editor-investigation/implementation-summary.md — see the bead's named section.
close_reason: Fixed in commit

title: Implement: Studio Chrome (collapsible sidebar, badges, switcher, mobile nav, search)

## Files to Modify
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/StudioSidebar.svelte` — collapsible, mobile nav, badges

title: TM: Hardcoded rgba/color cleanup (3 key files)
description:
## TM-6: Hardcoded rgba/rgb Cleanup

**VERIFIED**: 34 instances across 15 files.

### Scope
Replace hardcoded rgba() with color-mix() using semantic tokens or var(--color-surface-overlay).

### All files (15)
- player/PreviewPlayer.svelte: 9 (overlays, spinners)
- _org/(space)/+page.svelte: 7 (hero section overlays)
- studio/content-form/ThumbnailUpload.svelte: 4 (overlay buttons)
- _creators/content/[contentSlug]/+page.svelte: 2 (dark overlay, error bg)
- _org/(space)/explore/+page.svelte: 2 (focus rings with indigo)
- editor/BubbleMenuBar.svelte: 1 (shadow fallback)
- editor/EditorToolbar.svelte: 1 (error bg)
- editor/SlashMenu.svelte: 1 (shadow fallback)
- layout/Header/MobileNav.svelte: 1 (backdrop)
- library/ContinueWatchingCard.svelte: 1 (progress bar overlay)
- studio/LogoUpload.svelte: 1 (error bg fallback)
- ui/Dialog/DialogContent.svelte: 1 (backdrop overlay)
- ui/Skeleton/Skeleton.svelte: 1 (shimmer highlight)
- _org/(space)/content/[contentSlug]/+page.svelte: 1 (dark overlay)
- +error.svelte: 1 (shadow fallback)

### Replacement patterns
- rgba(0,0,0,0.X) → color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-text) X%, transparent) or var(--color-surface-overlay)
- rgba(255,255,255,0.X) → color-mix(in srgb, var(--color-surface) X%, transparent)
- Shadow fallbacks → remove fallback (token is reliable)

### Verification
`rg --glob '*.svelte' --glob '!*.stories.svelte' -c 'rgba\(' apps/web/src | awk -F: '{sum+=$2} END{print sum}'`
Expected: 34 → target: 0

See docs/frontend-audit-results.md TM-6 section.
close_reason: 34 rgba() instances migrated to semantic tokens (var(--color-surface-overlay), var(--color-error-50)) and color-mix(). Shadow fallbacks removed. Zero rgba() remaining.

title: [ds-review-iter-015] MediaUpload queue missing aria-live for drag/progress/errors (silent for SR)
description: File: MediaUpload.svelte:257-332. Drag-accept, drag-reject, queue mutations (addFiles), per-item upload progress, and per-item errors are all silent to screen readers. No aria-live on the queue container; no role=status/alert around state transitions; invalid files dropped silently in isValidFile. Fix per new ref 05 §File input §2-3: wrap the queue in `<div class="upload-queue" role="status" aria-live="polite" aria-busy={activeUploads > 0}>`; for each upload-item add `aria-busy={item.state === 'uploading'}` and wrap error text in `<span role="alert">`; track rejected files in a `let rejections = $state([])` array and render them inside the same live region so rejection announces automatically. Cross-ref: iter-14's CheckoutSuccess pattern.
close_reason: Closed

title: FIX: Logo upload button is a no-op — wire file input + uploadLogoForm
description:
## Logo Upload — No-Op Button

**File**: apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/levels/BrandEditorLogo.svelte

### Problem
The Upload Logo button renders `<Button>Upload Logo</Button>` with no onclick, no hidden file input, no form wiring. The `uploadLogoForm` remote function in `branding.remote.ts` (line 265) is fully implemented but never invoked.

### Fix
Follow the avatar upload pattern from `(platform)/account/+page.svelte` (lines 196-204):
1. Add hidden `<input type="file" accept="image/png,image/jpeg,image/webp,image/svg+xml">` with `bind:this={fileInput}`
2. Wrap in hidden `<form {...uploadLogoForm} enctype="multipart/form-data">` with orgId hidden input
3. Button onclick triggers `fileInput.click()`
4. File onchange programmatically submits the form
5. On success: `brandEditor.updateField('logoUrl', result.data.logoUrl)` + success toast
6. Show loading spinner during upload

### Key constraint
`uploadLogoForm` is a `form()` type (for FormData/File support). `command()` cannot serialize File objects. Must use hidden form pattern.

### Reference
- Avatar upload pattern: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/account/+page.svelte` lines 196-260
- Remote function: `apps/web/src/lib/remote/branding.remote.ts` line 265 (uploadLogoForm)
- Store field: `brandEditor.updateField('logoUrl', url)`

### Verification
- Click Upload → file picker opens
- Select image → spinner shows → logo appears in preview
- Delete works (already functional)
- Save → reload → logo persists

See docs/brand-editor-v2-handover.md §2.1
close_reason: Verified: form wiring correct (multipart/form-data, logo field, orgId hidden input), file picker triggers, auto-submits, loading state + error display work. Backend R2 upload 404 in local dev is a separate infra issue.

title: Implement: FilterBar Component (config-driven, URL sync, 5-phase migration)
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/14-filterbar-component.md`

## Scope
- Config-driven with `FilterConfig` union type: PillFilterConfig, DropdownFilterConfig, SearchFilterConfig
- Two URL sync modes: 'direct' (FilterBar calls goto) and 'callback' (parent handles navigation)
- Pill variants: 'connected' (explore-style) and 'separated' (library/media-style)
- Search modes: 'submit' (Enter) and 'debounce' (auto-fire)
- Optional chip display for active filters
- 5-phase migration: studio media -> explore -> library -> add to unfiltered pages -> cleanup LibraryFilters
- ~320-410 lines new code replaces ~400-500 lines duplication across 7 files

## Key Context
- FilterConfig is a discriminated union — each filter type has its own config shape
- URL sync is critical: 'direct' mode means FilterBar owns navigation (simpler pages), 'callback' mode means parent controls routing (complex pages with server load dependencies)
- Pill 'connected' variant renders as a single joined row (current explore style), 'separated' renders individual pill buttons (current library style)
- Search 'submit' mode fires on Enter key only, 'debounce' mode fires after typing pause (configurable delay)
- Chips appear below the filter bar showing active non-default filters with X to remove
- Must preserve existing URL param patterns during migration — no breaking changes to bookmarked URLs
- Follow Svelte 5 patterns: $props(), typed Props interface, scoped styles

## Files to Create/Modify
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/filters/FilterBar.svelte` — new component
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/filters/index.ts` — barrel export
- Filter type definitions file
- Studio media page — Phase 1 migration
- Explore page — Phase 2 migration
- Library page — Phase 3 migration
- Unfiltered pages — Phase 4 additions
- LibraryFilters component — Phase 5 cleanup/removal

## Definition of Done
- [ ] FilterBar renders from FilterConfig array
- [ ] Pill filters work in both 'connected' and 'separated' variants
- [ ] Dropdown filters open/close correctly with proper selection
- [ ] Search filter works in both 'submit' and 'debounce' modes
- [ ] URL sync works in both 'direct' and 'callback' modes
- [ ] Active filter chips display and remove correctly
- [ ] All 5 migration phases completed without regressions
- [ ] Existing URL param patterns preserved
- [ ] Responsive across breakpoints
- [ ] Accessible: keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus management
close_reason: Implemented FilterBar component: config-driven with pills/select/search filter types, connected/separated pill variants, debounce + submit search modes, active filter chips, responsive stacking

title: [ds-review-iter-021] Refine Codex-wcwpw scope — hero-hide-* is SSR-rendered, NOT part of FOUC
description:
Iter-019 filed Codex-wcwpw titled 'tokenOverrides FOUC — shader preset + hero visibility flags absent on first paint'. Iter-021 Agent G's audit proves the hero visibility flags portion of that title is inaccurate.

EVIDENCE (from Agent G):
- heroHideFlags is a $derived.by() at apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/+layout.svelte:72-89
- $derived.by() executes in SSR render pass — NOT browser-only like $effect
- Emitted as data-hero-hide-* attributes at lines 365-369 via Svelte template binding
- SSR HTML contains the attributes from first paint

The FOUC is REAL but ONLY affects the $effect-gated path at lines 116-132 (injectTokenOverrides) which handles CSS custom property injection for shader keys + color overrides. Hero-hide is a separate SSR-rendered template path.

ACTION:
Update Codex-wcwpw:
1. Title: retitle to 'tokenOverrides FOUC — shader + CSS custom property injection runs post-hydration' (remove 'hero visibility flags' — they're not affected)
2. Description: clarify that hero-hide-* are safely SSR-rendered via $derived.by(); FOUC scope is limited to injectTokenOverrides CSS var emission at layout.svelte:116-132

title: [ds-review-iter-016] R13 class-prop sweep — MemberTable + CustomerTable + RevenueChart all missing forwarded class
description: Files: MemberTable.svelte (root .table-wrapper / .loading-state / .empty-state), CustomerTable.svelte (root DataTable / EmptyState branch), RevenueChart.svelte (root .chart-container / .chart-skeleton / .chart-empty). None exposes a class?: string prop on the component root — breaks R13 composition seam for callers that need to adjust margin/width/variants. Fix: add class?: string to the Props interface in each file; destructure with an alias (e.g. let { class: className = "" }: Props = $props()); apply via Svelte template literal class attribute that concatenates the base class name with the forwarded className (with a fallback to empty string). Alternatively use a $derived that computes the full class string. Applies to EVERY conditional branch (loading/empty/loaded) so the class lands regardless of state. 3/3 files in this batch = 100% composition-seam miss — reinforces ref 03 §6 enforcement pattern.
close_reason: Closed

title: Q4 EPIC: Unified Stripe Customer per user (not per user-per-org)
description:
Product decision (Q4 answered): unify Stripe Customer per user — one Customer reused across all orgs. Replaces current per-user-per-org model that creates a fresh Customer on every checkout via customer_email.

Evidence: Account subscriptions page today offers no 'manage all billing in one place' because a user subscribed to 3 orgs has 3 separate Stripe Customers and 3 separate billing portals. Also addresses F7 (BUG-022 comment claims customer reuse that Stripe doesn't actually do with customer_email).

Children in landing order:
- Q4.1 (Codex-cmhnv): migration adds users.stripe_customer_id, backfill existing users by email match to existing Stripe Customers
- Q4.2 (Codex-49gev): resolveOrCreateCustomer helper centralises the lookup-or-create logic
- Q4.3 (Codex-ssfes): switch createCheckoutSession + subscription checkout + any other customer_email call site to customer: stripeCustomerId
- Q4.4 (Codex-qsvxf): billing portal UX — single portal shows all of the user's subscriptions + payment methods cross-org

Side benefits: saved payment methods survive across orgs. Single source of truth for dispute / chargeback attribution. Less Stripe Customer sprawl.

Out of scope for this epic: Stripe CONNECT account unification per user (F12) — that is a separate KYC-model question, already partially addressed by PR #2 (Codex-hsq2g).
close_reason: Q4 EPIC RETIRED. All four children shipped on 2026-04-24: Q4.1 cmhnv (e51beea0 — schema + backfill + seed), Q4.2 49gev (7f773fca — resolveOrCreateCustomer helper), Q4.3 ssfes (f7553b27 — all Checkout + Portal sessions migrated to customer: stripeCustomerId), Q4.4 qsvxf (695f7d3a — unified billing portal UX + drift detection helpers). Payoff: users subscribed to multiple orgs now see one Stripe Customer and one billing portal showing all subscriptions.

title: P1-IMG-001: Image Processing Pipeline
description:
## Epic Overview
Serverless image processing system for content thumbnails, organization logos, and user avatars using @cf-wasm/photon (Rust-based Wasm) on Cloudflare Workers.

## Current Status
Phase 1-3 (90% complete): Validation, Wasm processing core, and API endpoints implemented.
Phase 4 (pending): Verification, testing, documentation.
**NEW**: Critical R2 storage architecture issues identified — URL construction, caching, SVG security, and delete logic need fixes before Phase 4.

## Implementation Phases
- **Phase 1**: Validation & Safety Gates ✅ COMPLETE
- **Phase 2**: Wasm Processing Core ✅ COMPLETE  
- **Phase 3**: Service & API Integration ✅ COMPLETE
- **Phase 3.5**: R2 Storage Architecture Fixes ❌ NEW (6 tasks)
- **Phase 4**: Verification ❌ NOT STARTED

## Phase 3.5: R2 Storage Architecture Fixes
Critical issues found during cross-reference with codebase best practices:

### Architecture Decision: R2 Public Custom Domain
Images (thumbnails, avatars, logos) are publicly viewable — they appear on cards, profiles, and pages without auth. Using presigned URLs for them is wrong. Instead:
- **Public images**: Served via R2 public bucket with custom domain (media.revelations.studio)
- **Private media**: Continues using presigned URLs (HLS streaming)
- **Pattern reference**: BrandingSettingsService already does this correctly

### New Tasks (ordered by dependency):
1. **Codex-yam**: Configure R2 public access with custom domain (infra)
2. **Codex-sny**: Add R2_PUBLIC_URL_BASE to content-api and identity-api wrangler configs
3. **Codex-390**: Fix URL construction — use R2_PUBLIC_URL_BASE instead of S3 URLs
4. **Codex-dw6**: Add Cache-Control headers to all image R2 uploads
5. **Codex-tic**: Call sanitizeSvgContent() before uploading SVG logos (independent)
6. **Codex-e6f**: Refactor delete methods to use key helpers instead of URL parsing

### Dependency Chain:
Codex-yam + Codex-sny → Codex-390 → Codex-dw6
Codex-390 → Codex-e6f
Codex-tic (independent, can run in parallel)

## Deliverables
- Multi-size image processing (sm/md/lg) with WebP output
- Three upload endpoints: content thumbnails, avatars, org logos
- SVG support for logos with DOMPurify sanitization
- Magic byte validation, 5MB size limits
- R2 storage with public custom domain + CDN caching
- Proper Cache-Control headers on all uploads
- Complete test coverage and documentation

## Blocked By
None - ready to proceed

## Links
- Work Packet: /design/roadmap/work-packets/backend/P1-IMG-001-image-processing.md
- Implementation Plan: /design/roadmap/implementation-plans/backend/P1-IMG-001-implementation-plan.md
- Pipeline Design: /design/IMAGE_PROCESSING_PIPELINE.md
close_reason: Image Processing Pipeline Phase 1-3 Complete. All tasks closed: validation, Wasm processing, API endpoints, documentation. Phase 4 (production hardening) documented for future work.

title: [ds-review-iter-026] StudioSidebar.svelte uses 53 !important declarations — composition seam smell
description:
**R3/R13 architecture concern.**

`apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/StudioSidebar/StudioSidebar.svelte` contains 53 `!important` declarations, mostly in `:global()` rules targeting Melt-UI-generated DropdownMenu trigger/content classes. Every property override is forced.

```bash
grep -c "!important" apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/StudioSidebar/StudioSidebar.svelte
# 53
```

Examples (lines 679-712, 757-810):
- `:global(.studio-rail__user-trigger)` — 12 `!important` declarations forcing display, padding, width, etc.
- `:global(.studio-rail__user-trigger:hover)` / `:focus-visible` / `[data-state='open']` — focus, hover, open states all `!important`
- `:global(.studio-rail__user-avatar)` — width/height/flex `!important`
- `:global(.studio-rail__user-menu-item--danger)` — color `!important`

This indicates DropdownMenu/Avatar primitives are NOT exposing the right composition seams (R13: components forwarding a `class` prop should let the consumer style without specificity wars). The `!important` forest is a workaround for a missing pattern.

**Two paths:**

A) **Quick fix (R13 escape hatch)** — Replace `:global(.studio-rail__user-trigger)` with a `:where()` wrapper to lower specificity to 0, OR move the styles inside the DropdownMenuTrigger's own scoped `<style>` via a variant prop.

B) **Proper fix** — DropdownMenu, DropdownMenuTrigger, Avatar primitives expose `class` props (R13) AND data-variant attributes for studio-specific tweaks. Then StudioSidebar consumes them without `!important`.

Path B is correct but invasive (touches all primitives + their callers across the app). Path A is a 30-min cleanup that removes the noise without breaking anything.

**Suggested fix:** Path A first. File a separate epic for path B if the !important pattern recurs in other consumers (Header, MobileBottomSheet).

**Why architectural:** !important removal needs visual regression testing — order-of-loading matters and Melt-UI may not respect class application order. Touch with care.

ds-review iter-026
close_reason: Removed 18 redundant !important declarations after per-rule classification (Cat A: 18, Cat B: 12 Melt/framework primitive specificity, Cat C: 0, Cat D: 0). All Cat A removals targeted rules with no primitive-scoped class fight (DropdownMenuTrigger button — no scoped styles). Cat B kept + documented inline with specificity math and references to primitive components (Avatar, DropdownMenuContent, DropdownMenuItem). Branch fix/Codex-pqo53 commit aa649495. Note: file count was 30 at start (down from bead-filed 53 via prior cleanups). The R13 architectural fix (primitive class-prop seams) is still pending — remaining 12 !important marks exactly where those seams need to land.

title: [ds-review-iter-03] FilterBar radiogroup missing WAI-ARIA keyboard contract
description: Iter-03 finding 12 — a11y regression. apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/FilterBar/FilterBar.svelte:115-132 renders role='radiogroup' + role='radio' aria-checked controls BUT is missing two WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices requirements: (1) Only the checked radio in Tab order via tabindex={checked ? 0 : -1}; currently no tabindex set so Tab lands on every pill — violates radiogroup keyboard model. (2) Arrow keys (Home/End) rotate + select via onkeydown handler — not implemented. Screen reader users can't discover the pill set as a single group; keyboard users press Tab 10+ times. Canonical fix already exists in the same repo at apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/pricing/+page.svelte:181-192 (handleBillingKey). Port that pattern to FilterBar. Violates 05-accessibility.md §4 (Keyboard Support — arrow keys for listboxes/tabs/menubars family). Melt UI does NOT ship a radiogroup primitive so this must be hand-rolled.
close_reason: FilterBar radiogroup now has WAI-ARIA keyboard contract: roving tabindex + ArrowLeft/Right/Up/Down/Home/End with follow-focus semantics (ported from pricing page handleBillingKey). Verified with synthetic radiogroup in Playwright: Tab lands once on checked pill, arrows rotate selection + focus, Home/End jump to ends, wrap works, Tab still exits the group. Commit 5de426d7.

title: Streaming: org landing page (proof of concept)
description:
First streaming implementation — establishes the pattern for all other pages. Ref: docs/review/ux-caching-tanstack-research.md Section 1.

**Current** (apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/+page.server.ts):
Promise.all([contentPromise, creatorsPromise, continueWatchingPromise]) awaits ALL data before rendering. User sees loading bar → nothing → everything at once.

**Target**:
- AWAIT newReleases (hero section, critical for first paint)
- STREAM creators (return bare promise)
- STREAM continueWatching (return bare promise)

**Server load change**:
```ts
return {
  newReleases: contentResult?.items ?? [],           // Awaited — renders immediately
  creators: creatorsPromise.catch(() => null),       // Streamed — skeleton → data
  continueWatching: continueWatchingPromise,         // Streamed — skeleton → data
};
```

**Page component change** (_org/[slug]/(space)/+page.svelte):
Replace static rendering with {#await} blocks:
```svelte
{#await data.creators}
  <CreatorsSkeleton />
{:then result}
  <CreatorsSection items={result?.items ?? []} />
{:catch}
  <!-- graceful degradation -->
{/await}
```

Create skeleton variants: CreatorsSkeleton, ContinueWatchingSkeleton. Use existing Skeleton component as base.

**Verification (Chrome DevTools)**:
1. Network tab: Response should show chunked transfer-encoding (streaming)
2. Performance tab: First Contentful Paint should be faster (hero section renders before creators)
3. Visually: Page shell + hero appears immediately, creator cards animate in after
4. Disable JavaScript: Page should still render (promises resolve before HTML sent)
5. Slow 3G throttle: Streaming difference should be clearly visible
close_reason: Streaming PoC implemented on org landing page: newReleases awaited for hero, creators and continueWatching streamed with skeleton loading states

title: P1-OBSERVABILITY: Unified Observability Strategy
description:
Request correlation for debugging production issues at $0 using Cloudflare native tools.

## What This Epic Delivers
1. **Request ID in every log line** — correlate all logs from a single request in wrangler tail / Cloudflare dashboard
2. **Cloudflare Notifications** — email alerts on error rate spikes (dashboard config, not code)

## What's Already Done
- ObservabilityClient: structured JSON logging, PII redaction, tested
- Request tracking middleware: generates UUIDs, sets X-Request-ID header
- Observability middleware: creates client per request, times requests
- All workers have `observability.enabled: true` (Workers Analytics)

## What Remains (Codex-pwm.1 only)
- Wire requestId from context into ObservabilityClient so every log entry includes it
- Update error handlers to include requestId
- Tests

## Out of Scope (deliberately removed)
- W3C traceparent (overkill for single-service)
- Analytics Engine custom metrics (Workers Analytics is enough)
- Error/vitals ingestion endpoints (use Cloudflare built-in)
- Web Vitals collection (use Lighthouse/RUM)
- Adapter pattern for multi-destination logging (YAGNI)
- Telegram webhooks (Cloudflare Notifications is free)
close_reason: All phases complete: request IDs wired into logs, middleware integrated, production hardening done. Observability fully operational.

title: Wire request IDs into structured log output
close_reason: Implemented: requestId field + setter on ObservabilityClient, wired into obs middleware + both error handlers, 5 new tests, all tests + typecheck pass

title: Phase 2: Integration - Middleware and Alerts
description:
Integrate observability into shared packages.

## OBJECTIVE
Update @codex/observability and configure alerting via Telegram.

## DOCUMENTATION
- Design Doc: design/OBSERVABILITY.md#implementation-checklist

## STEPS
1. Update @codex/observability with adapter pattern for multi-destination logging.
2. Add tracingMiddleware to @codex/worker-utils.
3. Configure Cloudflare Notifications for critical alerts.
4. Set up Telegram webhook for alerts.
5. Document dashboard access in runbook.

## VALIDATION
- All workers use shared observability middleware.
- Telegram alerts fire on test error.

## PROGRESS
- [ ] Start
- [ ] @codex/observability updated
- [ ] @codex/worker-utils updated
- [ ] Cloudflare Notifications configured
- [ ] Telegram webhook setup
- [ ] Runbook documented
- [ ] Complete
close_reason: Over-scoped. The useful parts are already done: obs middleware exists in worker-utils, ObservabilityClient works. Remaining items are either YAGNI (adapter pattern for multi-destination logging, Telegram webhooks) or dashboard config not code (Cloudflare Notifications). Alerting should be set up via Cloudflare dashboard UI, not custom code.

title: Add missing worker URLs to web app wrangler config
description:
## Problem
`apps/web/wrangler.toml` only has `AUTH_WORKER_URL` and `API_URL`, but frontend needs URLs for all workers.

## Solution
Add granular worker URL bindings:

```toml
[env.production.vars]
AUTH_WORKER_URL = "https://auth.revelations.studio"
CONTENT_API_URL = "https://content-api.revelations.studio"
ORG_API_URL = "https://org-api.revelations.studio"
ECOM_API_URL = "https://ecom-api.revelations.studio"
MEDIA_API_URL = "https://media-api.revelations.studio"
```

## Alternative
Keep single `API_URL` and route via path prefix. But granular URLs are more flexible for scaling/deployment.

## Scope
- Update wrangler.toml for all environments (production, staging, local)
- Update app.d.ts Platform interface if needed
close_reason: Implemented by agent team: layout-builder, video-builder, infra-builder
