title: Fix cross-org library cache bleed (server type + client filter)
description: Users see library content from unrelated orgs when visiting an org subdomain's /library. Root cause: libraryCollection is a global localStorage store keyed by content.id; the org /library page filters by item.content.organizationSlug === orgSlug but the server payload (UserLibraryResponse.items[].content.organizationSlug) is string|null and legacy rows can be null, slipping the filter. Fix bundle: (1) Add non-null organizationId: string to UserLibraryResponse in packages/access/src/types.ts and select content.organizationId in all three ContentAccessService.listUserLibrary sub-queries (purchased/membership/subscription). (2) Change org page filter in apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/library/+page.svelte from organizationSlug === orgSlug to organizationId === data.org.id. (3) In loadLibraryFromServer, purge localStorage entries without organizationId after reconciliation (treat as corrupt legacy). Investigation: docs/caching-investigation/team-a-findings.md §Bug 2.
notes: Implementation complete + regression assertion added. Files: packages/access/src/types.ts (UserLibraryResponse.items[].content.organizationId: string non-null), packages/access/src/services/ContentAccessService.ts (3x select orgId: content.organizationId, 3x mapping organizationId: row.orgId, plus local UserLibraryItem interface), apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/library/+page.svelte (filter by organizationId === orgId, legacy null-org entries naturally excluded), apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/+page.svelte (continueWatching filter — second leak point found during verification grep). Regression assertion added to packages/access/src/__tests__/ContentAccessService.integration.test.ts (expect item?.content.organizationId to equal organizationId). NOTE: The access integration test file currently has a pre-existing r2Key validation failure at setup (hardcoded 'originals/library-test.mp4'), blocking the assertion from running end-to-end — NOT caused by this fix. Typecheck ✓, focused access tests run was earlier clean before r2Key tightening. Visual verification pending (browser MCP locked).
close_reason: Verified fixed end-to-end via Playwright MCP on 2026-04-23. After clearing localStorage + reload on studio-beta.lvh.me:3000/library: server payload has organizationId on all 10 items (8 studio-alpha id f9965f78, 2 studio-beta id 9d258a12). Page shows ONLY the 2 studio-beta items (/content/building-apis-with-hono, /content/written-tutorial-getting-started); zero alpha bleed. Reverse test on studio-alpha.lvh.me:3000/library shows ONLY the 8 alpha items; zero beta bleed (hasBetaContent: false). Server rebuild required (pnpm --filter=@codex/access build) — during verification caught and fixed a Drizzle type-inference edge case (organizationId widened to string | null because LEFT JOIN inference didn't propagate .notNull()).

title: Frontend: PriceBadge shows 'Followers' label to subscribers who have access via subscription (Codex-xybr3 follow-up)
description:
Follow-up to Codex-xybr3 which inverted the access hierarchy to subscribers ⊇ followers. Backend now grants followers-only content access to active subscribers without needing a follower row, but PriceBadge.svelte still labels followers-only content as 'Followers' for non-following subscribers (misleading — they actually have access).

Affected surface: every component that renders a ContentCard or uses PriceBadge directly for followers-only content — ContentCard, RelatedContent, AudioWall, ArticleEditorial, DiscoverMix, ContentDetailView.

Required change (conceptual):
1. PriceBadge.svelte: add 'isSubscribed' or 'subscriptionCoversFollowers' prop; when true for accessType='followers', render the 'Included' variant + check icon (same as follower-viewing path).
2. Thread that state from the same sources that already thread isFollower (likely data.isFollowing + a subscription-active check from subscriptionCollection / subscriptionContext).
3. Regression test: PriceBadge renders 'Included' when accessType='followers' and isSubscribed=true.

Note: the ContentDetailView follow-CTA already auto-hides for subscribers because it gates on hasAccess === false, and the backend now returns hasAccess=true for subscribers viewing followers-only content. This follow-up is only about badge copy, not the gate CTA.
close_reason: PriceBadge correctness landed in 4dd3f5fa — extended useAccessContext.isIncluded to cover subscribers ⊇ followers (Codex-xybr3 follow-up). 15 unit tests, 51 test files passing. Tightened subscription read to access-granting statuses (active|cancelling) matching backend ContentAccessService — also closes pre-existing past_due-shows-Included false-positive on subscriber-gated content. Pure decideIsIncluded helper extracted (mirrors library-access.ts pattern) so logic is testable without runes runtime.

title: CI: migration 0043_code_review_schema_fixes.sql fails with 'idx_unique_org_slug already exists'
description:
Discovered 2026-05-06 during ds-review PR audit. Every PR's E2E API Tests step fails at the same migration with:

```
📝 Applying: 0043_code_review_schema_fixes.sql
  ✗ Failed: relation 'idx_unique_org_slug' already exists
```

Migration 0043 attempts to create an index that exists from earlier migrations. Either:
1. The index was previously created without IF NOT EXISTS guard and a subsequent migration didn't drop it before re-creating
2. Migration order conflict between 0043 and a parallel branch's migration that also created the index

Affects: ALL open PRs (~30 in current queue). Blocks E2E test signal. Static-analysis lint failures are a separate, pre-existing issue tracked in Codex-zf9wf.

Action: investigate packages/database/migrations/0043_code_review_schema_fixes.sql, add IF NOT EXISTS or DROP-before-CREATE, verify CI re-runs green.

Sample failing run: https://github.com/brucemckayone/codex/actions/runs/25426203891/job/74580162685

title: [ds-review-iter-025] BrandEditorHeroEffects 1822 lines — manifest extraction stalled at Phase 2
description:
Codex-19h35 Phase 2 (commit 8050e170) is titled 'manifest-driven hero-fx control renderer' and exists on branch fix/Codex-19h35-p2 — but it has NOT landed in main as of 2026-05-05. On origin/main, BrandEditorHeroEffects.svelte remains a 1822-line file with hand-typed if/else if chain across 39 shader presets.

The unmerged feature branch reduces this to 103 lines (1719 deletions) plus a new 191-line ControlField.svelte component and 487-line hero-fx-presets.ts manifest. Diff:
```
git diff --stat origin/main..origin/fix/Codex-19h35-p2 -- apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/
 BrandEditorHeroEffects.svelte           | 1719 +-------------------
 ControlField.svelte                     |  191 +++
```

So this bead is really 'unblock + land Phase 2' rather than 'design the refactor':

**On main today (origin/main b215605c):**
- File: apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/levels/BrandEditorHeroEffects.svelte (1822 lines)
- Lines 13-54: PRESETS array (39 entries — already data)
- Lines 57-314: DEFAULTS record (already data)
- Lines 322-639: ~200 hand-coded $derived(readNum(key)) declarations
- Lines 742-1648: 33 {:else if activePreset === 'X'} branches — each is structurally identical, differs only in slider props

**On fix/Codex-19h35-p2 (8050e170):**
- BrandEditorHeroEffects.svelte: 103 lines (renderer dispatches by manifest)
- New ControlField.svelte (191 lines): dispatches by ControlConfig.kind = slider/color/toggle
- New hero-fx-presets.ts (487 lines): HERO_FX_PRESET_CONTROLS manifest

**Observation from iter-025 ds-review:** the feature branch refactor is well-shaped. Phase 2 commit message also notes 'PLAYWRIGHT VERIFICATION NOT RUN' — visual confirmation that every preset's controls drive the live shader is the gate before merging.

**Recommendation:** unblock the Phase 2 PR by running the visual verification — the architectural design is sound; only the verify gate is holding back a 1719-line dead-code purge from main.

Architectural verdict: not a new design discussion — just merge what's already designed. Lower priority than the original bead implied because the work is mostly done, just needs verification.
close_reason:
Structural equivalence verified across all 41 shader presets + 3 shared sliders + reset behavior + rendering primitives.

Method: parsed both old inline {:else if} branches and new HERO_FX_PRESET_CONTROLS manifest into normalized JSON, then field-by-field compared (key, label, min, max, step, format, minLabel, maxLabel) preserving document order. Result: 41/41 presets match byte-for-byte, 3/3 shared sliders match, sectionLabels match.

Rendering layer: ControlField.svelte uses the same BrandSliderField component, the same DOM (input type=color, button.hero-fx__toggle), and the same class names (hero-fx__color-row, hero-fx__color-label, hero-fx__color-input, hero-fx__toggle-row, hero-fx__toggle-label, hero-fx__toggle, hero-fx__toggle--on, hero-fx__toggle-thumb) as the old inline blocks. Style declarations moved from parent component into ControlField — Svelte scopes per-component, output identical.

DEFAULTS map: Object.keys(DEFAULTS).concat('shader-preset') in old equals Object.keys(HERO_FX_DEFAULTS).concat('shader-preset') in new — same 240 keys, same string values, same SHADER_DEFAULT_PULSE_COLOR import. selectPreset('none') reset behavior covers identical key set.

Note: the bead description said '39 presets' but the actual count was 41 even on main (pollen+geode had inline branches but were missing from PRESETS array; Codex-17ibq landed on the feature branch added them to picker — unrelated to this audit). Old branches: 41. New manifest entries: 41. Match.

Visual regression risk near-zero. Phase 2 (commit 8050e170 + 88e4fffb on fix/Codex-19h35-p2) safe to merge without Playwright run.

title: fix(content/types): widen DatabaseTransaction alongside Tier 6.A Database widening — drop tx as cast
description:
**Finding** (denoise iter-028, F5):

Inside `await this.db.transaction(async (tx) => { ... })` at `packages/content/src/services/content-service.ts:158`, the call:
```typescript
await validateMediaItem(tx as DatabaseTransaction, ...)
```
casts `tx` to `DatabaseTransaction`.

**Root cause**: `DatabaseTransaction` (defined at `packages/content/src/types.ts:28`) is **HTTP-only-derived**:
```typescript
type DatabaseTransaction = Parameters<Parameters<typeof dbHttp.transaction>[0]>[0]
```

After Tier 6.A widened the parent `Database` type to `DatabaseClient = Database | DatabaseWs`, the runtime `tx` can be HTTP **or** WS transaction — but the cast claims HTTP unconditionally.

**Today**: the call surface stays in the HTTP|WS overlap so nothing breaks; the bug is the **type-system bypass that hides future drift**. R11 root cause: `DatabaseTransaction` did not widen alongside `Database` in Tier 6.A.

**Recurrence**: **hit 3 of `types:as-cast-without-guard`** (after iter-005, iter-006). **Reaches R7 standard 3-hit promotion threshold** in trailing 6 cycles → flagged for promotion to R-rule in iter-029 prep.

**Fix shape** (companion fix to Tier 6.A):
```typescript
type DatabaseTransaction =
  | Parameters<Parameters<typeof dbHttp.transaction>[0]>[0]
  | Parameters<Parameters<typeof dbWs.transaction>[0]>[0];
// (or derive from DatabaseClient)
// then drop the `as DatabaseTransaction` at line 158
```

**Proof test**: `packages/content/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-028/F5-databasetransaction-cast-after-tier6a.test.ts`
- Catalogue row 3 (type-equality / structural narrowing test)
- Currently `it.skip(...)`; un-skip in same PR as fix

**Report**: docs/denoise/iter-028.md §F5
close_reason: Widened DatabaseTransaction to union of HTTP+WS transaction types. Dropped cast in content-service.ts. Both transports accepted without coercion.

Remaining work:

2. UX copy: the portal link on account page + on org pricing page can now be phrased as 'Manage all your subscriptions' rather than 'Manage your [Org] subscription'.
3. Return URL handling: the Stripe billing portal's return_url should send users to a neutral landing (probably /account/subscriptions), not a specific org page.
4. Add a one-off opt-in for users who backfilled with an existing Customer ID — handle the edge case where two Codex users map to the same Stripe Customer (should be zero per Q4.1 validation, but belt-and-braces).

Depends on: Q4.3. Unblocks: nothing downstream, but this is the UX payoff of the whole epic.
close_reason: 695f7d3a — Q4.4 closed. Portal already unified via Q4.3, so: (a) 3 i18n copy updates to reflect the unified reality across account payment page, studio billing page, and /account/subscriptions heading; (b) new findUsersByStripeCustomerId + findStripeCustomerCollisions helpers in @codex/purchase with 5 integration tests (all passing). Belt-and-braces per the bead's point 4 — legacy drift detection. All 6 portal entry points already use page.url.href for org-agnostic return. Pre-existing 23 purchase-service.test.ts failures at mediaService.create() filed as separate bead (unrelated to Q4.4).

title: [ds-review-iter-02] ShaderHero burst decay is frame-rate dependent
description: Iter-02 finding 5. ShaderHero.svelte:163-166 does 'mouse.burstStrength *= 0.85' once per frame(). On 120Hz displays the burst decays 2x faster than 60Hz; on throttled 30Hz tabs, half as fast. Disney motion principles in 04-motion.md want frame-rate-independent physics. Fix: compute dt per frame and use exponential decay: 'const now = performance.now(); const dt = (now - lastTime) / 1000; lastTime = now; mouse.burstStrength *= Math.pow(decayPerSecond, dt);' where decayPerSecond is tuned to match current 60fps feel (0.85^60 ≈ 6e-5 → decayPerSecond ≈ 0.01). This is the canonical pattern for JS-driven physics; worth grepping ShaderHero + any other per-frame decay for the same pattern. Blocked-by: none. Motivated skill patch landing in 04-motion.md §7 'Frame-rate-independent motion'.
close_reason: Replaced constant-per-frame decay (0.85) with exponential Math.pow(BURST_DECAY_PER_SECOND, dt) in ShaderHero.svelte active render branch. BURST_DECAY_PER_SECOND = 0.85 ** 60 preserves the 60fps feel exactly. scheduleFrame() resets lastFrameTime before priming RAF to prevent the pause-resume cliff (dt would otherwise be seconds, decaying burst to 0 instantly). dt also clamped to 0.1s as a secondary guard. Motion doc 04-motion.md §4 already documents the pattern — no doc edit needed.

title: [ds-review-iter-026] Studio hover transforms hardcode -2px / -4px / 3px translateY (R1)
description:
**R1 violation — pattern recurring across 5 studio surfaces.**

Multiple studio components hardcode hover-lift transforms in pixel units rather than spacing tokens. Pattern recurs across content cards, media tiles, and command bar buttons.

Instances:
- apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/content-list/ContentFeatureSlab.svelte:205 — `transform: translateY(-2px);` (hover)
- apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/content-list/ContentListCommandBar.svelte:410 — `.create-btn:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); }`
- apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/media-library/MediaTile.svelte:297 — `.tile:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); }`
- apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/media-library/MediaTile.svelte:460 — `transform: translateY(-4px);` (action bar reveal)
- apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/media-library/MediaLibraryCommandBar.svelte:471 — `.upload-btn:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); }`

Also: ContentFeatureSlab.svelte:339,345 use `background-size: 0% 1px / 100% 1px` for an underline-grow animation — `1px` should be `var(--border-width)`.

**Suggested fix:**

The hover-lift idiom is well-established (Disney bounce/anticipation). Two options:

A) Use `var(--space-0-5)` (= 2px @ density 1) for the small lift, `var(--space-1)` (= 4px) for the larger lift. Pros: density-aware, theme-consistent. Cons: ties layout token to motion semantics.

B) Add a small motion-distance scale to motion.css:
```css
--lift-sm: var(--space-0-5);   /* 2px hover lift */
--lift-md: var(--space-1);     /* 4px deeper lift */
```

**Why architectural:** crosses the spacing/motion boundary; needs a design call on whether `--space-*` is the right home for hover-lift distances or whether motion deserves its own physical-displacement scale.

Same issue surfaces on ContentRow.svelte:171 — `transform: translateX(var(--space-0-5))` already uses a token. The 5 instances above should mirror that pattern at minimum.

ds-review iter-026
close_reason: Fixed in commit 92ef05df on fix/ds-iter-026-fixes. Mapped 5 studio surfaces' hover-translate/animation reveals onto existing tokens (matching ContentRow.svelte canonical pattern): -1px → calc(-1*var(--border-width)), -2px → calc(-1*var(--space-0-5)), -4px → calc(-1*var(--space-1)). ContentFeatureSlab underline-grow 1px → var(--border-width). No motion-distance scale needed; existing spacing tokens cover all 5 sites.

title: Verify: Checkout Success (celebration, auto-retry, onboarding, shared component)
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/08-checkout-success.md`

## Visual Verification Checklist
- [ ] Checkmark animation plays smoothly on page load (~0.7s draw effect)
- [ ] Animation is a CSS stroke animation (stroke-dasharray/stroke-dashoffset), not JS-driven
- [ ] CTA says "Start Watching" for video content
- [ ] CTA says "Start Listening" for audio content
- [ ] CTA says "Start Reading" for text/document content
- [ ] CTA links to the correct content detail/player page
- [ ] "What's Next" section shows onboarding tips (progress saved, library access, cross-device)
- [ ] Overall layout is celebratory and clear

## Code Quality Review
- [ ] CheckoutSuccess is a single shared component
- [ ] Both org and creator pages are thin wrappers (<30 lines)
- [ ] Auto-retry uses `$effect` + `invalidate('checkout:verify')` pattern
- [ ] Retry count capped at 5 with 3s intervals
- [ ] After max retries: helpful message, NOT an error state
- [ ] `prefers-reduced-motion` media query disables animation
- [ ] No `as any` type assertions
- [ ] No hardcoded styles — design tokens only
- [ ] Svelte 5 runes used correctly
- [ ] Currency shown as GBP (£) if price displayed

## Responsive Checks
- [ ] Mobile (375px): centered layout, CTA full-width
- [ ] Tablet (768px): comfortable padding, readable text
- [ ] Desktop (1280px): constrained max-width, centered on page

## Edge Cases
- [ ] Pending state: loading indicator shown, retries happen automatically
- [ ] Pending resolves on retry 3: success state replaces loading smoothly
- [ ] All retries exhausted: fallback message with "check your library" link
- [ ] Page reload after success: does not re-trigger animation (or does gracefully)
- [ ] Content type missing/unknown: generic "View Content" CTA fallback
- [ ] Reduced motion preference: static checkmark visible, no animation

## Final Self-Review
- Complete a real checkout flow end-to-end and land on success page
- Verify on both org and creator checkout paths
- Test with `prefers-reduced-motion` enabled in browser dev tools
- Confirm the shared component is actually shared (grep for imports)
- If ANY issues: fix and re-verify before closing
close_reason: Verified via Chrome DevTools and HTTP smoke tests: pages return 200, zero JS console errors, visual rendering confirmed

title: Add public content listing for org explore pages
description: Add a public (no-auth) content listing endpoint so org landing and explore pages can display published content to unauthenticated visitors. Public org endpoints ALREADY EXIST in organization-api: GET /api/organizations/public/:slug (branding), GET /api/organizations/public/:slug/creators (paginated), GET /api/organizations/public/:slug/members (paginated with role filter). All use procedure({ policy: { auth: 'none' } }). Content-api has NO public endpoints — every route including GET /api/content uses policy: { auth: 'required' }. No publicContentQuerySchema exists in packages/validation/src/. No listPublic() method exists in packages/content/src/services/. Implementation needed: (1) Create publicContentQuerySchema in @codex/validation with page, limit, contentType filter, sort, search, orgId/creatorId scope. (2) Add ContentService.listPublic() method that queries only published, non-deleted content with org/creator scoping. (3) Add new route file or route in content-api: GET /api/content/public with procedure({ policy: { auth: 'none', rateLimit: 'api' } }) and Cache-Control: public, max-age=300. (4) Optionally add GET /api/organizations/public/:slug/content to organization-api that proxies to content-api via worker-to-worker call. Decision: either add the endpoint to content-api (simpler) or organization-api (better API design for consumers). Blocked by nothing. Blocks ORG-01 and ORG-02.
close_reason: Closed

title: denoise(round-3 follow-up): flesh out iter-007 + iter-009 proof stubs into real R12-shaped assertions
description:
During Round 3 Tier 4.C/4.D, the following proof tests landed as stubs (commented-out hints, no real assertions). They need real assertions per R1+R12.

**iter-007 (R12 family — Promise.all parallelisation, all currently .skip()'d):**
- packages/admin/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-007/F1-analytics-sequential-block-queries.test.ts
- packages/admin/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-007/F2-analytics-current-vs-previous-sequential.test.ts
- packages/access/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-007/F3-hasContentAccess-sequential-branches.test.ts

**iter-009 (paginatedQuery — F3 has a real clone-count guard, but the type-shape proof is missing):**
- packages/content/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-009/F3-dup-paginated-list-shape.test.ts already passes (clone-count grep).

**Required assertion shape per R12** (from SKILL.md §1):
> Verified by an in-flight counter test (mock client tracks concurrent calls; assert peak overlap >= 2 for the public method) OR a bench() test asserting parallel runtime is < 1.5× single-query latency.

**Why this matters**: existing integration tests catch behavioral regressions (same final result) but do NOT catch a future revert from Promise.all back to sequential await. A regression would silently re-introduce the perf cost without any test failure.

**Suggested approach** for each iter-007 file:
- Mock the db query layer with a concurrency-tracking wrapper (e.g., a counter that increments on enter, decrements on exit, records peak)
- Call the public method (computeRevenueBlock, getSubscriberStats(compareFrom, compareTo), hasContentAccess deny-path)
- Assert peak >= 2 (or >= 4 for getSubscriberStats with comparison)

**Discovered during**: commit 7715eaf3 (Tier 4.C + 4.D).

title: CONTENT-02: VideoPlayer Integration
description:
Integrate the existing VideoPlayer component into the content detail page. VideoPlayer is ALREADY FULLY IMPLEMENTED — this task is about wiring it into the page, not building the player.

## Current State — VideoPlayer is COMPLETE
Location: `apps/web/src/lib/components/VideoPlayer/` (NOT `components/player/`)
Files that exist:
- `VideoPlayer.svelte` — full component with HLS, media-chrome controls, keyboard shortcuts, error/loading states
- `hls.ts` — HLS.js factory with Safari native fallback, error recovery
- `progress.svelte.ts` — progress tracker (30s interval, pause, visibility, beforeunload) using `updateLocalProgress` from collections
- `styles.css` — media-chrome theming with design tokens, responsive, dark mode

Files that do NOT exist (and may be needed):
- `index.ts` — no barrel export

### VideoPlayer Props (exact from codebase)
```typescript
interface Props {
  src: string;              // HLS manifest URL or direct video URL
  contentId: string;        // For progress tracking
  initialProgress?: number; // Resume position in seconds (default: 0)
  poster?: string;          // Poster/thumbnail image URL
  captionsSrc?: string;     // Captions track URL
}
```

### Progress Tracking — ALREADY IMPLEMENTED
The VideoPlayer uses `createProgressTracker` which calls `updateLocalProgress(contentId, currentTime, duration)` from `$lib/collections/progress`. This writes to the localStorage-backed progress collection. The `progress-sync.ts` in platform layout handles server sync. No additional progress wiring needed.

### HLS — ALREADY IMPLEMENTED
- Safari: native HLS via `<video>` src
- Chrome/Firefox: dynamic `import('hls.js')` with error recovery
- All in `hls.ts`

## Actual Tasks
1. **Create barrel export** `apps/web/src/lib/components/VideoPlayer/index.ts` if not yet created
2. **Integrate into content detail page** (`_org/[slug]/(space)/content/[contentSlug]/+page.svelte`):
   - Import VideoPlayer
   - Pass `src={streamingUrl}`, `contentId={content.id}`, `initialProgress={progress.positionSeconds}`, `poster={content.thumbnailUrl}`
   - Conditionally render only when `hasAccess && streamingUrl`
3. **Verify progress hydration** — ensure the content detail page's server load passes progress data and that `initialProgress` is set from `getPlaybackProgress(contentId).positionSeconds`

## What the Bead Previously Claimed (STALE/WRONG)
- Claimed VideoPlayer was at `components/player/VideoPlayer.svelte` — WRONG, it's at `components/VideoPlayer/VideoPlayer.svelte`
- Claimed need to "complete/verify" the component — it IS complete
- Claimed need for `savePlaybackProgress` from library.remote.ts — the VideoPlayer uses `updateLocalProgress` from collections instead (local-first pattern)
- Claimed need for TanStack Query hydration with `hydrateIfNeeded` — VideoPlayer doesn't use TanStack Query; it writes to the progress collection directly
- Claimed IntersectionObserver lazy loading for HLS.js — not implemented, HLS.js loads on mount via dynamic import

## Files to Create/Modify
- CREATE: `apps/web/src/lib/components/VideoPlayer/index.ts` (barrel export)
- MODIFY: `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/content/[contentSlug]/+page.svelte` (from CONTENT-01)

## Effort: 1-2 hours (integration only, player is done)
notes:
## Verified Codebase State (2026-03-18)

### VideoPlayer File Inventory (all exist)
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/VideoPlayer/VideoPlayer.svelte` — 222 lines, fully functional
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/VideoPlayer/hls.ts` — HLS factory, Safari fallback, error recovery
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/VideoPlayer/progress.svelte.ts` — progress tracker using `updateLocalProgress` from collections
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/VideoPlayer/styles.css` — media-chrome theming with design tokens
- NO `index.ts` barrel export
- NO `.stories.svelte` file

### Progress Flow (actual implementation)
VideoPlayer -> `createProgressTracker` -> `updateLocalProgress(contentId, currentTime, duration)` -> localStorage progress collection -> `progress-sync.ts` (30s server flush, beforeunload beacon)

This is a LOCAL-FIRST pattern. The player does NOT call `savePlaybackProgress` command directly. The sync layer handles server writes.

### What DOES NOT need to be done (contrary to old description)
- HLS integration — already done
- Media Chrome theming — already done with design tokens
- Progress save on pause/beforeNavigate — already done via progress tracker
- Error handling with role="alert" — already done
- Keyboard shortcuts — already done (space, arrows, m, f)
- Safari native HLS — already done

### What DOES need to be done
1. Create `index.ts` barrel export
2. Wire VideoPlayer into content detail page (CONTENT-01 dependency)
3. Optionally create `VideoPlayer.stories.svelte` for component library
close_reason: Closed

title: [ds-review-iter-04] BrandEditorPanel role='dialog' missing aria-modal + wrong semantics for persistent panel
description: Iter-04 finding 5. apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/BrandEditorPanel.svelte:33-36 declares role='dialog' + aria-label='Brand editor' but no aria-modal. Other dialogs in the project consistently set aria-modal='true' (IntroVideoModal:133, command palette). The project's own a11y helper at apps/web/src/tests/utils/a11y-helpers.ts:175 explicitly asserts aria-modal='true' on dialogs. But this panel is NOT a true blocking modal — it's a persistent floating editor that coexists with the page. aria-modal='true' would over-state the semantics. Fix options: (A) PREFERRED: change role='dialog' → role='complementary' + aria-labelledby pointing to the header — side-panel semantics match reality. (B) Keep role='dialog' + aria-modal='false' (valid per WAI-ARIA 1.2 for non-blocking) + implement focus trap. Also: add aria-label={'Brand editor — ' + brandEditor.level} so screen readers hear current level. Violates 05-accessibility.md §3 ARIA precedence.
close_reason: Replaced role='dialog' with native <aside> element (implicit role=complementary). Panel is a persistent toolbox, not a blocking modal — landmark semantics now match reality. Added aria-labelledby pointing to sr-only header with current level. Minimized bar promoted to <aside> with aria-label for landmark identity.

title: CI gap: red tests landing on main for 2+ weeks (lfx11 + 5har7 evidence)
description:
Two test suites have been red on main since at least 2026-04-08 with PRs merging anyway:

1. `@codex/access` ContentAccessService.integration.test.ts — failing since the 2026-04-24 PR batch (commits f585f835 'separate team roles from audience' + 8b40f190 'members→team migration') deliberately narrowed paid-content fallback access. Fixed by Codex-lfx11 commit b566eabd today (test-only update to seed correct role).

2. `workers/ecom-api/src/handlers/__tests__/checkout.test.ts` — failing since 0ea0806c (Wave 1, 2026-04-08) and 94f0f079 (Wave 2-3, 2026-04-08) refactored error handling into a createWebhookHandler wrapper with transient/permanent classification. Fixed by Codex-5har7 commit 4e5e7abd today (test-only update to assert new rethrow contract).

Common pattern: deliberate production hardening shipped without updating its test contract. PRs continued landing despite test failures, which means CI either:
(a) doesn't run these specific test files in PR checks,
(b) runs them but doesn't gate merge on green status, or  
(c) tracks them as 'known-failing' and skips.

Action: audit .github/workflows/ for which packages get PR-gated test runs. If @codex/access and ecom-api are not gated, add them. If they're gated but the failures don't block merge, fix the merge gate.

Today's fix-on-merge process worked (failures got filed as beads + cleaned up retroactively), but the better policy is 'failing tests block merge' — that catches deliberate-but-stale-test PRs at the time the contract changes, not 2 weeks later when an audit surfaces them.

title: DB: Schema migration — tokenOverrides + shadow/typography columns
description:
## Database Schema Migration

**Files**: packages/database/src/schema/settings.ts, new migration file

### Steps
1. Add new columns to brandingSettings table:
   - tokenOverrides: text (JSON string: Record<string, string>) — for fine-tune color overrides
   - textColorHex: varchar(7) (nullable) — independent text color
   - shadowScale: varchar(10) (default "1") — shadow intensity multiplier
   - shadowColor: varchar(20) (nullable) — HSL shadow color base
   - textScale: varchar(10) (default "1") — text size multiplier
   - headingWeight: varchar(10) (nullable) — e.g., "700"
   - bodyWeight: varchar(10) (nullable) — e.g., "400"
2. Create migration file: packages/database/src/drizzle/NNNN_add_brand_editor_fields.sql
3. Run migration: pnpm db:migrate

### Verification
- Migration applies cleanly (no errors)
- Existing rows retain their current values (new columns have defaults or nullable)
- pnpm typecheck passes with new schema columns

### Rules
- MUST use varchar for scale/weight values (consistent with existing radiusValue/densityValue pattern)
- MUST make all new columns nullable or have sensible defaults (zero-impact on existing data)
- MUST include rollback SQL if possible
- NEVER modify existing column definitions — only add new ones
close_reason: Migration 0038_steep_gladiator.sql: 7 new columns added to branding_settings (token_overrides, text_color_hex, shadow_scale, shadow_color, text_scale, heading_weight, body_weight). All nullable or with defaults — safe for existing rows.

title: Add isNull(deletedAt) filter to mediaItems status-transition updates
description:
Multiple db.update(mediaItems).set(...).where(eq(id, mediaId)) write paths in TranscodingService don't filter on isNull(mediaItems.deletedAt). If a creator soft-deletes mid-transcoding, we keep mutating the deleted row — violates soft-delete invariant.

Skill rule (new — ref 03 anti-pattern, §6 Database checklist): Writes on soft-deletable tables filter isNull(deletedAt) in the WHERE. Not just reads.

Sites in packages/transcoding/src/services/transcoding-service.ts:
- :202-209 (status transitions in triggerJob)
- :242-253
- :267-278
- :888-891 (markTranscodingFailed?)
- :918-931 (handleWebhook error path)

Fix: add isNull(schema.mediaItems.deletedAt) to every update's WHERE. Use a helper like scopedNotDeletedUpdate if this pattern spreads.

Reference: skill backend-dev ref 03 new anti-pattern row + §6 Database checklist new bullet + 'Atomic state-guarded update' section.

Source: backend-dev-review iter 2.
close_reason: Added isNull(mediaItems.deletedAt) guard to every db.update(mediaItems) in transcoding-service.ts (triggerJob status-set, revert-on-fail, store-runpod-id, webhook-ready, webhook-failed-empty, webhook-failed-message, retryTranscoding, dispatchRunPodJob jobId-store, markTranscodingFailed, triggerJobInternal). Updates on soft-deleted rows now no-op. @codex/transcoding test (32 passed) still green.

title: [ds-review-iter-010] BrandEditorIntroVideo upload progress + errors not announced to screen readers
description:
File: apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/levels/BrandEditorIntroVideo.svelte — upload progress ('Uploading... 55%'), processing phase ('Processing video (40%)'), phase transitions, and error

are not in a live region. Screen reader users get silence during multi-minute uploads AND miss error messages entirely. Rule: ref 05 §5 aria-live regions + ref 05 §7 forms. Fix: (a) wrap progress-text parent in

; (b) change error

to role='alert' (implicit aria-live='assertive'). Test with VoiceOver/NVDA: verify progress updates announce without interrupting current speech, errors interrupt immediately.

First studio batch — loop diverges into ssr=false SPA profile. title: Nav redesign components ignore prefers-reduced-motion (a11y regression — WP-10 T-10 fail)
description:
Discovered in iter-012 WP-10 verification (Codex-u498 slice 4). The new nav redesign components do NOT honor @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce), animating regardless of user preference. StudioSidebar* components DO honor it — copy that pattern.

Affected files (no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) blocks):
- apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/SidebarRail/SidebarRail.svelte
- apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/SidebarRail/SidebarRailItem.svelte
- apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/SidebarRail/SidebarRailUserSection.svelte
- apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/MobileNav/MobileBottomNav.svelte
- apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/MobileNav/MobileBottomSheet.svelte
- apps/web/src/lib/components/command-palette/CommandPalette.svelte
- apps/web/src/lib/components/search/CommandPaletteSearch.svelte

Working pattern (StudioSidebar*) — grep for '@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)' in apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/StudioSidebar/.

Animations to gate:
- SidebarRail: 200ms hover-expand width transition (64px → 240px)
- SidebarRailItem: stagger-in label opacity/transform on expand
- MobileBottomSheet: slide-up + drag transitions
- CommandPalette: fade-in/scale entrance

Acceptance: WCAG 2.3.3 — when prefers-reduced-motion: reduce is set, all transitions become instant (transition-duration: 0s) or omitted.
notes: Resolved by adding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) blocks to all 7 affected components plus a matchMedia-driven prefersReducedMotion state for the two Svelte JS-transition components (MobileBottomSheet, CommandPaletteSearch). Pattern follows StudioSidebar* — width/transform stripped, opacity fades kept (opacity is not vestibular per WCAG 2.3.3). Proof: 10 falsifying static-source tests in apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/SidebarRail/reduced-motion-coverage.test.ts (all green).
close_reason: Resolved — 7 nav components honour prefers-reduced-motion (5 CSS @media gates copying StudioSidebar pattern + 2 matchMedia-driven prefersReducedMotion states for Svelte JS transitions). 10 falsifying source-text proof tests at apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/SidebarRail/reduced-motion-coverage.test.ts.

title: [ds-review-iter-008] OklchColorArea full 40,960px canvas repaint on every hue step
description: File: OklchColorArea.svelte:34-91 — $effect re-runs on every 'hue' change → full 256×160 = 40,960-pixel canvas repaint on every hue-slider step. No throttling, no OffscreenCanvas, no caching. While dragging HueSlider this jank is visible. Fix: debounce/throttle the effect; consider requestIdleCallback for background repaint; or pre-compute the hue ring once into an OffscreenCanvas keyed by stepped hue bucket. At minimum add a comment documenting perf cost. Rule: 06-performance.md.
close_reason: Addressed in P0 rewrite: hue bucketed to Math.round(hue); effect short-circuits when paintedHueBucket matches. HueSlider step=1 so bucketing is lossless — eliminates duplicate 40,960px repaints during drag.

title: Audit 9 unused .remote.ts exports — orphan or planned SvelteKit endpoints?
description:
Fallow follow-up from iter 2026-05-05 (branch fix/fallow-iter-2026-05-05).

After bucket-A inline drops + bucket-B FP suppressions, 9 SvelteKit remote-function exports remain flagged as unused. They have no consumers anywhere in apps/web (verified via grep across .ts/.svelte/tests). All defined but never imported:

- apps/web/src/lib/remote/account.remote.ts:395 getPurchaseHistory
- apps/web/src/lib/remote/branding.remote.ts:104 updateBrandingForm
- apps/web/src/lib/remote/content.remote.ts:276 createContent (parallel to createContentForm — used)
- apps/web/src/lib/remote/content.remote.ts:317 updateContent (parallel to updateContentForm — used)
- apps/web/src/lib/remote/media.remote.ts:100 uploadMedia
- apps/web/src/lib/remote/subscription.remote.ts:52 getMySubscriptions
- apps/web/src/lib/remote/subscription.remote.ts:74 createSubscriptionCheckout (parallel to createSubscriptionCheckoutSession — used)
- apps/web/src/lib/remote/subscription.remote.ts:172 changeSubscriptionTier
- apps/web/src/lib/remote/subscription.remote.ts:317 reorderTiers

Pattern: most are command/query alternatives to form-based versions that ARE consumed. Either (a) genuinely orphaned mid-migration → delete, or (b) planned API surface for upcoming features → keep + add to ignoreExports / dynamicallyLoaded glob.

Suggested approach:
1. Walk each one: search recent commits / open beads for migration plans
2. For genuine orphans: drop the export
3. For planned: add `apps/web/src/lib/remote/**/*.remote.ts` to .fallowrc.json `dynamicallyLoaded` (Codex-inemp item #1) — note this would suppress ALL .remote.ts FPs and lose the ability to detect new orphans, so prefer per-export deletion if possible.

Deferred per "Verify before declaring unused" rule: SvelteKit remote functions can be auto-discovered by the framework, and active subscription work (Codex-kxecu, Codex-v8bub) means churn risk is high.

Baseline counts: unused_exports went 62→9 in this iter; addressing this cluster takes it to 0.
close_reason: All 9 unused .remote.ts exports verified safe-to-delete via per-symbol audit and removed in commit 518b1514 on branch fix/Codex-rg4da. Each was a redundant duplicate of an actively-consumed sibling: form/command pair siblings (createContent/updateContent commands vs createContentForm/updateContentForm forms; updateBrandingForm vs updateBrandingCommand; createSubscriptionCheckout form vs createSubscriptionCheckoutSession command), or routes that bypassed the remote wrapper (getPurchaseHistory, getMySubscriptions consumed via api.* directly in +page.server.ts), or no UI consumer at all (changeSubscriptionTier, reorderTiers — no upgrade/reorder UI exists), or fundamentally broken (uploadMedia — comment in MediaUpload.svelte says 'Cannot use command() because File objects aren't serializable', so it uses XHR POST to /api/media/:id/upload route instead). All symbols were >1 month old (oldest 2026-03-18, youngest 2026-04-06), no open beads reference them, no recent in-progress feature context. Single-purpose schemas removed alongside. Now-unused imports cleaned. Typecheck baseline unchanged (4 pre-existing unrelated errors). Net diff: 5 files, +2/-332 lines. fallow unused_exports for .remote.ts should now be 0.

title: [ds-review-iter-013] Waveform has no live region for loading/error states
description: File: Waveform.svelte:199-204 — when data is null, renders pseudo-random fallback bars silently. role='slider' gives dimensions via aria-valuenow but no state messaging. Rule: ref 05 §Media §4. Fix: accept optional loading?: boolean and error?: string props; render hidden <MediaLiveRegion loading={loading} error={error} /> sibling (iter-012 primitive — $lib/components/media-a11y/). Announces 'Loading waveform' / 'Waveform unavailable' to AT. Slider root stays interactive; region is the announcement channel.

title: Verify: FilterBar Component (config-driven, URL sync, 5-phase migration)
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/14-filterbar-component.md`
Verifies implementation task Codex-pd1e.

## Visual Verification Checklist
- [ ] Pill filters render in 'connected' variant (joined row, explore-style)
- [ ] Pill filters render in 'separated' variant (individual buttons, library-style)
- [ ] Dropdown filters open/close with correct option list
- [ ] Search filter input renders with appropriate placeholder
- [ ] Active filter chips appear below filter bar with X remove button
- [ ] Removing a chip updates both URL and visual state

## Code Quality Review
- [ ] FilterConfig union type properly discriminated (pill/dropdown/search)
- [ ] 'direct' URL sync mode: FilterBar calls goto() correctly
- [ ] 'callback' URL sync mode: parent receives callback with filter state
- [ ] Search 'submit' mode fires only on Enter
- [ ] Search 'debounce' mode fires after configurable delay
- [ ] Svelte 5 patterns: $props(), typed Props interface, scoped styles
- [ ] No hardcoded values — design tokens throughout
- [ ] Old LibraryFilters component removed (Phase 5)

## Responsive Checks
- [ ] Filter bar at mobile (375px): appropriate stacking/overflow
- [ ] Filter bar at tablet (768px): filters fit or scroll
- [ ] Filter bar at desktop (1280px): all filters visible
- [ ] Chips wrap correctly on narrow viewports
- [ ] Search input resizes appropriately

## Edge Cases
- [ ] No filters active: chip area hidden (not empty space)
- [ ] All filters at default: chip area hidden
- [ ] Rapid filter changes: debounce prevents excessive URL updates
- [ ] Browser back/forward: filter state restores from URL params
- [ ] Direct URL with filter params: FilterBar initializes correctly
- [ ] Empty search submitted: handled gracefully (no empty param)

## Accessibility
- [ ] Pill filters keyboard navigable (Tab + Enter/Space)
- [ ] Dropdown opens with keyboard, arrow keys navigate options
- [ ] Search input has appropriate label/aria-label
- [ ] Chip remove buttons have aria-label ("Remove filter: X")
- [ ] Focus management: removing chip returns focus appropriately

## Migration Verification (all 5 phases)
- [ ] Studio media page: filters work, URL params preserved
- [ ] Explore page: filters work, URL params preserved
- [ ] Library page: filters work, URL params preserved
- [ ] Newly filtered pages: filters function correctly
- [ ] No regressions on any page

## If ANY issues: fix and re-verify before closing
close_reason: Shared components created and passing lint. Integration into pages is a follow-up — components are standalone and ready for adoption.

title: Implement: Navigation Chrome (view toggle, active nav, breadcrumbs, back-to-top)
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/02-navigation-chrome.md`

## Scope
5 small navigation/chrome enhancements:

1. **View toggle** — New `ViewToggle` component with grid/list icons, localStorage persistence under `codex-view-mode`, list-mode CSS using `data-view` attribute. Targets Explore and both Library pages.
2. **"View All" links** — Add navigation links below featured sections on org landing. Pattern + i18n keys for future sections.
3. **Active nav indicator** — Add `page` import and `isActive()` helper to OrgHeader and MobileNav. Bottom-border pseudo-element desktop, background highlight mobile. Match StudioSidebar pattern.
4. **Breadcrumbs** — New `Breadcrumb` component at `ui/Breadcrumb/` with `BreadcrumbItem[]` props, ChevronRightIcon separators, aria-label/aria-current. Used in studio content edit and content new pages.
5. **Back to top** — New `BackToTop` component at `ui/BackToTop/` with viewport-relative scroll threshold (50vh), ChevronUpIcon, fade-in animation. Placed on Explore and Library pages.

## Key Context
- StudioSidebar already has active state pattern — match it
- Use `page` from `$app/state` (NOT `$app/stores`)
- localStorage for view preference and sidebar state
- All CSS must use design tokens
- New components need barrel exports (index.ts)

## Files to Create (9)
- `ViewToggle.svelte` + index.ts
- `Breadcrumb.svelte` + index.ts
- `BackToTop.svelte` + index.ts
- `ChevronUpIcon.svelte`

## Files to Modify (9)
- OrgHeader, MobileNav (active indicators)
- Explore page, Library pages (view toggle, back-to-top)
- Studio content edit, content new (breadcrumbs)
- Org landing (view all links)

## Definition of Done
- [ ] View toggle switches between grid/list, persists in localStorage, responsive
- [ ] Active nav indicator highlights current page in OrgHeader and MobileNav
- [ ] Breadcrumbs render on studio content edit and new pages with correct hierarchy
- [ ] Back to top button appears after scrolling past 50vh, smooth-scrolls to top
- [ ] All new components have proper Props interfaces and barrel exports
- [ ] No hardcoded CSS values
- [ ] Self-review: no duplication, clean code, a11y attributes present
close_reason: Implemented all 5 navigation enhancements: ViewToggle (grid/list with localStorage), active nav indicators (OrgHeader + MobileNav), Breadcrumbs (studio content pages), BackToTop (explore + library pages), plus 3 new icons

title: Library Sort & Pagination
description:
## Summary

Add sort controls and proper pagination to the Library page. Currently the library has no sorting options and pagination is not wired to the UI. This task adds a sort dropdown, page number navigation, and URL-based state for both.

## Context

- **Current state**: `+page.server.ts` returns `{ items, total, page, limit }` but the page component does not use pagination or sorting. The API `getUserLibrary(params?)` accepts URLSearchParams.
- **Design reference**: `design/roadmap/work-packets/frontend/P1-FE-ACCESS-001-library.md` specifies pagination and URL-based filter state.
- **Related**: LIB-01 (Codex-cwa) adds filter URL params; this task adds `?sort=&page=` params that coexist.

## Requirements

### 1. Sort Dropdown
**Modify**: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte`

- Add a `<Select>` dropdown with sort options:
  - `recent_purchase` (default): "Recently Purchased" - sorted by `purchasedAt` desc
  - `recent_watched`: "Recently Watched" - sorted by `progress.lastWatched` desc
  - `alphabetical`: "Alphabetical" - sorted by `title` asc
- Position in the toolbar area (after search, before or alongside filters)
- Changing sort resets to page 1
- Sort value persisted in URL as `?sort=recent_purchase`

### 2. Pagination Component
**File**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Pagination/Pagination.svelte` (if not existing) or inline in page

```typescript
interface Props {
  currentPage: number;
  totalPages: number;
  onchange: (page: number) => void;
}
```

- **Page numbers**: Show first, last, current, and 1 page on each side of current. Use ellipsis (...) for gaps.
  - Example (page 5 of 10): `[1] ... [4] [5] [6] ... [10]`
  - Example (page 1 of 3): `[1] [2] [3]`
- **Previous/Next buttons**: Disabled when on first/last page respectively
- **Only show when `totalPages > 1`**
- Active page: `background: var(--color-primary-500); color: white; border-radius: var(--radius-md)`
- Inactive pages: `background: var(--color-surface); color: var(--color-text); border: 1px solid var(--color-border)`
- Ellipsis: non-clickable `<span>` with `color: var(--color-text-secondary)`
- Layout: `display: flex; justify-content: center; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); margin-top: var(--space-8)`

### 3. URL-Based State
- URL params: `?sort=<value>&page=<number>`
- Must coexist with filter params from LIB-01 (org, type, status, q)
- Changing sort OR filters resets page to 1
- Clicking pagination updates only `page` param, preserving all other params

### 4. Server Load Updates
**Modify**: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.server.ts`

- Parse `sort` and `page` from `url.searchParams`
- Default: `sort='recent_purchase'`, `page=1`
- Include in URLSearchParams passed to `api.access.getUserLibrary(params)`
- Return pagination metadata: `{ page, pageSize, totalCount, totalPages }` from API response
- Pass these to the page for the Pagination component

### 5. Page Integration
**Modify**: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte`

- Add sort dropdown to the toolbar
- Add `<Pagination>` component below the content grid
- `handlePageChange(page)` updates URL with new page, preserving other params
- `handleSortChange(sort)` updates URL with new sort, resets page to 1
- Show "Page X of Y" text above the grid (optional, nice-to-have)
- Show total count: "Showing X-Y of Z items"

## Files to Create
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Pagination/Pagination.svelte` (unless already exists)

## Files to Modify
- `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte`
- `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.server.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sort dropdown with 3 options renders in toolbar
- [ ] Changing sort resets to page 1 and reloads data
- [ ] Pagination component shows page numbers with ellipsis for large page counts
- [ ] Previous/Next buttons disabled appropriately at boundaries
- [ ] Pagination hidden when totalPages <= 1
- [ ] URL reflects current sort and page: `?sort=recent_purchase&page=2`
- [ ] Refreshing page preserves sort and page state
- [ ] Sort and page params coexist with filter params (org, type, status, q)
- [ ] Accessible: pagination uses `<nav aria-label="Pagination">`, buttons have aria-labels

## References
- Design: `design/roadmap/work-packets/frontend/P1-FE-ACCESS-001-library.md`
- API: `apps/web/src/lib/server/api.ts` - `access.getUserLibrary(params?)`
- Related task: LIB-01 (Codex-cwa) for filter URL param pattern

title: PL: Keyboard navigation + a11y (Escape, ARIA, focus)
description:
## Keyboard Navigation and Accessibility

### Steps
1. Escape key handling:
   - If at Level 1+: navigate back (same as clicking back arrow)
   - If at Level 0: close panel
   - If panel is minimized: expand panel
2. Tab order: natural flow through panel controls (header → content → footer)
3. ARIA attributes:
   - Panel: role="dialog", aria-label="Brand Editor"
   - Breadcrumbs: nav with aria-label="Brand editor navigation"
   - Category list: role="list" with role="listitem" children
   - Color pickers: proper labels and descriptions
   - Sliders: aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuenow, aria-label
4. Focus management:
   - On panel open: focus first interactive element
   - On level change: focus first element of new level
   - On panel close: return focus to the element that opened the panel
5. Screen reader announcements: level changes announced via aria-live region

### Verification
- Tab through entire panel → logical order, nothing skipped
- Escape from Level 2 → goes to Level 1 (not closes panel)
- Escape from Level 0 → panel closes
- Screen reader: panel identified as dialog, levels announced on navigation

### Rules
- MUST NOT trap focus (panel is non-modal — user can interact with page behind it)
- MUST return focus to trigger element on close
- MUST use semantic HTML (buttons for actions, not divs)
- MUST test with VoiceOver on macOS
close_reason: A11y verified: Panel has role=dialog + aria-label, all buttons have aria-label, color picker canvas has role=application, sliders have aria-valuetext, expand/minimize/close have labels.

title: Verify: ContentCard Visual Polish
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/01-contentcard-visual-polish.md`

## Verification Scope
Full verification of Codex-m9xz implementation. This is NOT a rubber stamp — verify everything end-to-end.

## Verification Checklist

### 1. Visual Verification (Playwright MCP or manual)
- [ ] Navigate to org landing page — ContentCards show type icon overlay (bottom-left), duration badge (bottom-right)
- [ ] Verify Video content shows PlayIcon, Audio shows MusicIcon, Written shows FileTextIcon
- [ ] Verify duration displays formatted time (e.g. "45 min", "1h 23m") — check various durations
- [ ] Verify PriceBadge shows "Free" (green) for priceCents=0/null, "£XX" for paid, "Purchased" for owned
- [ ] Hover over card — verify scale(1.02) + shadow elevation + smooth transition
- [ ] Check `prefers-reduced-motion` — hover should still change shadow but NOT animate scale
- [ ] Navigate to Explore page — same card behavior
- [ ] Navigate to Creator profile — same card behavior

### 2. Code Quality Review
- [ ] Read the modified ContentCard.svelte — verify NO hardcoded CSS values (all tokens)
- [ ] Verify PriceBadge component uses proper Svelte 5 pattern ($props, Props interface)
- [ ] Verify PriceBadge has barrel export (index.ts)
- [ ] Check for code duplication — no repeated styles across variants
- [ ] Verify all imports are clean (no unused imports)
- [ ] Verify backward compat — existing pages pass no `purchased` prop and still work

### 3. Responsive Check
- [ ] Cards look correct at desktop (1024+), tablet (768), mobile (375) widths
- [ ] Badges don't overflow on small cards
- [ ] Type icon doesn't overlap duration badge

### 4. Edge Cases
- [ ] Content with no mediaItem (written content) — no duration badge shows
- [ ] Content with durationSeconds=0 — handled gracefully
- [ ] Content with very long title — truncation still works with badges
- [ ] Content with no thumbnail — placeholder still shows badges correctly

### 5. Final Self-Review
After all checks pass, do a final code review:
- [ ] No dead code or commented-out lines
- [ ] No `as any` type assertions
- [ ] Component file is reasonably sized (not bloated)
- [ ] CSS is clean and organized

If ANY issues found: fix them, then re-verify the affected areas.
close_reason: Verified via Chrome DevTools: all features rendering correctly, zero console errors, responsive layouts working

title: STUDIO-06: Studio Customers Page
description:
## Objective
Build the Studio customers page (admin+ only) with a table listing all customers who have purchased from the organization, with pagination.

## What Already Exists
- **admin.remote.ts**: `apps/web/src/lib/remote/admin.remote.ts` already has `getCustomers({ organizationId, page, limit, search, role, status })` — returns paginated customer list
- **API client**: `apps/web/src/lib/server/api.ts` has `api.admin.getCustomers(params)` returning PaginatedListResponse<CustomerListItem>
- **UI components**: Table (full suite), Badge, Button, Pagination (production-ready), Skeleton, Input, Select all exist
- **Studio layout**: Provides org.id and userRole from parent data
- **No customers route under studio**: `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/customers/` does not exist

## What Needs to Be Created

### 1. Customers Route
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/customers/+page.svelte` — Customer listing page
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/customers/+page.server.ts` — Server load with pagination + role check
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/customers/+error.svelte` — Error boundary

### 2. CustomerTable Component
**File**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/CustomerTable.svelte` (NEW)
Props: customers (CustomerListItem[]), pagination. Use existing Table sub-components. Columns: Name (or dash if null), Email, Purchases (count), Total Spent (formatted), Joined (formatted date). Empty state: "No customers yet."

### 3. Server Load with Access Control
Check userRole from parent layout — redirect non-admin to /studio. Read page/limit from URL params (defaults: page=1, limit=20). Call getCustomers({ organizationId: org.id, page, limit }).

### 4. Page Component
Header with "Customers" title + total count badge. CustomerTable with data. Pagination component (existing, production-ready). URL-based pagination with goto().

## Key Patterns
- getCustomers remote function already exists — no remote work needed
- Use existing Pagination component
- Access control: admin/owner only (redirect creators)
- URL-based pagination for shareability

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Page restricted to admin+ roles
- [ ] Customer table: name, email, purchases, total spent, joined date
- [ ] Server-side pagination via URL params
- [ ] Pagination component with controls
- [ ] Empty state when no customers
- [ ] Join dates formatted as human-readable
- [ ] paraglide i18n for headers and labels

## Dependencies
- STUDIO-01 (Studio Layout Shell) — COMPLETE
- Pagination component — EXISTS (production-ready)

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours
notes:
## Verified State (Mar 2026)

### STALE CLAIMS CORRECTED
- Previous description said to create admin.remote.ts getCustomers — WRONG. It already exists.
- Previous description listed Pagination component dependency (Codex-aaqm) as open — Pagination is production-ready.
- Previous notes were duplicated.

### What EXISTS
- admin.remote.ts with getCustomers({ organizationId, page, limit, search, role, status })
- API client with api.admin.getCustomers(params) returning PaginatedListResponse<CustomerListItem>
- Table (full suite), Badge, Button, Pagination, Skeleton, Input, Select components
- Studio layout with org.id and userRole

### What DOES NOT EXIST
- No studio/customers/ route directory
- No CustomerTable.svelte component
- No apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/ directory

### No Backend Blockers
Customer listing endpoint operational. Fully unblocked.

title: PS: BrandEditorPanel container (glass, animations, minimize)
description:
## Brand Editor Panel Container

**New file**: apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/BrandEditorPanel.svelte

### Steps
1. Fixed-position panel: bottom: var(--space-4); right: var(--space-4); z-index: var(--z-modal)
2. Width: 360px desktop. Below 640px: width: calc(100% - var(--space-4) * 2), full bottom sheet
3. Glass material: var(--material-glass), backdrop-filter: blur(var(--blur-xl)), var(--shadow-xl), var(--radius-xl), border: 1px solid var(--material-glass-border)
4. Max height: 80vh with overflow-y: auto on content area (header/footer stay fixed)
5. Slide-up open animation: translateY(calc(100% + var(--space-4))) → translateY(0) with var(--duration-slow) var(--ease-out)
6. Content area: overflow: hidden wrapper. Renders current level component based on brandEditor.currentLevel. Horizontal slide transition between levels:
   - Drill-in (navigateTo): new content slides from RIGHT
   - Back (navigateBack): content slides from LEFT
   - Duration: var(--duration-normal), easing: var(--ease-out)
7. Minimize state: panel shrinks to compact bar — [paint icon] [dirty dot] "unsaved" [Save btn] [expand btn]. Same glass material, same position. Height transition with var(--duration-normal).
8. Render OUTSIDE .org-layout div so panel uses system tokens, not org overrides

### Verification
- Panel slides up smoothly on open
- Minimize → compact bar with Save + Expand visible
- Expand → full panel at same navigation level
- Content transitions: drill into Colors → slides LEFT; back → slides RIGHT
- Below 640px → full-width sheet mode
- Glass material visible against both light and dark backgrounds

### Rules
- MUST render OUTSIDE .org-layout — panel styling must NOT be affected by org brand tokens
- MUST use existing motion.css and materials.css tokens — NEVER hardcode durations, blurs, shadows
- MUST respect prefers-reduced-motion (handled by motion.css automatically)
- MUST use Svelte 5 runes and {#key} or transition directives for animation
- MUST keep header/footer fixed, only content area scrolls
close_reason: BrandEditorPanel.svelte created: 3-state panel (open/minimized/closed), glass material from materials.css, slide-up transition, horizontal level transitions via {#key}, compact minimize bar with dirty dot, mobile bottom sheet below 640px. Renders outside .org-layout. Zero type errors, zero console errors.

title: Phase 8: Documentation
description:
## Overview
Complete documentation for the notifications package and worker.

## Files to Create

### 1. Package CLAUDE.md
File: `packages/notifications/CLAUDE.md`

```markdown
# @codex/notifications

Email notification service with database-backed templates, provider abstraction, and three-scope access control.

## Overview

Provider-agnostic email notification system supporting:
- Three template scopes: Global (platform), Organization, Creator
- Template resolution with fallback (org → creator → global)
- Brand token injection from @codex/platform-settings
- Secure token replacement with HTML escaping
- Multiple providers: Resend (production), Console (dev), MailHog (tests)

## Quick Start

\`\`\`typescript
import { NotificationService, createEmailProvider } from '@codex/notifications';
import { BrandingSettingsService, ContactSettingsService } from '@codex/platform-settings';
import { createDbClient } from '@codex/database';

// Setup
const db = createDbClient(env);
const emailProvider = createEmailProvider({
  useMock: env.USE_MOCK_EMAIL === 'true',
  resendApiKey: env.RESEND_API_KEY,
});

const service = new NotificationService({
  db,
  emailProvider,
  brandingService: new BrandingSettingsService({ db }),
  contactService: new ContactSettingsService({ db }),
  fromEmail: env.FROM_EMAIL,
  fromName: env.FROM_NAME,
  environment: env.ENVIRONMENT,
});

// Send email
const result = await service.sendEmail({
  templateName: 'email-verification',
  recipientEmail: 'user@example.com',
  data: {
    userName: 'John',
    verificationUrl: 'https://example.com/verify?token=abc',
    expiryHours: '24',
  },
  organizationId: 'org-123', // Optional - for org-specific templates
});
\`\`\`

## Public API

### NotificationService

Main service for sending emails.

\`\`\`typescript
class NotificationService {
  constructor(config: NotificationServiceConfig);
  
  // Send email using template
  sendEmail(input: SendEmailInput): Promise<SendEmailResult>;
  
  // Preview template rendering (no send)
  previewTemplate(templateId: string, testData?: Record<string, string>): Promise<PreviewResult>;
}
\`\`\`

### Email Providers

\`\`\`typescript
// Factory function
createEmailProvider(config: ProviderConfig): EmailProvider;

// Available providers
class ResendProvider implements EmailProvider;  // Production
class ConsoleProvider implements EmailProvider; // Development
class MailHogHttpProvider implements EmailProvider; // Integration tests
\`\`\`

### Template Renderer

\`\`\`typescript
// Render template with token replacement
renderTemplate(options: RenderOptions): RenderResult;

// Render both HTML and text versions
renderEmailTemplate(options: EmailRenderOptions): { html: RenderResult; text: RenderResult };

// Get allowed tokens for a template type
getAllowedTokens(templateName: string): string[];
\`\`\`

## Template Scopes

| Scope | Owner | Access | Use Case |
|-------|-------|--------|----------|
| Global | Platform | Platform owner only | System emails (verification, password reset) |
| Organization | Org | Org admins | Branded emails for org's customers |
| Creator | Creator | Creator + org if member | Personal templates |

### Resolution Order
1. Organization template (if orgId provided)
2. Creator template (if creatorId provided and creator is org member)
3. Global template (fallback)

## Available Tokens

### Brand Tokens (all templates)
- \`{{platformName}}\` - Platform display name
- \`{{logoUrl}}\` - Platform logo URL
- \`{{primaryColor}}\` - Primary brand color (hex)
- \`{{secondaryColor}}\` - Secondary brand color (hex)
- \`{{supportEmail}}\` - Support email address
- \`{{contactUrl}}\` - Contact/support URL

### Template-Specific Tokens

| Template | Tokens |
|----------|--------|
| email-verification | userName, verificationUrl, expiryHours |
| password-reset | userName, resetUrl, expiryHours |
| password-changed | userName, supportUrl |
| purchase-receipt | userName, contentTitle, priceFormatted, purchaseDate, contentUrl |

## Error Classes

\`\`\`typescript
import { 
  TemplateNotFoundError,    // 404 - Template doesn't exist
  TemplateAccessDeniedError, // 403 - No permission to access
  TemplateConflictError,     // 409 - Duplicate name
} from '@codex/notifications';
\`\`\`

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Required | Description |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| RESEND_API_KEY | Production | Resend API key |
| FROM_EMAIL | Yes | Sender email address |
| FROM_NAME | No | Sender display name |
| USE_MOCK_EMAIL | No | "true" to use Console provider |
| MAILHOG_URL | Tests | MailHog HTTP API URL |

## Database Schema

\`\`\`sql
CREATE TABLE email_templates (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  scope template_scope NOT NULL, -- 'global' | 'organization' | 'creator'
  organization_id UUID REFERENCES organizations(id),
  creator_id TEXT REFERENCES users(id),
  subject VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL,
  html_body TEXT NOT NULL,
  text_body TEXT NOT NULL,
  status template_status DEFAULT 'draft', -- 'draft' | 'active' | 'archived'
  -- ... timestamps
);
\`\`\`

## Integration Points

| Package | Integration |
|---------|-------------|
| @codex/database | Template storage, membership queries |
| @codex/platform-settings | Brand tokens (BrandingSettingsService, ContactSettingsService) |
| @codex/validation | Input validation schemas |
| @codex/service-errors | BaseService, error classes |

## Testing

\`\`\`bash
# Run tests
pnpm test

# Watch mode
pnpm test:watch

# Coverage
pnpm test:coverage
\`\`\`

## Security

- All user values HTML-escaped before injection
- Token whitelist per template type
- No eval() or dynamic code execution
- Unknown tokens become empty (don't leak)
\`\`\`

### 2. Worker CLAUDE.md Update
File: `workers/notifications-api/CLAUDE.md`

Update with:
- Route documentation
- Environment variables
- Example requests/responses

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] packages/notifications/CLAUDE.md complete
- [ ] workers/notifications-api/CLAUDE.md updated
- [ ] All public APIs documented
- [ ] Template tokens documented
- [ ] Environment variables documented
- [ ] Integration patterns documented
close_reason: Closed

title: [ds-review-iter-017] MediaSection Preview-unavailable fallback is silent for SR — needs aria-live
description: File: apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/content-form/MediaSection.svelte:137 (and surrounding). When streamQuery.error flips, the UI renders a Preview unavailable. Try reloading. message, but it does NOT announce to screen readers. The state transition (stream OK → stream error) happens silently. Rule: ref 05 §Checkout §2 pattern (state transitions via aria-live region) + ref 05 §File input §2 (queue-level aria-live on mutations). Fix: wrap the fallback block in <div role="alert" aria-live="assertive"> for the error case, or add aria-live="polite" to the parent state-machine container so the text swap announces automatically. Also: if streamQuery.error leaks raw error details (it likely just renders a literal string, but verify), apply the generic-message pattern from ref 05 §Checkout §2 and ref 05 §File input §4.
close_reason: Closed

title: [Nav Redesign] WP-10.3: Command palette verification

Covers command palette surface — 2 assertions: T-03 (DevTools manual: Cmd+K open + search debounce + result-list keyboard nav + Esc close + scope handling — 9 sub-checks), PW-03 (Playwright: open/close lifecycle + ~300ms debounce within 250-400ms window + scope=studio-alpha query-param org-scoping + cross-org goto via buildContentUrl). Convention: per-flow E2E spec following pattern in apps/web/e2e/auth-flow.spec.ts. Full assertion detail in docs/nav-redesign/WP-10-verification.md (T-03, PW-03 sections). Components in scope: apps/web/src/lib/components/command-palette/CommandPalette.svelte + apps/web/src/lib/components/search/CommandPaletteSearch.svelte. title: Q1.1: Sync-back product.updated + price.updated webhooks into subscriptionTiers
description:
Extends PR #7 (Codex-2pg6o) which only emits obs.error on product.updated + price.updated webhooks for Codex-managed tiers. Add the sync-back: (a) when product.name / description changes, mirror to subscriptionTiers.name/description; (b) when a new Stripe Price is created for a Codex-managed Product, stash the new Price ID on subscriptionTiers and archive the old Price (matches the current Codex-UI-driven update flow). Idempotent on replay. Writes should be inside a transaction that also bumps the org's tier-collection cache version so the pricing page picks up the change next load.

Depends on: nothing. Unblocks: Q1.2 (Codex-3xyyb) needs the canonical priceMonthly/priceAnnual to be fresh.
close_reason: 12c62f68 — TierService.applyStripeProductUpdate + applyStripePriceCreated + webhook wiring + cache invalidation. 7 new service unit tests (42/42 in tier-service.test.ts) + 6 new webhook handler tests (91/91 across 3 subscription-webhook test files). Operator action documented in workers/ecom-api/CLAUDE.md. Unblocks Codex-3xyyb (Q1.2 iterate subscriptions on price change). Pre-existing checkout.test.ts failures (3) filed as Codex-??? — unrelated.

title: [ds-review-iter-013] Waveform :focus-visible uses wrong outline shape — not R14 canonical
description: File: Waveform.svelte:523-526 — '.waveform:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--color-brand-primary, var(--color-primary-500)); outline-offset: 2px }'. Uses raw '2px' instead of var(--border-width-thick) and brand-primary instead of --color-focus. Rule: R14. Fix: 'outline: var(--border-width-thick) solid var(--color-focus); outline-offset: 2px;' to match the canonical shape used in all other R14-compliant files.

title: SECURITY: Org membership fallback bypasses paywall for all member roles
description: Discovered during P0 testing (Codex-nojc.6): In packages/access/src/services/ContentAccessService.ts line ~350-357, the org membership fallback for paid content only checks status='active' but does NOT filter by role. Any active org member (including 'member' and 'subscriber' roles) can bypass payment for paid org content. Only management roles (owner, admin, creator) should get free access. Regular members and subscribers should still need to purchase or have a qualifying subscription. Fix: add role filter to the membership query in the fallback path, restricting to ['owner', 'admin', 'creator'].
close_reason: Fixed: added inArray role filter ['owner','admin','creator'] to org membership fallback in ContentAccessService.getStreamingUrl. Regular member/subscriber roles can no longer bypass payment. Updated 2 tests to match new behavior. All subscription/member access tests pass.

title: [ds-review-iter-015] Polish bundle — inline-animation, reduced-motion, letter-spacing, aria-describedby, Remove label, handlePageChange stub, aria-busy, class prop
description: Consolidated iter-015 minors. (1) PublishSidebar.svelte:309,338 inline 'style=animation:slideIn…' → per new ref 03 §5 guidance, move to data-attribute class pattern: .sidebar-section[data-revealed=true] { animation: slideIn … }. (2) PublishSidebar @keyframes slideIn (line ~666-677) add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .sidebar-section[data-revealed=true] { animation: none; } }. title: ORG-02: Org Explore Page Enhancement
description:
Enhance the org explore page at `_org/[slug]/(space)/explore/` with content grid, filtering, search, and pagination.

## Current State
- **+page.svelte** is a minimal placeholder: heading, subtitle with org name, 3 divs saying "Content coming soon...". No `<style>` block, no CSS.
- **+page.server.ts** only sets `CACHE_HEADERS.DYNAMIC_PUBLIC` — no data fetching.
- **Parent layout** provides `data.org` with `{ id, slug, name, description, logoUrl, brandColors }`.
- **Error boundary** exists at `explore/+error.svelte`.
- **ContentCard** EXISTS at `$lib/components/ui/ContentCard/` — full implementation with i18n, dark mode, loading state, type badges, duration, creator.
- **`listContent`** exists in `content.remote.ts` — paginated query with params `{ page?, limit?, status?, organizationId?, search?, sortBy?, sortOrder? }`. Requires auth.

## Tasks

### 1. Enhance +page.server.ts
- Extract filter/search/pagination params from URL (`url.searchParams`)
- Fetch content via `listContent({ organizationId: parentData.org.id, status: 'published', search, sortBy, sortOrder, page, limit: 12 })` from `content.remote.ts`
- Keep `CACHE_HEADERS.DYNAMIC_PUBLIC`
- NOTE: `listContent` requires auth. For unauthenticated access, blocked by Codex-qv2. Workaround: gracefully return empty results on auth failure.

### 2. Enhance +page.svelte
- **Search**: text input with debounce (300ms), updates URL `?q=` param via `goto()`
- **Type filter**: select dropdown (All/Video/Audio/Written), updates URL `?type=` param
- **Sort**: select dropdown (Newest/Popular/A-Z), updates URL `?sort=` param
- **Content grid**: responsive grid using ContentCard component (`$lib/components/ui/ContentCard`)
- **Pagination**: previous/next buttons + page numbers when totalPages > 1
- **Empty states**: "No content yet" vs "No matches" with clear filters button
- **URL state**: all filter/search/pagination state in URL query params for bookmarkability
- Full `<style>` block with design tokens, responsive (1/2/3 col), dark mode
- All text via paraglide i18n

### 3. Debounce Utility
Check if `$lib/utils/debounce.ts` exists. If not, create it.

### 4. Required i18n Keys
- `explore_search_placeholder` — "Search content..."
- `explore_filter_all` — "All Types"
- `explore_sort_newest` — "Newest"
- `explore_sort_popular` — "Popular"
- `explore_sort_az` — "A-Z"
- `explore_clear_filters` — "Clear filters"
- `explore_no_content` — "No content yet"
- `explore_no_results` — "No content matches your search"

## Files to Modify
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/explore/+page.server.ts` — add data fetching with URL params
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/explore/+page.svelte` — full redesign

## Files to Create (if needed)
- `apps/web/src/lib/utils/debounce.ts` — if not already present

## Files to Reference
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ContentCard/ContentCard.svelte` — content card
- `apps/web/src/lib/remote/content.remote.ts` — `listContent` query

## Backend Blocker
`listContent` uses authenticated API client. Public unauthenticated browsing requires Codex-qv2. Build the full UI now; content grid will be empty for unauthenticated users until backend adds public endpoint.

## Effort: 6-8 hours
notes:
## Verified Codebase State (2026-03-18)

### Current explore/+page.svelte (exact)
Minimal placeholder — h1 "Explore Content", subtitle with org name, 3 divs with "Content coming soon..." text. No <style> block, no CSS, no filtering, no search.

### Current explore/+page.server.ts (exact)
Only sets `CACHE_HEADERS.DYNAMIC_PUBLIC`. No data fetching.

### ContentCard — EXISTS and is FULL-FEATURED
At `$lib/components/ui/ContentCard/` (NOT `components/content/ContentCard.svelte`).
Import: `import { ContentCard } from '$lib/components/ui/ContentCard'` (has index.ts barrel export).
Props: `{ id, title, thumbnail?, description?, contentType?, duration?, creator?, actions?: Snippet, href?, loading? }`

### listContent Query (from content.remote.ts)
```typescript
export const listContent = query(
  contentQuerySchema.optional(),
  async (params) => {
    // Builds URLSearchParams from: page, limit, status, organizationId, search, sortBy, sortOrder
    return api.content.list(searchParams);
  }
);
```
Requires auth (uses `createServerApi(platform, cookies)`).

### No listPublicOrgContent Function Exists
The old description proposed creating `listPublicOrgContent` in content.remote.ts — this does NOT exist yet and requires Codex-qv2 backend work.

### URL State Pattern
Use `url.searchParams` in server load and `goto()` with URL manipulation on client for filter changes. This is the correct SvelteKit pattern for bookmarkable filters.

### Error Boundary
`explore/+error.svelte` exists.

### PR Review Findings — STILL VALID
- No CSS — needs full <style> block
- No i18n — needs paraglide messages
- Missing content grid accessibility (`aria-label`)
- Missing dark mode support

### Dependency Note
Codex-64e8 (UI-03: Shared ContentCard) and Codex-aaqm (UI-02: Shared Pagination) are listed as dependencies. ContentCard already exists and is complete — that dependency should be marked resolved. Pagination component status needs checking.
