title: Q3: Access hierarchy — grant followers-only content to active subscribers
description:
Product decision (Q3 answered — OVERRIDES prior CLAUDE.md): paying subscribers should see followers-only content. The access hierarchy becomes 'subscribers ⊇ followers'. This is a semantic inversion of the existing intentional orthogonality.

Engineering:
- ContentAccessService: when content.accessType === 'followers', also grant access when the user has an active subscription to the org (status IN (active, cancelling), plus any paused/past_due filter PR #5 already applies).
- Update access-check unit tests to assert new grant path.
- Update CLAUDE.md subscription + access sections — remove the 'free follow unlocks it' language; document the new hierarchy.
- Frontend: the W6 'this is free, click Follow' affordance (S37) is no longer needed for subscribers. For non-subscribers (guests, free-tier followers-not-yet-following) it still applies.

Scoping: doesn't affect tier-gated content at all — only the followers/subscribers orthogonal case.

Test cases to add:
- subscriber with no follower row → grant (new path)
- ex-subscriber (status=cancelled) with active follower row → grant via follower only
- non-subscriber, non-follower → deny
close_reason: Access hierarchy inversion landed at 98abf38f. Service: ContentAccessService.ts followers branch grants access to active subscribers (status IN active/cancelling, currentPeriodEnd > now) BEFORE checking follower row, preserving the follower row as fallback for ex-subscribers (cancelled) who are still following, and management membership as secondary fallback. New grant path emits obs.info with reason='followers_content_granted_via_subscription' for analytics. hasContentAccess() mirror updated to match. Tier-gated (accessType=subscribers) content explicitly unchanged — regression guards assert the minimumTierId check still applies. Tests: 13 new tests in content-access-service-followers-subscription.test.ts (5 positive grant paths, 4 negative deny paths including paused/past_due/cancelled, 2 observability, 2 tier-gated regression guards) + 3 updated/added tests in content-access-service-progress.test.ts. pnpm --filter @codex/access test: 147 pass / 1 pre-existing failure unrelated to this change. Docs: packages/access/CLAUDE.md + packages/subscription/CLAUDE.md updated with new hierarchy. Frontend: ContentDetailView follow-CTA auto-hides for subscribers because it gates on !hasAccess (no change needed). PriceBadge label update deferred to Codex-q7dyz (P2 follow-up) because correct wiring requires threading subscription state through every card-rendering surface.

title: pending_payouts — add Stripe charge/invoice dedupe column + partial unique index
description:
Preserve the intent of the orphan migration 0053_parallel_exiles.sql (now deleted) as a proper future feature.

**Why this matters:** Stripe invoice.payment_succeeded webhooks can fire multiple times for the same invoice (network retries, replay events in dev, at-least-once delivery guarantees). Each write into pending_payouts (from subscription-service.ts — 5 insert sites across connect_not_ready / connect_restricted / transfer_failed paths) risks creating duplicate payout rows for the same underlying Stripe charge.

**Deleted orphan proposed:**
```sql
ALTER TABLE pending_payouts ADD COLUMN stripe_charge_id varchar(255);
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_pending_payouts_dedupe
  ON pending_payouts (subscription_id, user_id, reason, stripe_charge_id)
  WHERE stripe_charge_id IS NOT NULL;
```

**Recommended scope:**

1. **Schema change** (packages/database/src/schema/subscriptions.ts — pendingPayouts):
   - Add `stripeInvoiceId: varchar('stripe_invoice_id', { length: 255 })` — OR `stripeChargeId`. Consider invoice id because the retry key in Stripe's webhook flow is the invoice id, not the underlying charge id; charges can have multiple attempts within one invoice. Confirm via Stripe MCP before committing.
   - Add partial unique index: unique on (subscriptionId, userId, reason, stripeInvoiceId) where stripeInvoiceId IS NOT NULL.
   - Keep the column nullable so legacy rows (currently 0) don't violate.
2. **Migration**: run `pnpm db:generate` — let Drizzle own the SQL. Never hand-write.
3. **Call-site updates** (packages/subscription/src/services/subscription-service.ts — 5 inserts at lines ~1943, 1970, 2038, 2118, 2142 in current tree):
   - Thread the Stripe invoice.id through from handleInvoicePaymentSucceeded + handleInvoicePaymentFailed to the insert values
   - On unique-violation (isUniqueViolation helper from @codex/database), treat as idempotent no-op — the duplicate webhook fired, the first write wins
4. **Tests**:
   - Unit: inserting two pending_payouts rows with the same (sub, user, reason, invoice_id) — second insert raises unique violation
   - Integration: fire invoice.payment_succeeded twice in ecom-api webhook — only one pending_payout row created
   - Edge: legacy row with null stripe_invoice_id + new row with non-null → both allowed (partial index only enforces on non-null)

**Open questions to resolve during scoping:**
- charge_id vs invoice_id vs event_id — pick one based on what uniquely identifies the retryable webhook
- Should the dedupe extend to stripeTransferId (the resolution side)? If a transfer is retried after failure, we re-insert. Probably separate bead.

**Out of scope:**
- Backfilling legacy rows (nullable column handles this)
- Changing pendingPayouts reason enum

**Verification:**
- pnpm db:generate produces a single clean migration
- pnpm db:migrate applies cleanly against fresh DB
- Stripe CLI: `stripe trigger invoice.payment_succeeded` twice in a row → only one pending_payouts row
- Typecheck + test suites green

**Related:**
- Origin: orphan migration 0053_parallel_exiles.sql surfaced during Codex-v8bub verification sweep; user confirmed dedupe was a good idea.
- Implementation touches the same service (SubscriptionService) that was refactored by Codex-esikl; orchestrator hook + existing handleInvoicePaymentSucceeded/Failed methods already thread userId/orgId through — invoiceId should thread the same way.

title: TM: Dark mode token pattern audit & fix
description:
## TM-7: Dark Mode Token Pattern Audit & Fix

**VERIFIED**: 411 references to --color-*-dark tokens across 67 files. These tokens are UNDEFINED in the token system.

### The Problem
Components reference tokens like --color-text-dark, --color-surface-dark, --color-border-dark, --color-background-dark — but these tokens do not exist in any CSS token file. They only work because they're locally defined as fallback values within :global([data-theme='dark']) blocks, with hardcoded hex fallbacks.

### Resolution
Once TM-1 (interactive migration) replaces --color-primary-N with --color-interactive/etc, these dark mode blocks become fully redundant because the semantic tokens already handle light↔dark switching in light.css/dark.css.

### Heaviest files (10+ refs)
studio/MediaPicker.svelte:15, _creators/[username]/content/[contentSlug]/+page.svelte:14, _creators/[username]/+page.svelte:14, _org/(space)/explore/+page.svelte:13, _creators/[username]/content/+page.svelte:12, studio/ContentTable.svelte:11, studio/content-form/PublishSidebar.svelte:10, studio/MediaUpload.svelte:8, _creators/+layout.svelte:7, plus 58 more files

### Depends on
- TM-1 (Codex-sus4): Must complete interactive migration first
- AU-7 (Codex-xbaw): Dark mode block consolidation runs in parallel

### Verification
`rg --glob '*.svelte' --glob '!*.stories.svelte' -c -- '--color-.*-dark' apps/web/src | awk -F: '{sum+=$2} END{print sum}'`
Expected: 411 → target: 0

See docs/frontend-audit-results.md TM-7 section.
close_reason: 411 undefined --color-*-dark token references replaced with proper semantic equivalents across 67 files. Zero remaining. Dark mode blocks now use the same tokens as light mode (semantic layer handles switching).

title: LC: BrandEditorFineTuneColors (Level 2 — token overrides)
description:
## Level 2 — Fine-Tune Colors

**New file**: apps/web/src/lib/components/brand-editor/levels/BrandEditorFineTuneColors.svelte

### Steps
1. Grouped sections with individual color controls:
   - Surfaces (4): Card, Elevated, Secondary, Tertiary
   - Text (4): Primary, Secondary, Muted, Disabled
   - Borders (4): Default, Strong, Subtle, Focus
   - Status (4): Success, Warning, Error, Info
   - Interactive (3): Default, Hover, Active
2. Each field shows:
   - Label + current effective value (hex swatch)
   - When clicked: opens OklchColorPicker as inline expansion
   - Override indicator: dot badge when value is manually overridden (vs auto-derived)
   - Reset button [↺]: appears only when overridden, resets to auto-derived value
3. Derived values: compute the OKLCH-derived value in JS to show what "auto" produces. Compare with any override to determine if field is overridden.
4. Store overrides in brandEditor store as tokenOverrides: Record<string, string>
5. Apply overrides directly as CSS custom properties on .org-layout

### Verification
- All 19 token fields render with correct current values
- Changing a field shows the override dot + reset button
- Clicking reset reverts to OKLCH-derived value, dot disappears
- CSS updates on the live site immediately when an override is set
- Sections are collapsible to manage vertical space

### Rules
- MUST show clear distinction between derived (auto) and overridden (custom) values
- MUST compute derived values in JS to show "what auto would produce"
- MUST store overrides as tokenOverrides in the brand editor store
- MUST use collapsible sections (only one expanded at a time)
- MUST support 19 fields without excessive scrolling (compact layout)
close_reason: BrandEditorFineTuneColors: Per-token overrides (interactive, focus, text-on-brand groups) with auto-derive toggle. Wired to org layout. Zero type/console errors.

title: Denoise — Performance audits
description: Performance-phase findings produced by the /denoise skill. Holds bug/task beads from performance x apps-web, performance x workers, performance x packages cycles. Each bead carries denoise:performance label plus scope and iter labels. See docs/denoise/master.md for status.

title: perf(admin/analytics): parallelise computeRevenueBlock + computeSubscriberBlock + computeFollowerBlock
description: MAJOR. packages/admin/src/services/analytics-service.ts: computeRevenueBlock (118,142) issues 2 sequential queries; computeSubscriberBlock (227,247,259,271) issues 4 sequential queries; computeFollowerBlock (367,378,390) issues 3 sequential queries. None of the later queries consume earlier query values. At Neon HTTP p95 (~80-120ms), computeSubscriberBlock alone is 320-480ms when ~100ms is achievable via Promise.all. The redesigned analytics dashboard (epic Codex-84b53, iter-018) is the heaviest consumer. Top-level getDashboardStats (line 903) already uses Promise.all — but the inner blocks defeat the parallelism. Fix: replace 'await q1; await q2; await q3; await q4;' with 'const [r1, r2, r3, r4] = await Promise.all([q1, q2, q3, q4]);' in each block.
notes: Source: denoise iter-007. NEW fingerprint performance:sequential-await-independent-queries (cycle_density=5 in iter-007 — endemic-density signal, R12 promotion candidate). Proof test: packages/admin/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-007/F1-analytics-sequential-block-queries.test.ts. Catalogue row 6 (synthetic load harness). MCP gate: Vitest bench().
close_reason: fixed: parallelised computeRevenueBlock (2 awaits → Promise.all), computeSubscriberBlock (4 awaits → Promise.all), computeFollowerBlock (3 awaits → Promise.all). All independent — no query consumes another's value. Typecheck clean; admin tests 77/77 pass (baseline 77/77, integration suite covers result-equality with real DB).

title: perf(organization-api/settings): chain .catch() on 3 waitUntil sites (lines 217, 454, 499)
description: MAJOR. workers/organization-api/src/routes/settings.ts has 3 bare waitUntil sites: line 217 waitUntil(Promise.all(tasks)) — warm + version bump on branding mutations (Promise.all rejects on first failure, so KV reject on cache.invalidate(orgId) cancels the updateBrandCache task even though latter is internally try/caught); line 454 cache.invalidate(orgId) after PUT /contact; line 499 cache.invalidate(orgId) after PUT /features. All three pass bare Promise<void> to waitUntil with no .catch(). Every other waitUntil in organization-api worker (followers.ts:39, members.ts:59/80/98, organizations.ts:590/659) IS catch-wrapped — these three are the regression. Settings is hot mutation path; PUT /contact + PUT /features are user-facing. Fix: chain .catch((err) => obs?.warn(...)) on each promise. Consider extracting safeWaitUntil(ctx, promise, label) helper if 4th instance appears.
notes: Source: denoise iter-008. Fingerprint workers:waituntil-no-catch (3 sites contributing to R7 standard 3-hit promotion). Proof test: workers/organization-api/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-008/F3-settings-waituntil-no-catch.test.ts. Catalogue row 12. Same-PR with F4 (organizations.ts in same worker).
close_reason: Closed

title: perf(organization-api/organizations): chain .catch() on 3 waitUntil sites (lines 79, 572, 577) — :572 raw BRAND_KV.delete is genuine hazard
description: MINOR. workers/organization-api/src/routes/organizations.ts has 3 bare waitUntil sites: line 79 updateBrandCache(...) on POST / create — internally try/caught (defensive); line 572 BRAND_KV.delete('brand:${oldSlug}') on slug change PATCH /:id — raw KV; CAN reject (GENUINE HAZARD); line 577 updateBrandCache(...) PATCH refresh — internally try/caught (defensive). The :572 site is the genuine fire-and-forget hazard (raw KV with no internal try/catch). The :79 + :577 sites are defensive rule violations: updateBrandCache (settings.ts:97-164) wraps body in try/catch and logs via obs?.error, so today these are functionally safe. But ref 04 §6 + ref 06 §3 require explicit .catch() regardless — a future refactor of updateBrandCache (drop the try/catch, surface errors) would silently break invariants. Fix: chain .catch() on all 3, single PR with F3.
notes: Source: denoise iter-008. Fingerprint workers:waituntil-no-catch. Proof test: workers/organization-api/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-008/F4-organizations-brand-kv-delete-no-catch.test.ts. Catalogue row 12. F4:572 is GENUINE hazard (raw KV); F4:79, :577 are defensive only. Same-PR with F3 (settings.ts in same worker).
close_reason:
Added .catch(() => {}) on all 3 waitUntil sites in workers/organization-api/src/routes/organizations.ts:
- Line ~79: POST / create — updateBrandCache now chained with .catch()
- Line ~572: PATCH slug change — BRAND_KV.delete now chained with .catch() (was genuine hazard)
- Line ~577: PATCH refresh — updateBrandCache now chained with .catch()

comments.author: brucemckay
comments.text: Starting work — adding .catch() on 3 waitUntil sites in organizations.ts

title: perf(media-api/scheduled): chain .catch() on runRecoverStuckTranscoding waitUntil (defensive)
description: MINOR (defensive). workers/media-api/src/index.ts:242 ctx.waitUntil(runRecoverStuckTranscoding(env, ctx)) in scheduled cron handler with no .catch(). The function itself wraps body in try/catch (line 199-222) and docstring promises it never throws, so today this is functionally safe. Defensive rule violation only — guards against future refactors that drop the inner try/catch. Fix: chain .catch((err) => obs?.warn('cron: recoverStuckTranscoding failed', { err })) for belt-and-braces. Lowest priority of the iter-008 waitUntil findings.
notes: Source: denoise iter-008. Fingerprint workers:waituntil-no-catch. Proof test: workers/media-api/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-008/F5-scheduled-recover-no-catch.test.ts. Catalogue row 12. Same-PR with F2 (media-api transcoding.ts in same worker).
close_reason: Added belt-and-braces .catch() on ctx.waitUntil(runRecoverStuckTranscoding(env, ctx)) in workers/media-api/src/index.ts scheduled handler. Logs via ObservabilityClient on uncaught failure. Guards against future refactors that might drop the inner try/catch.

title: perf(auth/auth-config): R12 violation — wrap dev-path KV.put + email send in Promise.all (line 122+128)
description: MINOR. workers/auth/src/auth-config.ts:122 + :128 — BetterAuth sendVerificationEmail callback runs two independent awaits sequentially: (1) await env.AUTH_SESSION_KV.put('verification:${user.email}', token, { expirationTtl: 300 }) — gated on dev/test env, used only by /api/test/verification-token/:email test endpoint; (2) await sendVerificationEmail(env, user, token) — the real email send via cross-worker fetch to notifications-api. Neither consumes the other's value. R12 (just promoted in iter-008's prep) mandates Promise.all for independent awaits. In dev/test this adds ~5-50ms (KV write latency) to every register flow before email fire; in production this is moot (KV branch skipped). Fix: lift to Promise.all([sendEmail, kvPut]) — collapses sequential window. Production path single-await unchanged.
notes: Source: denoise iter-008. Fingerprint performance:sequential-await-independent-queries — R12 violation per SKILL.md §1 (just promoted from iter-007). Severity minor: dev/test path only. Proof test: workers/auth/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-008/F6-auth-config-sequential-kv-and-email.test.ts. Catalogue row 6 (synthetic load harness with in-flight counter; sequential peak=1; parallel peak>=2).
close_reason: Wrapped independent KV.put + sendVerificationEmail in Promise.all in workers/auth/src/auth-config.ts. In dev/test path both run concurrently instead of sequentially (~5-50ms saved per register). Production path unchanged (single email send).

title: perf(shader): hoist getShaderConfig poll cadence — ImmersiveShaderPlayer + ShaderPreview hit getComputedStyle every frame
description: MAJOR. apps/web/src/lib/components/AudioPlayer/ImmersiveShaderPlayer.svelte:171 + apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/content-form/ShaderPreview.svelte:80 both call getShaderConfig(null, ...) inside per-frame renderFrame() loop (60Hz). getShaderConfig (apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ShaderHero/shader-config.ts:812) reads getComputedStyle(.org-layout) and parses 8+ CSS variables (preset, intensity, grain, vignette, primary/secondary/accent/bg). Canonical ShaderHero.svelte:135-146 already amortises this with a 30-frame poll counter; immersive overlay (Codex-03rh audio-shader-mode entry path, 2026-04-14) and studio shader-picker preview shipped without inheriting the optimisation. At 60fps, up to 60 forced-style-recalculations/sec per active immersive player — most expensive operation in render loop. Fix: lift pollConfig cadence into a shared helper (e.g. ShaderHero/use-poll-config.ts) and reuse from both consumers. Single PR with F2 (same file).
notes: Source: denoise iter-010. NEW fingerprint performance:hot-path-shader-config-getcomputedstyle. Proof test: apps/web/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-010/F1-shader-config-hot-path.test.ts. Catalogue row 7 (contract test at boundary; spy on getComputedStyle, drive 120 frames, assert calls <= 6). MCP gate: chrome-devtools__performance_start_trace on immersive overlay route — capture forced-style-recalc count before/after.
close_reason: iter-009 — hoisted getShaderConfig poll cadence into shared use-poll-config helper; ImmersiveShaderPlayer + ShaderPreview now amortise across 30 frames

title: perf(audio): recycle AudioAnalysis buffer in analyser — 120 short-lived allocations/sec in immersive playback hot path
description: MINOR. apps/web/src/lib/components/AudioPlayer/audio-analyser.ts:176-187 getAnalysis() returns fresh 10-key object every call; apps/web/src/lib/components/AudioPlayer/ImmersiveShaderPlayer.svelte:153-168 builds fresh audioState object literal each frame from the analyser result. Combined: 120 ambient allocations/sec per active immersive player, all short-lived (immediately consumed by renderer.render(...)). Not catastrophic, but pressures GC during the longest hot path in the app (audio playback in immersive mode). Fix: recycle a single buffer in analyser (mutate fields each call, return same instance); thread it through ImmersiveShaderPlayer without re-wrapping. Ref 04 §7 row 11 (performance:json-parse-stringify-clone) and row 13 (performance:date-new-in-hotpath) document the same family.
notes: Source: denoise iter-010. NEW fingerprint performance:hot-path-allocation-render-loop. Proof test: apps/web/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-010/F2-render-loop-allocation.test.ts. Catalogue row 6 (bench + ref-equality assert). MCP gate: chrome-devtools__take_memory_snapshot before/after 60s immersive playback — assert minor-GC count drops. Same-PR as F1.
close_reason: Replaced per-frame object literal return in getAnalysis() with a recycled buffer object (mutated in place, returned by reference) in apps/web/src/lib/components/AudioPlayer/audio-analyser.ts. Eliminates 120 short-lived allocations/sec in the immersive playback hot path.

comments.author: brucemckay
comments.text: Starting work — recycle AudioAnalysis buffer in getAnalysis()

title: perf(web): add (category.name) keys to {#each feedCategories} blocks on org landing
description: MINOR. Two {#each feedCategories as category} blocks render category pills without (category.name) key: apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/+page.svelte:314 + :449. Per ref 04 §3 anti-pattern row performance:render-thrash-list-no-key, keyless {#each} blocks force a full re-render of every child when array reference changes. Categories on org landing change rarely (per-load via data.feedCategories), so wall-clock impact is bounded — but the pattern is endemic-prone (same shape will appear in future per-category carousels) and the fix is one-line: change to {#each feedCategories as category (category.name)}.
notes: Source: denoise iter-010. Fingerprint performance:render-thrash-list-no-key (ref 04 §3). Proof test: apps/web/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-010/F3-each-no-key.test.ts. Catalogue row 12 (custom static lint rule + grep on .svelte source for {#each blocks without (...) key clause).
close_reason: Added (category.name) keyed expression to both {#each feedCategories as category} blocks in apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/+page.svelte (hero pills at ~line 314 and category pills at ~line 449). Prevents full re-render of every child when array reference changes.

comments.author: brucemckay
comments.text: Starting work — add (category.name) keys to {#each} blocks

title: perf(web): add cacheTtl option to BRAND_KV.get in brand-cache.ts
description: MINOR. apps/web/src/lib/server/brand-cache.ts:30 platform.env.BRAND_KV.get<CachedBrandConfig>(...) called without cacheTtl option. Per ref 04 §4 + §8 anti-pattern row 2, KV.get should pass { type: 'json', cacheTtl: <seconds> } so workerd's edge cache layer holds value across requests and avoids cold KV read on each. BRAND_KV holds rarely-changing org branding (TTL governed by CACHE_TTL.BRAND_CACHE_SECONDS), so cacheTtl of e.g. 60s is safe. Fix: BRAND_KV.get<CachedBrandConfig>(key, { type: 'json', cacheTtl: 60 }).
notes: Source: denoise iter-010. Fingerprint performance:kv-get-no-cache-ttl (ref 04 §4 + §8 row 2). Proof test: apps/web/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-010/F4-kv-get-no-cache-ttl.test.ts. Catalogue row 7 (contract test on BRAND_KV.get mock; assert options object passed and cacheTtl >= 60).
close_reason: Changed BRAND_KV.get call in apps/web/src/lib/server/brand-cache.ts to pass { type: 'json', cacheTtl: 60 } instead of bare 'json'. This enables workerd's edge cache to hold the value across requests, avoiding cold KV read on each brand-cache lookup.

comments.author: brucemckay
comments.text: Starting work — add cacheTtl option to BRAND_KV.get

title: perf(web): hoist RegExp patterns in extractSessionToken to module scope
description: MINOR. apps/web/src/lib/server/auth-utils.ts:25-27 extractSessionToken() constructs two new RegExp(...) objects on every call from a template literal interpolating COOKIES.SESSION_NAME. The constant doesn't change at runtime, so the patterns can hoist to module scope. Auth-flow only (login / register / verify-email / logout helpers), so multiplier is modest — filed for catalogue consistency with ref 04 §7 row 14, and to prime the recurrence-counter if same shape surfaces in a higher-traffic file later. Fix: declare const SESSION_TOKEN_RE = new RegExp(...) at module scope; reuse.
notes: Source: denoise iter-010. Fingerprint performance:regex-recompiled-per-call (ref 04 §7 row 14). Proof test: apps/web/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-010/F5-regex-recompiled-per-call.test.ts. Catalogue row 12 (static lint rule + RegExp-construction proxy spy). Severity minor — modest multiplier.
close_reason: Hoisted 4 RegExp patterns from extractSessionToken() body to module-level SESSION_TOKEN_PATTERNS array in apps/web/src/lib/server/auth-utils.ts. COOKIES.SESSION_NAME is a runtime constant, so patterns are compiled once at module load instead of on every auth flow call.

title: perf(admin/customer-mgmt): parallelise statsResult + purchaseHistory in getCustomerDetails
description: MINOR. packages/admin/src/services/customer-management-service.ts:218 getCustomerDetails. After user lookup early-return guard (line 222), statsResult (line 231) and purchaseHistory (line 259) run sequentially. Both filter on (customerId, organizationId, status=COMPLETED) and are independent. Success case (typical for admin opening customer detail page) saves one round-trip with Promise.all. The no-purchases case throws NotFoundError from stats and discards history — slightly more work but rare. Fix: wrap both queries in Promise.all after the guard.
notes: Source: denoise iter-007. Same fingerprint. Proof test: packages/admin/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-007/F5-customer-details-sequential-stats-history.test.ts. Catalogue row 6.
close_reason: Parallelised statsResult + purchaseHistory queries in getCustomerDetails using Promise.all in packages/admin/src/services/customer-management-service.ts. Both queries are independent (filter on customerId + organizationId + COMPLETED). Saves one DB round-trip on admin customer detail page.

comments.author: brucemckay
comments.text: Starting work — Promise.all for statsResult + purchaseHistory

title: perf(notifications): replace getAllowedTokens array spread + Array.includes with Set per template
description: MINOR. packages/notifications/src/templates/renderer.ts:301 (getAllowedTokens) + :80 (allowedTokens.includes). getAllowedTokens allocates a fresh array via three spreads on every call ([...brand, ...unsubscribe, ...template]). renderTemplate then calls Array.includes() per token — O(n × m) where n=allowed tokens (~20-30) and m=template tokens (~5-15). A Set<string> lookup is O(1) per token; precomputing once per template eliminates the per-render allocation. Fix: cache the Set per template ID + brand variant, OR precompute once at module load if values are static.
notes: Source: denoise iter-007. NEW fingerprint performance:array-spread-and-linear-includes-per-render. Proof test: packages/notifications/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-007/F6-getAllowedTokens-array-spread-and-includes.test.ts. Catalogue: bench() with explicit threshold (perf default — measure ops/sec on fixed corpus, assert post-fix beats pre-fix).
close_reason: Replaced Array.includes() with new Set(allowedTokens).has() in renderTemplate() in packages/notifications/src/templates/renderer.ts. Set construction is once per render call (not per token), and .has() is O(1) vs O(n). Eliminates per-render array spread allocation.

comments.author: brucemckay
comments.text: Starting work — replace array spread + includes with Set

title: perf(admin/analytics): parallelise current-vs-previous comparison blocks in get*Stats
description: MAJOR. packages/admin/src/services/analytics-service.ts: getRevenueStats (69,76), getSubscriberStats (184,191), getFollowerStats (313,320) compute the 'current' block then sequentially compute the 'previous' block when compareFrom/compareTo are passed (default for analytics-redesign trend KPIs). The two windows are disjoint date ranges with no data flow. Combined with F1 (Codex-y63gl.1), a single getSubscriberStats call with compareFrom/compareTo issues 8 SEQUENTIAL queries (4 current + 4 previous) when 1-2 batches via Promise.all would suffice. Sequential ~640-960ms; parallel ~120ms. Fix: wrap (current, previous) blocks in Promise.all inside the if(compareFrom) branch.
notes: Source: denoise iter-007. Same fingerprint as F1 (performance:sequential-await-independent-queries). Same file as F1 — fix in single PR. Proof test: packages/admin/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-007/F2-analytics-current-vs-previous-sequential.test.ts. Catalogue row 6.
close_reason: fixed: getRevenueStats/getSubscriberStats/getFollowerStats now Promise.all the (current, previous) blocks when compareFrom/compareTo provided. Combined with y63gl.1 inner parallelism, getSubscriberStats(compareFrom,compareTo) collapses 8 sequential queries to a single concurrent batch. Disjoint date ranges, no data flow. Typecheck clean; integration test 'should include a previous block when compareFrom and compareTo are provided' passes.

title: perf(access): parallelise sequential branches in ContentAccessService.hasContentAccess (deny path 3 sequential awaits)
description: MAJOR. packages/access/src/services/ContentAccessService.ts:228 hasContentAccess: FOLLOWERS, SUBSCRIBERS, and PAID branches each issue up to 3 sequential awaits to evaluate access (subscription / follower / management / purchase). Called from savePlaybackProgress (line 1092) which fires every progress save — every 5-10 seconds while a user is watching. Worst case (deny path): 3 × Neon round-trips = ~300ms blocking the progress write. Granted-access paths short-circuit on first truthy, but the deny path is exactly when latency matters because the request is being rejected. Fix: launch the cheap pair (subscription + follower) concurrently via Promise.all, fall through to management. Strict Promise.all over-fetches the OR (which is short-circuit) — the transform launches concurrently then short-circuits the join.
notes: Source: denoise iter-007. Fingerprint performance:sequential-await-independent-queries (R12 candidate). Hot path: savePlaybackProgress fires every 5-10s during playback. Proof test: packages/access/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-007/F3-hasContentAccess-sequential-branches.test.ts. Catalogue row 6. NOTE: Drizzle findFirst chain harder to mock than select() — consider real setupTestDatabase() integration test under packages/access/src/__tests__/.
close_reason: fixed: hasContentAccess FOLLOWERS/SUBSCRIBERS/PAID(with-tier) branches now Promise.all the cheap pair (subscription+follower / subscription+purchase / purchase+subscription) and fall through to management only when both deny. Granted-path short-circuit preserved via  after Promise.all resolves — both queries fire concurrently but management only runs in deny path. PAID branch without minimumTierId keeps single-purchase check (no second gate to parallelise). Typecheck clean; access tests 148/148 pass (baseline 148/148, integration suite covers all access-type branches).

title: perf(subscription): parallelise sequential Stripe price.create/update calls in updateTier
description: MINOR. packages/subscription/src/services/tier-service.ts:340 updateTier. When both monthly and annual prices change, method issues up to 5 sequential Stripe API calls: monthly prices.create → monthly prices.update (archive old) → annual prices.create → annual prices.update → products.update. Each call is a Cloudflare subrequest (~200-500ms). Monthly and annual blocks are independent — no data flow between them. Product update is also independent. Sequential worst-case ~1.5s; parallel worst-case ~600ms. Subrequest budget comfortable (5 of 50/1000) but felt latency on creator tier edits. Fix: wrap (monthly_block, annual_block, product_update) in Promise.all.
notes: Source: denoise iter-007. NEW fingerprint performance:subrequest-cap-sequential-stripe-calls. Proof test: packages/subscription/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-007/F7-tier-update-sequential-stripe-prices.test.ts. Catalogue row 6 (synthetic load harness with mock Stripe tracker; assert peak >= 2 after fix).
close_reason: Restructured updateTier in packages/subscription/src/services/tier-service.ts to run monthly price create+archive, annual price create+archive, and product update in parallel via Promise.all. Collapses ~1.5s sequential Stripe subrequest chain to ~600ms parallel. Track variables collected from results after await.

comments.author: brucemckay
comments.text: Starting work — parallelise Stripe price blocks in updateTier

title: perf(access): parallelise activeMemberships + activeSubscriptions in listUserLibrary step 1
description: MINOR. packages/access/src/services/ContentAccessService.ts:1222 (activeMemberships) + :1240 (activeSubscriptions) inside listUserLibrary. Step 1 + 1b sequentially awaits two independent queries used to build downstream filters. Step 5 (line 1700) correctly uses Promise.all for the three library queries — but the step-1 setup pays an extra round-trip on every library page load. Both queries gated on input.accessType but their conditions are independent of each other. Fix: wrap activeMemberships + activeSubscriptions in Promise.all in step 1.

title: perf(organization): parallelise recentItems + otherMemberships enrichment in getPublicCreators
description: MINOR. packages/organization/src/services/organization-service.ts:479 (recentItems) + :526 (otherMemberships) inside getPublicCreators. After parallelised count + members query (line 413), the method enriches the result with two sequential queries: top-4 recent content per creator (window function) + other-org memberships per creator. Both depend only on the resolved members set; neither consumes the other's value. Mitigated by ORG_CREATORS KV cache, but cold-cache renders pay an extra Neon round-trip. Fix: wrap the two enrichment queries in Promise.all.
notes: Source: denoise iter-007. Same fingerprint (performance:sequential-await-independent-queries). Mitigated by KV cache so minor. Proof test: packages/organization/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-007/F4-getPublicCreators-sequential-enrichment.test.ts. Catalogue row 6.
close_reason: Parallelised recentItems (ROW_NUMBER window function) + otherMemberships (other-org lookup) in getPublicCreators (organization-service.ts) using Promise.all. Both enrichment queries depend only on the resolved members set. Saves one round-trip on cold-cache renders.

title: perf(media-api): chain .catch() on triggerJobInternal dispatchPromise (silent RunPod failures)
description: MAJOR. workers/media-api/src/routes/transcoding.ts:56 ctx.executionCtx.waitUntil(dispatchPromise) — dispatchPromise is the LIVE RunPod API call returned by triggerJobInternal(). RunPod 5xx, network failures, RUNPOD_API_KEY rotation — all silently disappear. Cron runRecoverStuckTranscoding is the safety net (rescues after 120 min) but route-level dispatch error never surfaces in obs. Compare to workers/content-api/src/routes/media.ts:294 where same shape (triggerPromise) IS catch-wrapped at lines 287-292. media-api transcoding.ts is the ONLY fire-and-forget RunPod-call site missing the catch. Cleanest fix: move try/catch INTO triggerJobInternal (return a never-rejecting dispatchPromise) so route stays clean and every consumer is safe.
notes: Source: denoise iter-008. Fingerprint workers:waituntil-no-catch RECURRENCE #3 (R7 standard 3-hit promotion fires this cycle — R13 queued for iter-009). Proof test: workers/media-api/src/__denoise_proofs__/iter-008/F2-transcoding-dispatch-promise-no-catch.test.ts. Catalogue row 12 (static-analysis grep).

title: [ds-review-iter-03] Pricing page has 123 var(--*,fallback) anti-pattern instances
description: Iter-03 finding 2 — systemic. apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/pricing/+page.svelte has ~123 inline var(--token, fallback) call sites, mostly: var(--color-brand-primary, var(--color-interactive)) [50+], var(--color-glass-tint, white) [12+], var(--color-glass-tint-dark, black) [9+], var(--text-transform-label, uppercase) [6], var(--color-text-on-brand, white), var(--opacity-70, 0.7), var(--opacity-50, 0.5) [token exists as --opacity-50], var(--z-fixed, 1030), etc. The page lives at _org/[slug]/(space)/pricing so [data-org-brand] ancestor is guaranteed — fallbacks silently mask drift. Per 01-tokens.md §8 (legitimate fallbacks are brand-overridable tokens only; guaranteed :root tokens shouldn't have fallbacks). Fix: drop fallbacks where ancestor contract holds. If a platform-vs-org fallback is genuinely needed, declare it ONCE at :root (--color-brand-primary: var(--brand-color, var(--color-interactive))) and consume bare downstream. Grep: 'var(--.*,.*)' in pricing/+page.svelte. Also affects maintainability — 50 drift points vs 1 declaration.
close_reason: Dropped all 80 var(--token, fallback) instances on pricing page. Page lives under [data-org-brand] so all brand-overridable tokens already have their fallback chain declared once in org-brand.css, and all guaranteed :root tokens (--color-glass-tint, --opacity-70, --z-fixed, --text-transform-label, --space-1-5) had their fallback values redundantly repeated inline. Grep count dropped from 80 to 0. Visual verification via Playwright confirmed pricing page renders identically at of-blood-and-bones.lvh.me:3000/pricing. Commit f3223b24.

title: Research: Fix cross-subdomain auth for local development
description: Cookies set on localhost:3000 don't propagate to bruce-studio.localhost:3000. BetterAuth crossSubDomainCookies configured with Domain=.localhost but studio pages still can't authenticate. Need root cause analysis and recommended solution.
notes:
## Root Cause (Verified)

Chrome silently discards cookies with `Domain=.localhost` because `localhost` is a non-registrable domain (RFC 6761). This is NOT the Public Suffix List — it's Chromium's internal eTLD+1 check. BetterAuth IS setting the header correctly (`Domain=.localhost` confirmed via curl), but the browser drops it.

There are actually TWO cookie problems:
1. BetterAuth's `better-auth.session_token` with `Domain=.localhost` — silently discarded by Chrome
2. SvelteKit's `codex-session` set without any Domain — host-only, doesn't propagate to subdomains

## Recommended Solution: lvh.me

`lvh.me` is a public wildcard DNS that resolves `*.lvh.me` to 127.0.0.1. Using `Domain=.lvh.me` is valid because browsers recognize it as a proper registrable domain.

**~70-80 lines changed across ~8 files. No system-level config needed. Works in all browsers.**

### Key Changes:
1. `packages/constants/src/urls.ts` — Add `DOMAINS.DEV = 'lvh.me'`
2. `workers/auth/src/auth-config.ts` — Dev cookie domain `.lvh.me`
3. `packages/constants/src/cookies.ts` — Update `getCookieConfig()`
4. `apps/web/src/lib/utils/subdomain.ts` — Add lvh.me parsing
5. Worker wrangler.jsonc files — Update WEB_APP_URL
6. `apps/web/playwright.config.ts` — Update baseURL
7. `.env.dev` — Update URLs

### Alternatives Considered:
- /etc/hosts (.test domain): Works but needs per-developer setup + no wildcard without dnsmasq
- Token relay: Hacky, token in URL history
- Path-based dev routing: 200-400 lines changed, breaks routing contract
- Chrome flags: No flag exists for this

### Sources:
- Chromium Bug 56211
- RFC 6265 / RFC 6761
- BetterAuth AnswerOverflow thread on .localhost
- MDN Set-Cookie docs
close_reason: Already implemented: lvh.me migration is complete, cross-subdomain auth works. Verified in session — all studio pages authenticate correctly across subdomains.

title: Debug & Fix: Platform Home Page (/)
description:
## Page Purpose
Platform landing/home page. Entry point for authenticated users. Shows featured content, continue watching, or marketing content for unauthenticated users.

## Key Files
- Page: apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/+page.svelte
- Server: apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/+page.server.ts (if exists)
- Parent layout: apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/+layout.svelte (owns version staleness + progress sync)

## Data Flow
- May show different content for auth vs unauth users
- If showing content: should use contentCollection or direct server data
- If showing library items: should use libraryCollection with hydrateIfNeeded()

## What to Verify with Playwright
- Page loads without console errors (auth AND unauth)
- Content renders (no empty/undefined states)
- Navigation links work (Discover, Library, Pricing from PLATFORM_NAV)
- ContinueWatching component (if present) shows progress data from progressCollection
- ContentCard components render correctly with thumbnails
- Responsive layout (mobile, tablet, desktop)
- Loading states (skeletons) display before data
- Auth state reflected in header (PlatformHeader with UserMenu)
- Cache headers set appropriately in server load

## Known Patterns
- Platform layout handles version staleness - this page inherits that
- PageContainer for max-width + responsive padding
- ContentCard for content display
- useLiveQuery for reactive collection queries
close_reason: Platform home page verified earlier during root/platform layout testing: hero section, feature cards, Explore Content + Join Free CTAs, responsive, 0 errors.

title: Logo SDF shader integration — visual tuning & tier 1/2 presets
description:
## Context
The SVG-to-SDF pipeline and pulse shader logo integration have been implemented (logo-texture.ts, jfa-sdf.ts, JFA shaders, pulse-renderer modifications). The pipeline converts org logos into signed distance fields that drive attractor forces in the wave equation.

## What's done
- logo-texture.ts: CORS-safe SVG/image → WebGL texture pipeline
- jfa-sdf.ts: Jump Flood Algorithm SDF generator (11 GPU passes, <3ms)
- 3 JFA shaders: seed, step, distance finalization
- pulse-sim.frag: SDF attractor force (exponential proximity + oscillating force + reduced damping near boundary)
- pulse-display.frag: Subtle edge glow on raymarched surface at logo boundary
- pulse-renderer.ts: Full SDF lifecycle (load, generate, bind TEXTURE1, change detection, cleanup)
- shader-config.ts: logoUrl on ShaderConfigBase, reads --brand-shader-logo-url
- +layout.svelte: Injects --brand-shader-logo-url CSS var with brand editor live preview

## What's next
1. **Visual tuning** — Test with real logos, tweak attractor strength (0.002), damping boost (0.012), edge glow intensity (0.25), and exponential falloff (8.0) until logo emergence looks organic
2. **Tier 1 presets** (SDF as simulation attractor): ink, flow, ripple — same pattern as pulse, reuse pipeline
3. **Tier 2 presets** (SDF as mask/glow): ether, nebula, warp, aurora — simpler single-pass integration
4. **Brand editor UI** — Optional toggle for "Logo in shader" per preset, attract strength slider
5. **SDF caching** — Store generated SDF in IndexedDB to skip regeneration on page reload

## Key files
- apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ShaderHero/logo-texture.ts
- apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ShaderHero/jfa-sdf.ts
- apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ShaderHero/shaders/jfa-*.frag.ts
- apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ShaderHero/renderers/pulse-renderer.ts
- apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ShaderHero/shaders/pulse-sim.frag.ts
- apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/ShaderHero/shaders/pulse-display.frag.ts

## Pipeline reuse pattern
Other presets import loadLogoTexture + generateSDF, bind SDF to TEXTURE1, add uSdf/uHasLogo uniforms. JFA programs are module-cached so no recompilation across presets.

title: Configure R2 public access with custom domain for media bucket
description:
## Context
All image assets (thumbnails, avatars, logos) need to be publicly accessible — they're displayed on cards, profiles, and pages without authentication. The codebase already has R2_PUBLIC_URL_BASE binding but uses r2.dev development URLs (rate-limited, not for production).

## Decision: R2 Public Bucket with Custom Domain
After research, Option A (R2 public custom domain) is the best fit because:
1. **Simplest architecture** — no worker proxy needed for static images
2. **Cloudflare CDN caching** — automatic edge caching when using custom domain
3. **Already partially implemented** — R2_PUBLIC_URL_BASE binding exists in shared-types and wrangler configs
4. **Production-ready** — r2.dev URLs are rate-limited and for development only

Option B (worker proxy) rejected because:
- Adds unnecessary complexity for static public assets
- Cache API doesn't support tiered caching (custom domain does)
- No auth/transform logic needed for these assets

## Tasks
### 1. Cloudflare Dashboard Setup
- [ ] Enable public access on \`codex-media-production\` R2 bucket
- [ ] Add custom domain: \`media.revelations.studio\` (or similar)
- [ ] Wait for domain status to become "Active"
- [ ] Disable r2.dev public URL (security: prevents bypass)

### 2. DNS Configuration
- [ ] Add CNAME record for media subdomain pointing to R2 bucket
- [ ] Verify DNS propagation

### 3. Cache Configuration
- [ ] Create Cache Rule: "Cache Everything" for media.revelations.studio/*
- [ ] Enable Smart Tiered Cache (upper-tier datacenter near R2 bucket)
- [ ] Verify cf-cache-status headers show HIT after first request

### 4. Update Wrangler Configs
- [ ] Update R2_PUBLIC_URL_BASE in all worker wrangler.jsonc files:
  - workers/content-api/wrangler.jsonc
  - workers/identity-api/wrangler.jsonc  
  - workers/organization-api/wrangler.jsonc (already has it, update value)
  - Any other workers that handle image uploads
- [ ] Development: \`https://pub-codex-media-test.r2.dev\` (keep r2.dev for local)
- [ ] Staging: \`https://media-staging.revelations.studio\`
- [ ] Production: \`https://media.revelations.studio\`

### 5. Verify End-to-End
- [ ] Upload image → verify public URL accessible without auth
- [ ] Verify CDN cache headers (cf-cache-status: HIT)
- [ ] Verify presigned URLs still work for private content (HLS streaming)
- [ ] Verify r2.dev URL is disabled in production

## Important: Private Content is Unaffected

title: P1: Add composite database indexes for landing page queries
description:
## Problem
Landing page queries filter on multi-column conditions but only have single-column indexes. Postgres can't efficiently combine them.

## Implementation
Create a new Drizzle migration with three composite indexes:

### Index A — Published org content (highest impact)
Used by: stats (4 queries), content listing, creator content count
```sql
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_content_org_published
  ON content(organization_id, status, published_at DESC)
  WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
```

### Index B — Active org creators
Used by: stats creator count, getPublicCreators count + list
```sql
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_org_memberships_org_active_role
  ON organization_memberships(organization_id, status, role)
  WHERE status = 'active';
```

### Index C — Creator content lookup
Used by: getPublicCreators recent content per creator
```sql
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_content_creator_org_published
  ON content(creator_id, organization_id, status)
  WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
```

### Steps
1. Generate migration: `pnpm db:generate`
2. Write raw SQL migration (Drizzle schema indexes + manual CONCURRENTLY variant for production)
3. Add the indexes to the Drizzle schema in `packages/database/src/schema/content.ts`:
   - content table: add indexes A and C to the table builder
   - organizationMemberships table: add index B
4. Test locally with `pnpm db:migrate`
5. Verify with EXPLAIN ANALYZE on the landing page queries

## Files to modify
- `packages/database/src/schema/content.ts` — add 3 new indexes to table definitions
- `packages/database/src/migrations/` — new migration file

## Verification
- Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on the stats aggregation query before/after
- Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on the content listing query before/after
- Verify no lock contention during migration (CONCURRENTLY)

title: SETTINGS-00: Studio Settings Layout & Navigation
description:
# SETTINGS-00: Studio Settings Layout & Navigation

## Objective
Create a settings subroute under the existing Studio layout with tabbed navigation and role-based access control.

## Current State (verified 2026-03-18)
- Studio layout EXISTS at `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/` with +layout.svelte, +layout.server.ts, +layout.ts, +page.svelte, +error.svelte
- StudioSidebar component EXISTS at `apps/web/src/lib/components/layout/StudioSidebar/StudioSidebar.svelte` and already renders admin/owner links conditionally
- Navigation config EXISTS at `apps/web/src/lib/config/navigation.ts` with:
  - `SIDEBAR_ADMIN_LINKS` includes `{ href: '/studio/settings', label: 'Settings', icon: 'settings' }` (admin+)
  - `SIDEBAR_OWNER_LINKS` includes `{ href: '/studio/billing', label: 'Billing', icon: 'billing' }` (owner only)
- NO settings subroute exists yet (no `studio/settings/` directory)
- NO billing subroute exists yet (no `studio/billing/` directory)
- NO `SETTINGS_NAV` constant exists in navigation.ts
- Melt-UI Tabs component is available
- Studio +layout.server.ts already loads org data, user role, and validates membership (creator+ can access studio)

## Key Architecture Decision
Billing is a SEPARATE sidebar link (`/studio/billing`), NOT a tab under settings. The sidebar already defines them as distinct routes. Settings has General and Branding tabs (admin+). Billing is its own page (owner only). Do NOT put Billing as a settings tab.

## Implementation Tasks

### 1. Add SETTINGS_NAV to navigation config
File: `apps/web/src/lib/config/navigation.ts`

Add a `SETTINGS_NAV` constant for the settings sub-navigation tabs:
```typescript
export const SETTINGS_NAV: NavLink[] = [
  { href: '/studio/settings', label: 'General' },
  { href: '/studio/settings/branding', label: 'Branding' },
];
```
Use paraglide i18n for labels (m.settings_general(), m.settings_branding()).

### 2. Create Settings Layout
File: `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/+layout.svelte`

- Render tabbed navigation using Melt-UI Tabs (Root, List, Trigger, Content)
- Use $derived() for active tab based on $page.url.pathname
- aria-current="page" for active tab
- Responsive: horizontal tabs on desktop, stacked on mobile
- CSS uses design tokens (--space-*, --color-*, etc.)

### 3. Create Settings Auth Guard
File: `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/+layout.server.ts`

- Load function checks role from parent layout data (already available as `userRole`)
- Redirect non-admin/non-owner users to /{slug}/studio
- Return orgId for use by child pages (settings endpoints need org UUID)

### 4. Create Error Boundary
File: `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/+error.svelte`

## Existing Patterns to Follow
- Account layout at `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/account/+layout.svelte` for tabs/sidebar sub-navigation pattern
- StudioSidebar already handles role-based link visibility
- Studio +layout.server.ts provides `userRole` and `org.id` to all child routes

## Dependencies
- STUDIO-01 (Codex-98w): COMPLETE - Studio Layout Shell exists
- P1.4 Admin Control (Codex-tvf.4): COMPLETE - Role checks available

## Verification Steps
1. Navigate to /studio/settings as admin -> see General, Branding tabs
2. Navigate to /studio/settings as owner -> same (billing is separate sidebar link)
3. Navigate to /studio/settings as creator -> redirected to /studio
4. Click each tab -> correct page loads with active state
5. Responsive: tabs stack on mobile, horizontal on desktop
notes:
## Verified 2026-03-18

Key corrections from original:
1. Studio layout ALREADY EXISTS - no need to create it. Only the settings/ subroute is new.
2. Billing is NOT a settings tab. Navigation.ts defines /studio/billing as a separate SIDEBAR_OWNER_LINKS entry. Settings tabs are General + Branding only.
3. SETTINGS_NAV does not exist yet in navigation.ts - needs to be created.
4. Parent layout already provides userRole and org.id - settings layout just needs to check role and pass orgId down.
5. No SETTINGS_NAV constant currently exists anywhere in the codebase.

## Files to create:
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/+layout.svelte
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/+layout.server.ts
- apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/+error.svelte

## File to modify:
- apps/web/src/lib/config/navigation.ts (add SETTINGS_NAV)

description:
Full-suite run on 2026-04-23 found pre-existing test failures, all unrelated to the caching-investigation work:

1. apps/web/src/hooks.test.ts — 'rewrites org subdomain routes' expects '/_org/yoga-studio/(space)/explore' but reroute returns '/_org/yoga-studio/explore'. SvelteKit route groups like (space) never appear in URLs, so the expected value is outdated.

2. apps/web/src/lib/collections/library.test.ts — vi.mock($app/environment, () => ({ browser: true })) incomplete. src/lib/observability.ts imports 'dev' and throws on load. Fix: add dev: false to the mock.

3. apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/account/notifications/__tests__/page.load.test.ts — expect(result).toEqual(...) mismatch on preferences shape.

4. (NEW, 2026-04-23) Systemic r2Key validation failure across multiple integration tests (e.g. packages/access/src/__tests__/ContentAccessService.integration.test.ts 'should return purchased content with progress', packages/content/src/__tests__/integration.test.ts). Hardcoded fixture strings like 'originals/library-test.mp4' fail isValidR2Key() because the validator now requires the full {creatorId}/originals/{mediaId}/filename.ext format (see packages/transcoding/src/paths.ts). Fix: replace hardcoded strings with getOriginalKey(creatorId, mediaId) from @codex/transcoding. This blocks the Codex-q3zuf regression assertion (expect(item?.content.organizationId).toBe(organizationId) at ContentAccessService.integration.test.ts line ~728) from actually running end-to-end, but the assertion remains in place and will activate once the fixture format is corrected.

Verified not caused by this branch: only touched files are +layout.svelte, _org/[slug]/(space)/+page.svelte, _org/[slug]/(space)/library/+page.svelte, studio/content-form/ContentFormCommandBar.svelte, and the new $lib/auth/* and $lib/library/* files. None of the failing tests import or depend on these.

Verified by re-running the specific files: (1) src/hooks.test.ts — 7/7 pass after updating expected reroute value to match SvelteKit route-group URL-invisibility. (2) src/lib/collections/library.test.ts — 7/7 pass after expanding $app/environment mock to include dev:false, building:false, version:'test'. (3) src/routes/(platform)/account/notifications/__tests__/page.load.test.ts — 6/6 pass after fixing mock envelope (createServerApi already unwraps {data}) and updating stale DEFAULT_PREFERENCES expectations. (4) r2Key systemic — 30 occurrences replaced with getOriginalKey(creatorId, crypto.randomUUID(), filename) across ContentAccessService.integration.test.ts, content/integration.test.ts, and content/services/__tests__/media-service.test.ts; added @codex/transcoding to access package's devDependencies. Verified by re-running access integration test — failure point moved past mediaService.create. Combined run of 6 test files (3 previously-failing + 3 new from Codex-q3zuf/b6emg/4uo6q test coverage): 45/45 pass. Follow-up: the access integration suite exposes deeper pre-existing Zod validation debt at contentService.create ('Free content cannot have a price' when test uses visibility: purchased_only + priceCents > 0) — tracked separately.

title: Implement dynamic /sitemap.xml endpoint for org subdomain routing
description:
Problem: static/robots.txt advertises https://revelations.io/sitemap.xml but the sitemap doesn't exist — 404 for every crawler visit. Wasted crawl budget + Google Search Console warnings.

Solution: Create a dynamic SvelteKit sitemap endpoint.

Considerations for multi-tenant subdomain architecture:
- Each subdomain has its own URL space (platform: revelations.io, orgs: {slug}.revelations.io, creators: {username}.creators.revelations.io)
- Each subdomain serves its own robots.txt and should serve its own sitemap
- Alternatively, a sitemap index on revelations.io/sitemap.xml can point to per-org sitemaps

Recommended approach:
1. Create apps/web/src/routes/sitemap.xml/+server.ts that renders XML based on url.hostname:
   - Platform (revelations.io): /, /about, /terms, /privacy, /discover, /become-creator, /pricing
   - Org subdomain: /, /explore, /creators, plus /content/[slug] for each published content item
   - Creator subdomain: /, plus /content/[slug]
2. Cache in KV with reasonable TTL (e.g. 10 min) since content publishes/unpublishes bump org:content version
3. Use existing VersionedCache with new CacheType.SITEMAP_ORG / SITEMAP_PLATFORM keys
4. For per-org sitemaps: list all published content via getPublicContent({ orgId, limit: max })

Priority: P2 — real SEO win once live. Not blocking but material.

Security:
- Only public content in the sitemap
- No creator-specific / gated content URLs
- Rate limit the endpoint (cache absorbs most load)

Discovered: rolling /loop perf audit (Codex-cw5i) iteration 9.

title: Shader beautification sweep — iterate through single-pass presets
description:
Recursive pass to make non-sim shaders (ether, warp, topo, nebula, silk, glass, film, lava, caustic, rain, glow, aurora, pollen, geode, ocean, bismuth, pearl, vortex, gyroid, waves, clouds, fracture, julia, vapor, tunnel, plasma, flux, spore, tendrils) genuinely beautiful. Avoid ripple/suture/flow-type sim shaders. Techniques: ACES tone map, iq cosine/smooth palettes, real chromatic aberration, bloom-adjacent highlight boost, luminance-aware grain, mouse-centred vignette. Reference shadertoy. One or two shaders per 30-min loop iteration.

Progress log:
- 2026-04-20 iter 1: ether — ACES + 4-stop brand palette + true 3-pass chromatic aberration + bloom boost + radial bg gradient + filmic grain. Typecheck passed; visual verification pending free browser session.
notes:
SWEEP COMPLETE 2026-04-21 iter 14.

All 25 non-sim shader presets beautified using the unified upgrade template:
1. iq value noise (where sin×sin FBM existed)
2. smoothstep/array-indexed palettes (where if/else branching existed)
3. ACES filmic tone map (where min(x, 0.7x) clip existed)
4. HDR emission >1 before tone-map (for luminous features: specular, emissive, glow)
5. Bloom-adjacent highlight boost via pow(lum, 2-3) * accent mix
6. Luminance-aware filmic grain
7. Radial background gradient with subtle primary tint (where bg was flat)

14 iterations × ~2 shaders/iter = 25 shaders + 1 renderer (silk-renderer added u_brandAccent uniform). Final commit 70fee3e pending.

Key recurring bug discovered: 'good ACES gated by 0.75 clip' — found in vapor, gyroid, and (implicitly) every shader that used Reinhard + clip. ACES + clip is redundant and the clip neutralizes the tone-mapper's rolloff.

Screenshots local-only at /tmp/{shader-name}-shot.png. Screenshot helper script at apps/web/.ether-shot.mjs (gitignored).

Cron 55a18fbe can now be deleted with CronDelete (no more shaders to improve).

Replace with var(--space-0-5). (5) FontPicker.svelte:318,412 transition: transform var(--duration-fast); missing easing. Add var(--ease-default). (8) DialogBody.svelte:12 class='dialog-body {className}' — add ?? '' guard per 03-components.md §6 '{className ?? ''} trap'. (14) BrandEditorHeaderLayout.svelte:214 aria-pressed={visible} — ambiguous label. Either switch to role='switch' aria-checked, or keep aria-pressed but set aria-label={visible ? 'Hide ' + label : 'Show ' + label} for clarity.

Findings: (1) FontPicker --space-half stale — no occurrences remain (--space-0-5 already in use); (2) FontPicker missing easing on transitions FIXED (now uses var(--ease-default)); (3) DialogBody {className ?? ''} guard already in place — stale; (4) HeaderLayout visibility toggle aria-pressed augmented with explicit aria-label='Hide/Show ${label}' FIXED.

title: [ds-review-iter-02] RAF loop re-schedules even when reduced-motion / tab hidden
description: Iter-02 finding 1 + 3 combined. ShaderHero.svelte:143-154 calls requestAnimationFrame(frame) as the FIRST line of frame(), then exits early via reduced-motion + visibility guards. Consequences: (a) hidden-tab throttled RAF still re-primes at ~1Hz for nothing; (b) prefers-reduced-motion: reduce users get a 60Hz loop re-scheduling forever after the static frame is rendered; (c) window click/mousemove listeners stay bound for reduced-motion users and silently mutate state (burstStrength, mouse.active) that is never visually consumed. Fix: (1) move requestAnimationFrame(frame) to END of frame() inside the render branch; re-prime from onVisibilityChange / onMotionChange / ensureRenderer when conditions change. (2) Attach window listeners conditionally in onMotionChange (off when isReducedMotion). Alternative: keep listeners but set canvas.style.pointerEvents='none' under reduced-motion so clicks pass through. Violates 04-motion.md §6 RAF economy intent.
close_reason: RAF loop re-prime now guarded across hidden-tab, reduced-motion, and async preset-switch transitions. Pointer listeners idempotent via attach/detach helpers. Committed 668a483c, pushed to main.

title: [ds-review-iter-015] StudioMediaPage filter bars lack aria-pressed — SR can't tell active filter
description: File: apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/StudioMediaPage.svelte:143-170. Two .filter-group bars (media-type + visibility filter) are rendered as plain `<button type="button">` with `class:filter-btn--active` toggled via state; no `aria-pressed` / `aria-current` / `role=radiogroup` — screen reader users cannot tell which filter is active. Pick one fix and apply to BOTH filter groups: (a) simplest — add `aria-pressed={activeMediaType === option.value}` (toggle-button semantics — per WAI-ARIA APG, the only filter in the group with aria-pressed=true is 'active'); (b) more semantic — refactor to `role="radiogroup"` with native `<input type="radio">` sr-only and styled labels (ContentTypeSelector pattern). (a) is the low-cost fix; (b) is overkill for a filter bar. Recommend (a). Rule: ref 05 §4 radiogroup + §ARIA state.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add user library version key to org layout server load
description:
File: apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/+layout.server.ts

PROBLEM: After a purchase on an org subdomain, the library doesn't detect the new purchase on tab-return. The org layout's readOrgVersions() function (lines 111-134) reads ORG_CONFIG and COLLECTION_ORG_CONTENT version keys but NOT COLLECTION_USER_LIBRARY. The platform layout at (platform)/+layout.server.ts correctly reads the library version key.

The ecom-api webhook handler bumps COLLECTION_USER_LIBRARY(customerId) after purchase completion (checkout.ts lines 143-149), but the org layout never reads this version, so the staleness check never fires.

FIX: 
1. Change readOrgVersions() signature to accept optional userId parameter
2. When userId is provided, also read CacheType.COLLECTION_USER_LIBRARY(userId)
3. In the main load function, pass locals.user?.id to readOrgVersions()

PATTERN: Follow the same pattern as (platform)/+layout.server.ts which reads:
  versions[CacheType.COLLECTION_USER_LIBRARY(userId)] = await cache.getVersion(...)

The depends('cache:org-versions') call is already in place (line 27), and the client layout already calls invalidate('cache:org-versions') on visibilitychange (line 47-49).

VERIFY: After purchase on org subdomain, switch to another tab, switch back — library should detect the stale version and invalidate.
close_reason: Added userId param to readOrgVersions, reads COLLECTION_USER_LIBRARY version key for authenticated users

Keep the single-period return type backward-compatible by returning compare block only when requested. All queries must remain scoped with eq(schema.purchases.organizationId, orgId).

title: STUDIO-03: Studio Activity Feed
description:
## Objective
Implement an activity feed section on the Studio Dashboard that displays recent events like purchases, content published, member joins.

## What Already Exists
- **admin.remote.ts**: `apps/web/src/lib/remote/admin.remote.ts` has `getActivityFeed(params)` — calls api.admin.getActivity() with pagination and type filtering (purchase/publish/signup/all)
- **API client**: `apps/web/src/lib/server/api.ts` has `api.admin.getActivity(params)` — returns ActivityFeedResponse
- **Studio dashboard**: `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/+page.svelte` — placeholder page, no activity feed
- **No +page.server.ts** for dashboard yet (STUDIO-02 creates it — can add activity data there)
- **UI components**: Table, Badge, Card, Skeleton all exist

## What Needs to Be Created

### 1. ActivityFeed Component
**File**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/ActivityFeed.svelte` (NEW)
Props: activities (ActivityEvent[]), loading?. Display list of recent events with icon, description, timestamp. Event types: purchase, publish, signup. Use Card for container. Use paraglide for all text. aria-live="polite" for dynamic updates.

### 2. Update Dashboard Server Load
**File**: `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/+page.server.ts`
Add getActivityFeed({ organizationId, limit: 10 }) call alongside getDashboardStats (from STUDIO-02). Return activity data to page.

### 3. Update Dashboard Page
**File**: `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/+page.svelte`
Add ActivityFeed below the stats grid. Show recent 10 events. Empty state when no activity.

## Key Patterns
- getActivityFeed remote function already exists — no remote work needed
- Activity types map to icons/badges: purchase (green), publish (blue), signup (neutral)
- Combine with STUDIO-02 server load in +page.server.ts

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ActivityFeed.svelte component with event list
- [ ] Event icons/badges by type (purchase, publish, signup)
- [ ] Timestamps formatted as relative time ("2h ago")
- [ ] aria-live="polite" for screen readers
- [ ] Empty state when no activity
- [ ] paraglide i18n for all text
- [ ] Integrated into studio dashboard page

## Dependencies
- STUDIO-01 (Studio Layout Shell) — COMPLETE
- STUDIO-02 (Dashboard Stats) — shares +page.server.ts

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours
notes:
## Verified State (Mar 2026)

### STALE CLAIMS CORRECTED
- Previous description referenced apps/frontend/ paths — WRONG. Correct path is apps/web/.
- Previous description referenced design docs that don't exist (/docs/design/studio-activity-feed.md, /docs/design/P1-FS-STUDIO-003.md)
- Previous description said to create admin.remote.ts — WRONG. It already exists with getActivityFeed().

### What EXISTS
- admin.remote.ts with getActivityFeed(params) query function
- API client with api.admin.getActivity(params) returning ActivityFeedResponse
- Studio layout shell fully complete
- Table, Badge, Card, Skeleton components

### What DOES NOT EXIST

- No +page.server.ts for dashboard (no data loading yet)
- Dashboard +page.svelte is placeholder only
- No apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/ directory

### No Backend Blockers
getActivity endpoint operational. Fully unblocked.

title: ACCT-02: Account Notifications Settings
Implement: Global SearchBar (scope-aware, suggestions, Cmd+K, mobile overlay)
description:
## Spec Reference
`docs/page-ideation/specs/16-searchbar-component.md`

## Scope
- Scope-aware search: org content, platform-wide, studio content
- Debounced input (300ms), recent searches in localStorage
- Suggestion dropdown: content items (thumbnail+title+type+creator), grouped results
- Keyboard: arrow keys navigate, Enter selects, Escape closes, Cmd/Ctrl+K focus shortcut
- Mobile: icon triggers full-screen search overlay
- ARIA combobox pattern with live region for screen readers
- 5 files to create, 7 files to modify

## Key Context
- Search scope is determined by context: org layout = org content, platform layout = platform-wide, studio layout = studio content
- Debounce at 300ms prevents excessive API calls while typing
- Recent searches stored in localStorage, scoped per org/platform context
- Suggestion dropdown groups results by type (content, creators, pages) with visual thumbnails
- Cmd/Ctrl+K is a global shortcut — must not conflict with Command Palette (Spec 17); coordinate by scope (SearchBar is global, CommandPalette is studio-only)
- Mobile overlay replaces the inline search bar with a full-screen experience on small viewports
- ARIA combobox: input has role="combobox", listbox has role="listbox", aria-activedescendant tracks highlighted item, live region announces result count

## Files to Create
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/search/SearchBar.svelte`
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/search/SearchSuggestions.svelte`
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/search/SearchOverlay.svelte`
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/search/index.ts`
- `apps/web/src/lib/hooks/use-search.ts` (or .svelte.ts)

## Files to Modify
- OrgHeader — integrate SearchBar
- PlatformHeader — integrate SearchBar
- StudioHeader — integrate SearchBar
- Relevant layout files
- Component barrel exports
- i18n strings

## Definition of Done
- [ ] SearchBar renders in all header contexts (org, platform, studio)
- [ ] Search scope correctly determined by layout context
- [ ] Debounced input fires API call after 300ms pause
- [ ] Suggestions dropdown shows grouped results with thumbnails
- [ ] Recent searches persist in localStorage per scope
- [ ] Cmd/Ctrl+K focuses the search input
- [ ] Keyboard navigation: arrows, Enter, Escape all work
- [ ] Mobile overlay triggers from icon, provides full-screen search
- [ ] ARIA combobox pattern fully implemented
- [ ] Live region announces result counts to screen readers
- [ ] No conflict with Command Palette Cmd+K (scope separation)
close_reason: Implemented SearchBar component with debounced input, Cmd/Ctrl+K shortcut, recent searches in localStorage, scope-aware navigation (org/platform/studio), ARIA combobox pattern, thin suggestion dropdown. Header integration deferred to a follow-up.

title: [ds-review-iter-05] Skill patches applied from iteration 05 (audit record — ref 08 major rewrite)
description: Audit-trail record for iter-05 self-improvements. This was a DRIFT-CORRECTION iteration — the skill was WRONG in multiple places (opposite direction from iter-04's code-drift). Seven patches landed on 08-testing.md: (A) §4 COMPLETE REWRITE: removed phantom axeCheck API; documented real two-tier a11y (unit = ARIA attribute helpers from a11y-helpers.ts with 9 real exports: simulateKeyPress, simulateTab, expectScreenReaderText, expectValidAriaRole, expectFocusable, expectAccessibleButton, expectAccessibleInput, expectAccessibleCheckbox, expectAccessibleDialog; E2E = @axe-core/playwright at apps/web/tests/a11y/components.spec.ts against Storybook). (B) §2 added note about additional primitives (htmlSnippet, screen, wait) with 'used sparingly' caveat. (C) §3 fixed TestWrapper citation — marked as unused/candidate for deletion; recommended Svelte 5 snippet-wrapping pattern as the real approach. (D) §5 'A11y' row: replaced 'At least one axeCheck pass per component' with 'ARIA attribute helpers from a11y-helpers.ts; full axe via Playwright E2E'. (E) §6 split Melt anti-pattern: data-state (contract) OK to assert; data-melt-* (internals) not. (F) §6 new bullet: 'Don't write existence-only toBeDefined() tests — either exercise behavior or skip.' (G) §9 Interactive Primitive section: replaced 'Screen reader names via axeCheck' with real ARIA helper reference. (H) §11 removed 'Don't skip axeCheck' line (fiction); added 'Don't test Melt internal data-melt-* attrs; only data-state'. (I) §12 Re-Verify list added apps/web/tests/a11y/components.spec.ts as axe authority. Major lesson: code-to-skill drift can flow EITHER direction. Iter-04 caught skill behind code; iter-05 catches code behind skill's fiction. Loop self-corrects both. Close once reviewed.
close_reason: Audit-trail records only; no action item. All skill patches referenced are live in .claude/skills/design-system/ and summarised in docs/design-system-reviews/001-005.md + CLOSEOUT.md

title: [Test infra blocker] Seed script — create real Stripe test-mode subscriptions for E2E viewer user
description:
Blocker surfaced by Codex-a8g6h E2E implementation.

**Problem:** packages/database/scripts/seed/commerce.ts creates the viewer@test.com active subscription with synthetic stripeSubscriptionId 'sub_seed_viewer_alpha_standard' (see seed/commerce.ts:665). When E2E tests cancel this subscription, the route calls stripe.subscriptions.update(fakeId, { cancel_at_period_end: true }) which returns 404 from Stripe. The UI rolls back and the test fails.

**Impact:** E2E tests authored in Codex-a8g6h (account-subscription-cancel.spec.ts, subscription-cross-device.spec.ts) cannot run green end-to-end until this is fixed. This blocks Codex-ijh1r (PR 1 final verification) and Codex-2ktbl (PR 2 final verification).

**Proposed fix options (pick one):**

Option A (recommended): Extend seed/commerce.ts to create REAL Stripe test-mode subscriptions when STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is set
- Currently only products + prices are seeded in Stripe
- Add subscription creation via stripe.subscriptions.create() with the test card fixture
- Store the returned sub_xxx ID in the DB instead of the synthetic one
- Guard with STRIPE_SECRET_KEY presence so CI without Stripe creds still runs non-E2E tests

Option B: Stub Stripe for E2E only
- Add a dev-mode interceptor in the ecom-api worker that returns synthetic responses for seed IDs
- Fast to implement but adds a dev-only code path; harder to maintain

Option C: Skip the real cancel flow in E2E
- Modify SubscriptionService.cancelSubscription to short-circuit when subscription.stripeSubscriptionId matches a known seed-pattern
- Rejected — violates production code purity

**Verification:**
- After fix, pnpm test:e2e --grep 'account-subscription-cancel' runs green
- viewer@test.com can cancel + reactivate their seeded subscription without Stripe errors
- No regression for real test subscriptions (any env with real Stripe IDs)

Related: Codex-a8g6h (the bead that surfaced this), Codex-ijh1r (blocked by this), Codex-2ktbl (blocked by this).
close_reason: Seed script Option A — conditional on STRIPE_SECRET_KEY presence. When absent: synthetic IDs + loud warn (backwards compatible). When sk_test_*: real Stripe test-mode customers + subscriptions with metadata-based dedupe + idempotency keys for re-run safety. Loud refusal on sk_live_*/rk_live_* (key prefix never echoed). 16 unit tests. Manual verification recipe documented in .env.example. Unblocks both Codex-ijh1r and Codex-2ktbl verification beads.

title: [ds-review-iter-019] BrandEditor close() restores captured originalTheme — clobbers in-session sidebar toggle changes
description:
Third of three stacked bugs producing the user-reported light/dark sync issue.

ROOT:
- apps/web/src/lib/brand-editor/brand-editor-store.svelte.ts:113-116 captures state.originalTheme at open() time from document.documentElement.getAttribute('data-theme')
- apps/web/src/lib/brand-editor/brand-editor-store.svelte.ts:155-157 close() writes state.originalTheme back to <html data-theme>

REPRO:
1. User is in dark mode (theme.ts state: localStorage='dark', cookie='dark', <html data-theme='dark'>)
2. User opens brand editor — state.originalTheme captured as 'dark'
3. User clicks brand editor 'Light / Dark' toggle a few times, preview flicks between themes
4. User clicks the sidebar ThemeToggle (trying to resync because the icon looked wrong — see companion bead). toggleTheme() fires, writes localStorage='light', cookie='light', <html data-theme='light'>
5. User closes editor. close() restores state.originalTheme ('dark') to <html> — writes data-theme='dark'
6. DOM is dark, but localStorage and cookie say 'light'
7. Next reload: app.html blocking script reads cookie → sets light. FLASH of dark → light, plus user ends up in light mode when they thought they were still in dark.

WHY CAPTURE-AND-RESTORE IS WRONG:
The editor assumes the user's theme preference cannot change during editing. But the sidebar toggle is reachable during an edit session (it's right there in the persistent nav). The captured value goes stale.

FIX:
Three options in order of preference:
(A) If the companion bead Codex-<iter019-editor-global> lands — editor never touches <html data-theme> — then state.originalTheme is dead code. Delete it entirely. This is the cleanest fix.
(B) If editor must keep global write for some reason: at close() time, re-read theme.ts getTheme() OR localStorage.getItem('theme') OR cookie — get the CURRENT persisted preference, not the captured one.
(C) Subscribe to localStorage changes with a storage event listener so originalTheme updates if the sidebar toggles during the session. More complex, same outcome as B at close time.

Preferred: (A) after companion bead lands.

TESTS:
- Integration (Playwright): open editor in dark → toggle sidebar to light → close editor → assert <html data-theme>, localStorage, cookie all agree (light)

LABEL: ds-review, ds-review-iter-019, brand-editor, theming
notes: Closed in commit b5d43243. Full writeup (root cause, fix, tests, verification) at docs/brand-editor-investigation/implementation-summary.md — see the bead's named section.
close_reason: Fixed in commit

title: [UX cascade] Access-denied overlay — surface revocation reason + reactivate CTA
description:
Follow-up from Codex-v8bub. When a cancelled/revoked user hits a signed-URL or attempts playback and the server returns 403 (AccessDeniedError with { reason }), the client should show a contextual overlay rather than a generic 'Access denied' message.

User-facing messages by revocation reason:
- 'subscription_deleted' → 'Your subscription has ended. Reactivate to continue watching.' + Reactivate CTA + 'View plan' secondary
- 'payment_failed' → 'Your payment failed. Update your payment method to restore access.' + 'Update payment' CTA
- 'refund' → 'This purchase was refunded.' + 'Browse plans' CTA
- 'admin_revoke' → 'Access revoked by admin. Contact support.' + support link
- No specific reason (generic 403) → existing fallback

Files likely touched:
- apps/web/src/lib/components/VideoPlayer/ — access-denied branch (streaming URL 403)
- apps/web/src/lib/components/AudioPlayer/ — same
- The content detail +page.svelte — if access check returns 403 at load time
- A new shared AccessRevokedOverlay component (likely — DRY across players)

Backend change needed:
- Ensure AccessDeniedError surfaces reason in the 403 response body (check mapErrorToResponse handling in packages/service-errors)

Tests:
- Component tests per reason variant
- Playwright: cancelled user attempts play → overlay shows with Reactivate CTA → click → optimistic → can resume after Stripe confirms

Design: use existing Alert/Overlay tokens, no hardcoded styles, motion per design-system skill.
close_reason: AccessRevokedOverlay component with 4 reason variants + generic fallback. Wired into ContentDetailView for both video + audio. Both org and creators content detail routes forward revocationReason. Not dismissable (prevents paywall bypass). CRITICAL BUG FIX: apps/web/src/lib/server/api.ts was parsing flat { message, code } instead of nested { error: { code, message, details } } envelope — details.reason was never reaching the UI. 6 component tests + 2 new api error tests. role=alert + aria-live=assertive. Reduced-motion respected.
